reviews.review_text: Labeled as an introductory class though most assignments require you to have previous coding experience. She has a hard time teaching; she is full of knowledge but fails to express it to her students effectively. Her lectures are hard to follow and she tends to make several last minute changes to her assignments and their due dates.

Professor Najjar is pretty nice/calm overall, but it is hard to keep up during lectures as she stumbles over her words a lot, lots of pauses and stuttering. It is pretty frustrating trying to understand and follow along. She also makes lots of mistakes, typos and things. TAs also expect you to know the material when you need their help.
Barely made it out with a B. lucky to not have an F. massive difficulty jump from test 1 to 2. Her teaching is like a middle schoolers who can't teach.

Dr. Najjar makes computer science very accessible for beginners who want to learn. 60% of the grade comes from three exams and a final, the grading structure has enough flexibility that one bad test won't ruin your grade. There's a big jump in difficulty after test 2, so stay engaged. Attend labs, put in some effort, and an A is very achievable.

Take this class if you can self teach the concepts to yourself and rely only on the textbook. You go into the labs and the TA's expects you to know what you read on the textbook. If you are not good in coding  I do not recommend Prof. Najjar. It is not really an introductory course with this professor. No opportunities of extra credit or retakes.

Avoid if possible. Practice quizzes don't align with actual exam material, making prep difficult. Feedback is extremely slow; grades went unposted for two months, leaving me unsure of my standing between an A or a C. It was a discouraging experience because the instructor provided very little helpful guidance throughout the term.

Dr. Wilson is the greatest professor I have ever taken at UNC Charlotte. She always does their best to support students wherever they can. Super often she (or her TAs) lets students turn assignments in late if they need to, and she's always super understanding. Labs are kinda unorganized but super helpful if you pay attention.

This class was not good. I couldn't find any time to focus on my other courses and most of the learning was conceptual, not practical. She mostly just shows you some slides, has you do a quiz, then sends you out to do code and projects without truly understanding it. The Course Canvas is also unorganized. I will say the TAs were good.
she great-kisses

Terrible. Time wasted in class forcing participation, no real study material for tests, no learning material(she posts two videos at the start of the week made by another professor) never reviews this material in class. Tests contain content never seen or discussed in class. TA's making a fraction of her salary are more beneficial to my learning.

For the majority of the class, you will be presenting and watching others present slides in groups made based on articles you can just find online. There is also a term project with confusing requirements, told to choose something you are passionate about and would interest him. You can do all of this without taking the class and spending tuition.

Professor has a habit of walking up to students and bothering them, oftentimes when they're trying to work. This semester he is also incredibly late on our final grades. The due date for grades was supposed to be May 12 and we still have not gotten the grades for our final projects. This class is quite easy, but this professor will annoy you.

Pretty good professor. He is an older guy, so his style can be a bit out of touch but if you really pay attention it shouldn't be too hard. Kind of bad at explaining any problems you have though, but will also push you to try and achieve more than you thought, and pushes you to go further outside the class. Very nice and understanding.

For backend software engineering, you will definitely learn a lot. Her lecture videos are posted on canvas and are very easy to follow. The assignments are just challenging enough to build your proficiency with Node.js and Layered Architecture, without being overwhelming. She also clearly cares about her students and is very nice to talk to.
Professor Cao does not play.
Video lectures are long and excessive with very little teachings. The final project criteria was excessive and unrealistic regarding how deliverables should be contributed to.
Professor is pretty bad at her video lectures. Don't take her if you already have no interest in the topic.

I signed up for this course over the summer, even though she wasn't the instructor for my section. The canvas videos were all made by her. This course was laid out as an end to end tutorial which is bad in my opinion, there is no critical thinking in this course. Probably the easiest A ive ever had. That being said, she does explain things well.

Class is incredibly disorganized, and accent is difficult to understand. Lectures are all over the place and she never finishes on time. Assignments are kinda random and vary a lot in time/difficulty. 1 midterm that was only worth 10% of the grade. She grades attendance but it's not worth a ton.

Shes not a bad professor but her class needs more clarity, it is often disorganized. She has so many little things to cover everyday but never actually finish in class. She will randomly make assignments optional. The course content is good and useful, though the delivery isnt very good. The assignments also very alot in terms of difficulty.

A lot of assignments that can get annoying. That being said the absolute best prof for this class. The beginning is hard but later on it gets a lot easier. Class is 5x harder with any other prof. She re-teaches the basics of DSA so don't feel like you will get lost in advanced stuff (basically dsa 1.5 not really 2.0)

This course focuses on Databases, primarily using SQL. While there were several HWs due each week, the instructions and grading were very straight forward. Lectures did not typically last long, and often times ended with her helping with HW assignments. The workload in the class was more than many of my others, but the difficulty was low.

She is hands down the worst professor here at UNC Charlotte. Do not take this class if you actually want to learn something. This class has felt like a scam since day 1. Her accent is also extremely thick and if you are unfamiliar with it you likely will have a hard time understanding her. Poor wording on most of if not all tests/quizzes.

PTA's grade harsh, they are not great at feedback, and don't answer questions in any helpful way. Grading is very slow, so for the amount of homework, you're often repeating mistakes because you haven't gotten a grade for similar assignments back. If I took this in person with a different professor, I don't think it would be this bad.
Great professor. He is very aware of current trends and practical skills in software engineering. Classwork was not overly difficult but was conducive to learning.

the professor is really nice and the class so far has been easier than i thought. i was worried because he had such a low rating on here, but i think they changed the curriculum because the course hasn't been super difficult at all or work heavy, and i'm still learning a lot. overall would absolutely recommend this professor!

Bunch of extra credit. he is a chill prof. TAs do the grading and half the labs are already done for you. This guy on zoom is so free. The concepts are light and the prof dosen't care for most things which is lit haha. Attendence is taken like once a month and is a quiz where you put the word he said out loud. Freest section you can take for 3155

Took this class online during the summer. Had zero interaction with the professor, the TAs do all the grading. The course however is basically all slideshows with a brief introduction to software engineering topics. Several quizzes and an assignment due every week. The group project was sort of disorganized, easy A regardless.

My grandmother mother has been really sick. Dr. Sahoo has been really caring, helpful and supportive. I have 4 more classes this semester. No other professors has been such supportive. She is a great human being, listens to your problems and find out a way to help you succeed. She has deep command over the material. Great class

Very caring and understanding professor. I am having some personal issues, she has been consistently supportive. Lecture are brief and amazing. She mentions interview questions in classroom discussions which is really helpful. Take her if you can

I just had couple of classes with her, what an absolute pleasure to learn from her! In the first class of learning, I was not able to figure out few things, she sat with me, explaining me each steps patiently. I have never had such experience in CLT. Next class, again I observed her going over the steps with some folks diligently. Rare to find!

Honestly one of the better professors I've had. Explains concepts clearly and actually cares if students understand, not just memorize. Lectures are structured, organized, and make hard topics feel manageable.

Workload is fair . you have to put in effort, but it's never busywork. Exams are reasonable if you keep up with class and practice.

Very approachable and supportive. Will help if you ask questions and genuinely wants students to succeed. Also flexible and understanding when life happens (rare but appreciated).

If you show up for code review pay attention, and do the work, you'll be fine. Would recommend 100%.

1213 is a HUGE class, so you will only interact with Professor Whaley if you put in the effort to do so. Caring professor, confusing class structure. I had to guesstimate my grade the entire semester using RapidTables. Easy exams but you have to put in the work to self-learn.

Mr. Whaley mostly leads the online lectures once a week and leaves the labs to the TAs, so there isn't as much interaction with him. Although you don't interact with him as much, his online lectures are very entertaining, and he's very lenient with homework. The assignments themselves are not too hard, especially with previous knowledge of Java

Good professor and made it relatively easy to do well in the class. He provides extra credit opportunities and options to retake exams. I didn't do well on all of the exams, but found my grade to still stand at an A. Make sure to do the textbook assignments, show up to labs/lectures + TA review sessions, and do the module quizzes as MCQ prep!

Professor offers little real help and his online explanations feel random and unorganized. If you rely on your instructor to actually teach, don't take this class. The TAs are the only real support. The course is very confusing at the start, even experienced students struggle. I do not recommend. Save yourself the stress and choose wisely.

He is willing to help students if that is necessary like if you have technical issues but he's a professor you hardly see but he is there and he also cares about making sure you can do well in the class.

Absolutely appalling that this professor is still allowed to "teach", if you can even call it that. Zero lectures, Zero reading, just stolen content from youtube. Vague, confusing, and boring assignments that we were forced to use AI on because he taught us the programming language that the assignments were supposed to be done in.

Each project has an outdated and poor outline of every step that has to be taken to complete it. Prerequisites don't cover what has to be known and he doesn't go over it. He is also very strict on attendance even though he never teaches. He would VERY reluctantly open a poll everywhere for another 15 seconds if you miss the narrow window it's open.

To say he teaches is an overstatement that would make even Peter Molyneux blush. This professor does not teach you how to do anything, he instead opts to just give you 8 pages of instructions and let you figure it out. He responds to genuine issues with sarcasm, and he does not even seem like he wants to be there. Pass.

I have taken a few courses with Dr. Mejias and he consistently sets clear expectations with regards to due dates, what will be on tests, and where students may get stuck on assignments and how to overcome those issues.

He has a good grasp of the material. And also provides test reviews prior to exams.

This class was a joke. We were told first day that we "weren't going to be doing any coding" in a software development class... He then assigned us 5 projects, the last one consisting of 4 parts, and that was basically the whole class. But he would give us answers to literally everything except the final (you can use AI). Easiest A I've ever had.

I took this class in the Fall and the professor is great. His accent is good, and he understands the subject. He strictly adheres to this book - "Neal Davis - AWS Practice Questions" (kindle version). for all questions. He really wants his students to get the AWS certification (CLF-02 exam). I would recommend taking him for THIS course atleast.

Great at lecturing, but the exams are full of “gotcha” questions (mean: 61, median: 64). In the last few weeks he ran out of material, so class turned into watching student presentations at 1.5x speed. We still didn't get through everyone, and it cost us our entire final review session. It's obvious his heart is in research rather than teaching.

Requires HTB access code ($300) for class and won't budge. HTB is managed by TAs who consistently let you down. Independent HTB done prior as a student doesn't count towards this grade, he said the only reason was because of the deal he's got with HTB. Very little guidance offered, but the class content itself is very interesting.

Professor relies heavily on an outdated Google Doc syllabus that is very hard to navigate. Lectures consist of 2 hrs of boring content and sometimes explaining assignments. Textbook is a $300 HTB subscription that is weighted more than other assignments. Exams are alright but not much content to study. Great guy and knowledgeable not good teacher.

I had a good experience with this professor.  The projects are not too difficult, and he will demo the solution or most of the solution in class before the project is due.  He is a little disorganized, but everything is present on the syllabus.  The midterm and final are really difficult and do not line up well with the quizzes and projets.

This demanding course required building about two mobile apps per week and watching lectures. It focused on Android and iOS, letting students choose their platform. Industry-focused assignments supported self-paced learning and helped me build a strong app portfolio that performed well in internship interviews.

This course was very demanding, requiring me to build about two mobile apps per week and spend time watching the instructor's YouTube lectures. The workload was intense but rewarding. I learned a lot and have done well in internship interviews by showcasing a strong portfolio of apps built in this class.

The class was on mobile development with a focus on Java and XML languages. The workload is extensive with up to 2 assignments per week, however they are not difficult. The lectures are all online, while class time is spent working on homework assignments. The tests and assignments are all similar to his lecture videos.

Professor Shehab is an outstanding educator who integrates his software development expertise into the classroom. I appreciated his YouTube videos and the active learning methods he employed during lessons. Be prepared to put in the effort and master coding skills.

Professor is an exceptional instructor with a hands-on teaching approach and excellent instructional videos that make complex concepts easy to grasp. His focus on real-world applications has been invaluable in my day-to-day job as a software engineer. The projects I completed in his class helped me build a portfolio that led to my summer internship
There are surprise attendance checks and pop quizzes. Besides that, he is a good instructor, kind of funny, and is very open to helping however he can. Also a semi-heavy accent.

The professor is kind, but the class organization was all over the place. Easily the most disorganized course I've ever taken. No one I know who has taken 2181 has had a good experience. If you have to take this course for your major, good luck.
Heavy accent but good professor. He cares and will help

Flipped-style classroom, with slideshows gone over again in class. He is very open to re-explaining topics and truly wants his students to succeed. This class is not light in work, but there are plenty of lecture videos, coding videos, samples, and slides you can refer to. Great teacher!

The tests are difficult since they're all hand-written, and I often cut it pretty close to the timer even coming in with a lot of experience and studying beforehand. You definitely need to know your stuff, but Dr. Yan gives you plenty of resources and opportunities to learn it and his lectures are great. Definitely recommend.

I really enjoyed this class and thought Professor Shin was an excellent teacher. Nothing about the class was particularly difficult, however there are a lot of assignments to keep up with every week. Seems like he cares about his students and really enjoys teaching cs.

Easy class with almost no work. All your work is done in assigned groups so if you get a group you don't like, it might be tougher. The professor is very laid back but he expects you to try. He genuinely wants you to succeed but expects you to put in a little work.

This professor was very engaging and wanted us to succeed since public speaking is an important skill to master. As for the work, it's not difficult since the majority of it is group work. You are graded on very few things, so make sure to submit the best version you have, since, in my case, a bad grade will bring your grade down.

Most of the work in the class is done in predetermined groups so if you get stuck with people who don't show up or care to participate you are screwed. Showing up on time and participating is the majority of the grade. If you get a bad group it will drag your grade down as he does group grades not individual.

The professor was very hands-off, didn't really teach anything, you just do discussions/papers/power points, which are based on a vague prompt you have to research on your own. It was a gen-ed, so I guess that's a good thing. Very easy class if you don't mind writing a lot.

His class is an interesting one . Be ready since attendance is mandatory 
The class is competitive and requires interaction with classmates since there's a project given to groups the beginning of the class so does what you do the whole semester.
It's a great class .

Overall really bad. around 75 percent of us failed a test bc she didn't prepare us for it said their were different questions on it then what their actually were,and didn't give us a practice test until two days before I know 75 percent of us failed bc she gives us a class grade breakdown She also wouldn't curve the grade DO NOT TAKE her class

This is not an easy elective. You have to learn a lot of vocabulary and there are too many readings on ZyBooks that you must memorize for in-person quizzes. The teacher is alright, but it's just too much homework. Cheat sheets could make up for all the memorization though.

she was a good professor and gave great extra credit at the end of the semester. she has a few problems with her speech but it didn't matter too much. i think if i studied more i could've gotten a higher grade but i still ended with an A and didn't really put a lot of effort in.

She is flexible on attendance and grades but there are a lot of presentations and Labs she stutters a bit at times. As long as you complete the labs, discussion post, and final project on time this class will be fairly easy to understand The midterm and final were a bit long but she does give an option for a cheat sheet DONT skip a lab assignment

flexible. lots of presentations. lowkey had an annoying voice. the midterm and final were easy ! the project was fairly easy. just lots of really tedious labs and lectures weren't helpful. gave extensions if needed

online class but lots of group works and group projects. in-class and prep work is worth most of grade. assigns the same amount of readings and prep for grads and undergrads, so if your undergrad, it feels like this class is much more of my week then my other normal undergrad classes. Also only offered at night.

Physical Computing is probably my favorite course so far and Dr. Wilson is def 1 of my favorite profs! Take his class if you want to make neat stuff! There's a decent amount of work and it's hard to catch up if you fall behind, but Dr. Wilson is very helpful with questions if you struggle with a concept. Idk why the bad reviews, he's a great prof!

The professor was pleasant, but this class was effectively teaching you to use the worst possible tool for a given task. We would spend hours building low-fidelity, inefficient artifacts that would probably get you fired from a workplace if you got caught building on the clock. You complete dozens of assignments to produce unimpressive products.

Only thing I like about this class: During group assignments, you're only responsible for your portion. This week I have a project, quiz prep work 3x, and in-class work. It's the same thing EVERY week, besides the project and prep work is usually 2-1x. He hides your overall grade. Acting like I don't have 4 other classes. Very time-consuming :/

I absolutely loved this course and, though there is a lot of group projects and work, I felt like I learned a lot from it all that I'm still using today. His class is also very well-organized and was just a lot of fun. I would absolutely take a class with him again!

I am in my third year at this university and this has been the worst class I have ever taken. Get ready to teach yourself because the professor and the TAs will not reply to emails. I sent an email regarding a command that did not work and the professor simply said "the command did not work". The TAs also took days to respond.

Grading felt unclear and inconsistent. TAs handled most grading, and regrade requests went back to the same grader, so there was no fresh review. I would have preferred more professor involvement and clearer feedback. Students who value transparency just stay away.

Great professor who makes the class fun and interesting. It can get overwhelming near the end with an essay, test and final game project, but staying on top of the work keeps it manageable. Highly recommended for future game devs. He gives a lot of extra credit. The tests are the hardest part but with the test reviews the TAs run, it makes it easy.

Bahamon is a great lecturer, the tests were very hard though and he didn't really give any specific study guide, they had a lot of vocabulary that you just need to study for straight up. He's very understanding though, if you talk to him about something, he'll be up front about it.
Decent professor who cares about his students. That being said, the tests are hard and he puts a lot of weight on them.

I loved having Dr Ahmed for Artificial Intelligence (6150) and for Data Structures (6114). If you are trying to float through the class do not take him. He is very passionate about helping his students learn and is very purposeful with the way he teaches and the way he gives activities. Very hands on course work and many opportunities to succeed!

you will get sure A+ if you have access to his old questions. And his TAs are always indians, I saw they help the students who are indian in their own language during exams and i did not like that. anyways the grading was easy if you did everything right in assignments and projects.

ITCS 6114 is a data structures and algorithms based class. While no coding is conducted in the class, well over 30 different algorithms are discussed in great detail. The workload in the class is not heavy, There were not many HW assignments, test, or projects. However grading was very strict, and failing 1 assignment or test could lead to failing

Him being part of the class is a waste of time. The lectures is just reading off of slides verbatim. Class project is just busy work and strung together so badly that you are left confused as to what you have to do. Really needs to update his material it is severely outdated. Probably just another research professor who does not care. 100% avoid.

Does not teach well if at all. Just reads the slides. A good idea of how he lectures:
This is our problem, now this is how to solve it, *clicks slide* there thats how we did it.
Does not explain anything well and I'm not even sure he is doing some things right. I saw his same problem on youtube and they both had different answers. Stay away.

I appreciated his relaxed yet effective style. Instead of traditional lectures, he guided us through a hands‑on project, gave clear, constructive feedback through weekly meetings, and fostered a supportive atmosphere. Expectations were fair, deadlines reasonable, and grading transparent. Highly recommend!

Dobbs gives great feedback and is readily available, while being very hands-off. His
industry experience, adaptability, and openness to all feedback help students feel engaged. 

While the structure and grading can be slightly confusing, he listens and improves quickly, making the learning experience pleasant. Effort and asking questions= easy A/B!
The professor gives too much work and often time does not understand what he is teaching himself. Going to ask for help to him is like talking to a stone, nothing useful at all.
Prof. Dobbs was really helpful whenever we as a group or I individually had any questions

Professor Dodds emphasizes practical understanding over rote memorization and encourages consistent attendance, participation, and submission of work with strong grades. In ITSC 3155, he designs the class and content to closely mirror real-world computing environments.
Homework assignments are long, tedious, and don't really teach you anything.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!! 

If you can wait another semester to take it or you have a second option take that and don't look back. 

His homework is extremely complicated (even his TAs get confused), he doesn't go over any of the coding in class - just rambles about unrelated stuff

This is easily the worst professor/class I have ever had at UNCC

Nice man. Terrible class. This was by far one of the worst and hardest classes I have ever taken. The material in this class is so unnecessary and so confusing. Instructions are unclear and confusing. This class is basically in a foreign language.

Professor Wartell is an awful professor, he left the class a halfway through the semester and had another professor teach his class. He had no TA and had trouble trying to grade our first assignments. We never received any of our grades for the assignments until we had to bombard the TA of the other professor for grades. This class is unnecessary

There are 2 entries referring to the same guy on here. Hands down, best prof/mentor I've had. Gives great lectures and relevant advice to CS students pursuing dev careers. Yes, he goes fast but he records everything so if you miss something just watch the video (duh)? Absolutely take any course he teaches if you actually want a future in tech.

He teaches at a very fast pace, which makes it impossible to follow along during in-class demos.  The code that he teaches is not in the lecture slides and there is no textbook.  If you did not write down the code in class, you must either page through the entire recorded lecture video, or look it up online.  There is a huge push to teach yourself.

Krevart is okay but he lacks compassion, badly. Every week, he teaches you something new and expects you to have complete mastery over it. So either you do an assignment "right" or a HUGE chunk of your grade disappears, courtesy of his TAs who are very eager to take points off for minor mistakes. If you learn by trial and error, then avoid him.
Great professor, teaches well and actually gives real life examples. Can tell he cares about students and wants to set them up for success.

Easily the best professor in the CCI department at UNCC. It's not even a competition. The final project for this class forces you to make a project using the same skills and tools used by full-stack devs in the industry. It could also help boost your resume. The TAs are also incredibly skilled and knowledgeable about the class.

I took ITSC 2600 (Computing Professionals) as a transfer class. Professor Slane is very kind and understanding. There's one weekly reflection and occasional assignments. Guest speakers visit weekly and share useful resources and advice for student success. She also responds to emails quickly. Easy class with a good teacher who cares about students.

Ms. Slane's class is a great class to come in for 50 minutes to relax and listen to speakers from the multiple departments here on campus and from companies. Yes, sometimes the lectures can be boring, but for only 50 minutes they're not too bad. The work itself is easy, but make sure to do the assignments, so that you're not stuck with a B like me.

Lectures were basically ppl coming from different departments and talking. Attendance was taken thru pollev. Homework was light work, occasional discussion post or reflection. Had a final group project that we had plenty of time to work on and was fairly easy to do. Professor was nice and seemed to care about her students and their success.
She is the nicest teacher ever, and her class is very easy. Class can be a little boring, but only 50 mins.

ITSC 1600 is an easy course. The class mainly consists of different presenters from student organizations, companies, campus resources, etc. There are some interesting/useful lectures, but a majority of them are quite boring. The assignments are not based on any of the lectures but are relatively simple. Attendance was 10%, and there was no final.

By far the easiest class I have taken at UNC Charlotte. It was very self-directed and self-taught but the content wasn't challenging. Because everything was online (including test + final) and attendance was not taken you could easily pass this class with an A without ever showing up.

Professor Jugan is easy-going, straightforward, and informative. Attendance is optional and he even takes the courtesy of recording the lectures. This course has few assignments that aren't difficult at all, but it still feels like I learned a lot. A big plus of professor Jugan is that he's easy to reach outside of class!

I took this class on Zoom, attendance is optional and lectures are recorded. This is a project based course, you get to decide what you make. Since you decide, he doesn't give much guidance but is available for questions during class or office hours. There is very minimal work with this class, it's an easy technical elective.

Professor Jugan is a really good professor. He helps you understand the concept of unity and how to code a game from scratch. He is a leineit grader and he is very helpful. We had 4 homework assignments that stacked each time and we had the final game to present as our final.

He's a good choice for intro to AI. Super knowledgeable in the games industry and talks a lot about his experience. The study guides he provides are extremely helpful for the midterm and the final. There are 3 projects in the course. Attend class or watch the class recordings to get good insight on how to do them.

Don't recommend at all, especially online. Lectures are near incomprehensible and very difficult to follow, and usually never line up with the projects you are working on. You are expected to be good at C# and Unity, or have AI do all the coding. Midterm was set up to be near impossible. Not nearly enough time to complete any of it.

No clear instructions, difficulty in communication, terrible grading, no teaching, 0 understanding. 
You won't learn anything, arbitrary grading, no clear rubric followed while grading assignments. You will waste your time, better take difficult class than wasting your time on this. If the university is listening, please get rid of such classes.
An easy A at the cost of your sanity

I've taken Dr. Benedict for data mining and data visualizations, and she was my advising professor for my senior capstone project. Never in my life have I had a professor more relatable and understanding. Her classes have motivated me to change my career path towards data analytics, and as a student I could not recommend her enough.

She is very relaxed, no attendance. Her class felt like a youtube tutorial, there is very little content and everything can be easily found online for free. She speaks for about 15 minutes over some AI generated slides or the TAs will have a code demo, and the rest of the class you do whatever. This class was a waste of time and money.

She's young and fresh out of grad school, which means that she's really chill and understanding. It seems like she cares, but she doesn't actually have much control over the structure of the course. Typically generates assignments with AI. It seems like she's typically busy and doesn't devote much to the class. New to teaching and too slow.
Great for 2214, would recommend if you want a relaxed professor overall, go with graduate students over professors.

I've taken data structures under two other professors and she is by far the best.  If you get the chance this is the professor you want to teach you data structures if you truly want to understand what you're learning!

The Prof. was absent the entire semester. I feel like the TAs ran the class most of the time. The material was not too difficult to learn. If you don't have a good team, you will have to work extra hard to get an A. The class is writing intensive. You don't do front-end design in this class but you learn how/why you should design in certain ways.

Dr. Ras is very knowledgeable and very kind to students. Unfortunately he is just not a good lecturer. Students used to pack the room when his TA showed up for exam reviews. He is accessible during office hours and will give you a chance to make up for mistakes if you participate regularly. Only 2-3 exams will make up your entire grade.

I took 3 classes with him and his teaching is amazing. He's really good at explaining complex topics in a simple way with real-world examples. His classes and even recorded lectures are engaging. He encourages participation, invites industry speakers, and is very kind and supportive during class and exams. He is my favorite professor in uncc.

I took 3 classes with him and his teaching is amazing. He's really good at explaining complex topics in a simple way with real-world examples. His classes and even recorded lectures are engaging. He encourages participation, invites industry speakers, and is very kind and supportive during class and exams. He is my favorite professor in uncc.

The professor receives more criticism than deserved. He is very knowledgeable about the material and has a great sense of humor that makes lectures engaging. He encourages students to ask questions and is happy to clarify concepts. The class is enjoyable and rewarding if you stay focused and put in the effort.

Overall class is on visual optics using AI. Homework and projects are all coded in Python. He gives plenty of extra credit, and chances to make up grades. Bonus points were on almost all quizes, and the ability to re-do assignments were always available. No final, but a final group project is assigned
Just avoid the class. Dr Das is knowledgeable but has weird sense of humor and god complex attitude.

A profoundly confusing structure that meets homework wholly unrelated to what's taught in class. If you have taken Intro to ML get ready to relearn it for half the course instead of what you signed up for. The class has no actual rigor and can easily be completed, but don't expect to actually get the intuition behind the topic from the classroom.

Amazing professor that really enjoys teaching students. He really cares about students understanding what he is teaching and makes complex topics really easy to understand. Above all he is really knowledgeable in computer vision and knows well beyond the course work. If you he the chance to take his class ignore all other reviews and go for it!
bro is the king of yapping, most poorly ran class i've ever been in, it was a joke

One of the worst course experiences I have ever had. It was so unorganized and basically run by the TAs. The teacher would come run a Pol everywhere quiz and leave or lecture in a super monotone hard to understand or comprehend voice than leave and have a TA do a lab or something. My final grade was also suited incorrectly and had to get it fixed.

I swear every positive review was written by him. Professor consistently posts materials last minute, didn't teach/lecture until after midterms when whole class complained, only give quizzes and yaps over them. Extra credit and open book open note means you can get an easy A, but he does not care about this class at all. Grades come out super late.

He does not cover any material in class, just posts a PowerPoint that you are expected to review before class for the in class quiz. Extremely disorganized and often does not post material in a time for students to review. The TAs did most of the actual teaching in this class.

We learned more from a TA during one zoom call than we did with the professor. He would be in class for the quiz and then leave before we started doing assignments. He would leave a TA's in charge and gave her no instructions. He posted the solution to the practice tests the day of the test leaving us no time to understand how to do the problems.

Had the class do a closed-note pop quiz with no prior notice, then cancelled the final project and replaced it with a math quiz based on the results of said pop quiz. You'll be shocked to know that students will tend to perform poorly on pop quizzes when given no indication that pop quizzes will be given. Just one of numerous issues.

If you want to actually learn ML, I would advise against this professor. He admits that he makes the class very easy so that students don't fail, with no exams or in-person quizzes, and labs that are basically fill-in-the-blank with no thinking required. He doesn't lecture very well and doesn't really understand how to answer questions.

He's a great professor. The class is very manageable as long as you keep up with the labs and the capstone project. The best part is that he uploads all the lab assignments at once, so you can work on them at your own pace instead of waiting week by week. Overall, very organized and easy to follow, and you'll do well if you put in the effort.
I really enjoyed taking this class, Lectures, assignments and labs were super straight forward and all the content of the lectures were on canvas day 1 of class so you can go at your own pace
Well paced assignments that keep you involved, good lectures. Not overly difficult, well balanced class.

MANY opportunities for extra credit, mostly based on how good your projects are. The course is project heavy, with three presentations on your dashboards. There's a decent amount of homework toward the beginning but less as the semester goes on. Dr. Yang is lenient when grading projects, gives great feedback, and she's very caring. No tests.

This class is a lot; the difficulty rating is more for the class than Fu. Do not underestimate it, regardless of professor. Fu isn't great, but is knowledgeable and will assist students. But he does not respond to emails, is sometimes unprepared for lectures, difficult to understand via Zoom, and has office hours only once outside of class days.

Can be a lot of homework in my opinion. Not coming from a cybersecurity background made this class a little more intimidating and I think that 3200 should be the only pre req for this class to prepare students. I do think this is a useful class. Lectures could've been more engaging or maybe 1 session could've been a lab session instead.

This class is extremely hard the projects suck. You aren't given a lot of information to go off of so your doing tons of research. He uses HTB for homework and as your learning resource. Do not procrastinate in this class your grade will reflect it. However, he will push back deadlines because he knows the class is hard.

The work can be difficult so it's recommended to start on assignments early, but definitely one of the most useful class you can take. Professor Fu is very passionate about cybersecurity and caring regarding students. He also extends deadlines as necessary so students can get work done on time.

Dr. Fu is a caring and generous professor who wants his students to succeed. He's willing to work with you if you put in the effort. A minor drawback of his class is that some assignment instructions reused from past semesters can be outdated, and new assignments may feel a bit experimental. Overall, his class is fair, enjoyable, and worth taking.
She will never respond to her emails, doesn't care about her students and you will not learn anything from her class. NEVER TAKE ANY OF HER CLASSES
Easy A, essentially just show up to class, but other than that she's a really good teacher.

Dr. Johnson is personable and intentional. She gives great feedback and inspires you (furthered by her own extreme endeavors in life). The course is easy as long as you participate in classes and do the assignments, but besides that, class with her is so rewarding that even if there were lots of difficult assignments it would be worth it. 10/10

The professor is extremely energetic, fun, and understanding. There is prep work before each class that takes no more than 30 minutes to finish, and a semester-long project. You will do frequent group assignments. This class was fun, and felt like talking with some friends more than an actual lecture. I cannot recommend her enough.

Well-organized and well-planned course, assignments felt well-spaced and clearly explained. All of the material covered important skills; nothing felt like it was just there to fill a time slot. 

The only point of criticism I have is that she could have been clearer/more specific with feedback on assignments.

Dr. Johnson is an incredible professor. You will learn a lot by taking any of her classes. She does everything to help you succeed. There were group projects, they were fun and easy. When taking her CTCM class, she heavily emphasizes the communication part. Also, be ready to be cold-called! An amazing professor that I would definitely take again.
I took his course . Biggest mistake I have ever done

Srinivas cares about students understanding of and engagement with the content, however the Mob- Robotics content is a lot of long equations and math, and while the concepts aren't terrible, you will probably feel lost a lot. Be ready for pop quizzes. He is an understanding and reasonable professor, so always ask questions

I enjoyed the Analysis of Algorithms course under professor Akella. 

Most assignments were using pen & paper with the intent of writing good pseudocode/ expressing the solution well. That allowed for better focus on the intuition of algorithm analysis without getting caught up in language semantics.
oh yes Srinivas akella is the best!!!!!!

Entire class is confused. Total of 3 grace days for late assignments. That means if you submit one assignment 3 days late, you've used all your grace days, for the ENTIRE semester. The very first assignment had a mistake, forcing people to use their grace days. Pop quizzes count towards grade. Very tough homework. Bad accent. Lessons aren't helpful
I LOVE SRINIVAS AKELLA HE IS THE BEST PROFESSOR AT UNC CHARLOTTE
classes are long but very confusing, not really sure what he wants. Ambiguous concepts and unclear instructions. avoid it.

Overall if you can learn through powerpoints and know all 150 slides and the codes in c++ on the test...which is 60% of your grade... no notes,no study guide,no openbooks and the "Q&A" days he hosts to answer questions about the test just leads back to the powerpoints.... you will probably pass... otherwise you will fail...

Erik Saule is an awesome dude. Don't get me wrong. But OH MY GOD this class will eat you alive if you are not prepared for it. You will pretty much have to teach yourself everything and the tests are abosolutely brutal. His 3160 class is alos completely different than other professors who offer the same exact course. DO NOT take his section.

Definitely worth getting to know him during office hours. Tests were 50% of grade, no study guide or information about the tests given. He is SUPER knowledgeable about the subject, but you don't code at all in class, just learn concepts. Tests on paper, closed book, coding on paper that was a first. Lenient grader-ish, but you need to put in effort

Dr. Saule is so down to Earth and easy to talk to. This is the first time he's taught this course and if you are very interested in learning database systems, take him. However, if you are not and just taking it on a whim or because of concentration, take another professor because their work is easier than his. Compared with friend's professor.

He is very knowledgeable when it comes to his field, and a fair person when it comes to grading. Even though the assignments and exams were pretty tough, he made efforts to guide students on the correct path. You will need crazy discipline to clutch in this class if it's your first time with actual C++ writing and using Centaurus.

It was alright, just that instructions are very unclear. Towards the end he made us present 4 different times. Quizzes at random and could not be made up. He was a nice person but just the course structure I believe was not the best. The notes absolutely did not help for any of the tests, I had no idea what to even study. This is for ITCS - 4121

Just took him, this is here for people about to take his Information Visualization class, so pretty chill, small class, projects are free and labs too, but exams not too bad, you will do fine if you study. Class attendance on Monday is optional, and Wednesday is mandatory; quizzes at the end of Wednesday, just look at the slides. Drops lowest quiz
Taking 4121 class, not really any reviews, but so far pretty chill, try your best to not fall asleep in class, lecture will make you fall asleep, but just so far, its pop quizzes and labs

Prof Subramanian has been teaching this material for so long that he has lost all passion and interest for it. Lectures have maybe 30 minutes of material tops but he drags them out to 75 minutes. Keeps you in class for extra time for no reason. Assignments are inconsistent and include useless group discussions. Avoid.

Project-heavy class (75%) with a small percentage dedicated to quizzes (25%), usually pop quizzes. If you want to take what you learned in algorithms and data structures to the next level, TAKE THIS CLASS! One project and quiz every 1-2 weeks. They are difficult projects, but they will teach you a lot! (We used the Bridges API to visualize DS&A).

personally i wouldn't take him again just because attendance was mandatory even though he just read off the slides the whole time. i didn't even go to class after 2-3 weeks because i physically couldn't sit there anymore and listen to him go over slides and learn nothing. it wasn't engaging either. i learned more by reviewing slides myself.

Felt more productive flipping through the lecture slides for 5 minutes instead of going to class. Lecture quickly lost ~80% of participants after the first 2 weeks of class. He is very difficult to understand and not great at explaining all the contents. The pacing is very slow and extremely difficult to follow. Better off to teach yourself

Overall, he was a good professor. He offered several opportunities for extra credit and he graded assignments with fairness. Although attendance was mandatory, it was only a very small percentage of the final grade. As long as you do all of the assignments and study some for the tests it's not very difficult.
Great professor!

Professor Yang is definitely a new professor but is very smart. It may take some time for him to understand what you are asking for but he cares for his students very much. If there is any inconvenience to where you can't take a quiz, he will let you take it another day. He is always willing to help you in any way.
Interesting lectures.
Dr. Zadrozny is a great professor. Would recommend his Machine learning and Introduction to natural language processing courses.
This is a natural language processing course and the TAs can barely communicate in english.

ITCS-4111. Lectures about the subject in a way that meanders through the textbook without providing tangible and workable examples of how to problem solve/code. Has a final project about content that we've barely talked about (neural networks) and purposefully ignores when students bring up issues with accessing data necessary to complete it. 0/10
Have taken both 4353 and 4360, both were the same. Boring to listen to, but class is easy enough if you stay on top of the busywork assignments. Pick a good project group, it matters.
This course teaches nothing, no skills. Prof is well spoken, but I learned nothing useful that will help me in my work. She is bit dismissive of students' comments. Would not recommend this course.
Waste of time. Easy A but nothing useful to learn. Prof discusses random things which is not useful for anything

Clear organization and structure of the course material. Dr. Faklaris has the most inviting presence not only outside of class, but how she involves students' voices during lectures. Individual projects to synthesize industry-standard artifacts are relevant if building your portfolio. Would take any of her courses, even if irrelevant to my focus.

Very interesting class, one of the nicest teachers at UNCC, gave out candy and was nice about everything. Very understanding and was lenient with her grading and her assignments were creative. There was a lot of reading involved though.

I was skeptical after reading harsh reviews, but she surprised me. After we bombed Quiz 2, she canceled personal plans—despite having a newborn—to hold a review session outside of class time. She patiently walked us through problems and seemed like she genuinely cared about our success. Her lectures showed improvement over time.

She will say "you are not spending enough time" when she doesn't give out much materials to study. The TAs carried the class, were very helpful. She won't help you out much. There were several times she was lecturing on the next chapter when we had quizzes in the same period. Overall I won't recommend taking with her unless you know a lot of stuff.

Doesn't really lecture and expects you to learn from just doing activities. Also when she does lecture she's typically not prepared for them & has to remind herself. One time a student asked a question for clarification before a quiz and she said she doesn't have time for questions. This did not only happen once but twice.
Loved this class. Super fun, really cool to get to do a project in a K12 classroom. Class itself is incredibly easy, just some easy busywork assignments , nothing very hard.

Dr. Rorrer is very caring and understanding, and the expectations for this course are thoroughly explained and reviewed through Canvas. She is flexible and considerate of students' needs, and she clearly has a passion for the work she does. I would definitely recommend!

I kind of felt bad bc me and my friends would always laugh and be disrespectful be she didn't care. 500wordessays,discussion posts and a midterm which is visiting a school and making a presentation and the final same thing but just a follow up. TBH I think the easiest A ever. Attendence was "supposedly" counted but I find that hard to believe.

Class is pretty easy and Rorrer was always very nice. The presentation we did at the end with the elementary school was actually pretty fun. Enjoyable class but my section met 3 hours every week in the morning.

Dr. Schlueter is an exceptional professor who truly cares. Despite many responsibilities she supports students with passion, even standing with us in lab even when the TA could run it alone. Lecture-heavy, but no exams, one final project, and labs, though time-intensive, are rewarding and full of valuable learning. She truly has a gift for teaching

Very knowledgeable professor who wants students to truly understand the material rather than just read off the slides. Asking questions in class is highly encouraged. The HWs are very well designed and complement the lectures.

It is recommended to ask questions during the lectures if you do not understand, since some students may think his lecturing speed is fast. Don't give up if you get a bad midterm score, there are other opportunities to make up for it. When doing the homework, I found that the accompanying "notes-*" notebook files were valuable in providing context.

Very kind professor, and never gave unnecessary work. If he finishes his slides 30 minutes early, which he does a lot, you will get out of class 30 minutes early. Class content isn't easy and sometimes its hard to understand with a bunch of equations on the screen, so its helpful to teach yourself a bit with help online to visualize more.
Such a nice person and he is all about the students.

AVOID. Entire class was student presentations. Assignments could have been interesting and geniunely challenging, but we are not taught how to do them. Half of the class was talking through every presentation and he did nothing to stop them. Cannot control or manage a class. Do not take!

One of the best professors in this university, yes the course is difficult but the in-depth explanations he provides are great. I would definitely recommend if you want to have a deeper understanding of databases. When I took this course, it was his first time teaching at this university. I can only guess his courses will get even better overtime.

Know python and its libraries and how to use jupyter notebooks. Best if u had taken data mining before. Brush up on calculus, linear algebra and stats as this class is also math heavy. Pay attention in class. The midterm was weird because there was stuff on it that we didn't learn. He did do extra credit. No final exam, only final project.

The slides dont provide much information. The lectures can be confusing at times. The midterm could have been great if he didnt have a bunch of time-wasting multiple choice questions (if you actually pay attention in class, the free response questions would have been ok). Youtube videos for every topic (sometimes hours). Lots of Calculus/Linear Alg

Poor systems for almost everything, didn't know if we would have a final for most of the semester. Lectures were entirely off of slides just filled with equations. Wants you to watch YouTube videos to learn everything instead of teaching it in class or applying it in class. Gave a practice midterm and then the midterm was entirely different topics.
I took him last semester this professor explains the concepts more better then other I had this classes, be ready for heavy lecture but study before class more impro

Very disorganized lecturer. Would show slides, then be confused by what was on them because they weren't ones he made himself. None of us learned anything from him. Several of us were using ChatGPT just to put his topics in and have ChatGPT explain the topics. I saw he was teaching another class I could have taken and immediately dropped it.

The class is ok, but be prepared to spend the whole day until night in the lab. Everything takes long time to finish, but the grading is according to your work. If you know the standard lab techniques you don't need to take this class.
Gives out so much work each week.
Inefficient use of class time; expect to stay late every session. Very dismissive of student inquiries and creates a tense classroom atmosphere. Communication is not professional.

He is probably one of the best professors I have ever had. He is very knowledgeable when it comes to mathematics, and he can also translate that to students. He has a lot of resources to help understand any topic he will cover in Canvas. You have to put in the work, but you WILL learn, and an A is very attainable!

He is the best math professor I have had. He is so passionate about math and it really shows- the guy has been doing this for a LOOOONG time. He explains Calculus II like it's basic algebra!! He makes it so much easier than it really is. If you really want to learn, definitely take Calc I and II with him!!

What more can be said. Dr. Taylor is, in fact, the goat. Had him for Calc 2 and 3, and I don't think I've learned as much from a professor since. He doesn't hand out free A's, but the classes he teaches aren't easy by nature either. He is more concerned with you learning all of the material, and by the end of his class, you WILL have learned. 10/10

I don't even need to say anything. I've had him for Calculus I II and III. It doesn't matter if another proff is good, Dr. Taylor IS the GOAT. I would only say though that you should watch out for Calc III, most other proffs have it as a "free class" Dr Taylor makes you actually learn it instead of handing you an A.

Not much to say. The few times I went to class I realized I learned more by teaching myself the material through the homeworks and the tests. His tests weren't really hard. He's kinda endearing because he just talks about his life during class but that means he doesn't talk about math much. His accent is a little difficult but not impossible.

All the people complaining were probably just using AI the whole time, before every exam there would be a pretest that was very similar to the actual test. People complain that he rambles like so what, professors are human too.

He canceled class many times during the semester. He is not willing to work with you with grading even after sending him a screenshot of him saying he would. He doesn't really teach he speeds through everything. Not friendly when you email him. Tests are google form and multiple choice so you can't get any points back for the work you did.

Pretty good class for me at least, his accent is pretty easy to understand after a class or two of getting used to it. Lectures are very heavy but he wrote his own notes and gives them to you which is pretty nice. Exams are light work too
I would not mind taking this class again with this professor, he cares about his students

First off, I want to say that Dr. Dai is a very kind and nice person. However, he is very ineffective at teaching. He has a very strong mandarin accent that makes him hard to understand. I had to teach myself a lot of the math due to the lack of understanding. If you struggle in math, do not take him.

Excellent teacher, really helps you learn. Makes Calculus not as intimidating. Exams are not terribly difficult; she gives you lots of practice opportunities that are similar to the exam questions prior to exam day. If you have good attendance at the end of the semester, you have the opportunity to retake one exam on the last day of class.
Shes an amazing teacher who cares a lot about her students. Always has a positive attitude and is extremely helpful to her students.

She's absolutely amazing. I have nothing negative to say about her. I'm not one to be good at math but as long as you go to class and pay attention you will be great. Always practice she gives plenty of opportunities. I always knew what was coming. Also the amount of extra credit she gives is amazing. Easy A if you study !

Professor Linker is the GOAT. she does a great job of explaining complex topics. She cares about you and so if you are confused, you can ask and she will slow down and explain it again. She is the best. TAKE HER

One of the best professors I have ever had. I was super nervous to take calculus because of horror stories I have heard but I thrived with her. She truly cares about you and wants to see you succeed. I hate math but enjoyed learning from her. If you need to take calculus, TAKE IT WITH HER AND HER ONLY!!!!!!
Weird guy.

Best teacher for those who can self-study. Very generous curves on tests, can give great help when asked. The in-class lectures were a but dull, but that's to be expected given the subject. Overall, would recommend.

Teaching style isnt the best so lectures arent that useful, however she is a very very generous grader and curves exams. So if you're more of a self teaching student and prefer somewhat easier exams with curves, then shes right for you.

Professor Shaban is a mixed bag - she can be great at explaining concepts, but her teaching style isn't for everyone. The class relies heavily on self-learning from the textbook and online resources like Unstuckstudy. Attend lectures and stay on top of the quizzes to succeed.

Calc2: Its a hard class and don't take her class if you expected an easy way out, she's amazing because of her lectures and how fair she is. Clear deadlines and take advantage of her office hours. It's a challenging class but believe it or not her best interest is to see her students succeed. Very fair and kind, ask her questions if needed!

She is very knowledgeable about her field. I would say complete your homework on time but if you cannot for some reason then you can ask her for some time, she's very accommodating. She gives a few extra credit opportunities throughout the semester. Attend the lectures cuz they're very helpful.

Calc 2 is a hard course, but DO NOT CHOOSE MS. TAYLOR. I compared my tests to another teacher here on gradescope, her tests covered the hardest material, and were significantly longer. She also rushes teaching in class, with no time to write down notes. She isn't a bad explainer, but with how hard she makes the class, go with someone else.
I'm not great at anything numbers but I believe she explained things well.

First things first I would like to say calculus 2 is a very hard course in general, no matter what professor you choose you will still struggle somewhat. Desire tends to make the course harder than it needs to be, and this is mainly towards her unit tests. Desire's unit tests are very difficult, and are harder than the finals. Pick someone else.

I took Professor Taylor for Calculus 2. I have never met a colder instructor. It felt like she was purposely going out of her way to make the class even harder. She never replied to emails and always seemed to be annoyed when giving lectures. I did learn the material, but my experience in her class was very negative and caused me a lot of stress.
She could have been lenient, but she chose not to.
Isn't understandable and skims through important topics making everything more complicated.

Firstly, you wont understand her. Secondly she will go over the new material but, when its time to do the in class work it will be something she never covered. If for whatever reason you take her.... sit in the middle rows, do the homework because that will help you more than she will, PLEASE DO THE STUDY GUIDES ITS BASICALLY THE TEST!
She is kind and very approachable. Is readily available and helpful outside class and knowledgable about her concepts.
Great professor! I recommend her to all my friends.
I enjoying being her class, her teaching style is amazing, better than any professor I have had!!

Dr. A MATH 1340 class is not difficult however she is a very strict grader. She gives really good feedback and allows you to redo the assignments with full credit. For the online class late points are not a thing we have until May 4th to turn everything in with no penalty. I would take her again for 1341.
Very heavy accent, which isn't her fault, but it is hard to understand her. She teaches the class as if we should just know everything, and if you get something wrong, she will yell and call you out.

She does have a strong accent, but in my class, all homework was due at the end of the semester. She pushes you to learn. She will yell at you, but don't take it to heart. Most of the time, I just laughed at it when she wasn't looking. She's very sweet, but you have to APPLY yourself. She's very reasonable and would even allow test corrections.
Tests will have the most random questions that are hard to look at visually (pictures) and to understand. Very frustrating, but class is easy enough. Lectures are long and boring though.

She cannot explain trigonometry to people learning pre-calculus for the first time. A huge saving grace for this class was my preceptor. She also broke student privacy rules and lashed out on the class because a couple of people were not paying attention by giving us a quiz that none of us understood. She also has a huge accent which made it hard.

Dr. Houston is a nice guy but he's not good at teaching. His lectures are hard to follow Homework and Test reviews differ significantly from the actual Tests. I emailed him regarding concern of grades and he didn't respond. I later talked to him in person only for him to say "I don't know what to say to that" Take this class with Loc Nguyen instead

Dr. Houstons linear algebra class is hard but it is organized well. The lectures can be somewhat helpful and he does attendance daily. If you stay on top of the homeworks, rework them before quizzes, and study the test reviews given before each test and get to the concept dont just memorize you should do fine. Dr. Houston is kind and respectful.

Dr. Huston's lectures comprised mostly of writing and explaining concepts and examples on the whiteboard so I would recommend hand writing what is on the board and some of what is said as explanation. Everything is done on paper including attendance, mostly problems related to that days lecture, and tests/quizzes. There is homework due before class

So much homework (which felt rushed), and so much learning is dependent on his lectures and reading heaps of the textbook. He is kind and the course is very structured, however some of the content is really hard to grab a hold of. His lectures are sometimes inconsistent, he will get his own examples wrong or teach something the wrong way at times.

Dr. Houston is an excellent expositor of mathematics. It's thanks to him and this class that I actually understand linear algebra on a deeper level. lecture notes are available every day and if you keep up and try to work ahead you'll do fine on the tests. It's not an easy class but Dr. Houston makes it much more manageable and fun.

Very good professor, exams are pretty much exactly as the exam review videos he posts on canvas so make to to study those. The only downside is one homework assignment due after every class(not worth much of your grade though).

He is very good at explaining and getting you to understand the material. Attendance was taken via a question you turned in at the end of class (the answer didn't matter; just the fact that you submitted something). You can tell he loves his job and knows his stuff down to the core. Good professor overall.

This professor is great. He offers a myriad of resources and attendance was offered for extra credit. I found this class extremely difficult but professor Dow was supportive and never made me feel bad about having a difficult time with the class.
One of the nicest professors ever. Make sure to attend all lectures (the textbook doesn't really help) and always get the homework done on time.

Weekly 1 quiz & 2-3 HWs (10 questions), all straightforward. Very test-heavy: 3 Tests = 45%; Final = 30% (can replace lowest test score or entire course grade if exceptional). +5 extra credit to final average for perfect attendance via exit tickets. Professor Dow is patient, an excellent mentor, and the best teacher ever. I wish him all the best.
This professor would come to class and write random examples on the board. Gets mad when you ask questions. I had to withdraw from that class. If you want to pass don't take it with him
Idk what the other person is talking about. This guy is great and a really good proff. Just put in the work and pay attention.
do not take this professor. he sucks at teaching. please please please dont take him. you will need all the self motivation ever to pass this class. find someone else
He is very difficult to understand and only gives four grades for the whole year. Doesn't teach, just talks.

If you literally need this class, then take it, but be ready to learn everything on your own, he knows his stuff but can't teach it. If you're not enrolled in niner course pack, don't purchase his textbook, it is free on his app "Intro Stat". The logo is a bell-shaped curve, that and drawing chips out of a box is the only thing learned.

Dr. Diao, is a good professor, attendance is mandatory with quizzes counting each class. Three tests and a final all open note, the lost test is replaced by your homework grade which is super helpful. Lectures can get confusing to follow and you have to spend some time out of class to understand content.

Give this man an award. He's an amazing professor and genuinely cares about your wellbeing. He has four tests (3 course tests and 1 final) and he drops your lowest test score and replaces it with your average from assignments and in class attendance quizzes (which are like 4 easy questions to ensure you're paying attention.

He's a super nice professor who really wants people to do good. 3 tests and a final and he drops ur lowest test grade for ur homework grade. Tests are pretty easy and the final is made up of questions from test 1-3. You can use all your notes and his lecture notes on tests+final, so if ur not getting an A or B it's probably ur fault.

Very nice and funny professor. Attendance is important, with a daily participation "quiz" grade. Easy to follow the lectures, but make sure to take notes. Homework is through WeBWorK, which can be a pain to work with. Tests are online (but in-class), and are open note.

Professor Diao is a caring, humorous instructor who makes the course content very accessible. The structure of exams and use of study guides make this class manageable, especially if you utilize resources like the textbook and unstuck study for extra practice.

Free A, you wont learn anything, 40 point bonuses for each midterm if you volunteer. If you actually want to learn the material do not take him, but im js giving him a 5 cuz he makes my life 20x easier compared to other professors who teach calc 3.

Worst professor I've ever had I couldn't care less about a free A, I should've dropped this dude when I had the chance. Spends 30 minutes max reading a vague class lecture note page with illegible hand writing with an indecipherable accent. I've learned absolutely nothing and anytime I've had a question he just shews me off. Joke of a professor.

Nice professor that teaches the content well but has a thick accent, making his lessons difficult to understand. He posts lecture notes that are amazing quality and genuinely help in understanding the material. You are only graded on 3 tests and the final, and the lowest test grade is dropped so it isn't too stressful. Tests are just like the HW.

Took his class for my graduate program, didn't understand a thing. His accent was very thick and the language barrier made it difficult to ask questions. He wants you to get an A but the whole time you feel like you're failing because he talks as though you understand at the same level he is. One of the most stressful classes ever.

Graded on 3 tests+final throughout the semester, one test can be replaced. Tests are EXACTLY like the study guides with diff numbers. The easiest class I've ever taken. Sometimes gave heavy extra cred for participation Has a heavy accent and is pretty quiet. Don't take if you want to understand the material, take if you want an easy grade.

Took Calc 3 with him twice. Both times were nothing short of incredible. His teaching style def is interesting but the homeworks and the tests are similar and he allows cheat sheets for the exams, (two for the final). Very caring and understanding about missing quizzes too. All round GOAT.

Professor Vainberg's Calc 3 class can be challenging, but attending lectures and using resources like Unstuckstudy can help. Stay on top of the WebWork assignments and you'll do well. Overall, a decent class if you put in the effort.

This class was harder than it needed to be. He relies to much on textbook reading to be lecturing with no planning. Consistently got problems wrong. He isn't trying to be bad, but calc 3 is 90% concepts and that's something he can't teach. Only passed from him easing up on the tests and changing the scale so he does care about his students.

I made a RMP account just to give this guy a good rating. He is extremely caring and understanding. All tests are on webwork which is a mixed bag; good cause you get multiple attempts, bad cause you get zero partial credit. Practice tests are usually not like the actual test so be careful. Just keep up with all the webwork hws and you'll be golden

A good way to get by this class is by sticking close to the webworks, he only uses webworks for all exams and Homeworks, which isn't bad, however learning from the professor can be dreadful. I used another source to learn calculus 3 and most exams and Homeworks are copied verbatim. You will get by doing the homeworks and studying them for the exams

He is a quiet speaker and has an accent. It's kind of difficult to read his handwriting so you're gonna have to teach yourself. He gives 3 tests. He's very nice but impossible to reach outside of class and doesn't answer emails.

I went to every class, took notes, did the homework, and every test is worded differently than what he gives you to practice. He then wonders why the average score is a 65. Also, I know he tries his best, but you CANNOT UNDERSTAND WHAT HE IS SAYING

Can barely understand anything, tests aren't reflective of what's in class OR on the practice exam. He words it all differently, then wonders why the average test score is a 65. Don't take this class if you want to pass Stat.
Very difficult lectures and beware of the test also very hard.
He reads of slideshows and that's essentially it. You have to do a lot of outside reading, no explanation of formulas or what everything is

I saw the 2.5 and expected him to be a super hard professor however he is actually amazing. Hes very nice and understandable in the way he teaches, and always makes sure the class knows whats going on before he moves on. The workload is not bad, MUCH easier tests than other calc 2 classes but worth a little more overall. 10/10 professor!!

1. Very hard to understand accent.
2. Bad attitude towards students who ask questions.
3. Often 5 minutes late to class, and takes his time to start the lecture, clearly doesn't value your time.
4. Tests are weighted so heavy that quizes/reviews/homeworks are basically pointless.
I took the risk of ignoring his bad rating. Do not make my mistake.

He will make you feel like you understand the content in class only to turn around and give tests that are 10x harder than any review, hw, or quiz done on the material. Compared to other sections, his tests are made to cause pain, and he doesnt curve!

Dr Awadallah is a good professor. He does problems in lecture and explains how the procedure and answers questions without judgement. Typical WedAssign homework that you had in Calc 1. Gives partial credit and curves. The final was very hard, but curved. It takes a lot of time devotion for homeworks. Sometimes homeworks can take hours.

dont take this guy bro. i feel like ive been waterboarded when i walk out of his class. i cnat understand the terrible accent, and bro cant answer a question to save his life. there is no avail in bro, only pain
I couldn't understand a word of what she said. None of her lessons felt complete or understood.
Worst professor acts like a kid teaching = 0%, torturing = 100% stay away from this walking nightmare
Nice professor, slightly confusing lectures
Not the best at explaining concepts

Lectures are really what is bad about this class. She will yell the whole time, and will make you stop doing anything else(any tech put away). You will just listen to her for an hour with no relief. The class itself is pretty easy, tests are not hard. But the lectures you attend twice a week will be miserable.
It was a great math class. Professor Reed cares a lot about her students and everyones success. I had a lot of fun in her class

Reed is a good professor but her class is unorganized. She gave us a course calendar at the beginning of the year which I just follow my test dates (the only thing she sticks too). She puts due dates of assignments at the last minute and makes group assignments due the same day even if it is a weekend.
Great teacher, all lectures were very well organized and easy to follow. Tests were slightly difficult.
Very understanding, the coursework was easy, no tricks.

I've never had a math professor like Reed. She's an amazing teacher who always checks in to make sure everyone understands, and she makes math enjoyable.

If you need help, talk to her right after class or reach out to the TAs. Sit in the front, stay engaged, and you'll have a great experience with Professor Reed.

Lots of homework and group assignments. Prepare for assigned seating which is the group you will be working with every class period. Not very approachable and can be demeaning when asking her a question, privately at least. Interactive and does give good comparisons from math to other subjects. No clear course plan though. Gives study guides.
Only downside is that she takes a long time to grade assignments.

At first I thought she was a bit scatter-brained but as the semester when on, I understood why she teaches the way she does. VERY BIG ON GROUP WORK!! Each month you will have an exam on the unit and you will do well if you take notes and study before each test, even if you're not the best at math.

Such a fun and easy math class. If you like realistic math that isn't super heavy on formulas and complex equations, this class is for you. Professor Birdsong is super sweet and there is lots of extra credit opportunities.

Pretty good professor, never really had problems with her, which says a lot considering how rude a lot of UNCC professors are. We had an easy course so I rarely had to ask for help. She provides plenty of resources and teaches the class pretty well

Honestly, he's not a bad professor. The only problem for real is his accent. It can be hard to understand, but he uses a doc cam for his note taking so I just copied everything he wrote down and that got me through the class. The exam was hard though.

His class was fine; he has a very thick accent, which makes the first couple of weeks of class an adjustment. He doesn't do homework, so your grade consists of the online quizzes and tests. The 3 tests are actually like the practice exams, so if you can do those, you will pass the class. I would not take STAT 1220 with him if given a choice.
