His accent is hard to understand at first, but gets easier through the semester. All of his quizzes are online and the textbook is optional but recommended to be used like homework. He has 3 tests and a final that make up most of your grade.

Amazing Professor! Don't believe what anyone else writes (they didn't try), if you take the time to practice his practice exams you will always get an A. Practice exams are word for word what will be on the test. Don't be shy to talk to him! He cares about all the students and amazing to talk to!

I really enjoyed this class and he is an amazing teacher who sets you up for success. Only downside is the attendance policy, you can only have 4 absences. Other than that, if you study and reach out the the TA or professor then you can make it through with a pretty good grade.

Lots of Hawkes homework, but they're easy 100%'s. It's also mandatory in the curriculum for the university so that won't change depending on professors. She's very clear and personable. I recommend taking from Professor Crosby if you struggle in math. I looked forward to her classes because they were relaxed and she cracks a lot of jokes.
This professor gives great lectures, attendance almost every class, great professor that actually teaches calculus, ive never taken any kind of calc and she does a great job explaining everything.
Caylah Crosby is one of the best professor's I have ever had in years of schooling. I'm not good at mathematics but she is very engaging, helpful and kind.

I really enjoyed taking Calculus with Professor Crosby. She is very supportive and takes her time to ensure students truly understand the material. Her teaching style is clear, patient, and effective. Take her if you can !

Professor Crosby is the best. She treats every problem like you've never seen it before and it helps you learn the concepts so much better. There is a lot of homework, but the tests are pretty easy. She also posts practice tests on canvas that are super similar to the real ones. The final is no joke though
I couldn't understand a word she was saying so I dropped the class
Very difficult to understand.

She doesn't care whether you do well or not. She has a very heavy accent, and is very hard to understand. Gets upset whenever no one responds to her question (even though it is answered, but she can't hear it. Would not recommend this professor for anyone.

This professor was extremely difficult to learn from due to her accent, its almost intelligible. She also auto grades based on the website homework is given, so if you were to answer a question correctly but it gets marked wrong due to a syntax error then you would be given that grade. I would never recommend this professor.

This professor does not allow any type of syntax error in home assignments, for example I would answer a question correctly but there would be a basic syntax error in the homework assignment, and she would not go over the work manually. Also has an extremely thick accent to a point where its almost intelligible.

If you have trouble understanding word-heavy or cluttered lecture notes, do not take this class. He is frustratingly difficult to understand due to his accent and tendency to spend very little time on a topic. Be prepared to use the textbook because trying to follow his notes is fruitless. Avoid at all cost, or at least try to take another teacher.
Terrible lectures, weird grading, and a heavy accent with terrible take-home notes. Would not recommend to take unless you can learn the pretty difficult material from just the textbook

Difficult to understand in class. I've had very good teachers with thick accents but Sonin specifically mumbles and is hard to hear. Outside of class he provides no resources and says to solve problems on textbook which doesn't tell you the answers. Most of your grade is rare homework problems which you can only solve by looking up how to do them.
Was very hard to follow during lectures as his notes were structured differently and was very confusing. You are better off teaching yourself the textbook as to not get confused by his teaching.

Difficult to understand. His accent and handwriting are too difficult to follow. His lectures are printed out handwritten notes. I had to drop this class because he was too difficult to follow. He gave a lot of hw and a test each month. I then retook the class with a different professor and figured out this course was easy af.

She's a nice lady and can be helpful at times but I wouldn't recommend her. She's hard to understand in class and is unable to answer a lot of questions. She treats her TA pretty poorly as well (always yelling at her to do stuff that she is incapable of doing.) I genuinely still do not understand statistics and I have learned nothing in this course

Dr. Jutmaan is a sweetheart. That being said, her lectures are ineffective and leave you with weekly pre class assignments/videos for learning core concepts. There is minimal teaching of foundational information during classes. In class, she just gives you the answers for most assignments. Her syllabus is 8 different modules. Just a jumbled class.

Dr. J always re-explained statistic concepts (with an understandable accent). Watch Canvas videos and do the Realizeit assignments while taking notes because those will really help you in the 5 exams (including the common final). You can retake the practice tests, and some of the questions from those will be similar to the ones on the actual exams.

She has a very heavy accent and speaks very quickly, but she will slow down if you ask her to and is very open to answering questions. This was more about learning Microsoft excel than it was doing math, which made it that much harder. Many quizzes and tests(4 and a final exam) which are a big portion of your grade.
She is not good at speaking English and she doesn't teach you the material you have to watch videos at home to learn the material
Lessons with clear, understandable, thoughtful notes. Makes the content seem easy, though it is still difficult. Little grades: 3 HW, 3 Test.
BEST TEACHER!!!

No one is as straightforward when it comes to explaining problems as Dr. Safronov. Differential Equations is not an inherently easy class but Dr. Safronov makes it feel simple. Cannot recommend him enough.

Oleg is the goat when explaining how to do math equations. He will walk you through each step and explain why he is taking it. His tests are straightforward, and he will tell you what will be on them. He is extremely nice, but his only fault is his monotone voice, which put me to sleep once or twice.

He explains the topics clearly and goes over the problems in detail. He usually goes over what problems are going to be on the exam the lecture before the exam which is helpful. Three HW assignments, three exams, and one final. Truly one of the best math professors I've taken!

This class was a waste of time; lectures were so easy to the point I didn't even pay attention. Literally the easiest A ever. His lectures were pointless. I did other homework during class and then taught myself the material 5 mins before the exam and got an A every time. Highly recommend this class to fill a GENED.

His lectures are long but oh well thats every class. This guy is awesome and hes the nicest professor I've ever had. You can tell he loves his job and actually cares about the students. If you get a question wrong but explain your reasoning u can still get partial credit. I didn't study at all, and I passed with a 97. I recommend taking his classes

Professor Wantland is a fantastic teacher. He cares about his students, and making sure they understand concepts more than anything else. The biggest challenge was a lack of an organized Canvas page, and shifting due dates. Despite that, the class was easy, informative, and one of the most fun classes I've taken.

Professor Wantland was one of the best math teachers I've ever had! The class itself is fairly work heavy and the Canvas page is overly complicated - but Prof. Wantland was an absolute pleasure to be around. It's very clear that he is genuinely passionate about his work and his lessons reflect that.
Pretty funny guy. Tries to break it down maybe even a bit too much sometimes. Anyone who didn't get it really wasn't trying

His lectures were okay. He's not mean but he's very dry and not particularly nice either. The worst thing are the daily quizzes for attendance which make things unnecessarily harder and will bring your grade down for no good reason. If you can avoid him do so there's no reason to make math harder for yourself by taking his class.

Very tough course, lots of homework. Attendance was not mandatory, but there was an understanding quiz for every class graded, so if you missed class, you missed a quiz. Dry speaker and every class is a lecture. There was extra credit, but the tests were very difficult. The final was not as hard but requires a lot of studying.

Not the worst option in the department but not the best. His lectures are really the only bright spot from my experience. With how many tests/quizzes are given keeping a good grade is definitely tough, especially since none are dropped.

Three rating because he isn't the worst but doesn't match my needs for learning. He is a very fast teacher, it's hard to keep up if you don't understand. He doesn't seem very welcoming, doesn't enforce a hand textbook or post videos for people to go back and watch. If you are good with Calc 2 then take his class, if not then I don't recommend him.

Very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very good professor

The professor is knowledgeable and helpful, but be aware that the Calc 1 department structure is very rigorous. Exams make up 70% of the final grade, which leaves little room for error. Even if you have a strong math background, the grading scale is strict.

Professor Bland makes Calculus I feel like middle school math. Go to his class; it counts as attendance grade but he will make today's topic easy to understand. If you complete the homework and quizzes early, you will receive extra credit. Tests count as a large portion of your grade though you shouldn't fail them if you at least study.
Now I can't prove to you that God is real, but I can prove to you that Robert Bland is the greatest math professor of all time.
Amazing professor, I should have spent more time studying outside of class for the exams.

A lot of this class was "Automated" in the sense that the online tools used were responsible for grading, which was good 95% of the time, but when the online tools were wrong, you had to email him, he usually responded within a day or two politely. There is a lot of online homework, but as long as you don't let it pile it's not bad.

Professor Daher was overall a great teacher. He is willing to help with anything and shows you how to do it by hand and calculator. Your grade is made up of 4 tests, homework, and a final. Do the work and you will be fine. Also, if you attend most of his classes, he will round your grade up a good chunk at the end of the semester.

The grade is based on all tests, which makes the course hard. If you want an A, do not take this class. He has 2 online tests, which makes it a little better, still needs to do well on the final anyway. He will round your grade if you go to class, but not much. Not many things to study. Overall, just a confusing course in my opinion.
Professor Daher was a great professor. He was very clear on every subject matter and used many example similar to his tests.
Good professor, just make sure you show up to class and pay attention and you will be fine!

Very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very very good professor

The professor is knowledgeable and helpful, but be aware that the Calc 1 department structure is very rigorous. Exams make up 70% of the final grade, which leaves little room for error. Even if you have a strong math background, the grading scale is strict.

Professor Bland makes Calculus I feel like middle school math. Go to his class; it counts as attendance grade but he will make today's topic easy to understand. If you complete the homework and quizzes early, you will receive extra credit. Tests count as a large portion of your grade though you shouldn't fail them if you at least study.
Now I can't prove to you that God is real, but I can prove to you that Robert Bland is the greatest math professor of all time.
Amazing professor, I should have spent more time studying outside of class for the exams.

The lectures are nothing like the homework for the most part, and you have to learn a lot by yourself. The only pro is that the test and quiz questions are structured exactly like the homework, so MEMORIZE how to do the problems. There is also no partial credit, which sucks.

He is very bland and gives terrible lectures where he just runs through problems very quickly with no explanations. There is also no partial credit on exams so small mistakes are critical. If you plan on taking  him prepare to do a lot of learning by yourself outside class.

This class wasn't exactly bad, but the professor was not good at teaching AT ALL and the homework's were NOTHING like the lectures. I practically had to teach myself everything on top of going to class. The biggest gripe I had was having midterm 3 two days before the final exam, which makes little to no sense.

The main thing about this class is your attitude going into it. You need to be able to keep up with the work and be able to study on your own. The teacher will go at a very quick pace, not be nice when answering questions, and expect you to understand everything at his pace.

His lectures can be confusing so you end up learning most of the material yourself. Weekly quizzes that are not too difficult if you have done the homework. Tests are on lockdown browser inclass, so no partial credit but you get 3 attempts per question. The tests are about the same as the homeworks.

Very bad teaching as his online course has no lecture videos for you to refer to.
If you want to learn the content, you would have to get your own textbook, and learn it completely on your own without any aid.
Also very harsh and not lenient at all. Would never take him as an instructor for anything.
if you can't learn everything from a textbook with no guidance this class is for you if you want someone who teaches this man ain't it
Harsh grader, not lenient whatsoever.

Trust this review, he teaches at a great pace and makes the material easier than all the other stats professors. He provides loads of extra credit opportunities throughout the semester and all tests and assignments are straightforward. No trick questions, tests are 'open note' and extremely similar to the study guide. Easy A if you take him.

Automatic fail if you miss more than 3 classes, but extra credit for perfect attendance. He doesn't consistently take attendance, its random. Rather boring lectures and strict with devices. He gives out coding projects which aren't too difficult, coming from someone with no python experience.

This class is interesting but can really get you if you don't lock in. Missing classes will be terrible for you as there is no canvas course or any resources for this class except the papers he hands out in class.  The major downside is this class is 90% exams, which is a shame considering a project would be really interesting for it. Overall hard.

Good professor, material isn't too hard if you pay attention during class and review from the book. That being said, the material isn't the easiest, and sometimes you have to sift through irrelevant parts of book. Practice the examples he gives you. Midterms are like the HW, and final was like the midterms. Only bad thing is he doesn't use Canvas.

This class is interesting but can really get you if you don't lock in. Missing classes will be terrible for you as there is no canvas course or any resources for this class except the papers he hands out in class.  The major downside is this class is 90% exams, which is a shame considering a project would be really interesting for it. Overall hard.

Good professor, material isn't too hard if you pay attention during class and review from the book. That being said, the material isn't the easiest, and sometimes you have to sift through irrelevant parts of book. Practice the examples he gives you. Midterms are like the HW, and final was like the midterms. Only bad thing is he doesn't use Canvas.

Awesome guy! Really cares about learning as a process, so is very willing to give back points on assignments. If you have his class: chat with him! He is so nice but kinda quiet unless you start a conversation

You work in small groups to solve problems at the board. Tests are similar. Ok class and home work. Good interactions in class with instructor and other students. Would definitely recomend if you need to learn the material.
Had a good experience. Attendance required. Lots of writing in the course. Group tests are great and can resubmit assignments.

Worked problems at the board and explain to others who had questions. Never had a class where you stand most of the time. Learned a lot and feel confident to teach geometry that was hard for me. Definitely recommend to others as he is nice and answers questions.

The class is very different. You have to work problems in groups at the board. He gives feedback on your work. Then write your solutions later. Not much lecture but you learn a lot by discussing. Same for the tests.

Great professor. She does a lot to help & gives you the best opportunity to succeed. Extra credit opportunities. Just come to class and take the HW seriously. Although the professor is great, self-study is highly recommended. Gives prior year final exams for finals prep. All expectations are clear, just put in the work because the info compounds!

The absolute best math teacher! She explained everything so well and provided a tremendous amount of support both inside and outside of class if you were confused about anything. She was also super sweet and gave mom vibes. Highly recommended!
I really enjoyed her class and it made me love math again (you have no idea how much I hated math). Everything is recorded and as long as you do the homeworks you'll be okay

I loved her, she was really sweet and an easy grader. Her class would just be going over problems and don't hesitate to ask for help. The Hawks homework would get tiring but it wasn't too bad and she'd usually extend the due dates. She had open note quizzes and attendance was "mandatory" but you could really just access the link from home.

as someone who's always struggled with math, her lessons and lectures were always very clear and easy to follow. i missed a few classes when i was sick, and i was still able to catch up easily because of how accessible and helpful she is. she was real with us, too, and would communicate when she had an event in her personal that would impact her.

Professor Page was a chill dude, Although he takes his classes seriously, He helps people with their problems and doubts and is always happy to help. He will be straight forward when it comes to testing. He always makes sure everyone understands the lectures and problems. He is pretty hilarious and will make even the hardest concepts bearable.

Professor Page really is an interesting human, but likable. I would say he is an average teacher, not great, but he will elaborate on concepts if asked. He will often go on random tangents during class. I thought it was kinda fun; others felt like it detracted from the lesson. Also, I loved the fact we got out of class at least 10 minutes early.

He is fast paced but I love him. He always clarifies. His lectures are available and straightforward. Work is always exciting. He is incredibly helpful and amazing in the humanities. Without knowing it, he helped me through very rough times. He is very personable and understanding of parking issues. I have arrived 10 to 20 minutes late. I love him!
Can be confusing but will make sure you understand if you ask questions, will also go out of his way do extra examples if asked.

He whistles at you in class. Very slow at responding to emails. Takes forever to grade. Overall not a great teacher in my opinion. I wouldn't take him again if I had the choice but the class was tolerable.
Just do not take him

Professor Crosby is very nice but his classes are not useful to learning even though calculus is not that difficult of a course the curriculum taught at uncc is not for pure math majors. Attendance is mandatory for a grueling 8:30am class twice a week.

Dr. Crosby is not a bad person at all; however, he is simply just not a good instructor. Lectures feel very pointless and don't cover the material to the extent that is covered in the homework. The textbook is also not very helpful for the homework. Attendance was also graded for correctness which is strange. Overall, he is just ok.
Really nice professor, great class. His lectures go fast but he'll slow down if you ask. Attendance is mandatory and there are in class assignments that you'll totally miss if you don't show up.

I cant speak for calc 2 as I think that class is hell with any prof, but for calc 3 Dr. Crosby is fantastic. His lectures are pretty good, its a bit hard to keep up if you don't print out what he gives you but I did fine. His test reviews are very accurate to the tests. Also, he is by far the most caring professor I've ever had. I love his hat.
Really good professor! He grades based on a point system and gives out quizzes and tests, so be prepared for those

Do not take him if you can, especially online. His lecture videos are terrible and don't prepare you for the tests or quizzes, which are the only grades for this class except for the final which is worth 40%.

Mr. Klipp is okay, I had him online so don't know much about him in person. His canvas is organized, and the class is solely graded on tests/quizzes to grade your performance. Once you get a bad grade or two it is hard to get your grade back up. He replaces your final exam grade with your class average (if it's better) if you do every assignment.
Overall, makes it easy to learn and doesn't mind when corrected about problems.
Decent professor but the Canvas was a bit disorganized.

Professor Gute is the best!! She's so funny and relaxed. She goes through the notes with you and shows you how to do each problem, and puts homework on the board if you need help. Her due dates are flexible as well. She offers test corrections and it's clear she knows her stuff. Definitely take pre-cal with her if

She is really nice and funny during lectures and she will help you to make sure you pass the class such as offering extra credit and providing study guides that are similar as the exams. As far as her actually teaching the class I personally had to teach myself a lot of the material because she teaches really fast and it is very hard to keep up.
Very good prof, Only reason I struggled was because of laziness and low test grades, take the extra credit stuff and study before tests.
Cool dude.
He sounds unsure when he teaches it puts you to bed and the homework is due before you are taught it the tests are so bad too class average was consistently a 50-60

STAT 1222 do not take him, very unsure and doesn't teach you a thing. Seriously you cover everything in your homework that is due before you even learn about it in class. Adds a bunch of random material in lecture that you don't even need to know. Be prepared to fall asleep and teach yourself everything, this is your warning.

Professor Bakita was alright, it was okay at first but he was just very unsure and sometimes hard to understand. He's a nice guy but definitely very awkward, assignments were easy but the group projects killed me, hardly any cooperation and basically had to do them mostly by myself. I wouldn't recommend him, I would try my luck with someone else.

There are three group projects, but my group skipped the last two. For Project 3, I asked if I could just do my part; he said no, you must complete peers' sections if they don't participate to get full credit. But he gave an extension. The class is heavy with four assessments and a final, tough if you work. He teaches unsure, maybe new.
Extremely hard class. Dr. Sun seems to be very caring and knowledgeable and wants her students to succeed, but she just cannot teach on a lower level. Everything she teaches goes over our heads.

This course will be an easy A if you take the time to do the homework. He bases his test questions off the homework, and he never gives ridiculously hard questions. Attendance is not mandatory but I recommend attending lectures because he gives good analogies and explanations.

Professor Chen was my favorite this semester. He explains concepts very clearly and makes horrible problems bite-sized and, dare I say, easy. You'll need the textbook for the few homework problems he assigns each week. The first few weeks will test your algebra... but after that it's smooth sailing. Attend all lectures and rework problems. Great!

Clearly explained the course material in lectures, however, he didn't show students what they did wrong on tests, which made it difficult to learn from mistakes. Also used a physical rather than digital textbook, which is less convenient and less helpful.

Dr. Chen, or Goat Chen as we called him, was very clear about what was on the tests a few days before taking it. Granted the test grades are heavy, with the final being worth a third of your overall, but as long as you show up to class and do the homework (and there's barely any homework), then you're golden. Also, a good bit of an accent.

Dr. Chen is the goat if youre reading this just take his class, I skipped at least a lecture a week and bagged a pretty easy A even after choking our last regular test really hard. HW problems are just like the test because they're both just problems out of the textbook. Dr. Chen has a thick accent, but I did not have any problem understanding him.

He's a nice guy, tells funny jokes from time to time but his accent is hard to understand and he is very disorganized when he teaches, if you take his course, I hope you know a decent amount of calculus yourself because you're going to need it

Professor Roy is decent at best. He does offer extra credit (by doing assignments 2 days before the due date). However, his accent is very heavy, and it's hard to follow his lectures. He made lots of mistakes this semester and didn't finish teaching new material until the last day of class. 
Note: His BCQs are fine, just do them before class.

Possibly the worst decision I've made in university was to take him as my Calc 2 professor. The class is graded almost exclusively on test performance and attendance. His accent makes it very hard to follow lectures and there is almost no time to write down and understand the material before we're onto a completely new topic.

Cant understand a word he says, his hand writing looks like Japanese, had to teach myself everything, this guy needs to stick to research and stop teaching to be completely honest his class is a joke. I stopped showing up half way through the semester, never went back, and still passed. Holy, im glad I never have to take his class again.

The guy seems like a good and funny dude, but god does he suck at teaching material. I really wanted to like him but as the semester has gone on I've lost hope. Every lecture seems to leave students more confused and attendance is graded. If you enjoy teaching yourself material, this professor is perfect for you.

Amazing professor overall. His lectures, although long-winded and not the most engaging, are very informational, and he always gives demonstrations and examples. What you see on the slides he provides is all you really need to study with, and homework is more complicated than any of the tests/quizzes. Kind and understanding, very passionate.
Professor Nguyen is wonderful. His tests are almost identical to the practice tests he reviews a class before. He is a very lenient grader and does a lot of examples during class.

Practice exams being 90% identical to the actual exam. Difficult to understand accent, but a nice guy overall. Speaks softly so he can be hard to hear. Very, very easy grader. His attendance requirement is strict so be weary. Any more than 3 absences, you drop a letter grade. Fair enough trade for easy exams I'd say.

He teaches the concepts really well and he gives you a practice exam before tests that look identical to the test just with different numbers, he also allows you to bring a cheat sheet. The homework can be long, but there's only one a week. He does give attendance quizzes in class and if you miss more than 4 he will drop you a letter grade.

I registered for this course two days ago and I must say that the professor is very hard to comprehend his teachings techniques as I felt clueless every lecture and had to self-teach myself which of course nobody wants to do. I had to spent countless hours of studying and causing unnecessary stress on myself.

Professor Li is a brilliant mind that has a passion for what she teaches. She actively seeks out talented students and seems to talk about her subject with joy. Sure she has a slight accent and can be hard to understand and sometimes gets lost into the proofs and such during lecture which makes it even harder to follow along. Only thing negative.
Incredibly nice teacher, without her I wouldn't understand anything.

Incredibly sweet, entertaining, and helpful. Analogies may be confusing to some but I understood, I'd say thats subjective. She knows what she's doing and is a quick lecturer, but thats the curriculum. She will slow down if you ask. Has an accent but it doesn't hinder anything. Admits when she makes mistakes and is flexible, offers partial credit.

Professor Li really wants her students to succeed. She has amazing out of class communication, she responds faster than any professor I've had before. Her lectures are not attendance mandatory, they can be fast paced but she checks that students are on track. Her exams are reasonable and curved and she is overall just really nice.

The material is still difficult but presented effectively. If you take her and put in a reasonable amount of effort, it'll be hard to fail this class. I thought I failed the final and somehow got an ended with a B. The lowest course grade for the class was a 62; she either was curving or throwing huge amounts of partial credit. Take her if you can!

Practically hands out A's, which might be great for students who dont care. But if you are actually interested in learning the material, prepare for unstructured lectures consisting of muttering and confusion.

Ms Adriana is really a sweet professor who teaches Matrices and linear algebra in the most fun and interesting way. The material can be very confusing towards the end of the semester but her grading structure is super light with Homeworks(30%), Freehand Activity(10%), Group activities(10%), 2 Midterms(20%) and Final Exam(30%). TAKE HER! EASY A

The homework was super easy (20min a week), only two midterms (15% each), and a final (30%). She offered extra credit on the final and curved your lowest midterm grade. Her midterm/final review guides are similar to tests. Only downside is the HW doesn't really teach you much, so you have to cram before the midterms and final to learn the material.

Great professor, very caring, wants to make sure you understand, and doesn't have a lot of work. However, sometimes she isn't the best at explaining things during lectures. I would recommend to anyone that wants to know applications with matrices (so coders, data analysts, etc) this professor because she's the only one who will do this.

If you're looking to take linear alg, please take her. Originally had a different professor who made the content very hard to understand. I switched to her class and finally understood concepts.Attendance not mandatory, and gives opportunities for extra credit for every test.Course is spread out it doesn't feel like you're rushing through content.
Great professor, easy tests and homework. Attendance was highly encouraged (10% group work).

Ms Adriana is really a sweet professor who teaches Matrices and linear algebra in the most fun and interesting way. The material can be very confusing towards the end of the semester but her grading structure is super light with Homeworks(30%), Freehand Activity(10%), Group activities(10%), 2 Midterms(20%) and Final Exam(30%). TAKE HER! EASY A

The homework was super easy (20min a week), only two midterms (15% each), and a final (30%). She offered extra credit on the final and curved your lowest midterm grade. Her midterm/final review guides are similar to tests. Only downside is the HW doesn't really teach you much, so you have to cram before the midterms and final to learn the material.

Great professor, very caring, wants to make sure you understand, and doesn't have a lot of work. However, sometimes she isn't the best at explaining things during lectures. I would recommend to anyone that wants to know applications with matrices (so coders, data analysts, etc) this professor because she's the only one who will do this.

If you're looking to take linear alg, please take her. Originally had a different professor who made the content very hard to understand. I switched to her class and finally understood concepts.Attendance not mandatory, and gives opportunities for extra credit for every test.Course is spread out it doesn't feel like you're rushing through content.
Great professor, easy tests and homework. Attendance was highly encouraged (10% group work).

Professor Weaver was by far the best math teacher I've ever had. He is so kind and funny, he truly cares about all his students. Where you sit matters, that will be your group for every group project. He gives test corrections after every test, so do those. Showing up to class is the best thing you can do for this course, it's easy to fall behind.

Mr. Weaver teaches STATS 1222 where you can understand and he also works with all students in order to make sure you understand the material and breaks it down for you. The coursework is mandatory but if you attend the classes the lectures and activities in class help so much!
He was a great teacher, it wasn't him that was hard it was the actual work. I actually learned here and had some good laughs with the people I sat and grouped with.

I wish this course had more respect for its students. I felt that it was very difficult to find everything in canvas and always felt like a maze to find the assignments worth 400 points. Frequently the lectures would be off topic, purposely confusing and hard to follow along. I would not take this course again.

Professor Weaver made statistics very manageable, and this is coming from someone who dislikes math. This is the type of class where your attendance does matter, and showing up will help you be able to get a good grade. The tests can be challenging, but he gives you practice tests in advance to help you prepare. I would take this course again.

Only 3 tests and a final but lots of extra credit to cushion the test grades. Attendance technically isn't mandatory but gives extra credit for showing up and taking in-class quizzes that are graded for participation. Pay attention during lectures because he's one of the only good lecturers I've had in a stem course. Not much hw btw.

I took MATH 8275 - Dynamical Systems with him. Didn't prepare for the class well. Lectures were confusing and had a lot of mistakes. Some students in class indicated the mistakes in lectures many times. Acted as if this class is a burden. Thinking we need points more than knowledge. Very aggressive if someone have opposite opinions. Super redflag!

Dr. Brian has been the best professor I've taken. The material can be hard, but he makes it make sense by giving practical explanations. The homework is harder than the tests, but it is worth it. He cares about connecting with students and remembers everyones name. There are plenty of quizzes, which are relatively easy and are just for attendance.

The content covered in this course is HARD. But Dr Brian has such a tangible love for mathematics (aka: he is a total nerd) that you cant help but try your hardest to connect to the subject. Hard homework, medium tests and easy class quizzes.
Difficult course, but only because of the content, not the workload. Dr. Brian is a genius and very approachable.
It feels like he doesn't know the material he's teaching

He was great at explaining things although at time he can go a bit to fast (I took the class in the morning so I might have not woken up fully yet). The TA was awesome at explaining things and he was also very chill.

The class itself is easy to learn through just Hawkes assignments. Attendance is mandatory through poll everywhere but missing a few classes will barely hurt your grade. Fridays are asynchronous and Hawkes assignments are not too hard. The TA is very useful. Lectures are not the most useful but he offers a lot of help and the prof is a funny dude.

He is an amazing professor and is super engaging. The only thing I'd say is just a lot of Hawkes. I did well in the class, even though I skipped a lot at the end (I don't recommend doing that, it tanks your grade). Easy class if you pay attention. Take Professor Vic!

Professor Cifarelli was honestly awesome. I had a MWF class at 8am and at first he made fridays online, and then just started sending out videos asynchronously. Theres three tests and a bunch of homework, but every 1101 class has it. I would take him again if I had the chance. He also does attendance on poll everywhere.
She has no idea what she is talking about. ever. She constantly makes mistakes and derails the lecture. The entire class has been lost from Day One and it's only gotten worse.

Prof. Powell is very quirky lol- She is very sweet but can get off topic, and she often messes up the lecture. However, I found her class very easy, and she teaches well. I found all the material easy to understand and got A's on almost everything.

DONT TAKE HER CLASS! She's very kind but does not know how to teach the material. She constantly messes up I'm talking at least twice a day, asks us if she did questions properly, and just overall doesn't know what she's doing. Her class is easy tho it's just the fact that you won't know what she's talking about or what to do so teach yourself.

This class is fairly easy and she allows unlimited attempts on homework. She makes sure to frequently review assignments to make sure everyone understands the material. The class does tend to get repetitive, but you know it's for your success. Tests are just like homework and in class assignments, if not easier. 10/10 would take again.
This professor dosen't know how to teach. I had to teach myself on youtube. Do not sign up for her class if you want to learn. If you're not a fan of corny jokes, definitely do not take her

He's kinda a robot. He lectures the ENTIRE 75 minutes, no seconds wasted. No personality, lectures are painful. But other than that hes a solid teacher. Will go on tangents about theory sometimes but shows lots of examples. Homework is meh, tests are relatively easy. Do the study guides, homework, show up, make the cheat sheets and youll be fine.

Spring 2025, This class is annoying, teacher is one of those people who believe in fairness and those teachers are always annoying and if anything bad happens to you in real life, he doesn't care, like he will put down 0's and no make ups. Very little extra credit, tests are too long which class never finishes, take the other stat teacher plz

Statistics is just a difficult subject but he was a decent teacher. He understood the content well and was good at articulating complicated topics. Eighty percent of the entire course grade was two tests and the final which is half the grade, often the tests are longer than you have time, no curve. Allowed us to use a note sheet on the tests.
Very generous grader on what's already a very manageable course. Good class for teaching the foundations of statistics.

This professor's lectures deliver the course topics in ways that are easy to understand. He is lighthearted and kind. A generous grader. We only had two tests being a midterm and a final, similar to questions seen on the homeworks. Overall, if I were to take this course, this is the professor I would choose every time.

very very kind and considerate of students. takes lots of care to explain concepts in a way that students can better understand them, is a lenient grader and gives lots of extra credit. best math professor that i've had at UNC Charlotte.

This professor is so kind and sweet. I did not take 3122 but 3128, it was not an option though. Two midterms and one final. Homework is easy and super helpful. Cheat sheets are allowed on all test, and he cares so much about his students. Honestly couldn't recommend a better teacher. - Oh also, he took attendance, but it was only for extra credit.

Kind professor who wants to see his students succeed in his class. Lectures were dry but he does an excellent job of breaking things down to ensure they can be more easily understood. We had 4 HWs and two tests (midterm and final). Took attendance about a dozen times during the term for extra credit on the next test. Would take again, 100%!

The professor teaches directly from his notes and sends out all assignments and notes by email. He does not use Canvas or any other online systems. He is very slow to respond to emails, but is better for going to his office hours.

He does not use Canvas, but sends notes in advance.  Lots of extra credit on tests.  Explained the concepts well, and his homework problems were tough but informative.  His handwriting is really hard to understand, but I got used to it by the end of the class.  He leaves feedback on homework and tests, but I couldn't always read it.

There was no canvas page and everything was sent by email, which was not too difficult. He sends out all the notes so it's difficult to attend or pay attention in class. He kind of just walks through his notes. Very lenient grader, as long as it seems you have the right idea you get credit. He's a nice guy, so was easy to pass, hard to understand.

Dr. Christou is one of the best teachers I've had during my time at uncc. I found that she explained things very well and is easy going. Just be sure to show up to class because she does take attendance everyday. I see other people mentioning her accent and while its noticeable, it was not an issue whatsoever.

Best class I've had all 4 years. Clearly explains concepts. You're able to review the lectures. The midterms were like the homework, and the final was like the midterms. Homework once a week. Two midterms. She was super kind and give me flexibility when I was going through a personal situation. She even gave candy. Super sweet and I love her.
The greatest professor of all time, hands down. I would get her for any and every class that you can.
She a good  teacher just had a strong accent
Dr. Christou is great. Lectures are very detailed and she gives you everything you need to know. Very approachable as well if you need help with anything

For exam prep be sure to work through every practice exam and at least understand the work to succeed in the actual exam. If you come everyday, show interest, and ask Professor McGoff questions it will surely put you in the positive. I one time overslept a midterm and Professor McGoff let me make it up in his office. Genuinely positive and helping.
He doesn't grade too harsh. Very considerate. His tests are A LOT like his practice exams/reviews, so make sure you look at them.
Great teacher very easy to understand but most of your grade is made up of the exams with no extra credit.

Provides great resources to study for quizzes and exams. If you study them, you will do very well. All homework was due by the final exam and grade is dependent on in-class quizzes and 3 exams. Would take again.

His explanation is fantastic! However, I don't recommend taking this class in the summer! Why? Because I think linear algebra is a big math, which you need more time and more practice. I got a B+ for this class. If I have a chance again, I would take it for fall or spring instead! Tip: Use ChatGPT to understand the lecture!

Dr. Li always had thorough lectures prepared for class and posted slides ahead of time for preview. She answered questions and worked through problems carefully, even going over quiz and test problems to prepare us for exams.

Dr. Li is a great Mathematics and Statistics teacher. She sets very clear notes that you can use within her classes to study on your own, and her review material is very similar to the tests she provides. Overall, great teacher for stats courses.

Dr. Li has a pretty standard Mathematics teaching style. She would mainly explain the Mathematical foundations of the topic, some of the intuition behind the statistics, and then transitions to application of the concept to R. She posts all the lecture notes and writes clean code so it's easy to follow where she's coming from.

The textbook is recommended, but you can find it online if you know where to look. Mock tests are very important, as the actual exams cover similar concepts. The final exam is comprehensive, so it is best to prepare for it consistently from the beginning of the course.

Dr. Margarint was a great professor and taught the course's content well. The test were similar to the homework and study guides and rarely had curve ball questions. The attendance policy was very strict (more than 3 absences was a failing grade). If you know you will show up to ever class and do all the homework you will do well.

Real class: 3128
Great professor. Great lectures (pay attention and notes!). Tests are difficult but he does mocks tests before which are great study material. Weekly homework every week that doesn't have a test (easy)

I LOVEEE Professor Vlad, he's the goat. We bonded over Radiohead and David Lynch. His lectures can get a bit boring, and his handwriting could use work but I just ask for clarification. All the notes are posted and there's barely any homework. He gives mock exams which are very similar to the actual tests and he provides so many resources.

Professor Vlad was a great professor! As someone who isn't strong in math, he provided everything I needed to pass the class! His tests are short, practically identical to the mock tests he gives to students for practice! Attendance was mandatory, and homework was given once a week, a few questions each!!  He also responds to emails quickly!!

Wow the previous reviews make no sense. I was scared bc of them, but after taking the class I can definitely say those reviews are wrong. Bo explained everything well and gave us really good preparation material for exams and good guides on how to complete the SAS homework. Sure he has an accent, but I don't think he's that hard to understand.

He's a Phd student they have alot on their plate. Reviews say hes hard to understand, I dont think this is the case although he does have an accent. He explains very well and is accomodating. Everything in this course was too easy imo, I needed it for my minor but its basically just a copy of STAT2122 with a more indepth look at regressions.

Professor tang was such a chill teacher who responded to my emails in literal minutes, I dont understand the negative feedback he has gotten, maybe he has improved but as of 2025 he is a amazing teacher, on average its about 1 assignment a week and 3 test a semesters, so if thats homework and test heavy for you, good luck with future classes

He can be difficult to understand in the lectures but as long as you do the work and study for the exams you'll be fine. Allows cheat sheets on the exams and the exams are very similar to the practice exams. Had no trouble getting a passing grade in this class.

Most students complain that he has a heavy accent, which is true, but he is knowledgeable and nice. The issue is his pace because if you do not get one part, then it's impossible to even catch up. He goes on tiny tangents about an equation or math lesson but then says, but you won't need that because that's for graduate classes.

He is very kind and is helpful with answering questions clearly. He gives partial credit on free responses on tests and gives curves. Calc is a hard course but I am happy I took it with this professor. Attendance is not mandatory but I recommend not missing a class to avoid falling behind.
The goat he will answer all your questions in detail and always has very helpful and informative examples every class. grades fairly quickly too and very fair, partial points given for work etc.
Took for CALC 3. Mainly just went through the entire textbook chapter each class lol so it was a bit lackluster. Exams were easy if u did the exam review, not a hard class.

The professor is very caring about his students, but the professor would do several mistakes throughout his lecture. The professor mostly wrote the Notes on the talk board and did not provide several examples. I would say that he did try his best in order for his students to understand, but he always did at least two-six mistakes in a lectureCALC 3

Nice guy with very clear and good lectures. He drops your lowest test score and gives you bonus questions on tests for extra credit. He also grades everything fast and fair so you always know your grade and what to improve on. Highly recommend him for Calc 2.

Mr. Reynolds lead my alg workshop in the fall, and he did a really good job. The workshops go over math and alg basics, and in your professors class you learn alg.  Because the workshops do basic math, they can be pretty boring, like adding fractions boring, but Mr. Reynolds was great at teaching everything and at keeping the energy alive and fun.

Prof. Reynolds was amazing, he's easily one of the best teachers I've ever had!!!!!  Calculus II is very hard, so be ready, but Prof. Reynolds does a really good job of making hard concepts manageable, plus he posts really good notes. No matter the class, if Prof. Reynolds is teaching it, I will immediately sign up for it.

By far the best math teacher I've ever had, he's super caring and has great energy.  He's really big on learning the how's and why's of a math idea, so that students can tackle any type of problem. This guy's always ready to help students, I've never had a teacher who cares as much as he does, he really does want everyone to succeed

Professor Reynolds was amazing, I had him in spring 25. Very good lecturer,  does a great job of breaking down big concepts and is really good at explaining problems. Always posts problems discussed in class, which were often variations of the HW problems. He's awesome, one of the best math professors I've ever had!

Josh is a great instructor. He is very concise with his work for the most part and easy to access. He is super understanding of your schedule, even with mandatory attendance. If you have MATH-1101 with a workshop, hope you get him as your workshop lead.
Lectures were all over the place. There was no clear structure, and the syllabus didn't match what we actually did in class. I felt lost most of the semester.
Grades way too hard. Took off points for tiny things and never explained why.
Does not respond to emails!!!! I would recommend a different professor if you can. Also very rude if you ask questions he does not like.
Very unclear when teaching. Made lectures very confusing

He's one of the nicest math professors I've had. He made the material very simple to understand and was always open to questions. He's a very forgiving grader on HW and tests, and the HW is fairly short (~5 questions per set). The tests were also pretty simple as a result of the practice tests he gave, which were nearly identical to the actual test

I love her so much! She's so understanding and is always there to help out if needed whenever she has time. Her office hours are so helpful as well, so engaging and even sometimes actually fun. She made me regain an interest in Math that I thought I lost ever since I started college. WOULD RETAKE HER CLASS 100x AGAIN, 100/10!

The majority of grades were based on Hawkes assignments, which were easy A's. Lectures were very detailed and easy to follow, and always open to questions. Hosts many office hours and is willing to work with you to meet one-on-one.
Ms. Walsh taught the material exceptionally well and made herself available after class to assist with questions or problems we had.

THE BEST professor to take for Diff Eq hands down. Lectures are easy to follow and well explained, gives great feedback on homework and does everything to prepare you for exams. Very approachable if you are not understanding a topic and it's easy to get extra help if you need it at her office hours.

Keara Walsh made Diff Eq easy and fun! She breaks up the content into bite sized pieces and answers all of the questions her students have. She is available frequently by email, office hours, or exam review sessions. Attendance is taken multiple times throughout the semester and as long as you go to class, you'll get the grade you want!

Not a lot of Homework, three tests in total. She curves grades generously and gives good feedback on homework/tests. You'll teach yourself whatever wasn't explained clearly in-class, but she posts annotated lectures and the lecture slides. Textbook is helpful. 10/10, great professor.
Just reads off slides the entire time. No interaction, no real explanations. Might as well skip and teach yourself from the textbook.
Lectures are confusing and all over the place. Gives tons of notes but barely explains anything..
Easily the most boring class I've taken. Just reads off slides in a monotone voice for an hour. Attendance felt pointless.
She does not respond to emails and lectures did not seem prepared at all.
Well meaning and caring. Willing to help students understand the course material.

Really enjoyed his class. You can honestly tell how much he cares about the subject and making sure his students understand. I emailed him before our final to clarify something and he got back to me very quickly and understood where I was coming from. Class is kinda tricky, but it is standardized between sections. Overall 10/10, would take again!

The main reason for the lower score is simply because of how confusing lectures can become and because of how test-heavy this class is. Other students in the class often lose track during lectures and work on other assignments during class time. Tests are also worth around half of the grade; however, there is a curve usually.

Professor Feng is the nicest guy ever! He really cares about his students and wants everyone to understand the material. His English can be a little hard to follow at times, but he's super patient and always explains things clearly. Overall, he's the GOAT — kind, helpful, and a great Calc 1 professor at UNCC! #hethegoat #best2everdoit

Very nice and caring professor. It can be hard to understand her at first, lectures are somewhat boring, and homework can be tedious. Tests are pretty similar to homework and in-class examples, but are difficult to complete in time. Attendance is mandatory with a sign-in sheet, and occasional participation problems in class.

She is a very caring professor and will reach out if you are struggling. Her lectures are okay but she does review content background very well. She gives optional homework as well as required assignments (less often). Attendance is mandatory but a small part of your grade. She did do exam curves where needed.

The lectures are very boring, and I feel like we focused more on learning formulas and definitions than solving problems on the board. Dr. Gao is a caring professor. There is tons of extra credit. The tests are hard and tough to prepare for, but Dr. Gao is a lenient grader and the tests are out of 110pt, not 100pt.

Lectures boring, read of slides, and interacted little with the class. She made us so unprepared for tests by giving us very easy test reviews and then making the test incredibly hard and nothing like the test review. Some of the questions she puts on the test are so time consuming it's impossible to finish them within the time limit.

She was truly caring. I went to her office hours very often and received a lot of help. The tests were a little challenging, but they were still based on the practice tests, and she gave extra credit.

I took MATH 2164 with her in the summer. She was very caring and treated every student with respect. Sometimes we asked her 5–10 questions in class, and she answered them quickly and kindly. The tests were tricky but she graded leniently. She has an accent, but her notes are clear.

She has a thick accent but she did her best to explain the material in lectures. The tests are heavy, although they are all multiple choice. I asked whether she could curve my grade to an A, but the answer was no. My overall grade did not drop at all.

She is an okay professor, but hard to understand due to her accent. It's better to learn the material before class to better understand her lectures. She doesn't give much extra credit at all compared to the other MATH1120 classes. Also, 2 points off your overall grade if you ask to round your grade. UCAE tutoring really helped for her classes.

She was truly caring. I went to her office hours very often and received a lot of help. The tests were a little challenging, but they were still based on the practice tests, and she gave extra credit.

I took MATH 2164 with her in the summer. She was very caring and treated every student with respect. Sometimes we asked her 5–10 questions in class, and she answered them quickly and kindly. The tests were tricky but she graded leniently. She has an accent, but her notes are clear.

She has a thick accent but she did her best to explain the material in lectures. The tests are heavy, although they are all multiple choice. I asked whether she could curve my grade to an A, but the answer was no. My overall grade did not drop at all.

She is an okay professor, but hard to understand due to her accent. It's better to learn the material before class to better understand her lectures. She doesn't give much extra credit at all compared to the other MATH1120 classes. Also, 2 points off your overall grade if you ask to round your grade. UCAE tutoring really helped for her classes.

Overall a great professor. His teaching style is a bit unorganized and fast-paced. We had 7 homeworks and 2 midterms and a final. The 7 homeworks are graded out of 50 each and due each week. He does give half points if you show enough work for the midterm and final. He does grade things late. study the slides and pay attention to the formula

At first, I struggled with his teachings because his slideshows were from past professors. So, I was nervous going into the first and second test but always announced that we could use two sided cheat sheets. I found the tests to be easy because of the cheat sheets and told put something down so you get partial points. He wants everyone to succeed!

Great professor. I took his online course during the Spring of 2024. There is homework due every 2-3 weeks. Our only tests were the midterm and final. The homework is graded out of 50 and there are 5 problems. You can get a bad grade if you do not do the homework well. I like his teaching methods-- he draws on the power points.
Class is very easy so far. It is lecture heavy but there is one homework assignment for each chapter. He gives us a week to complete it and it is easy.

By far best professor I have had at UNC Charlotte.  Teaches at the perfect pace and is very responsive to questions. One tip for the class: Do your homework, don't get behind, and it should be an easy A.

AMAZING PROFESSOR! Really kind and clearly cares about us understanding the material. Catered to different learning styles and made sure we were well-prepared for every single test. I was scared to take this class because I haven't done a math class in years but Professor West-Page made me feel very confident in my skills. One of the best around!

great math professor! lectures were super helpful, and they are very available outside of class. the test reviews are literally the best and get you very prepared for the tests. my piece of advice is to just attend lectures

They were so nice outside of class and they are great at explaining things. Throughout the semester, I would go to talk to them, and went to office hours where they were super helpful. They really want you to do well and they give great test reviews before the exam.

Professor Zhou was great! We had two midterms and a final for the class. The tests were fairly reasonable as long as you reviewed her homework and lecture notes; you'd be fine. Her lectures are a bit fast paced but you can ask her questions along the way. She isn't a tough grader either.

Her lectures can get a bit long because she doesn't interact much with the class, she reads off her slides and works out problems. However, she is very knowledgeable and the content she presents is very interesting and useful. She makes the class a little too easy, I think it needs to be a bit more math heavy and more SAS coding.

Professor Gartland was an overall great instructor however I did struggle with following along at some points because he likes to skip steps. There were a couple lectures that I would get a bit lost during some points but as long as you ask for clarification he is more than happy to explain.

He is a great professor, although he might tend to skip some steps, but you can always ask for clarification, he is a great professor to ask question, he wants you to pass. But remember, DO YOUR HOMEWORK!! He will not give late credit on homework at all.

Mr. Gartland was a very strong lecturer, he would tend to skip steps in his lectures, but he is really good at answering questions. I would say that you definitely need to practice the hardest problems per every section to do good in his test. He gives lots of partial credit so as long as you do not give up you will get a good grade on the test.

This was my second time taking 1242; I never thought I would understand the material as clearly as I do now. I took MAT 1242 with Professor Latimer. The way he explained and demonstrated the material was very clear, and He was happy to answer any question to help clarify the content, yes even 'dumb' ones. I would 100% take the class with him again.

most of the work is busy work and she is extremely tough on grading it. we have about 2 assignments a night. only good thing is you can revise and resubmit. not worth the headache. Also can be very rude.
TAKE HER, TRUST

The only math teacher I was able to actually learn in so far. Her homework was so understandable and she was very patient and caring for you to succeed. She taught in easy ways and made it simple to understand complex topics. Would absolutely recommend especially if math is not your strong suit.

I absolutely loved Professor Wheeler. Not only is she extremely kind, you can tell she genuinely cares for her students. She is excellent at explaining math in a way that makes sense, and is always willing to meet if you need extra help. Highly recommend!

This professor is the best math teacher I have ever had. She explains things so well and will go step by step if anyone doesn't understand. She holds a lot of study groups and helps as much as she can. Taker her class!

Zane Clark is the best professor in the Mathematics department. Super Chill and funny guy. It was his first time teaching Calculus 2 over the summer but his tests were too easy compared to other professors like literally I had a 99.79% in the class before the final and his final was so light that I completed the final in just half an hour. THE GOAT
If you have a heavy course load outside of this class please dont do this you have to be willing to sacrifice a lot of your brain space and time for this.

Expects you to learn everything before class, very fast pace and all over the place when actual teaching. If you taking this for pre-nursing, I highly recommend doing it a community college online. Literally quite impossible to get a B or higher in this class. And the homework assignment on aleks is pretty frustrating to do too.
Boi im prayin 4 u
Her lectures are all over the place so you have to teach your self before and after class. Which will take you hours depending on topic.

Dr. Striplin says a lot of information in lectures that you are expected to remember, so I recommend always taking notes and focusing on aleks. Doing the study guides with another person helped me learn better, but it's different for everyone. Make sure to study multiple days for tests and take go to TA sessions, or you won't pass this class.

I have always been good at chemistry. I passed AP chem with flying colors and skipped gen chem 1 because of that, however with the way she lectured, I couldn't grasp a thing. I had better luck copying and interpreting notes from my peers. This class is tough and I didn't feel like she cared to help me grasp topics when I struggled. Not fun for me.

Chem 1252 is a challenging course, but Mrs. Michaels makes getting a C or higher achievable. As long as you watch the PCA videos and attend the lectures, you will pass the course. She is very understanding and provides a lot of materials to ensure you pass the course. Overall, if you have to take Chem 1252, this is the professor you want to have.

You basically have to teach yourself everything. Class time is only 50 minutes, which is way too short for the amount of information being taught. Her lectures mainly focus on what the concepts are rather than practicing the math problems that mainly show up on the tests. She really likes to throw curveballs on the tests!!!
Worst for Lab. Changes due dates with

Worst lab instructor. Never helps when I have a genuine problem, and always puts the blame on the students even when the TA or herself makes a mistake. Labs aren't graded until 2 weeks later, no makeup or extra credit. Never lets you go over the lab to understand what you missed, rushes material, and worst of all changes due dates without notice.

Being completely honest this class is a bit difficult but Dr. Horger makes the class a bit more tolerable as he does alot of examples in class. He is really nice and does take time to answer any questions that you may have. Also some advice is his exams and weekly quizzes are very similar to the problems in the textbook so he does rely on it alot.

Truly the glue holding UNCC's chem dept together. He's a great prof + definitely teaches w/ a lot of structure, which my ADHD brain needed to pass this class on the 3rd try. He definitely expects you to put in effort outside of class, but in return he is patient, charmingly dorky, & open to all questions. Do the reading & problems before class!

I ended up withdrawing from the class because I picked up an overnight job, but I truly wish I could have stayed. I asked a lot of questions- he never had any problem doing extra problems to explain, and he always broke it down in a digestible way that made it clear to me. He was incredibly patient and cared about student success. Gonna miss it

I had Dr. Horger for CHEM 2131 and I did not like him but in CHEM 2132 I felt more engaged and his lectures were much clearer and structured. He does not do exams, only a quiz every other week. Pay attention in class and do the homework assigned and you will do fine in his class. He is a harder professor however it prepares you for the ACS final.

This is simply a difficult class! He's a great professor but you have to put in the work, know your stuff, and you CANNOT fall behind. Read the textbook! He gives a ton of practice problems from there and shares previous quizzes (no keys). Your grade is 65% 8 quizzes (lowest gets dropped), 25% cumulative final & 10% homework/in class assignments.

PLS for the love of God, don't take him. If you think you will make it, he will prove you wrong. All the bad thing said on him is just the tip of the iceberg. Save yourself from a withdrawal of a failed grade for yourself and your sanity.

Dr Etzkorn's class is more difficult than other teacher's 2131 classes. If you care about chemistry and want to learn the content, take him. Lots of homework. If you show up to office hours he will try to help you. He offers a lot of extra credit. I spent about 20 hours / week studying and doing the homeworks. Do all the homework, it helps!

A lot of what is said about him on here is true. His class is very difficult and you are graded essentially on three tests and a final exam (all compose about 75% of your final grade.) With that, you only need a 60% to make a C and, for the students who didn't withdraw, he gave a very generous 100 points of extra credit assignments.

Unfortunately this professor expects his students to dedicate a ridiculous amount of time to his class every single week, please take this into consideration for your schedule. I dropped the class because of all the homework assigned and his expectation of students to fully understand the material before lecture (then why have class?).

Dr. Etzkorn is the only professor I ever warn people about. You passing this class is based on his opinion, rather than number grades and he ended up only letting one person pass. Other professors even warn people about him. He is incredibly smart but cannot make the information easy to understand and provides no resources.

She was a great organic professor, she was reasonably hard as a professor, but nothing too crazy. If you do your weekly homework and attend problem sessions you will have a blast in her class. She is very caring and respectful.

Had Dr. Wells for Orgo 1 and 2 and she was a great professor. Lectures are easy to follow, exams are straight forward if you study a lot, homework each week but isn't too much. Accommodates students' needs as well.

Dr. Wells is a great professor. She is well knowledgable of the material and makes organic chemistry very easy to understand. I recommend doing the book problems and read her assigned book chapters. I would take her again if I could :)

I took Orgo 1 with Dr.Wells, amd her class is the first time in a while I felt set up for success. She has detailed lecture notes that leave me with a better understanding than the textbook, and the test questions mimic what we do in class, as well as the practice problems in the texbook. You MUST attend class, or you'll be overwhelmed.

Dr. Wells gives you everything you need to excel in this course including plenty of practice tests and problem session sheets that are very similar to the exams. Anyone who does not pass taking her just does not want it bad enough.

I found Dr. Krueger to be a very good lecturer. Definitely read the textbook, it helps significantly with the quizzes. Do practice problems from the textbook. I thought the quizzes were pretty straight forward if you read the textbook.

RUN. She makes Striplin's class seem like sunshine and rainbows! I'd switch to a Monday at 8am to get away from her if I had to. I would rather drink a gallon of colonoscopy prep right before a 3-hour-long final exam instead of taking another class with this woman. If you get stuck with her, go ahead and make an appointment with CAPS to save time!

There was multiple times where she would use google to tell us what were the answers. Sometimes she was very lost with the worksheets and then had the nerve to grade hard on worksheets that she waited till the end of the semester to grade. I would rather drink jeans through a straw and swallow a watermelon whole than retake this.

Terrible. Nitpicks the crap out of everything. Has to Google basic chemistry concepts. Accused me of sharing my prelab with a classmate even though I did not. Literally takes 5-7 business days to reply to emails. Often late, lectures for up to 45 min, and takes it out on us when we can't finish
If possible switch sections to a different instructor.

She seemed ready to work with her students. But, when somebody would ask her for help with a topic in lab, she wouldn't know how to help, was scatterbrained/flustered. I know the lab system is hard but stay away unless you want someone who isn't sure of the course material and will be openly upset at your class for not knowing exactly what to do.
He made the class fun and exciting. He was very hands-on and knowledgeable.
Really awesome professor. Make sure to study up for tests because they held most people back
He made the class fun and exciting. He was very hands-on and knowledgeable.
Really awesome professor. Make sure to study up for tests because they held most people back
He made the class fun and exciting. He was very hands-on and knowledgeable.
Really awesome professor. Make sure to study up for tests because they held most people back
Very nice professor! PCAS before every class, 4 exams then a final. Memorize her notes and the class should be manageable.

She is a pretty average professor. She is not as bad as people say, but she isn't a Dr. McKenzie/Slowey. She does not have problem session, so take that as you will. She does have a very structured lecture outline which is nice, and you do genuinely learn a lot from this class. Make sure your chem 1 knowledge is solid.

Be student who participates and gets to know your professors. It will be extremely helpful for your success in the class. The curriculum is difficult and it takes ALOT of work outside of the classroom to master the material for tests & the HW can be heavy depending on the week. However, she cares for her students and their performance in the class.

The practice questions in class and on the practice exam are nothing like the actual exam. There is no problem session like every other chem professor has. Rushes through the lectures when short on time and doesn't explain things much.

DO NOT take chemistry with her. Can't actually explain any of the concepts in depth. Lost 2 of my exams, before even looking she made it seem like I had faked taking the tests, and had me look through every students exams to find mine because she didn't see it, they were both there. I honestly don't understand how she still has a job.

Really bad. When assignments get turned in she and her TA's are very picky on the answer you put in like lets say its .5397 but the answer is .5340 your not getting an ounce of credit even if you got it right almost nuh uh and most of the exam review is sometimes not even on the test and is pulled from another question or thing you didnt learn DONT

Only reason why I took her was due to the fact she was the only chemistry teacher available. If I had to choose again I would choose someone else then her. She grades poorly and there is no resubmissions for it and when we get answers wrong she doesn't explain why we got it wrong, and then refuses to publish grades after a week notice. Don't take.

Marks assignmnts wrong when correct to see if you check. Losing marks for proper work.

No clear focus on how to submit assignmnts or prgrms to use. Lose marks while learning topics without opportunity to correct mistakes. get asked same question for more marks before having received the correction in the first place. potentially losing more marks.

Took as Hybrid. Doesn't post lecture slides on Canvas. Assignment directions can be confusing if you miss class.Attendance isn't part of the grade. Email responses are slow despite saying she will reply within 48 hours on business days. Tests and the final are 65 percent of grade,so other work barely affects it.

I took the hybrid CHEM-1200 course with this professor. We met once a week and the rest was online. Assignments and attendance barely affect your grade exams determine almost everything. If you miss class, you're out of luck because no lectures or PowerPoints are posted.

For this class … I would Pick a god and pray

He's an amazing lecturer, I had never taken an upper level chemistry course and learned a lot in his class. Take the time to prepare for exams, but he gives practice exams and teaches all information in class. Would DEFINITELY recommend his class to anyone taking CHEM1251.

An entertaining lecturer, you will not get bored listening to him. If you take his class seriously, then success should come. Practice, practice, and more practice. Reading the textbook proved valuable conceptually, and in-class polls are graded on accuracy
Literally loved him so much. I wish I could have him teach me every other chem class I have to take but alas that is not an option. If you can take him, do it.

Actually goated prof. Smart, funny, engaging, and helpful. Go to class and office hours if you want a good grade. Actually the best OAT. Very test heavy, so know your material. The class builds on itself.
absolute goat

He is a really sweet professor, but the work he provides for us for the exams is NOTHING like the actual exams. Our class average on the exams is really low, and sadly, it seems like I must retake the class because of the exams.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU CAN HELP IT. Not only is Professor Shelton rude and has an attitude when you ask questions, but he will not help in any way if you are trying to understand the material better. His exams are insane, not anything like the "study guides" we have. The TAs will be your saving grace and a blessing.

He's overall a good guy but your grade is really only determined on how you did for the exams. Lots and lots of homework that doesn't really go towards your grade at all. TA's don't go over how to solve problems rather than just give you the answer for problem sessions. Overall wouldn't recommend!

Hybrid course. Homework consists of discussions and problem sets that don't affect your grade. Tests are the only assignment that control your grade. Professor himself is very engaging and lenient. Sometimes handwriting is difficult to understand, he is very accessible. TAs give answers when doing problem sets rather than explaining questions.
Overall not a bad class but it would've been nice if he was actually in class for questions. You bassically have to teach yourself and practice otherwise you will indeed fail!!!

Dr Walter was by far one of my favorite professors! He is fun, kind, respectful, responsive and very knowledgeable. He was always down to meet with students and answer questions and you could tell he really cared about your understanding. O chem king for real. If you can take him please.

This professor was ahh sss. I had technical issues and she did not give a f. Like she just gave me a zero. Crazy part is that I handed the lab in before it was due and time to fix it but she was like I already graded so she gave me a zero and did nolt let me resubmit.
