This professor by far is the GEM of the chemistry department, even surpassing anyone at UNCC. The way she teaches and her dedication to the difficult subject and her effort to help them learn is MASSIVELY EXTRAORDINARY. Take this course with Prof. Pavel & YOU WILL SUCCEED. TAKE HER !!!!

I had a GREAT time! She may come across as harsh/cold, but DON'T take that personally. Very passionate about chemistry and willing to help. She is funny and will joke around with you. She is worth getting to know, and I would love to take all labs from her forever! She doesn't play favorites. Her pre-lab lectures run long.  Love her!

Great chem professor. Homework is hard but are very useful for tests. She gave extra credit so take advantage of that. Lectures are good and aren't the easiest to follow but will answer any questions thoroughly. Does not require a textbook and she says attendance is mandatory but it really isn't. Take her if you can.

She is passionate about Chemistry and will do her very best to ensure all students understand. ASK QUESTIONS! she will take as much time as needed to explain, she also gives guided notes (not for grades, just for personal use) to make sure you understand key topics from each chapter. She uses the extra practice questions on the tests, so do them.

Great professor! He's pretty tough and holds high standards, but he's also incredibly fair and genuinely wants his students to succeed. He pushes you to do your best and gives some of the most thoughtful advice, especially when it came to developing and refining our proposal documents. One of the most helpful and motivating instructors I've had.
One of the most engaging and supportive professors I've ever had. He cares about student learning and are always available to help!

Dr. Afonin has impressive knowledge and experience in grant/proposal writing. He uses real-life examples and teach you about what a real grant application look like. Speakers from academia, industry and gov lab are invited weekly to share their perspectives for career planning. He will push you hard but I find it very helpful for my career

I must say that Dr. Afonin's teaching style is very interactive and he is excellent in conveying the course materials that I like most. He is very knowledgeable and successful in his field, so he always tries to motivate the students to step out of their comfort zones and face challenges. He always provides constructive feedback on assignments.

Thanks to the intimate class size, we were able to receive incredibly constructive feedback on each of our proposals. The clear and challenging expectations pushed me to develop invaluable grant-writing skills that will be extremely beneficial as a Ph.D. student and in my future career.

Really nice guy, knows what he's talking about. He expects you to read the textbook before coming to lecture, and kind of goes over what was in the book. Uses a lot of example problems during lecture to teach the material. Only downside is he grades by a few things- quiz every friday, 3 exams + final, and one extra credit assignment.

Like any organic chemistry class, it's going to be extremely difficult. Quizzes every Friday, no homework to help boost grade, and 4 exams. Professor is great, easy to talk to, and truly does seem to care about your learning but the exams, in my opinion, are just so hard.

quizzes every week, which were given regardless of how prepared the class was. Tests were difficult and he provided little feedback. He taught the class as if we already know the material, and didn't help in office hours. other classes were insanely ahead of us.
The lecture is boring, I honestly learn more from the textbook. There are no homeworks, quiz every Friday, so exams and quizzes weigh a lot.

really friendly guy, but his lectures aren't helpful at all in terms of content on his tests. a quiz every friday, the way he lectures is not really teaching, more like just telling us what it is. also expects us to have prior knowledge on everything.

Not only is he very rude to his students, The projects in this course are unbelievable and his expectations are insane. there is a special topics project, multiple exams, 2 presentations with 10 page reports, and a closed book final. The professor does not have a canvas page DO NOT take any course with this guy.

#1 OG. Top Dawg.

Very knowledgeable and engaging with students. It's a tough class but as long as you're actively participating Dr. Slowey does the absolute best to help you understand something. So ask questions. Try to keep the same energy because it makes the lectures so much better. Overall definitely one of my favorite professors of all time
Dr Slowey is knowledgeable and explains concepts clearly. He uses examples during lectures. Exams are challenging. Practice tests are provided. He is approachable and office hours are useful.

Good professor, he is clearly VERY passionate about chemistry.

He goes through material very quickly but takes questions before moving on during lectures. Content builds off of previous units in this course so do not get behind. Just study and attend office hours outside of class if you don't understand something and you will be fine.

Funniest and chilliest chemistry professor I have ever had. Goes through lectures quick but provides a lot of opportunity to do practice problems and ask questions. It is important that you go to class with at least a preview of the textbook, so you're not totally lost in class. Practice problems are harder than the actual exams. Easy pass :)

Dr. Slowey is a good professor.  He demonstrated some growing pains like errors to problem solutions, but I wouldn't expect to see this next semester, as this was his first at UNCC.  It is of note that he gives relatable examples and of all the professors I've had, he is by far one of the most approachable, easiest to access, and willing to help.

Dr. Fessler was a great professor and fair grader. His class is based on 10 quizzes and 3 exams and a final. He provided 3 extra 8 points each and practice problems. In his class, you need to read the book and know the contents of the chapter very well by the end of each week. If you lose point go and talk to him with your reasoning.

The course is based on just quiz and test grades both of which are made up of very few questions that were often about very random things discussed in the course. Not nearly enough practice problems were given. Smart guy but doesnt teach in a way that is easily understandable to someone taking the course for the first time.

Professlor is an extremely knowledgeable guy about organic chemistry, but he is not effective at teaching students who do not have as deep of an understanding of chemistry as him. This course is entirely based on quizzes and test (the extra credit is mandatory if you want to pass). Would recommend staying away unless you have a PhD understanding.

Dr. Fessler ran this class exactly how Dr. Walter would. He gives extra credit on each test, gives plenty of practice to do outside of class, and cuts out information from the textbook that he doesn't feel matters. He isn't amazing at lecturing due to his inexperience, but, as a chem major I have had a lot worse!

I do believe Dr. Fessler is a good professor. However, if you are a bio major I would stay away from him. His explanations are very chemistry-heavy and do not relate to biology since that isn't his background. As a chem major myself I have enjoyed his class a lot. He is a strict grader, however, his exams reflect the practice exams given.
Great professor, engaging lectures, at times might be confusing, but very down to earth professor! would take class with her again
Newer professor but seems bored when lecturing. I've also heard that both of her grad students left her lab already in her short time here. Exams are difficult.

Notes are easier to read than Etzkorn's, but exam/final questions are impossible and barely relate to anything discussed in class. Lectures were disorganized and skipped around topics a lot. Practice exams provided (with no answer keys) were nothing like the actual exams we were given. Lectures and textbook did not prepare me for these exams.
Dr. Ge is a new lecturer and is nice and always prepared to teach. Only qualm is that she is prone to making small mistakes at times during her lectures. Hard class! Read that book cover to cover!

The ppl complaining are mad that she isnt the same prof as Troutman. The exams had copy-paste questions from the textbook problems she gave us. Shes a sweet lecturer and curves exams, and weekly canvas quizzes. Shes passionate about her field and knows what shes talking about. I wished her exams werent free response heavy tho. Read the textbook lol

No extra credit offered. Generated email response to all students, showing no effort to make the response personal. Took a month to get back to my emails. Goes fast through lectures and does not have patience with students.

Intro to chem. She is very nice and lets you retake the first 3 exams, they aren't too hard. Although it's an intro class and the topics are very easy, I highly recommend studying due to the memorization of elements, polyatomic ions, etc. Overall a very simple and normal introductory course with a good prof

This is an introductory chem classes and she teaches it like an introductory chem class. She lets you retake the first 3 exams (which is uncommon in the chem department), helps you tremendously in office hours and is very understanding and cares for you as a student. She understands that chemistry doesn't come naturally to everyone and thats okay!

Professor Kant is a very clear and organized instructor. Her assignments were fairly simple, and she thoroughly explains well during her lectures and such. I like how she switches her activities up, so it isn't just lecture all the time. Professor Kant is a lovely individual and does well with being supportive and available to all students.

super laid-back and sweet. I went to her office hours and emailed back and forth a few times she's really nice and accommodating if you build a relationship with her. I've heard from most of my class that it's their hardest class but if you pay attention during lectures she does a great job explaining and it was one of my favorite classes!
If you can avoid having her as a professor, do it.

She does not teach during class. She just hands out worksheets and there are no notes. You are expected to teach yourself the material, and there is a lot of homework. When asking a question her explanations are not helpful. I attended each class and still struggled. Her communication is also not the best. I would not recommend this course.

Hardest class I have ever taken. She does not teach, and you essentially have to do everything on your own. I have never cried over the amount of homework I've had to do until I've been in this class. There is too much work and I believe that we aren't even learning what's supposed to be in the curriculum. I am glad to be leaving this class.

Be prepare to teach yourself!

She's the kind of teacher that make you learn on your own, and if you ask for help it's unbelievably vague. When she does help us,she's more or less teaching to herself, and not to the class.

ALSO,Get ready to take a quiz/test on something you have no clue how to do, AND THEN get the notes on how to do it afterwards.

Awful teacher, doesn't teach at all, just gives busy work and expects everyone to understand. When asked for help literally answered with “No”, if you can avoid her classes, please do. Be prepared to have to have a tutor.
GOATTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT

Condescending mannerisms, ignored emails and then would put you on blast in a course announcement. Grading is very nit picky by the TAs, and LabFlow is a nuisance to success. Lecture slides never made sense, and no leniency on any assignments. Write your due dates down in advance, she does not allow for extension.
She was an amazing lab coordinator and was always willing to help.
Good lab. You be mixing potions.

She answers questions in a manner that is respectful and understandable. Was extremely helpful during the labs and will get your group on track if you get lost. Be prepared to write A LOT during the pre-labs.

Dhiman takes forever to grade assignments and when she does, she'll be extremely tough. She'll eat in the middle of lab and wear AirPods the entire time. Usually she'll be on the phone talking and if you ask her a question, she'll act like you're bothering her. She's little to no help and only assists if Rabinovich is present.
Professor Foley is a good teacher but the class itself is very hard so be prepared!

The topics are given very broadly and he wants you to spend OUTRAGEOUS amounts of time diving deeper into the topics on your own. There are pop quizzes, so show up. The test are written response, and no multiple choose! Don't take if you have more than 4 classes! Also, he says read the book, but also says don't memorize the book…
A lot of work and boring

Don't know why this is even considered a biology lab. You do maybe 2-3 labs actually related to what you're learning in lecture. Didn't receive the grade I wanted because he wasn't willing to accept the fact that we accidentally submitted the wrong document with PROOF that we did it in class and submitted it IN CLASS.

For a one credit lab, you get graded ridiculously harshly. Everything is busy work and even if your questions only require a two sentence answer, be prepared to write a paragraph FOR EVERYTHING or be prepared to get a bad grade. TA was very passive aggressive and for a lab, you have to make your own project. You only actually get to do like 2 labs.

This is quite literally the most ridiculous class I have ever taken. ZERO point in the difficulty level, much less for a 1 credit hour class. Honestly shame on UNCC for even continuing to have this class the way it is because there is absolutely no reason for it to be 8-20 hours of work a week. No experiments only lectures and projects.

Mrs Bates is the best teacher I think I have ever had. She cares so much about her students and our success. Shes very engaging and a great hands on teacher. If i could take her again, I most certainly would
Mrs. Bates is the best!!! She is such a caring and sweet professor. She is very interactive, and loves what she does.

Professor Bates is absolutely amazing! She has set the standard for being a phenomenal educator, you can tell she truly cares about the success of her students. She is very kind & understanding, also love her humor! She makes it easy to get an A in this course. Test 1 & 3 are on the easier side, test 2 is a bit more challenging. Take her!!

She is literally an ANGEL. Lectures make sense, she's so caring in class and over email, everything is explained clearly. Tests are very straightforward. Read the book and review class notes and you'll do great. Attendance is not really mandatory, but you will definitely do better if you go! One of my favorite professors!

I LOVE HER!!! These reviews truly weren't lying. She has to be one of the BEST professors I've ever had. She's extremely sweet and you can tell that she cares a lot about our success. She doesn't try to trick us on exams and makes reading guides for each unit to help us. I'd take her a MILLION times over. Pay attention, engage + attend office hrs.
3 exams with a cumulative final that replaces the lowest exam. Easy DB assignments, weekly pop quizzes, silly assignments, and LOTS of textbook reading. Class is manageable if time is put into it
Pretty funny dude but expects way too much from students
Participation matters. Mr. Goff really cares about his students but his exams require extreme critical thinking and often are very very in-depth with what he goes over in lecture.

His tests are hard for the majority of people, but if you study and ACTUALLY read the textbook and not skim it, you will get a good grade. He is very funny and personable, provides lots of real world examples. Make flashcards on key vocabulary, type down his lecture information. Go to his office hours if you have the time.

He is very funny, and emails you back most of the time.  His lectures are super interactive and honestly are very entertaining. His exams are extremely hard. You need to take ur notes ahead of time, and just annotate them with what he says. The class was difficult to do well in.

Overall, a great professor with clear knowledge of the course material. Lectures were well structured and organized in a manner that allowed students to work through and understand the information without becoming overwhelmed.
Her powerpoints are very good and she literally gives you fill in the blank notes that are really helpful. I think it's really hard to fail this class unless you do nothing.
Lectures during the first class of each week, followed up by an activity to cement understanding during the second class.  Dr. Somayaji wants everyone to do well.
Wonderful sweet lady. But the tests are SO hard. Attendance is mandatory. She drops one test grade though

The only reason I didn't get a higher grade in this course was because it took me until the final exam to learn the study methods that worked best for me in this course. Dr. S really cares about her students and wants to see us not only succeed in her class but also in the medical career we pursue. Beware of heavy course material!

Took this class in Fall 2024. Exams are challenging, but if you study the powerpoint slides and follow the note outlines you'll be fine. Reviewing homework questions also helps as sometimes they will appear again on the test albeit reworded. Review sessions with TA are also helpful. Be careful though, because it is a lot of information to memorize.
She is not polite and caring. and doesn't let us to ask the questions in the class.

She's friendly and approachable, but not an effective teacher. The course relied heavily on lectures, and the exams were based on her PowerPoint presentations. The chapter outlined notes she provided were essential for my success.

Take her class if you're a good tester. Outside of this class she is an incredible resource and person, and she's incredibly kind. Unfortunately, the tests are effectively 50 trick questions and there is no way to get extra credit or test corrections for her class,. If you're going try this class under her, only use the PPTs and go to lectures.

Don't worry, Bullock's inability to concisely summarize material in lectures is coupled with her insanely detailed and nightmare inducing exams. This would be less annoying if her exams were based on the textbook, but they're not- and they're also nothing like the homework assignments. Avoid.
Dr. Wells makes her online classes incredibly engaging. Not only was the content interesting, but her video lecture with quizzes also helped to absorb the content.

Overall, she is a nice person, but her late work policy is ridiculous and unforgiving. She accepts no late work, even if you submit 5 minutes after the deadline. She claims she cannot make exceptions to accept late work, but there would be no need for an exception if she didn't have a crazy policy. Don't take if you work or have a busy schedule

This professor honestly isn't very good. She often makes mistakes using technology (in an online class) and I just didn't get much out of the two courses I took with her. If you have a genuine interest in learning biology then I would take another professor's class.
She is a great professor. There is a group project over the course of the semester but very easy.  This class is truly light work!!

Dr. Wells is a fantastic professor. Come to class prepared each day having done your reading and assignments and you'll do great in her courses. She is passionate about conservation and is a great biologist. Highly recommend her classes.

She's really easy to talk to and nice. Will re-explain things if you ask. Her lectures are boringly mind-numbing BUT the class is easy-- no tests except the final, just reading guides, learning catalytics (like poll everywhere), weekly chapter quizzes as HW and worksheets graded on accuracy. If you need an easy bio prof Harris is the way to go.
Not understanding gives no grace to students. It doesn't take much to be understanding and personable. But she ain't got that
The class was easy. Her TA mainly ran the class so I can't really rate her as a professor.
She is a great teacher, who is helpful and understanding. The material in this class is tough and extensive, however she offers many resources and grades very graciously.

Professor Harris is incredibly kind and an excellent teacher. She makes biology simple and clear, even for students who don't love the subject. There are 4 exams and a final; ours were open notes, but that may change. Homework is straightforward, and her expectations are clear. Overall, a very good and fair class.

The TA that taught my class was Janki, she was a very sweet girl. There's a quiz in the beginning of each class 3-4 questions. The material was easy and her teaching was concise. There wasn't any homework (unless you didn't finish something in class). The final is a presentation, which was easy, just cant use flashcards.

Amazing lecturer and gives activities in class to understand the material. A lot of homework tho, but honestly it helps your grade. Attendance was mandatory but you can miss 3 days. Exams were straight from the learning objectives so make sure you study those, there were 3 exams. Final was optional if you were happy with your grade. Sweet professor

Class was fairly simple, lectures helped understand concepts a bit more indepth. Easy homework, but exams are 60% (there's 3). The study concepts she gives are basically the questions on exams. There is a textbook, but it wasn't used and when it was needed she supplied the pages. Pollev questions for attendance.

Took 3144 w/her and loved her!!There are 3 exams not including the final exam which is optional. There was also a group project which will be presented at the end of the course. Very interactive class and tests were fair. Tests consisted of multiple choice and free response questions but you should be good to go if you study the chapter objectives

Dr. Wisner was an amazing professor! I loved going to her lectures, and she made the info really easy to understand. There are pollev questions in every class. The exams were not terrible, the final exam is optional if you're happy with your grade. I would def take her again!!

100% of your grade is from exams. He changed the dates of his exams a month into the semester. After telling all the students to plan accordingly. Online test can only be taken during class time. There are no makeup tests under any circumstances. Most questions are written response, but he will take off points for defining additional terms.

This class was TOUGH. Lockdown +webcam for exams which you wouldve barely passed had you been allowed to have open notes. Going to class helps in his exams but his classes are boring. Seems like he lost his passion for teaches and now just does it for a paycheck. It is possible to get a B but no shot you'll get an A. Only grades are exams.
Tough grader, would not take again

WORST PROFESSOR EVER! SAVE YOURSELF AND DON'T TAKE HIS CLASS! I am a straight-A student and this professor basically ruined my chances of getting into a grad school because of how strict he is with his rules and is not easy-going or understanding at all.

Powerpoints = Confusing
Tests = Confusing wording
No calculator/paper (Dihybrid cross/chi square is impossible) 
4 Exams, Optional final, but better hope you get the final cuz it cant be dropped
Monotone and boring
Little care for students
Snarky/Pretentious comments in emails and announcements
Document which explains "how to success", all lies.

He's difficult to understand during lecture and rambles. If you ask a question, he'll just repeat what he said previously and won't try to explain it differently. Graded by 4 exams (22% each)&pollev, No final. Very difficult to succeed if you don't do well on an exam because there is no hw/extra credit to bring up your grade. No responses to email

He has a very heavy accent, making it harder to understand. His class is based on exams only of 300+ slides, he has no hw no projects, just exams and pollev. He does not answer questions, and even if you participate, he will let you know you are wrong. His reviews are not helpful either. I would not recommend taking him.

Amazing lectures on virology, very informative and up too date with current events. A fair lecturer as well with the tests not being to hard as you are allowed a cheat sheet to work with. I would recommend taking his class heavily, and you will easily get an A.

This is the third class I've taken with Valery, he might be one of the best professors in biology.  He doesn't give HW or Projects or Papers or group work; just 4 exams and attendance which is taken with PollEv and graded for accuracy.  He allows a cheat sheet on exams.  Build this sheet from his lecture slides and you'll do fine.  10/10 for Valery
You can barely understand him, he rambles during every lecture and doesn't answer questions. The tests are based on about 300ish slides and they are not easy. DO NOT TAKE HIM

Tests and quizzes were online. Lab TAs taught all of the material. Labs were difficult to understand in a group setting, in addition to the post-lab questions. The written lab reports take up most of the grades, so meet with the CC (it does help). The class is hit or miss based on your lab TA. Do not miss class and turn all assignments in on time.

This lab was very frustrating. Labs themselves were fine so long as you can just do what is in the instructions but everything else is very annoying. 3 research papers with extremely vague and unhelpful instructions, constant cancellations, and extremely slow labs. Not super difficult if you can make sense of the lab reports but good luck with that

Easy class. She's just extra. The labs don't correspond with what we are doing in the lecture. Papers didn't help understand the material. Decent amount of work and it seemed unnecessary. Easy A though. Just follow the rubrics
Dr. Pass is so sweet and caring. She always looks out for the best for her students and will always work with you no matter what. You can tell she loves teaching and cares about all of her students.

Make sure to be at every class, if you miss a lab you will have to make it up for an hour-long one. The midterm is very easy, however the final is challenging. Provides a study guide but then puts different material on there. During the final some questions were just blank so I'm not sure what that was about.
Dr. Chi is great. He's very funny and engaging. The class is very easy. There is so much extra credit. I never read the textbook and got an A on every test.

Dr. Chi is a great professor. Very clear that he enjoys what he does. I unfortunately couldn't attend all of his lectures due to biochemistry and had to devout a lot of time to it, but he is very genuine and I believe he doesn't want anyone to fail at all. Very funny guy and knows how to keep students engaged while delivering his point across.
Still no clue how to study for test. Getting good grades in all classes except this one. Teacher is not clear on what to study for the test that makes up majority of grade.

TBH only got an A in the class because of the extra credit. He is a really funny person and incredibly knowledgeable, but if you wanna meet him personally it gets really serious. Like a 180 on the personality, but his class wasn't bad but at times there were points where he would fly through lectures. Cool guy tho.

Dr. Chi is a really caring professor and very funny! He gives lots of bonus points, the final is not cumulative, and the homework and paper assignments are pretty easy! However he goes so fast in lectures and spends a lot of time talking about topics not aligned to exams. The study guides are useless, and this course is HARD

I really enjoyed having professor Ferdous. I took her class online and she was always quick to respond and helped with any issues that my group encountered. The class was challenging but she provided study materials to be prepared for each exam. I would really recommend this professor to anyone taking BIOL-1110 online.
Assessments weigh 60% of the grade... and they are hard as hell.

Group discussions that had to be presented, or an individual presentation/group presentation each week. Never had a real lecture, which sucked because the information in the course would have been super interesting.  Weekly group assignments in college is not ideal and is extremely difficult due to differences in schedules. Very unfortunate!!

Dr. Ferdous makes what could be a really neat class awful. The tests are EXTREMELY information heavy and you basically have to memorize everything on the slide sets to do good on the exams. The average exam grade throughout the semester was around a 60. She blamed the class for the bad exam grades after every exam. Do not recommend at all!

This course was more difficult than it needed to be bc of this professor. She seemed to want to help when the entire class was struggling, but made it feel condescending. No matter how much you can study, memorize, or learn, there is not enough time for the material to be fully understood before exams; which were short answer/MC.

This course had only four exams, which focused on small details. The lectures were unfortunately not very engaging, presented in a monotone manner, with little enthusiasm, which made it difficult to stay focused. Most of my learning had to come from teaching myself the content outside of class, since lectures didn't offer much insight.
DO NOT TAKE 3166 in person with this professor! Only graded on exams and exams are difficult! Does not email back promptly. Please dont be dumb and take this class for credit with him. Reg

The grade is 100% based on exams with no study guides. He did provide extra credit for attending weekly review sessions, and a few practice quizzes. It is hard to understand what he is saying, and the lectures are not paced well or very helpful.

Exams are difficult and is your ONLY way of passing the class. You can get extra credit (it's not a lot) by attending the study session, but it was hard to attend because they were all during my class times. Everything he says in lectures is just word vomit of info. Unless his polices and teaching change, DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS & SAVE YOUR MONEY.

Professor is so unengaging and shows little care about the class. He doesn't teach, he just talks. 4 exams make up your entire grade (and they are extremely difficult and specific). No resources other than unorganized slides, textbook, and practice quizzes that look nothing like the test. NOT OPEN NOTE (as of fall 2025).

Very receptive of class feedback and it is a bio class, come to class and don't leave early. She is such a wonderful person! There are group projects that the class could do without but good class, great teacher. Reading/explaining the textbook was useful for me, take detailed notes in class, she mentions things that aren't always on the slides.
Good luck teaching yourself everything!!

Prof Lassiter is SO RECEPTIVE of class feedback. After the class did bad on one of the exams, she changed teaching method & genuinely helped my grade & made me motivated to learn bio a lot more. She offers lots extra credit (I didn't get an A on a single exam, i got an A+ on the class) Gives credit for participating and showing up TAKE HER!!!

This teacher is the best she is so helpful and will listen to your questions and genuinely wants to help do better. She gives so much extra credit because she wants to help your grade. She makes to keep it engaging as well as interactive. Her tests are pretty simple as long as you go through the slides and review material.
This class is a class where you have to teach yourself.

Very interesting class. The material was fun to learn. Exams were long, free response only. No lockdown used. The homework assignments rarely aligned with the material we were doing in class. Not sure I cared for the format of the class but it wasn't very hard.

Will teach you a lot about molecular biology, introduce you to open source websites for analyzing genetics - stuff you can mess with on your own, useful if you are interested in graduate school/research. Highly recommend. Not much structure, req discipline. Exams short, know red ppt terms. Grades lenient. Tough around the edges, good prof.

You won't know where you stand in the class until a week before finals, she is terrible at grading!! Really rude and mean when you contact her in any way. Knows students are struggling in the class and refuses to change. Her tests are around 20 long answer questions and she only gives 1 hour to complete it. Fells like she wants people to fail.

Worst bio class I have ever taken. Super unorganized, a lot of assignments that are more related to Bioinformatics than Molecular Biology. Assignments do not relate to the lectures at all. Lectures are also just videos of her reading from slides that are super detail heavy so it feels as if I have not learned anything.

TERRIBLE TEACHER!! she only reads from the slides during her lectures. does not explain how to complete the hw assignments. gets annoyed when we ask questions. has 1 hour of office hours/ week, and sometimes she won't be in her office then and will not notify anyone. Worst professor I ever had. Acts like she hates teaching and her students.

The course seemed interesting, but I ended up dropping/withdrawing. Seemed fun until she graded homework. She would claim she's not looking for a "perfect drawing," but was SUPER critical, would leave harsh feedback, and grade very harshly. Lectures were also unorganized and super fast. You would be better off finding another class.

such a fun class and great professor. goes over a LOT of topics, from all of arthropoda, to apiculture and med-vet. exams are straightforward with 20-30 ec available for memorizing families. hw is drawing NC insects, and grades fairly. very flexible, engaging lectures, and a professor committed to getting students interested in the world of insects

I absolutely recommend this class. Dr. Stellwagen is a very passionate professor. As much as I hate bugs, I loved this course and became fond of bugs. There's a lot of information to memorize, but it's definitely doable (took this course while taking biochemistry). She offers a lot of extra credit and sometimes extra credit for attending class.
Dont recommanded him!

Dr.Yan is a great professor, he made me interested in the field of dev. biology. the class content is TOUGH, with so many different concepts to shove into your brain, but it is still doable. His lectures are hard to follow at times, but he genuinely wants the best for his students. i would take this class again just for the content! nice prof!

Dr. Luo's class makes some of the mysteries of grad school clear. This course is extremely valuable for grad students and should be offered every semester, even pushed as a core course. We rarely get the chance to flush through our ideas and writing in this depth.
So inspirational! Really pushes you to think in a holistic point of view and think critically about the impact of your work.

You can definitely tell that she cares about her students, providing fast responses and useful feedback on class assignments. Can be a tough grader. Exam format was different from the norm and challenging, but overall interesting class.

Not very understanding. Textbook was given online - from an intro level class. Most info was severely outdated hypotheses. Quiz questions barely made sense. Final exam was 40% marine viruses, we had 4 slides. Harsh grader on participation, hurts you when others don't participate. Same answers got different scores.

The professor was great. Engaging, encouraged questions, treated everyone with respect, challenged the way we thinked and provided timely feedback. I enjoyed the course content and learned a lot from this class!

Took her for BIO-3111, nice professor but lectures are boring and the tests are INSANE. Quizzes are to be done at home and no homework otherwise so you can focus on studying, but even that won't be enough to be prepared for the tests.
I took her for BIOL 3111, but that's not an option to select. She's nice, but she mostly just reads off lecture slides. Tests are difficult so make sure you study.
She's amazing. Take her if you can!!!!

Dr. Johnson is kind and very enthusiastic w/ a dry sense of humor. Be aware that this class is extremely dense in material. To be successful, attend every class, do the reading beforehand, & don't procrastinate studying for exams! This class is fun but requires work!

I took her for both micro and another bacteria class. I like the layout of her class mainly being lectures and only 1 assignment before exams. Her exams aren't too bad you just need an understanding of the info more than just what her slides say. Overall she is not a bad professor, it's just a lot of info you need to take in which is normal!

Tests were 60% of our grades & rather tricky. We had a project due at the end of the semester (w/ an initial rubric). The wk before class ended, she updated the rubric w/ new requirements which was a huge inconvenient for those who completed the project. She changed our online tests to in-person randomly. Doing well in the class almost impossible.

This was an okay class. Dr. Johnson sometimes, especially near the end of the semester, would fly through the slides in class and not thoroughly explain those topics. The assignments we do outside of class have little to no benefit in helping us learn the material, it was just busy work, not about lecture material which added more stress.
She's super sweet. One of my favorite classes!

4 exams where she allows 1 page notecards for some. She'll tell you when quizzes will be so attend class. Quizzes right before exams are extra credit for the exam. She also has flexible office hours in case you have any questions. There was one project 10% of grade where you volunteer for 6 hours and submit proof and a reflection. Overall 10/10.

Dr. Pruett is new @ UNCC. She is very sweet and really wants you to succeed. Weekly quizzes are straight from her slides. There are in-class worksheets and Pearson homework for every unit (P.S. some quiz/test questions come from them!). Tests are quite fair, and for heavy vocab units she allows a one-sided flashcard and gives a lot of extra credit.

This class was extremely difficult;one of the hardest I've taken. She's a tough grader, but also one of the best professors. She truly values student input and teaches critical thinking, not memorization. Lectures are fun, but exams (even open note/book) are brutal. Be ready to work outside.

Loved the topics explored in the class! Lecture-heavy, exams are about 70-80% written response. Still, they aren't really about memorization of lecture content but more about what is the bigger picture we can pull from the world of fungi. Weekly Perusall Reading and discussions are fairly simple just a bit long to read. Interesting course though!

Attendance is not required. He talks more about the derivation of formulas/theories INSTEAD of doing practice problems with us/applying concepts. Answers emails enjoys his job, but is not the best at teaching us what we ACTUALLY NEED TO KNOW HOW TO DO for the exams. The EXAMS ARE 60% of your grade. (2 exams plus a final).
do NOT take this guy. take physics 2 at a community college and cheat, you will never use this again, unless you're a stinky Electrical Major
Lectures tended to be bland and unengaging. The TA's classes were much better and helped me out a lot. External sources were also very helpful for learning the material.
Bro made more mistakes than relevant points, if I didn't have a good TA I would've been cooked. He cares more about his drawings than the actual topics and is always screaming at his iPad.

Aktas handwriting is like chicken scratch. The physics department in uncc is the worst. The attendance on day 1 of lecture = 100%, day 2 = 50%, day 3 = 4% and very soon the physics department will only be teaching walls not human beings. Pathetic. Avoid him at all costs TAKE IT AT CPCC!
This professor is impossible to follow, don't take him.

I can not give the full scoop on Dr. Leilabady because I did not attend the lectures. As for the course itself, please start studying early, get your hands on previous practice exams. There are only two midterms and a final. Your grade in the class is based of those 3 test, nothing else hardly. Studying past exams and the practice sets are vital!!

Do NOT treat this like any class you've treated before. The test averages are in the 50's year around. You WILL fail this class if you do not make it your top priority. Study WEEKS in advance. Leilabady is there to teach you the concepts, you must do the practice on your own. This is a massive weed out class so prepare yourself.

I was not used to Leilabady's lecturing style because his way of teaching would be coming up with example problems and showing the process of deriving answers. Most times there would be no numbers, just notation to follow, so it was hard for me to keep up with sometimes. Lecture is more supplemental than anything. Self learning helped me get an A.

Dr. Leilabady is not good, but you are basically forced to take him so get ready. Monotonous, poorly structured lectures that don't teach you anything for one of the hardest conceptual classes you will take. Will randomly call on you for pseudo-class participation, but everyone stops going to lectures after two weeks anyways.

Dr. Farida is the best professor I had this semester. Even though the content of the course can be difficult to understand, she has a plethora of resources available. She offers study hall hours with herself or her TAs. She offers extra credit and bonus questions to help pat your grade.

Dr. Jacobs is great, gives extra credit and has lots of availability during the week for office hours on zoom led by her or one of the TAs. Do the homework, extra credit assignments, and go to review expos and you'll be fine.

She is such a kind professor, and offers plenty of partial/extra credit. I am not great at physics but she made it easier. One thing: you can skip lecture and rewatch the videos she posts, but do not skip recitation sessions since they are attendance based, have worksheets, and weekly quizzes. Quizzes aren't too bad. She drops your lowest.

I was originally very skeptical of this class because of all the low reviews, however, Dr. Jacobs is a wonderful professor who clearly cares about her students. She has several study halls that are easily accessible and provides numerous extra credit assignments throughout the semester.

I took her over the summer She is very nice but overall she is super strict so listen to her directions when taking test and do not look like your cheating!!! She does offer a lot of extra credit but please watch her lectures and practice the problems since they are similar to the test.

He is a decent lecturer that has an accent that sometimes makes things difficult to understand, and sometimes he does not explain concepts well.  He answers questions in a convoluted way.  I would recommend studying with friends or looking up supplemental content online to understand things clearly.   He does offer ec for attending all lectures.
Pretty funny dude, topics weren't very difficult, and lectures were pretty good and easy to understand definitely better than some of the other physics professors.

These bad reviews are mostly false. Dr. Gerges is a decent teacher. He does have an accent, but you can still understand what he's saying for the most part. As long as you study all the practice and recitation problems, you can definitely get an A, or a B+ at worst.

He was an okay professor I thought he was okay at teaching but honestly I just stopped going halfway through the year and instead watched the videos on canvas. Theres not really homework or anything so if you wanna do good u just gotta make sure you put in the effort to practice throughout the year

He is very bad at lecturing and explaining topics. He just reads from his notes every lecture but it's still confusing. He doesn't stay on a single topic and explain, he jumps from topic to topic because he gets distracted. Luckily attendance isn't mandatory but he does say he will curve your grade if you miss less than 3 lectures.

Gerardy is a kind guy; he genuinely wants to help but can go off on tangents. The content is difficult, the size of the class is big. Have a strong trig/algebra background before this class. If this class is the only option for you, PLEASE go to study halls and office hours. Him and his TAs are very kind and helpful and want you to succeed.
Get ready for a bunch of homework. The tests have random things on them. If you're not religious, find one and start praying.

This class had very many assignments. The grading criteria was very confusing. The class is test heavy. The professor himself is very good, nice, and he is a very good teacher. The professor gives every student time even with hundreds of students if you ask for help.

Worst of the worst. Attendance is mandatory. Group work. Placement test on the first day of class, which is graded and we haven't even learned anything yet. Rambles too long for a simple topic and gets off topic.

Very passionate and is great at explaining things in multiple ways if you don't understand. Knows the content very well and it's hard to do bad in his class. Grades fairly. Offers SO MUCH extra credit at the end of the semester, you just have to put in the effort. I finished his class with a 100. Final was worth 100 out of 112 possible.

Currently taking 3220 and learning more from YouTube than this guy's lectures. Everyone who's taken Linear Algebra agrees that the way he teaches the subjects is weird, and that's not even a prerequisite for the class. In addition he teaches to the blackboard while body-blocking, once was an hour late and made a point about refusing to apologize

she was overall very sweet and the class was pretty easy. it felt more like a gpa booster than a good class for if you liked astronomy. I think the lab really aids in the experience considering its a fully asynchronous class. her lectures are alright and watching the videos are for a grade. overall she gives a lot of extra credit at the end

I didnt like that we had to purchase the book just to do Homeworks since there's no reading but her lecture videos are required for attendance.The project instructions made it seem that you could work independently but thats not the case, therefore most people will work on something they have no interest of. Overall good/caring professor.

Very unorganized canvas page, to the point where major assignments can be overlooked, and her video lectures are not the easiest to understand. I would recommend referencing the textbook over her for the work in the class. Class overall wasn't that hard, just seemed lazily put together, but she's very nice and generous with extra credit

EXTREMELY unorganized professor, assignment descriptions make little to no sense, group work is required in a fully asynchronous class, attendance was taking for watching the videos which had a constant noise in the background that gave me a headache, the syllabus is a mess, her answers to questions are incomplete most of the time.

Dr. PA is a nice professor. She seems to care about her students, and for the most part responded in a timely manner; which I respected. My only issue was the short time frame for our tests, as they were online so I expected them to be open for more than one day. Overall, though, she was sweet and the topics we learned were interesting.

Did not use canvas so I didn't know my grade most of the time. He tries to explain things the best he can. Don't miss class because somethings get confusing. I used Radiology Tutorials on Youtube to understand the class.

Great guy, I thought he was super funny. Lots of great guest speakers, one of which inspired where I want to go in my career. The only hard part is the final project, which was hard because we had to research a physics topic and I don't know that much about physics yet lol

Dr. Fried is a condescending, disrespectful, empathy lacking individual. Picks on students in workshop, does not respond well to feed back, and is a nuisance to the learning environment. He needs to be removed from all physics departmental positions. Ruins peoples passions of grades and any motivation to succeed in their academic careers.
The class itself is difficult but he does his best to break it down and is very understanding if you need extra assistance or accommodations for any reason.

This class was hard! He rambles during most of his lectures, but he knows his stuff. The quizzes weren't horribly hard but did require lots of studying. He was a lenient grader and drops lowest grade in each category. He was always willing to answer questions and enjoyed participation. Brought real materials into class to demonstrate.

He is a nice guy, but he makes physics so hard. The practice tests are nothing like the actual exams and I genuinely don't know how people do well in his class. The TAs are amazing, very passionate about physics, and so is Dr. Heindl, but he is very monotonous and it's easy to get lost in class.
This teacher is a secret IDF agent that will not help you reach your desired level of academic success.

Mr Heindl is a great professor. Always shows up with a positive attitude and even is humorous during technical difficulties. Replying to other ratings: lectures can get boring but Heindl does what he has to do: teach. His lecture notes are more than enough to understand a topic. 1 day is lecture, the second is practice problems.

In all honesty he's a good professor, the only harp on him goes to the difficulty of the class unfortunately. He genuinely wants to help people understand it, he typically has one day of lecture and the other with practice problems. I would 100% take him again over the other two available physics professors if I had the choice.
Just talks a lot during lectures and youll get lost everytime. I stopped going to lectures after a few weeks to save myself the time.

I took quantum mechanics with Dr. P. It is an incredibly difficult subject but he structured the class very well and so it was hard, but not frustrating. He made sure to teach the subject matter before the homework was due and went light on us the week before any exam so we could prepare.

13 Homework assignments, 0 graded by the time the final exam showed up. He does return your exam grades quick but actual exams only can be accessed in his office during office hours. Extremely boring lectures and is always behind schedule to the point that we had 2 exams on the final week of classes with the final while still missing 2 more exams.

Poor time management; consistently late and holds students past class. Lectures are repetitive with scattered notes. Feedback is nonexistent, with homework remaining ungraded for months. Assignments have short 3-day turnarounds. Grading is heavily weighted on exams (85%), and the professor speaks very quietly. Some online material is available.

Really nice professor, but can be boring at times. Asks a lot of questions during class to try an keep it engaging though. Assigns lots of homework, sometimes with 2-3 days till its due. Exams were fair and I never looked at them after the fact but im sure he's a lenient grader. He also came to my job and tipped me good, so he's my goat.

Tests aren't super hard. Beaman is a terrible teacher, so prepare to teach the course yourself. He runs over time on every class and comes to each class late. He doesn't really care about students doing well or understanding the course. He strictly abides by his syllabus and doesn't round. Personally would never take another class with him.

Beaman's Dynamics is interesting. He is probably the easiest Dynamics professor at the school. But on the other hand, his lectures were a snoozer. I thought his tests were very reasonable and on the easier side which is great. The homework was annoying, as he would randomly post them and have them due in 3 days. Take for 3121, but idk about 3122.
You get what you put in for her class. The tests so far have been based on what we do in class.

Professor Smith is genuinely one of the greatest professors I have ever had. She is very transparent about expectations, grading, and what to expect on quizzes and tests. She genuinely cares about her students and by the end of the semester will have everyone's name memorized. I would highly recommend taking Professor Smith.

I'm currently taking MEGR 2141, and I have nothing but great things to say about Dr. Smith. She genuinely cares about how well the class is learning, always willing to answer questions, and takes the time to walk around and give individual help. Good energy, clear lessons, welcoming environment to participate. 10/10 would recommend.

Tough class but lecture was pretty easy to follow along, homework and additional study materials were all focused and helpful. If you were confused, she was good about going more into detail, and she's accessible outside of class.

Dr. Smith is the goat, if you have yet to take Statics you need to take it with her. All her presentations were thorough and comprehensible, which definitely helps in a class that can be challenging at times. She also made extra lecture videos which were extremely helpful, especially for practice. Great resources and the best professor I've had.

I had her for the online section, but her lecture videos were fantastic, and the practice problems were always indicative of what to expect on the exams. She always made herself accessible for office hours too which I found very helpful when I got stuck. Could not ask for a better Thermo professor.
to pass this class you literally have to go to class and the information will osmosis into your head. i swear. as dr. vrikkis is the kindest prof you'll ever have, avoid taking with anyone else

Vrikkis is such a good professor. I did have to drop the course because it was too heavy to take over the summer along with other two other classes, but she thoroughly explains everything and wants you to know the material. No homework, but lots of quizzes. And the tests are worth 90% of your final grade. Tough class, but she knows her stuff.

Why can't every professor be like Vrikkis. She is incredibly organized, excellent lecture videos, and actually wants to see you learn. I had her for thermo 1 and 2 and also heat transfer. She also holds office hours several times a week and are extremely beneficial to go to. Her grading expectations are clear and fair. You would have to try to fail

Great Professor! She brings a lot energy to lectures and lays out the material in a digestible and interesting way. Would absolutely take classes with her again! Grading policy was clearly laid out and the material you need to know for exams was very clear beforehand.

Dr. Wei is a very great person. Many students think he is a tough professor because this is their first class where they actually have to think like an engineer. Although the lecture is hard to follow, his office hours are helpful, that is if you go and show effort. Just be prepaired to put in alot of outside class studying, per usual.

Dr. Wei is interesting. He is not the best professor and always stresses reading the book. I would agree with reading the book, that is the main way you will succeed and understand what is going on. Make sure you understand the homework and the in-class sample problems because he will most likely pull the exam questions from there.

DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR!!! Not only is he terrible at explaining things, but he also explains how to do something the most complicated way possible. He then gives us the most difficult homework problems imaginable. The other 2 statics classes only had around 5 homework throughout the semester while we 24!!! Worst professor I've ever had.
Very hard grader. Doesn't fully explain problems when solving then in class and expects you to know everything as soon as it's explained

Offers office hours, graded only on attendance quizzes (7%) and 3 tests (93% no final exam). Have to show up to lecture, or at least read the book in depth. Homework isn't credited, but you SHOULD do it. Dr. Qiuming Wei doesn't pull any punches, but he rewards hard work. My EOS grade was curved from a solid B to an A because of the effort I showed.

Nice person. Just don't expect much from him. Better off self studying at times. But remember to go to class, cause somedays he will give a quiz, which will count as you attendance and other times he won't so just go to class.

In Hong Yang is a funny guy, but I am going to be honest, this class is a dice roll, I enjoyed my time in it, but I walked in and walked out feeling like I learned absolutely nothing. Every test was curved to around a C average, so honestly I'd say its hard to mess up the class. If you're smart and can learn at home, take his class.

His lectures are somewhat disorganized and usually just off slides he speeds through. One homework before each test, open-note quizzes some classes, tests are usually curved to have a C average. If you learn the material on your own and do the practice problems he gives you, you should pass easily. Funny guy just not the greatest teacher.

Honestly, he is a nice guy. Sorta quirky and funny, but he does not teach well. He just skims through the textbook, and then when it's time to go over examples, he assumes we all know how to do it and won't finish the problem. His accent is honestly not an issue. His teaching style is the problem. It is not effective.

The worst professor I have had and the only RMP review I have left (as a Junior Mech E). I took statics twice, and it was night and day. Lectures and the course as a whole was unclear and not straightforward. Does not cover all prerequisite concepts for solids. Showed emails in class from department about how his passing rate was too low.

He gives credit if you go up to the board. His normal exams were not bad, at least not bad enough to fail the class, and 3 of the exams had at least one question that was on the slides. You have to remember that you are responsible for studying at home.
talk to him during the class and he'll be on your side

He is one of the worst professors in the engineering department, never has any idea what he is talking about, and will never explain anything. His favorite answer to any question is "you know this already." Tests are pass or fail for the coding portion, with no in-between. NEVER take this man! If you can, get around him!!!!!
If haram was a person, it would be him

Even though his average on his exams was a 50, his regular exams were not bad. If you do the homework and questions from the powerpoint, you will do okay. The issue is his final that is worth 30% of your grade. The final was really hard compared to all of his exams.

Unclear grading criteria, Hid canvas grades and you cant even calculate it on your own because his weights didn't even = 100% on the syllabus. No lecture material on canvas either. Doesn't help a lot of the screens in the classroom just didn't work so you couldn't even see his lectures half of the time. You're gonna have to learn matlab on your own

His lectures are super clear and he knows his stuff. Go to class, it will save you a lot of confusion at home. Ask questions when you have them. Do homework. Understand the material. While tests resemble the homework somewhat, the tests are more fundamental. You can use notes on tests, just no online resources. Take him if you can.

Dr. Tabarraei is very smart and knowledgeable. He knows Abaqus well and gave us 1-2 Abaqus problems in each computer session. He allowed us to work on them ourselves before solving them. The class was interactive. I will take with him again.

WORST FACULTY EVER!!! His lecture is totally based on theories from books, and does not solve example problems! he is extremely careless about his course! not professional at all! He does not give any homework solutions so you do not even know how to solve those problems and in exams, he asks the most difficult questions!

This was the second course I took with him. I learned a lot from him, he combined theory and computer sessions which was very helpful. During computer sessions he gives 1-2 problems to solve using Abaqus and he makes us try to solve them ourselves first. The theory part needs lots of matrix algebra, but for that he let us use MATLAB.

Extremely easy class. Keanini focuses more on theory side of thermodynamics which means you'll solve a lot of things in terms of symbols algebraically. His tests are take home and graded on effort so you can get everything incorrect and still get a 100. Would recommend if you want less stress while you're taking JD or SD.

I love this professor. His material is focused more on symbolic thermodynamics, instead of doing problems with numbers in them which is so much easier to understand. quizzes announced ahead of time, tests are take home, homework can be submitted late. he's always available to answers any questions

Dr. Keanini has a very unique teaching method. It's very improvisational and responsive to the needs of the students. All of his lecture notes are available online, including ones from previous years, which is incredibly helpful. He is very supportive and helpful while encouraging independent critical thinking and study. Excellent professor.

You probably wont learn anything from his class but if you want a stress free class since you're likely taking Junior design or senior design with this class, I would 100% recommend. Multiple choice exams with confidence intervals which make it nearly impossible to fail this guys class. Might have to study for an A but its a free B elsewise.

If you want an easy pass, take him. I never felt like I had to worry in his class, even on test days. Homework's were worth a lot which was nice and the test averages were low, but he curves a ton! He also provides Extra Credit to help students. He wants everyone to pass, he's not out to get you. If you want a low stress class, this is your guy.

Dr. Scott Kelly was an awesome professor. I had him for both Dynamics 1 and Dynamics 2. His homework was easy.  Tests were multiple choice, and he allows division of points across multiple answer choices and curves very fairly. He gives a generous amount of extra credit opportunities. He tells really good jokes and plays hangman during class.

(took his megr 2240). TAKEEEE HIMMM HE'S AWESOME. HE WANTS EVERYONE TO PASS! His curve is ample, as well (in comp meth). He's about the effort as well as the understanding. He does exams online, so you may be tempted to always skip and cheat. Don't do that.His lectures are a treasure trove, he's lowkey a genius who will explain PhD level stuff to u

I had an A till the end of the semester where I bombed the last test(I skipped every homework and class, my bad fr). Class is very doable and he wants you to do well. Homeworks got pretty difficult as the semester went on but he's open to helping you out. Tests were a large part of your grade(85%) so doing the homework is very necessary.

Materials is a hard class regardless who you take it with. Zhang goes over the material thoroughly and quickly so it can be hard to keep up at times. Exams are difficult but he gives a practice exam and material to help prepare for each one. Final is optional as well. also grades on a scale where an 85 is an A and 10 points down onward.

His grading scheme is really stupid. Expect to spend 10 hours on 10 HW which aren't worth 9% of your grade. Quizzes 6% straightforward. The exams are very hard and are graded very harshly 25% each so one bad grade will easily tank your grade totaling 90% pre-final. Final is optional but only 10% which in most cases is not beneficial. Fire this man.

I made my schedule around his fluids class only for it to be taught with half assed powerpoints and videos. Got sassy when you asked a question and we only had class 1 day a week. The other two were online videos and a TA that had not a clue in the world. I still to this day don't understand how him having a second kid is a me problem

I had heard nothing but good things about Professor Bachman, so I rearranged my entire schedule to take him for fluids. The first half of the semester went great until he had his second kid. He then taught 1 day a week and had his TA "teaching" which consisted of him reading word for word off the PowerPoint and being unable to answer questions

You can tell Dr. Bachman still understands what it is like to be a student and teaches accordingly. Good in class slides and example problems. Tests are only 3 questions each but easier than the homework problems. Each unit builds upon the last so make sure you understand everything so you dont get left behind later. Lots of extra help sessions.

Honestly, a really good professor. The course has an exam every two-three weeks that are only a few problems each, but he gives partial credit. Homework is graded on completion and effort instead of correctness which is a lifesaver. Just make sure to follow along the example problems in class and you should be good.

10/10 on all fronts. Teaches like he remembers what it's like to be a student. Many tests but on less material. Topics are explained very well, not too heavy on derivations. Recommend attending on Fridays.

This is hands down my favorite professor at this school. 2240 is a tough class if you've never coded before, but he goes out of his way to help you understand. There is so much practice material on Canvas and he genuinely wants you to succeed. Tough class but not because of him. Had practice exams, practice quizzes, etc. The goat

Comp methods is a pretty easy class, but I learned everything on my own using the StudySession youtube channel. I found it really difficult to understand what he says during lecture, so i always did hw for other classes in the mean time. There will be a quiz EVERY day, but he'll tell you what to study and drops 3 lowest grades.

This review is for MEGR 7175, Engineering Analysis II. I will say that I worked very hard to do well in this class. You need to be willing to put in the time and effort. If not, your grade will reflect that because the exams, quizzes and homework is difficult. He provides all the tools you need to perform well.
All his exams question comes from homework and lecture notes and he teaches in a very effective way.

I took 2 grad level math courses with him. Till date, he is the best math teacher that I have ever experienced. He cares a lot about students. He always finds way to teach difficult math in a step by step and easy way. Best point is, he never forgets to appreciate all types of queries, no matter how silly they are. Highly recommended!!!

This is a BRAND NEW course for the BRAND NEW concentration. There is a syllabus, but we didn't follow it. The only assignment graded is lab notebook. He was kinda busy all the time. We supposed to have lecture once a week, included labs, and he only showed up 5 or 6 times in total. At the end of the day, this is just get you into the concentration.

This class was difficult. The exams were 10 multiple choice questions. You could only select 2 that you received partial credit on and there was an extra problem that was bonus. This format made it very hard to do good on exams since the question was either right or wrong. Luckily the final consisted of problems we did before

Great lecturer that provides useful lecture notes and examples. Homework can be very time consuming, and I heavily disliked the 10 multiple choice exam format, very little partial credit is given. I struggled on each exam despite studying many problems, but his optional final was very easy and replaces the lowest exam, it got my 79 to an A.

Dr. Wolek was a fantastic instructor for Dynamics 1. While the class was very difficult, he did the very best he could to make sure that we understood the material, and did the best we could on the tests. He might have spent a little too much time showing the derivation instead of the application of the equations, but still very helpful regardless

Great professor. Quality lectures and very knowledgeable. Complete the homework and you will do fine on the tests. 

Would recommend to anyone.

This class is hard without a doubt. Professor wolek teaches well and precise. If you want to pass this class you have to go to lecture and pay attention. The only bad thing is his office hours. They were only an hour long on fridays, but he responds very quickly to email and piazza.

Great Professor. You will regret asking stupid questions. If you don't do the homework you will struggle a lot. If you can do the homework you can do well on the test. He doesn't give much time on test though. He also tends to have mistakes in the homework. Lectures are entertaining but its not critical to attend if you just want to pass.

Dr. Schmid is extremely knowledgeable in machine elements. The other ratings are correct in saying he is a tough professor and the material is tough. However, if you put a considerable amount of effort into his class, you will be fine. Study the homework and handouts he provides and it won't be too bad. He is sarcastically funny and has good jokes.

Dr Schmid is one tough Professor but the man is hilarious, knows his stuff, and does his best to make everyone succeed. I personally wish I could take another class with him as it is quite an experience. Dr. Schmid is a lot harder than Dr. Tipton but if you are interested in truly learning about machine elements, his class is great for it.

Dr. Chen is a decent lecturer and a great teacher. He does have an accent, though it never prevented me from grasping what he was saying. The class is fairly difficult and the lectures alone may not always help you to fully grasp a concept. When things are not so far so good go to his office hours, during them I felt that Chen really shined.

He is a very good person and a thorough teacher. Some lecture material would get lost in his accent or the sheer length of lectures, but he usually reviewed it. Chen is very passionate about the material and explains it in a good way, you just have to be good at taking tests since those are basically the whole grade.

Dr. Chen was enthusiastic about the content, but sometimes his heavy accent got in the way of understanding lectures. He knows his content but he struggles with leading a class through it; the occasional example problem was always voluntary and only a few people ended up working them in front of the class throughout the semester.

3122 is a hard course as it is, but Professor Ghasemi made it easy by assigning homework and exams through an online software which he also provided support about during his lectures. He was also the type of professor to provide supportive feedback and comments directly to students individually. Not sure why he is rated so low to be quite honest.

Homework, tests, and final are online and open note. Everything is pretty similar to homework and class examples. Very easy to just never go to class and just teach yourself the material by doing the homework.

His very first sentence wasn't welcome to heat transfer but rather "Despite what you may have heard, I'm not Voldemort."  95% exam weighting so don't mess up lol. Take in the summer with Dr Vrikkas if you can but if you have to take it with Lee, I would recommend watching John Biddle's heat transfer playlist on yt. Only reason I passed.

Heat Transfer is hard in itself, but he doesn't make it any easier. Lectures involved multiple sections a class with MAYBE 1 example per section. His lessons consisted of the theory behind the equations and how they were derived (when its given in the textbook), not on uses or applications. Tests were open note, but most of the class still failed.

Grade is not on a normal scale, its based off the median course grade, so you never know what you truly have. Barely does examples and gives homework to "challenge you" but then grades it on accuracy, with hw averages being in the 50s. Tests are open note and are similar to his practice tests just with some tweaks, averages are still in the 40-60s.

I feel as if Professor Joyee has gotten better over the years based on these past reviews. Her teaching style is not too great, and she does tend to whisper while explaining practice problems which could insinuate a lack of true understanding of the subject. Overall the exams were pretty easy, and the TA was very helpful as well.
The lectures really aren't at all helpful but attendance is mandatory so expect to teach yourself

Mr. Dalton is a good teacher who will help whenever you have questions. He would tend to talk the entire length of the lab section (especially the first few weeks) so I had to use a lot of makeup time to get parts done. Be kind, show up on time, get your parts checked before they're due, and you'll be fine. Showing you care matters a lot.

This is a tough class because almost all the machines are new material. you'll definitely mess up a lot, but dalton answers every single question and it's important to him that you understand the material. he's always able to check parts ahead of time and guide you.

The GOAT of the machine shop! He's very nice, and does not want to fail you. You can probably email him and work something out with him if the machine shop hours in Sophomore Design aren't cutting the time needed to make your parts. He's very nice, and very flexible, and a good lecturer.

A great professor, you either get a 100 or a 0 on every assignment depending on if your parts are within tolerance or not. He will precheck parts before they are due and clearly shows every step in the machining process before you get to machine yours.

Professor Dalton is very helpful overall. He only gives either a 100 or a 0 if you make any minor mistakes on your parts. He gives you the opportunity to submit one week late for a 50. Its's best to have him pre check your parts in advance. My only gripe is that he once told me in a rather mean tone "I'm not checking anything else, it's past 5pm."

This is the best professor I have ever had at UNC Charlotte.  This professor really cares about his student's academics and will provide many examples, detailed answers to questions, and many opportunities to earn extra credit.  My only complaint is that he seems to be very busy and is hard to find outside of class.

This is one of the best professors I've ever had at UNC Charlotte.  He really cares about student success.  He takes the time to really break things down and explain it in a way that students can understand it.  He's very knowledgeable  in a range of engineering topics.  I love how his class has projects.
brother gave me attitude when I asked for an extension on homework after my cat died.

Overall, I think Dr. Tipton was a great professor. Lectures mostly consisted of 30 minutes of reading from slides and 20 minutes of math problems. Lots of extra credit opportunities. The final grade is very exam test heavy. The only thing I would change is the format of tests, as there is a lot of material to learn, having notes would be helpful.
Tipton is a nice professor but he has changed his exams. They are no longer open notes. He will give you the topics that will be on the exam, so his exams are a little bit more difficult.

Tipton is a cool guy. The slides are boring but to be fair, they are Dr. Hodgins slides I believe, not Tipton's. For the tests, it is open note, all you need to do is "CTRL F" on the slides and you should be alright. The homework + group project was the most interesting part of the class. Shouldn't have much trouble passing the class if you try.

Good person, not a great professor. Lectures involved him clicking through hundreds of slides and briefly touching on topics. Expect the tests to contain the most niche material touched on in class and HW to be irrelevant to the questions on the exams. Tests are online in the classroom. A group project as well.

She was an awesome teacher and does a great job with her lectures. Her exam problems are similar to lecture and homework problems.  Attend every lecture, pay attention and follow along, do all homework problems, and practice redoing lecture, homework, and quiz problems within a few nights before the exams and you can easily get an A.

Dr. Marjan Molavi-Zarandi was a great professor. I was considering dropping the class but I am glad I didn't. The TAs grade the Hw but she grades the Exams but she gives a lot of credit. She requires the textbook but I was able to find a pdf file of it. Attendance isn't mandatory but if you miss a she posts the lecture slides. She gives quizzes.
She is fantastic, and one of the best professors I've had at the university. She cares about and emphasizes learning over memorization, and has a true passion for teaching.

Don't buy the textbook, she gives you everything you need. Very caring and genuine professor, is always willing to tweak lectures/material/dates if you kindly ask. Her powerpoints are not as effective as her lectures so I would suggest no skipping. 2 tests at 20% each but we were allowed formula sheets and problems were similar to hw/quizzes.

A great professor who puts a lot of thought into his slides and lectures. He relates the components and methods you learn in the class to his work in the car simulation industry. Take good notes and go over his slides before tests and quizzes. Be aware that the multiple-choice questions will be more complex than the math!

Professor Stover is great. The homeworks are easy as long as you follow his slides and examples, which he goes over in class and posts on Canvas. His lectures are a little hard to stay focused, but it's because of how much material he has to cover in a 50-minute class.

Not a bad professor overall but I took AP Computer Science in high school and hated it. As I came into this class, I found it quite difficult because I was already sick of coding (loops are the worst part). His programming exams are quite awful as you basically need to memorize code. He he does allow equation sheets on his numerical methods exams.

Computational Methods is a tough class if you're going in without any programming/scripting knowledge, and that's most students. Dr. Stover understands this and will go out of his way to help you IF YOU ASK HIM FOR HELP. He also allows a hand-written note sheet for exams, which is a life saver. Great professor.

He encourages everyone to get A's. His grading scheme was tough consisting of one mid term worth 40% of your grade, a final exam worth 50% and homework worth 10%, but he was very generous with grading the exams and gives lots of credit. He was helpful if you came to his office hours and he asked for feedback on how he can improve the class.

Not a bad professor, but his class was one test worth 40%, HW 10%, and final 50%. He was very leneint in grading the test and homeworks weren't difficult. Does a decent amount of examples and bases his lectures off the textbook.

My second professor for Thermodynamics and he was wayy better at explaining the problems.
