He always tried to help us out if we didn't understand, and we could do 2 or 3 short documents on different topics of thermodynamics for up to 10% extra-credit. I would definitely take another class with Dr. Zhu.
Ahh Ron Sass just stop teaching My brain is about crash out. Sam Shue please save me Hes torturing me day and night

He's not the best professor ever, but also not the worst. He is very knowledgeable and good at teaching, however even though attendance is not required, if you miss even one day, your COOKED, he post nothing on canvas, and even in a blue moon when he does post the slides, many of the key definitions are marked out purposely, and many pop quizzes.

Three midterms and one final make up 85% of the grade, with only three short homework assignments all semester. Notes and quizzes were often posted late (if posted), lectures were hard to follow, and there were no study guides or practice problems. Grades weren't visible until the end, which made it hard to track progress.
Ron Sass + Logic, Embedded = Pure Disaster prepared from scratch His class time is a joke
Ahhhhhhhhhhhhh this class is raping me day and night nonstop please help me Ron Sass + ECGR 2181 = Disaster He should be thrown out of the university
Shue u have a special place in heaven U are an angel and a mighty savior for the ece department. Ron Sass would have to take special classes to learn from you on how to teach a class. #SamTheGoat
There is a reason everyone loves him. I have taken a class with him, am currently, and will be taking more.

One of the best professors in the department! Makes the hardest concepts easy to understand. There were quizzes once a week on topics we went over the previous week. In addition if you have the grade you want in the class, you get to skip the final since he drops the lowest exam grade.

SAMUEL SHUE. This name is inevitable in the ECE department because of his unmatched teaching skills and his friendly vibes thats keeps the lectures lively and engaging. Logic systems design starts of easy giving students an impression that it is a baby class later it gets harder Shue's Formula for success Homeworks-->Quizzes-->Midterms-->Final Exam

Dr Shue is a great teacher, and his class is honestly pretty easy. There is one quiz a week, but they are simple and cover material learned the week prior. (for us) Optional final if grade is an A. uses great slides and always links to additional resources if needed.
Helpful lectures that explained a lot of difficult material, but the tests were quite hard, and there often wasn't enough time to complete some of the coding sections.

Dr. Yawo is great at reading the room and makes sure he understand where his students are at in understanding the material, he often asks during lecture for feedback on his course structure and acts on the input given. He appreciates a good question and uses them to improve. He is a sweet person and can laugh at a good joke. Go to office hours!

He does greatly care about his students and is easily accessible outside of class. If you go to him before a test he will lay out clearly how he plans to format it for you. That being said his tests are rough, he will throw you curveballs and you will not have a fun time.

He genuinely cares about his students success and is always (weekends excluded) available for a meeting or an email if your confused. He'll layout important equations and give multiple examples for most of the topics you're learning. I've taken three of his classes so far and I'll definitely be taking more in the future.

Very well respected. He teaches all the hard classes like 2112, 2254, 3111. There‘s no other professor I would rather have for those courses. He cares deeply about his students and wants us all to succeed
Introduced a new format and curriculum. The content was relevant and challenging enough for modern Power Electronics.
The  professor supports student's research skills
Great professor!

The class is terribly structured. Files are broken. Expectations are unclear. You're also just expected to have all of the time in the world outside of this one class. What's worse is they know these are the issues but refuse to fix them.
Good professor just make sure you actually go to all the classes not just the first, second and last class. The tests are take home tests that are open matlab.

Dr. Cox is a very knowledgeable teacher. He is patient and caring and will walk you through problems as many times as you need. Lectures can get kind of boring if I am being honest, but that is more due to the nature of the material rather than his skills as a lecturer. ECGR 2254 is boring but he does his best to teach it.

Do not take her as a PhD supervisor. She lacks ability and knowledge to supervise an undergraduate thesis project let alone a PhD student. She will misuse for your visa status as an international student. She will tell you false things at the start of the PhD which she is just telling you to lure you in. Then will back out during the 2nd/3rd year.

Do not do masters thesis with her. She barely deserves to be a faculty in department. Bad hire. Takes the thesis students like a joke. Just tries to work with one faculty or another so that the publications are increased without doing her part in the project. Constantly jumps from one person to another. Has not worked with one person consistently.

Absolutely do not recommend for Masters thesis research or any other research courses with her. She lacks the ability to supervise any sort of research as she just lacks knowledge. She just works with other faculty and tries to colloaborate with them so that her research is published. Lacks sufficient knowledge to do research on her own.

Dr Cecchi is not a capable professor for teaching advanced electrical engineering courses. She barely knows her stuff and her grading has been so much titled in favor of students who are good to her. Doing Masters thesis with her is useless as you do not get any sort of help from her. Lackluster instructor with below par knowledge.

Dr Cecchi barely knows what she is teaching. Her Masters thesis students are suffering from her lack of knowledge about the subjects she is supposed to know. She will shout at you if you question her about something specific about the course. Test her about something. She will refer you to the TA because she herself has no clue. Maybe a D instruct.

The kind of guy that should be writing code in a basement, not interacting with people. Every conversation with him is grating. Like every question you ask is a bother. Exactly what you think of when you think of overly semantic reddit moderators and stack exchange fanatics. He is definitely on the spectrum.
Shes a very nice person that cares about her students, the class can be boring but that can be said about any circuits 1 class.
Good lectures to explain the course material; covered most of the things that showed up on tests and quizzes. Very helpful online textbook that provided useful examples.

She cares and gives people the opportunity to do better on midterms. The grading isn't as harsh as other professors. Recitation was on a Friday afternoon, so there wasn't a point in going because nobody else went. 
 If people were upset about their grades, they should look at that as a reason instead of blaming the professor.

Saqib is not really a bad teacher, but her lectures are boring and loses track of what she does. She compensates with lenient grading and being SUPER flexible with assignments and even tests. Do not correct her, though, she'll get butthurt and say that is “another way to solve” the problems. The TA was cool, he interacted with the students.

Very nice professor, but terrible lectures. Class was 5:30 pm which might be the reason for her poor lecture quality. Her quizzes and tests have bad formatting, with questions being cut in half and just poor wording. If there is another professor take them instead.
Bro gave us 2 quizzes in the same class on the last day of class.
I have a really bad sleep disorder, but thankfully whenever I struggle to fall asleep I can just put on one of his lecture recordings and sleep like a rock.

Overall this course is just calculus word problems with 1000 different random formulas. It's quite boring, conceptually interesting but Chowdhury just makes it bland. Tests are very broad in scope of material, and your expected to remember how to solve MANY different types of problems. 

Not extremely difficult, but overly time consuming and bland

His lectures weren't super interesting but he was good at answering questions and the homeworks and exams were very fair. I liked how we could choose our groups and our topic for the final project. Had him for 4171

I hate power systems because of this course and this professor. His lectures are so boring, and the homework is grueling busy work that is impossible to complete with the provided textbook, as the textbook is so poorly written it does not supply the information needed for the problems. This course would be very easy if taught properly.

He is very good. He goes over practice problems and teaches you everything you NEED to know and not random stuff. He answers emails quickly and really knows what he is doing. Attendance was not mandatory (for Network Theory I).

He is the most down to earth professor I have ever seen. He really wants his students to understand the material for a dry and boring course like Network Theory 1 but his tests would require to think a lot to do well but he graded very generously. Each time I took a test I would think I did bad and ended with As on all of them. TAKE HIM!

Amazing lectures, variety of examples to make sure students are ready for tests. If you are a lazy person don't take with him. But if you would like to really learn circuit analysis with a high level of understanding, I recommend him.

Horrible tests and boring lectures. This professor says everything is easy in this class but his tests are noting compared to the homework sets. His lectures make you sleep since its early in the morning and a lot of students are failing his class because of him and he doesnt curve them. Worst professor ever. AVOID HIM

The professor was really good at teaching the class, especially for his first time teaching. He gave many examples in class, and made sure kids were prepared for any scenario they may encounter. It was a bit slow in the beginning, but later the pace picked up. He was open to answer any question, and was a fair grader. Overall a great professor.

I've seen other reviews about this prof from a long time ago, I don't know if he's changed but in my experience he's far from the worst. My critique is that what's on the test is vague and we don't get formula sheets, but he always is willing to work with you on your grade if you bomb a test. He let me retake a test I failed to let me keep my B.

I warn against being taking him as your PhD advisor. He is totally the opposite of what he shows on the outside. He is a vindictive person and regularly shouts at his students off the record. He will show in front of everybody that he is friendly but he is totally the opposite in private to his PhD students. He is a good emotional manipulator.

Do not take Dr. K as your PhD advisor. He makes students beg for funding. Once I asked him to release the funding letter. Then when I asked him next time, he says I forgot. I asked him again. He did the same. I had to ask him 3 times before he released the summer funding. Then he asked me are you in need of the money.

Dr. K is not a good PhD advisor. He does not have any verbal or mental boundaries and does not respect other people's boundaries. He submits student's papers 2 minutes before the deadline to intentionally insult the student even though he knows the deadline 5 months before. He is not what he shows in the lectures. THat is just for public face.

Dr. K is not a good PhD advisor. He shows on the outside that he is friendly but he is a rude person who can cross personal boundaries. He has no boundaries in his mind and can say or do anything as he wills. He has shouted at me multiple times. He has temperament problem. Avoid him as a PhD advisor.
His field of research has no future. Don't waste your time and energy and money with with him.
Great professor. Made me want to pursue a degree in electrical engineering. Very thoughtful
Dr. Zhao is a great professor who knows his stuff and does a great job of relaying information in a way that students can understand. I would highly recommend taking him for any courses you can.

Test heavy, but he offers a lot of extra credit. He is very clear with test content, and it was all open note + using matlab, which works out great. Overall a good professor, I only needed a 3% on the final to pass, and i just showed up, did the hw, and got B's on all the tests.

I really enjoyed taking Foundations of Reinforcement Learning & Optimal Control with him. He has a gift for explaining tough concepts in an easy-to-understand way. It wasn't just about getting through the material to him; it was about making sure we really understood it. I learned a lot and felt supported throughout the course. Highly recommended!

Arafa's lectures were well thought out and planned. At the beginning of every class period 5-10 minutes were spent on review which made retaining information much easier. The class information is interesting but can be difficult to wrap your head around.

Not a bad professor overall, his class consists of homework (25%), weekly quizzes (25%), and 3 exams (50%) but they are straightforward, and attendance wasn't mandatory. He drops your lowest exam grade and your lowest 3 quiz grades. He also has a final extra credit project that can boost your final grade by up to 25%.

Dr. Li excelled at teaching this challenging subject. She showed a clear commitment to student success by dropping the lowest quiz grade and providing support, particularly with the difficult final exam. I would highly recommend and gladly take another course with her. look forward to seeing her in ecgr-4111

He's very passionate about what he teaches, very friendly and will help you with anything you need. Anytime I had questions he took the time to fully explain the answers to each of them until I understood. He's probably one of the best professors I've had. Do the readings, participate in class and at the very least meet him halfway.

I really enjoyed this class! You have to read a lot on your own, which took some time, but overall it was manageable. He goes through the readings in class so you can understand them better, so I usually took notes to review later for assignments and tests. Very straightforward and the lectures can feel long but its not too bad.
Greg is an awesome professor. He doesn't make the class difficult at all, and his lectures are very helpful. He is friendly and funny sometimes too. Easy class, Greg is awesome.

I don't think the class was difficult, as long as you actually do the readings and pay attention in class. Professor's personality is a bit much sometimes, but his passion for making his students actually learn is refreshing. Made me consider taking a philosophy minor because the content was so engaging.

I LOVED this professor and class! The lecture does get a bit boring but honestly if you just do the reading and pay attention, it is so easy to pass. Professor Convertito was extremely understanding with a few of my personal situations/ life outside of school. Communication is key for this course and just do the readings! Good class engagement
It was a fun class, too much to read for a P/F class though

Professor Ganapini just sums up the readings in class, though she explains them really well. Her only grades are quizzes, and she doesn't give much that actually makes you learn the material. Also, she doesn't give clear directions on how to do things or explain what she wants. She answers emails but is hard to get responses from in person.
yap yap yap yap yap

He was very sweet and passionate about what he was teaching! My only complaint would be the homework assignments; we had to read a lot and annotate the readings. Other than that, the class was pretty interesting and easy.

Only graded on 3-4 tests (I don't remember), so study! Tests are based on what she says in class and the notes. There is two extra credit assignments for 3 points each, but one is like 2 paragraphs and the other is like 6 pages. Also 2 extra credit questions were test. She really knows what she was talking about and it was an interesting class.

Avoid this class if you want an easy elective. The prof is passionate and knows her stuff, but tests are based on what she says in lectures and not what's on the slides, so attendance is a must. Grades come from 4 tests and 2 all-or-nothing extra credit assignments. Guest speakers and the documentaries were a highlight.

The teacher is nice and respectful, but I didn't like the class much, it was SK. There was 4 test and each has 2 points extra credit paragraph you can do. The presentation ar really disorganized and so hard to study.
Only has four grades. TESTS, I hate it but I like the professor. Shes respectful and knows what shes talking about. if you are a good test taker, you should take her!
Entire class based off of four tests. Only extra credit assignment seems to intentionally have a weird due date. Professor claims that class cannot be passed unless you attend every lecture.

Professor is engaging with some opportunists to go experience things haven't done before. I was nervous about assignment, but see why she made us do it. She does some cool easy class opportunities so u have to go to class. Prof Harmtna makes class easy to talk about hard topics. She does a good job of listening to all sides and knows her stuff.

The best professor in the CJUS Major. Incredibly helpful to all students. Class was organizes really easy to understand but still was challenging. I liked I could discuss things in more depth than other classes. I can recommend to go to the lectures there is participation grade and make note cards for exams. You will learn life long stuff.

I'm sure Hartman is great, but she should NOT be holding online classes. All semester, it seemed she had no idea how to operate Canvas without constant typos, miscommunications, and unclear instructions. It is VERY reading-heavy, and there are several written assignments; I've been told she doesn't require this of in-person sections, though

Prof Hartman is engaging and knowledgeable. While the class has a lot of work, she provides helpful feedback. I found Unstuck AI and the free textbook to be great study aids alongside the lectures. Overall, a challenging but rewarding course.

It took a bit for me to engage with Professor Hartman, but once I did I like her style. She is a good teacher who is VERY passionate about equal justice for all, especially marginalized communities. Goes over American histroy of how we got to today in the US. Also learned about masculinity and patriarchy. Online course w lots of content but was ok.
Great professor, always made the class very fun and interesting even at 9am! Attendance is not mandatory but he gives extra credit for attendance randomly so go to class.

Amazing professor. Lectures were very entertaining. Talked slowly enough to take notes on everything. Everything on the exams is from the lecture slideshows. He gives you a study guide that is exactly like the test. Gives random extra credit quizzes, and you get one extra credit point just for being there. 100% take this class.
Super funny and a great lecturer.
Not a bad teacher at all, just didn't have assignments, so if you do badly on your exams, you won't do well in the class.

Professor McKee is good. He keeps his lectures fun and engaging, and teaches well. However, the class only has 3 tests, the first being the worst. He doesn't post slides on canvas so attendance is needed but not mandatory. Pop quizzes are also very common, but only for extra credit

I took graduate seminar with Dr. Exum. Although we met with a different professor each week, Dr. Exum was incredibly supportive & always available to help students, especially if you were sick & couldn't make it. Super easy entry class to help students adjust into the grad program!

Very easy class. Honestly if you study his lectures and read the best book you will apparently ever read in the CJUS major, this class is an easy A. This is a very engaging professor which made learning enjoyable, even when it got to the boring stuff. There are only a few assignments, so make sure you study.

This professor is very engaging. There is only a few assignments and a required final but there are detailed study guides for each exam. you have to do a police ride along or an alt assignment. it was one of my favorite classes!

Easiest required class for CJUS major. Class consists of weekly quizzes that are open note and can be completed in 30-45 minutes. He also allows you to complete assignments 2 weeks in advance so you can finish the class early. You will have to create a professional resume for the class, so if you already have one, you're one step ahead. Awesome guy

This is a good professor! there are some flaws like he doesn't have a lot of grades in the grade book so when you take test or a quiz it will be a big percent but he gives videos and explains things very well and also gives study guides. He gives some cool opportunities you get to do! he is easy to talk to and funny sometimes. I recommend him!
all her class is: 2 exams and discussion posts every week. they are easy all you have to do is follow the rubric.

Divita was a great professor just make sure you are proactive about your group work with is the point of this class. Go to class and speak cause it apart of your grade. Weekly textbook quiz, it's easy and you can take it with classmates. Definitely take her if you can.
Really easy to pass. You just have to participate. It's fun to hear eveyrone's opinions on the material. She's also experienced in the field so it's cool to hear her perspective.
There was not a lot of homework in this course. Just a discussion post each week and 2 exams throughout the semester. Great class and she graded fairly.

Divita was honestly a very good professor, I enjoyed her class. She does grade on participation and attendance, and when i signed up i didn't realize it was almost like a public speaking class because of group projects/presentations. She wants to see her students succeed, so overall I would take her class again. I'm not very extroverted either.

The grading is not lenient or considerate whatsoever. Accepts no excuses at all, nor does he provide leverage for medical situations aside from bedridden illnesses. Offers no extra credit. Very difficult to succeed when constantly met with life's inconveniences. Lacks empathy.
Do not take please

While he is a professor who knows his information well, his grading is super tough. There is only six quizzes, five discussion boards and then a group podcast, that carries your whole grade. Grading was tough and you will have to go over and beyond on the discussion boards to receive a A/B in this class.

Really condescending and inflexible. The material is straightforward but the classroom environment is not the vibe, and the way the class is taught makes it difficult for students to retain info. Also makes u buy a book her wrote smh

Not a difficult class, the quizzes come straight from the textbook and (boring) lectures. The professor is readily available and answers any questions. Grading criteria is very clear and you are given detailed feedback. You have to buy the physical textbook, but buy it used - it is so much cheaper, just make sure you buy the right version.

I was nervous at first bc of the ratings but she was pretty good! Attendance doesn't count but if you don't go, you miss all the lecture notes which are important. She was very nice and explained things really well especially so that we could understand. No homework just an in-class assignment sometimes. I'd take her again, it was a nice class!

Perusall is awful and required, with no instructions. It requires an access code, which you've paid for if you opted into Niner coursepack. She has no idea how to get that code to you nor does she care to. I emailed the library who eventually sent me ~a code~ four days after I requested it, it didn't work. She has no idea what she's doing.

She is very hard to understand and she teaches like we are on the same level as her education. Very frustrating, many group project and you lose most of your grades on it because some student would not do their work and when you explain it to her she would not fix the problem and act like it never existed. Take it online is better.

The only things she graded was in class assignments (all of them were 25% in total) and 3 tests, which were 25% each. Most of the material that was on the exams, were not on the study guides she gave us to prepare for the exams. When we would ask her a question in class about a group assignment too, she just basically told us to "figure it out".
Disorganized, laughed at students for asking questions, unclear rubric, takes off points for minor mistakes. Would not recommended.

Professor Arrigo is so caring and knowledgeable. It is clear he just wants his students to learn and succeed. Had 3 tests that were directly from the textbook and 1 group project. He has a lot of knowledge to share and you will learn a ton if that's what you want. Easy class with a great professor.

He really wants to see his students succeed. He's very kind and will especially go easier on you if he sees you participating in class more. It seems like a lot of writing but it seems easier as the course progresses. There is a 12-15 page final paper but it's a lot easier because all of the previous writing assignments have built up to it
Bad professor he thinks his class matters more than it does.

Dr. Kuhns was an amazing professor. I don't really understand his low score on here. If you are a responsible student and can sit through a three hour lecture twice a week, which are interesting, for 7 weeks then you will do fine. Dr. Kuhns is very flexible and will work with you if you put in the work. It is very analytics heavy! Don't miss class!

Great professor, definitely a new favorite. This was a half-semester class compiled of 6 labs, 1 midterm, and 1 final exam. 2 extra credit opportunities as well. I'm doing the crime analysis concentration and this was easily a favorite class. He's laid back but fair, as well as extremely helpful and knowledgeable on the subject. Just do your best!
The class was easy but not my favorite professor.

He's very hard to communicate with and only gives a strict 5-hour window to take exams, even though they're auto-graded through LockDown Browser. I asked for a short extension due to a Wi-Fi outage, but he accused me of lying. Exams are 50% of the grade, yet he acts overwhelmed despite teaching mostly asynchronous, auto-graded classes.
I've worked closely with Dr. Cooper & she continues to be incredibly supportive of me & all of her students. She is a bit slow on grading but overall, incredibly kind & a great speaker.
1/10 professor. NO LATE WORK ACCEPTED UNDER NO CONDITIONS even if your great gam gam died or the world explodes. Took 10% off my grade for adding an extra source from my reference page.

I am shocked to see ANY negative ratings. Cooper is smart, passionate, funny, and relatable. Her lectures were never dull, always easy to understand, and sparked some excellent discussions within the class. If you are lazy, don't take this class. You WILL have to put in work, but it's so worth it. I learned SO much from this class.

A lot of assignments that felt very unnecessary, and a 7 page long paper as the final that was on topics that felt very useless for the course. She's also a tough grader and I felt like I hated this course throughout the semester even though this is my minor and I usually enjoy CJUS classes. Would not take again.
She is extremely unprofessional. She is very open about her being an ex-con and stealing.

Overall pretty good professor 3 exams with lockdown browser so make sure you study for those because they can be challenging and 4 assignments throughout the semester that she gives at least a 2 week notice on. As long as you do the assignments on time & accurately and study for the test you'll get an A. Make sure to do the extra credit.
Very easy class. Just look at the powerpoints and you will do fine. The tests are open note but she still requires lockdown browser and webcam. She is easy to reach through email.

Self taught course. Discussion post every week that very easy. I never even opened the textbook that she requires. Everything is on her power points. Very caring professor! Three exams open browser with webcam but open book open notes.

She is cool & easy going, but her assignments were never explained well. Attendance isn't mandatory, but you won't really know what's going on even if you attend every class. Also have never been in a class where we have had to ask for a rubric for assignments until now, this happened multiple times.

The group projects will be a lot of work, and there will be multiple presentations. However, Dr. Reid does what she can to help everyone. Even if her lectures are *very* disinteresting, it's really difficult to complain. You can expect a lot of writing, but Dr. Reid actually gives lots of class time to allow you to work on things like that.

Dr. Han is a great professor. I had him in undergrad & took him again this semester for data analytics as a grad student. He is definitely a tough grader but if you take advantage of him being in his office & make sure to pay attention to instructions, you'll do fine. He wants all his students to achieve & pass!

this was for an undergrad course in analytics .  I had never taken an excel course before so I was so nervous. If you study and put in the time for your assignments you will be fine! He is available during office hours too so for sure do that if you need to. He is an awesome professor, also he takes attendance so being present is important!

First half of the semester is a review of RM from undergrad. There is a midterm, lit review, eval methods, presentation, attendance. Sometimes rubrics/instructions were unclear. But Dr. Han is available outside of class and by email. Take advantage of that. He is always willing to work with you as long as you try.

Great experience, I took her for CJUS 4379. Theres weekly assignments, quizzes and discussion posts but theres plenty of time in the week to get it done, assignments are also really interesting and her online lectures + assigned readings are interesting as well. I recommend!

Have not finished class yet but current grade 100%

2 weekly assignments (short ppr and paragraph) based on in class material and readings 

10 pg ppr due 

Good personality- truly cares for students and lectures = test material 

Tests easy if study/read/pay attention in class

PUT IN EFFORT 

AMAZING ALTERNATIVE FOR MATH BASED STATS + WI CLASS

I didn't do my best all semester, but Dr. Harris provided plenty of opportunities to raise my grade! The assignments were straightforward to complete. I just had trouble with the exams because I didn't really read the textbook. Make sure to do that and watch the lecture videos. Other than that, it should be an easy class!
Dr. Harris is an incredible professor!

Dr. Petersen is one of the best professors I've had at UNCC! I took him for Intro to Courts and Research Methods and highly recommend him. His lectures are clear and engaging, rubrics are straightforward, and feedback is helpful. Workload is very manageable with Poll Everywhere attendance, LockDown quizzes, and two writing assignments.

Very low key class, mostly lecture but with at least one poll everywhere for attendance. Textbook is not needed, but to complete the bi-weekly written assignments you will need to use the articles he provides. Overall Mr. Petersen is an awesome professor, very laid back, helpful, and up to discussion during class, I highly recommend!

You will need to actually read the textbook and take notes/study to get an A. Exams can be confusing but she will go over them with you after if you ask. You have to show up to class because she does 6 pop quizzes. Overall pretty nice and engaging lectures
If you guys just attend every class you will definitely get an A+

Prof. Jakeway is AMAZING! She's very understanding. Canceled class multiple weeks: did work from home. Can leave class when done with work. 1 weekly group assignment done in class. no homework or readings. no test or final. Final is group presentation that you work on throughout the semester. flexible with attendance but fun to show up. Easy 100!!

Professor Jakeway is my favorite professor. I've taken many courses with her and she allows you to decide to take a class online or in person depending on your schedule needs. She requires attendance in person but most of your grade is from showing up. Online is a bit more difficult but the assignments are clear.

Professor Jakeway makes this class engaging and fun. The personal insights and cultural comparisons are fascinating. With the help of unstuck study, I aced the course. Highly recommend for anyone interested in German and American cultures.

The BEST professor/teacher I've ever had! Not just in college but my whole life. This class was optional to take in person or online so I chose online. The subject matter was interesting for me because the class was about German culture, laws, and history and I like learning about that. It's literally the easiest class I've ever done. Take her!!

This was for AMST:3050. The professor is very understanding of your personal schedule outside of school and is willing to help. Truly cares about her students and seems to care about the topics she teaches. We had to read about 6 books, they weren't too long, and the assignments were easy after reading the material.
Very neutral class since I had it online, but be prepared for LOTS of work and reading each week.

Super easy online class! 2 assignments a week that are either a long reading or documentary. The class is very interesting and graded by multiple choice canvas quizzes or discussions. Midterm is cumulative from class assignments, would definitely take her again!

Took this class as a 6 week summer course and it ended up being a great fit even with a busy schedule.  She posted all the homework for a few weeks at a time so that you could work ahead.  Hardest part was the quizzes due to the small details she would test you on.  Overall I loved this professor and would be happy to have her again.

Dr. Watts was great! Hybrid course so most of it was online and we met IRL once a week. Most of the time IRL was canceled. 2 weekly quizzes (vocab + general chapter) per week based on textbook, and up to 2 discussions a week. Final exam was optional and final project was an infographic. She's very sweet and has good energy. Pretty easy course.

I was extremely nervous about taking ITLN 1 and 2, but with her it was one of my absolute favorite classes I have ever taken here at UNCC in my 2.5 years here. She was always engaging, and she does not grade harshly. Just show up and show that you care. What matters most to her is showing that you care!!

Be ready to teach yourself a new language. I took her for Elementary Italian 1 and I had to teach myself. We go over things, but very fast, she will barely go back if you ask questions. She speaks very fast and will speak in italian because she expects you to be fluent already. If you ask her to slow down or explain it in english, she won't.

One of my favorite professors ever! She is easy to get along with, willing to talk and extend deadlines. There's a lot of homework, expected for a language course. She's very understanding and loves teaching, making her class memorable for me. 10/10, would recommend.
Very clear professor, gets straight to the point. Follows the textbook, has strict attendance policy. Very kind and keeps class engaged. Just go to class and do the homework and you will pass.

Loved Professor Rains! On test days when I couldn't focus, she let me work in her office. She is very accommodating! Her lectures are to the point, and she also gives several opportunities for extra credit in class and outside of class. If you participate in activities with the Italian club you could get some bonus points on exams!

You'll think you did an assignment right and then he'll take points off, no feedback or anything. It's hard to understand his criteria even though he lists it honestly. 

Do the assignments early and you'll pass with an easy A, just don't expect to gain much from this course in the long run.
Easy class. Do all the assignments and you will do fine. He stopped replying to the last couple of emails I sent though, so if you have pressing questions, I would go to his office hours.
Loved her class getting to go to Paris was amazing! We will miss her!

Mrs. Houston is a fantastic, sweet professor who cares greatly for her students. Class participation is very important, along with online assignments that do not show due dates on canvas (my only issue). She isn't very tech savvy canvas wise but I learned so much and was always happy to go to her class. Easy class, tests are very straight forward

Jane Houston is one of the kindest, most caring professors I have had at UNCC. All of the French professors are amazing, but she really cares about the success of her students and goes above and beyond to make sure you understand the material. Her teaching is straightforward. She is passionate and loves all of her students. Def take her class.

Very sweet professor. I faced some hard time this semester and she helped me a lot and extended some assignments and allow some time for me to return them back. She is so caring and nice. She always smiles and she is not tough grader. If something wrong with your paper she gives you time to work on it !

Professor Houston is so sweet her teaching just isnt up to date. the papers she shows can barely be read by students, even those on the front row. If you listen to what she says, its easy to pass as long as you participate and do well on the papers. the tests will be the most important part of your grade. they are tough and she is a tough grader

This professor is absolutely amazing. Every class she was able to make fun and interesting. I really feel like I learned a good foundation for French. She was also always available outside of class whenever we had a question. Tests also were very easy because she would give us points for trying and translating sentences.

I loved this class; the teacher is very sweet and caring. If you need to take a language course, I would take her class. She is a ray of sunshine, although the lectures are really fast-paced, the professor makes it worth it. One of my favorite professors ever.
She's really nice and easy to talk to. Her class is interesting and you get a lot of practice in class. She's very helpful and not a harsh grader.

If you need to take French, take it with this teacher, I promise you. 10/10 AMAZING!! Her class is engaging and genuinely fun, and her sense of humor makes learning even better. She cares about your success and makes sure you understand the material before you leave. Tests and quizzes are easy IF YOU STUDY. She's one of the nicest teachers at UNCC.
She is so amazing and so caring! I took 1202 and 2201 and they were so easy!

Profe Cisneros is wonderful! It's been a while since I've taken a Spanish class, and I can say that she really helped me build my confidence. She cares deeply about her students and tries to encourage you to ask questions and try your best, even if you make mistakes. The VHL assignments were a bit much, annoying, but they were good practice!

Profesora Cisneros truly cares about her students and their wellbeing. She is tough on you but it is for your own benefit in the end. She is a wonderful profesor, she adds her own personal experiences in her lectures which makes you feel more connected with her and her topics she teaches, for a better understanding.

I loved this class with Profe Cisneros! She cares deeply about inspiring students and applies real world concepts to the class. The assignments not only practiced Spanish, but also genuinely had me reflecting on my identity. There are a couple of small presentation assignments and in class writing compositions that are graded fairly. I recommend!

She will assume everyone is on the same page and you should know everything from the previous classes (I took the placement test and did not take any previous classes). She teaches very fast. It's hard to keep up. There is heavy homework load and a lot of testing with exams and compositions. She doesn't teach the material well to keep up with it.

Prof. Cisneros gives you the attention you give her. if you don't care neither does she BUT if you care about the class and your grade she will stop at nothing to support you in whatever way necessary. I'm so bummed shes not teaching 2202, I will take whatever i can with her in the future!
All online responses that were graded on the grammer more than the content. Use a grammer check if you take her class.

I love Professor. Dal Pra so much. She is one of the best professors I've ever had, and she is super flexible. She likes to change the courses depending on what interests her students. She is an easy grader for the most part, but when people start taking advantage of her, she starts grading slightly more harshly.

Daniela is an amazing and caring professor. She is very fair and knowledgeable. I have taken multiple classes with her and her classes never disappoint; they are hidden gems. UNCC is very lucky to have this professor. Just show up, participate, ask questions, do the work and you will be fine.

Proff. is very passionate and talks about the topics in cinema with proper information but she is VERY repetitive. She will pause the films to talk about exactly what (and will) happen/ed. Participation is HUGE and even grades harshly if you don't praise the topic enough. You take class notes for everyone and even with that, you'll be graded harsh.

He is an awesome professor at his core, his classes are not hard nor are his assignments. I would however advise anyone considering taking him for an elective in addition to major requirements to look elsewhere. The amount of assignments is absurd and you will not keep up if you have other demanding courses that also have a ton of assignments.

Bledsoe's class was very easy, featuring online quizzes, discussion posts, journal entries, and papers tied to approximately 11 documentaries. The grading was lenient, allowing for three missed classes without penalty, and students often received extra points for quality assignments. While Bledsoe attempts humor, it can be cringeworthy at times.

Great professor! I took his anime class, JAPN-3140. Not sure if the code changed, but it wasn't listed. There are a lot of online assignments, but they really don't take long to complete. There are a couple of long lecture videos, but they were interesting. Mainly watched anime/documentaries in class.

Prof. Bledsoe is an okay professor. I took JAPN1502 (previously 1201 I think) a gen ed class. Its easy as long as you keep up with the 6+ weekly online assignments including 4 discussion videos and multiple quizzes based on lecture videos. He doesnt teach in class we just watch documentaries, all the actual learning comes from the online lectures.

I had him for JAPN 1502. There is a lot of homework in this class, however, if you do it while the class is going and do not wait until the last second, it does not seem like a lot. He is a great professor and cares about his classes that he teaches. I would 100% take him again. I do not have a problem with him at all.
Mori Sensei's stricter schedule makes it easier to stay on tract to learn Japanese but it's nothing impossible. It is a class that can be done at any reasonable pace you want to do.

I absolutely loved her class and teaching style. I felt challenged and extremely motivated throughout the semester. It's pretty fast-paced but you get used to it because it's very structured, and it didn't feel like we rushed over any lessons. Overall, one of my favorite classes I've ever taken!

This class was so much fun, it felt more like a group of people practicing Japanese together than a traditional class.  There is a lot of work to do but it genuinely does help you learn the material, I didn't have to study at all outside of the assigned work.

I loved Mori Sensei's Japanese course! She is very kind and genuinely cares about her students and their success. Japanese is hard (that's a given) and the course is fast-paced but grading isn't too harsh. Tons of homework but I found Genki work to be fun. The TA Yuki-san was great too! :)

I heard that Mori-sensei is intimidating but I didn't get that feeling with her. I would take her again if I could. She's caring and brought snacks closer to finals. If you have any questions, don't hesitate to ask her for clarification. My favorite class of the semester.

Professor Stone is as average as they come. I thought he did an okay job but didn't do much to prepare students for quizzes or exams. He would give pages of the textbook instead of taking the time to make a simple study guide. He's not a bad professor but I think there are better professors to take that will make the class way easier.
Professor Stone is an excellent professor. You have to really pay attention if you want to learn, but it's a super nice grader. Extremely understanding and cares about his students.

Prof. Stone is an amazing person and good teacher. I would definitely recommend taking him. The only reason I didn't do well was bc of personal circumstances and couldn't catch myself up. He did try to help me and work with me, but I just think Spanish wasn't my thing anymore. Anyway, DEFINITELY TAKE HIM!!!

He gives alot of homework, kind unorganized sometimes. One oral presentation but not infront of the class. He helps when it comes to compositions but test review "study" is just pages in the textbook. Slow grader. Final was cumulative.
Really kind professor, but be prepared to do lots of homework

Really kind teacher and very easy to work with! I took this class online, but probably be better in person. There is a discussion board almost every week where you watch movies or read the textbook. One midterm, a mini project on an Arab country, and a final interview. Not difficult just follow the rubric. You must show your face for the project.
She is very nice and her class is relatively easy just a class where she talks and you listen.overall kind teacher.

If you are learning Arabic for the first time, Dr. Noha is the best professor. She perfectly explains everything and makes learning the language enjoyable. She annunciates letters very well and emphasizes where there might be any confusion. There is a lot of homework, but they are for your benefit. Plus, she drops 3 lowest HW and attendance grades.
she was terrible, NEVER take her class PLEASE.

You are expected to read textbook chapters before coming into class, gets disappointed if you do not. She is very cooperative- makes the deadlines favorable to the students. She is a tough grader with no clear rubric for discussion boards. Very heavy DB class.

Attendance is mandatory, but I missed a few classes, and it only hurt me a little. He answers emails quickly and is always willing to help. You literally just watch movies about Latin American history and do some quizzes and canvas/discussion posts. Very easy general education course.
This class almost made me start a therapy that I've been denying for years. Do not recommend.
class was overall pretty easy, mostly graded on movie and textbook quizzes. midterm and final were both online but had lockdown browsers and had essay prompts.

Amazing professor, only saw him once or twice tho. The TA Diana was beyond amazing as well. The class does have a weekly discussion board which you have to answer to two classmates, two quizzes, and watching a film + reading textbook. There's two exams, pretty easy but both include short and long essays. Overall great experience!

Dr. Dalton appeared in class for the first two weeks of the semester. I was excited for this class; I wanted to learn more. And I feel like I did, but I could've interacted more if there had been someone actively talking and explaining. The TA did a great job with what she had to work with. The workload can be heavy, so stay on track.
Really easy going semester, profesora makes learning really accessible and she is super kind and understanding!

She is a very good person, but by far the worst professor I've had at UNCC. I can't recall a time she was fully prepared; she's a super tough grader, and she's quick to throw out accusations towards students about using AI or translation. Again, really nice person, but that does not make a good professor. This class was the worst class I've taken

She graded rough drafts of papers as if they were final drafts, which made things difficult. Even some of the native speakers struggled with the grammar in this class. Overall, I just think the grading was too tough. It wasn't too much work, though, and I really enjoyed being in class with her. She made things super fun!

I had a hard semester in regard to mental health, and she was incredibly supportive. I opened up to her and she gave me ample time to make up assignments and gave me grace for late work. She is a very understanding and kind woman whose care for her course and love for teaching shines through.
Professor Lawson was amazing. She made learning and coming to class fun. Homework was completed online and class was used for practice. Tests were easy and fairly graded.
Very sweet and caring. Explains everything in depth and repetition is key for this course. She's very understanding, homework is a lot but it's not hard.

Homework is due every night before class. They will tank your grade if you don't do them. Accessible outside of class and is very Spanish beginner-friendly. Super sweet and only wants her students to succeed. Take her if you can.

Amazing professor! The sweetest person and passionate about teaching. Willing to explain concepts, accessible outside of class, and welcomes questions. Grades fairly and wants her students to succeed. Homework due every night before class. Stay on top of assignments and prepare for exams and you will do well. Highly recommend her!
Everything we did went along with the tests and homework.

Absolutely the sweetest Spanish professor at UNCC. She genuinely cares about everyone and tries her best to make sure you understand the material. Learning a new language is definitely challenging, but as long as you do the homework and attend the lecture then you will be ok. Lectures are a bit of repeating whatever the homework was however

Accuracy is essential, and translating into Spanish is not an easy task. As a native speaker, he has been very helpful in improving my skills in the discipline. Translation requires a level of precision that goes beyond simply speaking Spanish.
The worst professor I have experienced at UNC Charlotte. Very rude, tough grader and does not care about his students. Avoid this professor at all costs.

actually one of the worst professors I have ever had. first B I have received here after previously getting all As, and Ive taken multiple other spanish classes with other professors and I was fine. he does not teach, the only thing you will learn on is the class work book which quizzes take forever to read for and do. DO NOT take him.

He is the worst professor in languages . Like he is so rude when you ask questions. He thinks he knows everything and his Spanish sounds broken , his Spanish grammar is awful. He grades so late and gives unclear instructions. He makes no sense in his assignments. avoid him and take native speaking teachers:) they know what they are talking about.
He is the worst in the translation depa

Nagano sensei FTW!! She is so funny, sweet and a great instructor. Has great energy and will help you when you ask, no matter the question. TA Akari was very nice as well. Both graded work within a 2-3 week window. Only downside was a classmate, Mr. Tristan, whom we all tolerated but Nagano made sure to silence him if he got too rowdy.

To be fair, a lot of my experience in actually learning in this class came from the homework. Lectures are mostly just review but still very helpful for retaining what you learn during personal study. Professor is very sweet and helpful in correcting your mistakes.

Lectures heavily and grades tough. One thing I did not like was how she did not give us any links to her slides or any sort of study guide for her exams (there are two exams). Would not recommend anyone who thinks its a easy class.

Nagano Sensei was super sweet. She was really sympathetic towards my situation and really tried to help me get back on track and made sure to keep checking in on me while I had a NIGHTMARE of a sem. And btw, Japanese is one of the hardest languages to learn for native english speakers-- FSI category 5 (didn't know that until it was too late lmfao)

Super nice and chill. There is a lot of work but I mean it's learning a language.
Obviously there's a slight issue with accent and occasionally mispronouncing or forgetting a word in English but her accent is relatively easy to understand.

Took her again for JAPN1202, the format was a bit different but easy to adjust to! Japanese is of course a difficult language, but she gives a lot of good feedback and is always willing to help one on one. Would recommend!
Really fun. Sweet. Always willing to help out. Even though Japanese is really hard she made sure everyone participated and kept everyone on track. Honestly one of my favorite classes.
Homework every day and plenty of quizzes and tests, but they're not too bad if you study enough. Yokono sensei is very sweet and caring and always happy to help!

yokono sensei was probably the nicest most respectable professors i've had so far. shes always willing to help outside of class, and gives really good feedback on tests/assignments/etc. this course is pretty fast paced so expect lots of work assigned and keep up with lectures. she catches ppl lacking so make sure to focus up in class.

Yokono sensei is awesome! I took her study abroad course last semester and JAPN 1201 this semester, and she's incredibly caring and knowelegable. She wants to see you succeed, is willing to answer any questions, and responds to emails very quickly. She's a great professor to take if you're just starting out with the Japanese language.

I took her honors research methods class! Overall, she's an amazing professor. Super sweet and caring, always asked us if we needed anything before class. Made the honors research process very comprehensible. I would highly recommend the Languages, Cultures, and Translation departmental honors program.

Dr Stedman is a really amazing professor. Her classes are definitely difficult, covering a lot of content but she does a good job at making the material easy to digest and she is very understanding. She has helped my French improve more than any other professor in my 7 years of French learning just in the way that she encourages participation
Great guy, very engaging. Homework is a bit confusing, but otherwise effective professor. Sets up meetings for students to tell him if they're struggling with whatever, so he's a good communicator.

Nice guy, but he overestimates the value of his class. Be ready for him to have you create 5 different accounts on 5 different platforms. Never posts the homework in canvas, so get ready to have to hunt down your own homework. Will 100% force you to join a zoom call if the school cancels class.
Simple and enjoyable course, he's always in a great mood and very accommodating.

I was nervous about taking a foreign language class, but Dr. Hobgood made it easy. I had so much fun learning French. He is very enthusiastic and engaged. You can tell he truly loves what he does and wants to connect with his students. He takes time to make sure that everyone is comfortable with learning a new language and that they understand.

Dr. Hobgood's class has been the shimmering light helping me through the semester. 1202 is easy in nature, but of course, learning any new language is a challenge, but Hobgood makes it a breeze. The class is engaging, interactive, and very social! Make sure you are doing the HW on time and showing up to class!! Love Hobgood :)
BEST PROFESSOR EVER! The class was so easy and fun I enjoyed it so much.

She is very sweet and understanding. I will say you should decently pay attention if you're not already familiar with Russian because this class requires a lot of speaking, but even for someone like me who doesn't enjoy talking in class it's not that bad. Be sure to get the textbook because all the assignments are in it.

Mrs. Kate made me fall in love with learning the Russian language. She is the best Professor I have had thus far. She will remember your name and little details about yourself. Just a very caring professor and she made every lecture enjoyable. The class is very participation heavy which I found to be fun even as someone who is shy.

The most caring professor I have met while at Charlotte. She offers extra credit for just writing the Chinese character on the board. She will even ask her classes if she should push assignments back due to how many characters there are to learn. Overall, Chinese is a hard language to learn. However, 老師 makes it simple. You have to apply though!
Easy class. Just do the work and it's an easy A.

Took a themes class, Chinese Movies in the U.S.. She is so kind and goes above and beyond to accommodate you. This class is as easy as it gets and definitely a filler for most students, which she absolutely understands and doesn't try to change. 

Some people really do find their calling.

(•‿•)
Maybe the easiest course at the university. Three essays and weekly discussion posts, neither of which are graded remotely strictly. Professor Mueller is very sweet.
She made me wish I had minored or even majored in Chinese. Great professor, very caring and ready to help. Class had slightly more load work than anticipated but I would do it all over again!
She's an amazing teacher. She's very good at her job and makes Japanese very fun and engaging. And she's very easy to communicate with.

Professor Banerjee is really kind and lets you makeup work and re-take chapter exams. I took this class online during the summer so it was fast-paced. She does require you to have your camera on and you have to go to the lecture to understand what's going on. Attendance is required. She gives additional examples and explanations to the textbook.

Don't take Banerjee-sensei's class if you truly want to learn Japanese language and culture (JAPN 3130, business). She will repeat the exact same thing as the book, rather than giving you more hands on examples. At the end of the day, I am thinking what's the difference between self study and taking her class.

I took her class over the summer, so the course was much more condensed and faster paced. Professor Banerjee was a little intense but always meant well for students, but be prepared to be called on in class. She gives you the option to pass of course, but it was always a little anxiety inducing for me haha.
She is very caring for when people are sick as long as you email very easy class I took her 1502 french movies class and I would 100 percent take her again she is very smart and funny.
Thank you for a great semester Dr. Domle

Dr. D was absolutely fabulous. She has been my favorite professor so far and her teaching is very accessible and easy to understand. She is a delightful person and I always left her class in a good mood. The lectures were very digestible and she is very cooperative with students! She inspires me to want to continue learning French! Oui oui🇫🇷

Can not recommend this professor enough!! Not only is she hilarious and caring, her class is so inspiring! I never thought francophone culture would interest me as much as it has and I give full credit to Dr. D. She is thought provoking and teaches you how to analyze french culture on a graduate level. The grading is easy, participation matters!

French is her first language, so she's perfect to teach the class. Love her personality, she's funny and interacts wonderfully with students. She will postpone quizzes and exams if the class is unprepared. Only complaint is that I struggled to feel like I learned much, but I may just be bad at languages. Had tons of fun in her class anyways.

I  wish had her for the FREN 1101 and 1102 . She is super knowledgable and really passionate about France. It was difficult at times to keep  up but as  long as  you stay on track of  the  homework and come to class. Overall- she is a good professor and you  will learn a lot from her.

She is very passionate about French and linguistics. There is a lot of homework but it goes well with the in-class work so it definitely helps. She offers a bunch of extra credit assignments that actually help you learn the language better. It's a fast-paced course and she takes attendance so definitely go to class.

I had her as my advisor and professor. She's kind and cares about her students, although I think she could've been more demanding or more interactive. I would catch many students dozing off in the class but she wouldn't say much about it. Lessons were average at best.

I was not looking forward to taking foreign language class but Professor LaMarca is a great teacher. She is agreeable and she wants her students to excel. My expectations were proved ill-informed as the class was fun and engaging.

I really liked the way she taught. She didn't move too quickly, and made sure everyone understood the lesson before moving on. If we needed more practice she gave it to us, and helped us do it. It is a lot of online homework, but if you try it really improves your test grades. She was very funny and social, even though my class was only 7 people.

Great selection of films and presentations regarding the time period and the styles associated with it.  plenty of extra credit opportunities ranging from attending club meetings to in-class participation.  Only 2 exams.

Just watch films and finish a film guide. 2 tests (one midterm very easy). You get 3 absence's and than he starts taking off points. Dont sleep in his class because he will wake you up. Its easy and his powerpoints are not boring.

This professor is so awful. My very first day of class, he locked the door before the class even started and didn't let us in. We waited for whole 10 minutes outside his class and when we knocked he open the door and said "WHAT?!" and closed the door on our face.
She makes the class easy for you just show up and complete the work. Don't be afraid to ask questions. Class is overall pretty easy, the only tough part was the listening portion of the midterms.
I took her course online during the summer semester. Her lectures were very engaging with a lot of activities that helped me learn the material. Attendance is mandatory.

I had her for 1101 and 1102. I love prof Burke. She genuinely cares for her students. She is very accessible outside of class. She preps you well for quizzes and exams. There is a decent amount of homework, but it's regulated by the head of the span department, so all span students do the same homework. Nothing negative to say about her.

If you want a spanish professor that does not take attendance, she is not for you. She really cares about her students and tries to make sure everyone is understanding of the material. The class can be boring, but that really is not her fault because she has a set curriculum. Overall, I enjoy her teaching and this was my second class with her.

I had Professor Reed for SPAN 1101. Her lectures are easy to follow and have a lot of class participation so they are easy to follow. She gives study guides for tests and quizzes which prepare you well. Class is easy if you do the home work and show up to class. I am taking her again next semester for SPAN 1102.

Shamburg sensei was very sweet and a nice teacher. it helps that her class is like kindergarteners learning in which you get called on, participate, and do lots of assignment in order for the material to stick. I liked the oral exams and the way she formatted her grading criteria.

Sensei Shamburg's class is well-taught and ideal for quick learners. However, its fast pace makes it challenging for some, especially as the semester progresses. The class felt too short to cover the necessary material, and the preparation time for oral tests (~1-2 days) can be anxiety-inducing. BTW, she will have you speak as she goes by in rows.
Sensei Shamburg is so informed and an excellent teacher! Would take this class again in a heartbeat
Great prof, had her for elementary Japanese 2. She is very sweet but she will call on you.

I was nervous when I transferred to school and struggled with test taking. Shamburg sensei went out of her way to support me when I was struggling, I never failed again after that. Motivated me all the way through graduation. Thank you.
I loved this Spanish class. Mr. Gonzalez made this very manageable to complete. I felt prepared with this class.

Professor Gonzalez is friendly and understands his material very well. He truly cares about his students succeeding. SPAN 1101 can be a little intimidating for new learners but going into the course with background knowledge from high school is very helpful. If you are struggling, he wants to help.

Professor Gonzalez is an amazing teacher. He shows that he cares about his students and is very engaged in our learning. However, participation is a part of the grade. So, if you sit in the front you're more likely to get called on.

Professor Gonzalez is an excellent teacher—passionate, humorous, and dedicated to helping students succeed. He prepares us thoroughly for quizzes and exams, ensuring we master the material. Fair and reasonable, he allows flexibility with deadlines. Truly one of the best professors I've ever had.

I took Kaffen's class on Japanese Film and admittedly, I found the class a little boring. However, I feel like professors can really make or break a class. He was very kind, respectful, encouraging, and the feedback he gave on my writing was beneficial to my growth as a writer. He was very lenient and I think he genuinely cares about his students.

Professor himself was nice and clearly passionate about the subject. However I think the misunderstood the nature of this class and don't think his teaching methods fit it. Really lecture heavy, lots of difficult to understand text to read, really theoretical/philosophical discussions. He's more "theory" than "practicum". You've been warned.

I didn't expect my Anime studies professor to be the best lecturer I've ever had. You'll get out of the class what you put in, it's easy enough to coast and skimp on the readings and not participate in class discussion, but he makes it fun enough that you'll want to participate. Really caring guy too, was super lenient with deadlines and stuff

Professor Diaz is a great professor and genuinely cares about her students success. Attendance isn't necessarily required but it is 5% of your grade; but missing a few classes won't hurt you. She explains things really well in her lectures and even simplifies confusing concepts that did not really make sense to me in the textbook.

I really enjoyed taking this class. The work isn't difficult and Prof. Diaz is very willing to give feedback when asked. Pretty much all the assignments are on VHL and that doesn't transfer to canvas so I would check that separate website for due dates instead.

Graciela is an amazing professor. I would 100% take her again. Attendance and participation are 5% of your grade, so it's not a huge deal if you miss class, but it helps to be there because her lectures are more helpful than the HW for quiz/exam prep.
She's a great French professor. I had her for 2202, and intermediate French difficult. Study hard, practice daily, and you will be fine. She knows what she's teaching!

Very organized. She wants you to learn and helps you willingly. Lectures are fun and helpful. Textbook HW takes time and you have something every week. She gives extra credit on every test. Tests are fair, if you study it's not bad at all. I learned a lot and enjoyed her class when I didn't know any French. Definitely would take her again.

If you don't have prior French experience stay far from Professor Surget's class. Prior knowledge is expected and you will struggle without it. There is a TON of homework, ranging from an hour and a half to 4 hours before each class period. Lectures are boring and participation is huge. Would only recommend if French is something you love.

This professor is not good at explaining things and many times doesn't even understand your questions. She also has a hard to understand accent. There is almost no good practice with talking to other classmates to practice, which makes no sense considering its a language class. Class is not engaging at all.
She has her own book to teach we have as two quizzes every single week. Awful teacher, at UNC Charlotte.

I took COMP 301 with this professor and the class itself was fine. However, in any aspect beyond that, it becomes clear he doesn't care about supporting students. Despite being Director of Undergraduate Studies, he offers no guidance and seems largely indifferent to student success.
Bro cannot teach but at least the class was easy. He's pretty nice too

Previous review sums up the whole class perfectly. The entire content is just obscure web dev lore that nobody cares about. Even if you work in front end i doubt you will ever need to know this random stuff

kmp is a nice guy but a horrible professor. the fact that he expects us to learn web dev purely with bullet point notes is a testament to his lack of care. class is an easy A- but hard A. assignments are outdated, lectures are copypaste readings of bland bullet points, tests ask the most obscure things. overall the worst cs class ive ever taken

Tested on minute details on purely black-and-white bullet point slides. The lectures are just him reciting the bullet points and sub bullet points with no real elaboration or useful implications. Assignments are convoluted and outdated with modern practices. Strict grading scale, extremely rare to get an A on largest assignment (no one got a 100).
i didnt listen to the ratings. i prayed to Jesus that they were wrong and i could tough it out. he made me lose faith in the Lord.

Very difficult to contact and a very unclear grading criteria. Professor Snoeyink was kind and funny but from someone with no data structure experience this class was hard to understand, he said a lot of things that went straight over my head and even though I did okay overall I do not feel that I learned a lot and did not enjoy the class.

Jack's a genius, although perhaps not good with people.

He holds students to the same bar he does himself, which might explain the deluge of negative ratings, but I didn't find it too bad myself (and I didn't attend office hours + skipped most lectures).
What are the other reviews talking about... This material is easy no matter what prof lol.

Never grinded harder for an A. I depended a lot on the TAs and the office hours. Do exercises from the book, do the practice quizzes. Get a team

On the final I had to translate stuff from English to my native language in order to understand some of the exercises. I have been in university for 2 years I've never needed to do that.

He is chill IMO.

Prof. Brent is the best systems professor in the department! His classes have been foundational for the career path I want to take up. He has a clear grading criteria and is very fair. His lectures cover a lot of content but he tells us some lighthearted stories in between to keep us going. He's awesome!!

Easiest A of my life, but I would never take another class with him that you actually need to pay attention to because his lectures were boring and repetitive. Anyone can take this class if they need a free Comp Sci elective.

Tbh, 562 with Brent was way too easy and I would have wanted to learn more about the math behind all the machine learning algorithms that we were taught. I also would have liked to learn about more deep learning topics.

Easiest comp class I've taken tbh. He was okay when I took him for 211 but for this 590 class (3d graphics) there are no exams and pretty much the whole grade is group projects that are easy to get 100s on. Don't need to go to class at all, there are some days when he takes attendance but it's rare and usually he tells you on canvas ahead of time.

First I agree with all 211/311 comments. However, his 590 is completely different, it's one of the easiest course in CS department. All group projects, but you don't really have to work with them, you can go solo. Brent won't care. TAs are lenient graders. Just make sure to submit everything on time because he has almost 0 tolerance for late work.
Awesome guy! Clearly loves AI ethics and sets high standards and helps you succeed in the course.

Best professor! I am not a CS major but I learned a lot from his class. Every single time I asked for help, whether over email or in person, he was there. He is
generous and open-minded.
I LOVE HIM! Very enthusiastic, brilliant, and compassionate prof, someone invested in student success.
Professor Gaikwad was easily amongst my favorite professors at UNC; he is approachable and caring. You'll learn a ton in class while also gaining outside support for well-being and career.
Best Prof. Highly recommend!

Dr. Ghani does not require too much from his students, and you can put in very minimal effort and still receive a solid result. His class was very test-focused with some coding assignments. The class is very relaxed and all the info that you need is easily accessible online.
Easy class but lectures are pretty boring
Professor Ghani was excellent at teaching data structures but I felt that the material covering algorithms was lacking. Overall it was a good class and the lectures were available online.

It could be a hard course if you really want to understand everything in this course (for people with no much foundation for data structure in general). However, Dr.Ghani made it easier through lecture videos and very detailed powerpoints (which cover EVERTHING on exams). 
It's a bless that tests are not hard! Just make sure to read materials.

There is no attendance grade and all lectures are recorded, which is nice. But content of the class is just  kinda boring. Dr. Ghani speaks monotone throughout the lecture, but honestly who can blame him? At least the exams are not bad. You just have to watch the lectures closely, and he gives generous deadlines for the homework assignments.

Class is doable bc he's a lenient grader. Problems and posted materials are enough that you can teach yourself algorithms outside of class. Pacing is fast but Kevin does a solid job teaching hard material. Tests are 50% from problem sets and 50% completely unsolvable, but he curves.

Over half of the exam questions are from problem sets with new questions being very difficult to get right. Algorithms is typically a difficult class but the pacing of this course is very fast since each lecture covers ~2 problem types. Lectures are pretty good but are definitely not enough to good on tests. Class avg curved to B+.

- Grading criteria is generous (65 is a C)
- Content is difficult but useful
- HW is 40% of grade and easy A
- No attendance
- Tons of high quality resources

The course encompassed difficult material and difficult exams. Requires a deep understanding of each algorithm and they get more complex throughout the semester. Expect to spend a good chunk of time studying. Midterm averages were always low 70s. Final was even harder. Still, Kevin was supportive and did a good job teaching.

Really chill guy who is obviously passionate about this class and teaching as a whole. I thought he explained concepts pretty clearly for a difficult class. The material is interesting and a good bit of it is useful for interviews. Midterms are 5 Qs and always have one tricky question that most people don't solve but he curved so median was B+.

Overall, it's a very generous class. There was ample extra credit on assignments, which allowed most of the class to earn an A. The course was pretty straightforward until the final month, when LISP was introduced. I'd strongly recommend starting assignments early, especially to take advantage of the extra credit offered for early submissions!
I forbid you from taking this class.

This class is hard for all of the wrong reasons. Prof. Dewan's assignments are nightmarish to decode (some reaching 35 pages in length), and his slides/lectures are verbose with many typos. Way too many hoops have to be jumped through to setup assignments, request help, or participate in class. Everything just feels artificially difficult.

The professor's assignments are unclear and overly wordy, making instructions hard to follow. Many students struggle, not due to the material but because the writing is poor. English fluency is an issue, and it hurts the learning experience. Lectures are fine, but written work needs serious revision. Using Grammarly, AI, or a fluent TA would help.
i wish i could go back and never have taken this class
My favorite class I've taken in the CS dept so far! frequent quizzes but generous drop policy and they aren't too hard if you are staying on top of the material. very fair imo

Too many quizzes and lab which was a sure fire way to make everyone feel burnt out. I did not enjoy this class. Fair warning. Avoid professor if possible. Lectures were disorganized and it was hard to study.
She's pretty cool and relatable. She also listens to her students and changed her methods based on student feedback.
KAKI IS THE GOAT!!!!!

She makes COMP 311 a breeze. Actually cares about her students and wants the best for them. Lectures are easy to follow, quiz heavy which makes it easy to stay consistent with studying, very accessible outside class. Genuinely loved her and the class. ENROLL WITH PROF RYAN SHE'S AWESOME TRUST ME.

COMP550: Sridhar is a really great person who definitely cares about his students. However his lectures are pretty unorganized. Instead of slides, he projects his handwritten notes that are almost illegible. His exams are worded ambiguously and can be difficult, but his homeworks are perfect. Curve was generous and ensured no one got less than a C+

I'm not sure why the ratings are low. I found that he was a pretty good professor and was really kind. I think a lot of people rating low just didn't show up to classes regularly or office hours. Attendance isn't mandatory, but basically is if you want to do well, since it is a harder class. His handwriting can be difficult to understand at times.

Really cool dude, he's one of my favorite comp sci professors. I also thought he was a good teacher. It is tough material, probable should have went to office hours and would have probably done better.
