Very knowledgeable, not mean, gives extensions, gives extra credit, but teaching was just not great. Difficult to follow handwriting and lectures. I don't think I really absorbed anything. Maybe the material is just extremely difficult, but the homework and exams felt impossible. You don't know your grade until the end, and it's too late by then.

If you wanna take any class with this guy, think thrice. That's the worst professor I've ever had—unclear grading, low-quality teaching, and tough exams. It'd have been okay to make exams tough if his teaching were good. Please, for your own good, if it's your last semester, postpone graduation and take 455 with someone else.

Professor Montek is very generous with grading and always available after lectures to answer questions. His TA, Andrew, was also helpful. The lectures were enjoyable, particularly for students who enjoyed COMP 311, and the final project was engaging and fun. The only downside is that the labs are almost weekly, so this course moves quite quickly.

No exams, just 9 assignments and a final project. While the class wasn't too bad throughout the semester, there were. very unclear rubrics and grading criteria's for the assignments and no grades were put in until after the final project was due after LDOC, so you don't really know what your grade is ever.

Most amazing comp sci processor I have ever had. The most genuine educator and mentor to have on your side. Not an easy class but he did whatever he could to make sure each and every student was on the right track

He truly cares so much and is the sweetest guy ever. Lectures can be a little boring at times but he makes sure you know what you need to know and he allows for a lot of creative freedom on your final project. Actual king Montek

The best professor I've had at UNC. Patient, forgiving, flexible, accessible, and genuinely cares about your success. The material is difficult, but this course offers experiences that you won't find anywhere else. You'll create a working processor with I/O support, then create an Assembly program for it. Lenient grading, but you must put in work.

COMP 590: mcmillan is passionate about ai, yes, but the grading criteria is so unclear. the class shouldn't be this ambiguous if he's using ai to help him with everything. no coding, just prompting. he uses ai to grade your assignments, which are on google forms??? our exam was on there too. the ai that grades your problem sets is weirdly harsh too

- Exams were open computer, so very easy to do perform well
- HW problem sets were 40 % of grade and they were easy As
- Attendance is through in class QR code's half the time posted prior to lectures, half the time not, so sometimes attendance is not required
- Lecture heavy
- Content is confusing and difficult, but open computer exams = easy

He is genuinely on something for allowing AI on exams. I'm not even kidding midway through I just stopped teaching myself and learned how to use AI properly. Hell, the median on the second exam was 76, and I got a 94 with an exam completely done using Gemini on deep research mode. I still don't know why I got some qs right. Waste of time.

I've genuinely never been more bewildered at a class. I can't name a singular concept I took away this sem; the midterms and HW are ridiculously hard. They're Open-AI, and you absolutely need to use AI to get through them in time, except the questions are made harder so that AI can't solve them so no one ends up solving anything.

A genuinely ridiculous class. I am bewildered by the choice to make every single exam stupidly hard,  open internet, open note, and open ChatGPT for an INTRODUCTORY class. The tests are made to be ridiculously difficult, the TA's can't help because they haven't learned any of the material being taught, and most of all, the lectures are pointless.

Prof. Jasleen's lectures were amazing! I would feel guilty if I missed one. If you go over the textbook and took good notes in class, homework can be tackled. The exams are based off the concepts tested in the homework. The professor also goes over the tricky ones really well. Prof. Jasleen is a hidden gem in the CS department, very inspirational!
Don't take this class unless you hate yourself.

This has been the most demanding course I've taken.

Pros: The lectures are clear; participation is encouraged & can boost one's grade; the course offers a hefty curve.

Cons: Both exams were very challenging (with averages in the 50s); homework assignments are time-consuming (about 10 hours each); the amount of material covered is overwhelming.

Tests are almost like physics problems with tons of math (avg was in 50s for midterm and final). Labs and written HWs take about 10 hr per HW and there is a week or two for each. I think Prof Kaur is a good lecturer and the course is really useful (but you have to self-study for HW and tests). Class got curved to an 80 + a participation bonus.

I thought this course was a good intro to internet protocols and how the internet works! Rewarding, though stressful at times.
A more traditional-style class reliant on lectures/note-taking, but Dr. Kaur's explanations were consistently clear. Homework and coding labs were demanding, so be prepared to pay very, very close attention to details.
Great professor and gave us so many materials to learn the class. 126 had so much content and so many things to memorize, but it was doable.

Class was pretty hard even with Comp 110 as a background. You have to pay attention during lectures to follow along with the class. Stay on top of understanding and you will be fine. Exams are kind of hard and you need to know nuanced things. Didn't really enjoy it due to my grades, but chill class.

Tessa is GREAT! The honors section was very interesting, and everything is graded fairly. Easy class. Group projects/presentations were low stakes and fun. Occasional reading quizzes, but they are not weighted heavily. 100% recommend!

Tessa is wonderful! But as someone with zero coding experience, the class was a bit challenging. The assignments were a lot of work and a bit difficult, but the tests were fairly straightforward with a double sided cheat sheet. The class covered a lot of content and was pretty stressful at times. So IMO, it's not an easy A but doable to get an A

Takes an interest in her students and helps to make the most out of the TA experience. The class is not heavy with material as the difficulty of the course lies in the TA work and responsibilities you have outside of the class

She changed the course from what it used to be, now with more weight on tests instead of building the actual compiler (assignments). Her lectures are super boring and her slides are poorly written and will sometimes contain wrong information for something that will be on an exam. Assignments are very unbalanced, unclear and don't guide you at all.

I took this class in 2024 and loved it. The labs are tricky, but manageable with OH. The professor is very engaging in lectures and super passionate about security. I loved to learn from her! This class is on a uni-level standard. Definitely challenging, but in the best way possible. Don't let other reviewers fool you, take this class!

Sturton's 590 (formal methods for security) is a great research-focused CS elective. No programming; homework is reading research papers. Grades are from ~10 very doable quizzes and a group research project. She takes a while to grade but is exceptionally kind and helpful in OH. Recommend if you liked COMP 455 or prefer discussion-based classes.

0 detail of slides. Practice tests? Oh u mean polleverywhere, yes pollev questions r highly related to tests, do that. Labs are busy work with screenshot, I hope they can come up with some actual original up-to-date labs that teach u stuff. Exam grading is like Teemo, annoying, u would get points off for somethign stoopid. Professor is chill, tho.
I really liked this course and I thought she was very kind and knowledgeable. One thing though is that there is a LOT of material for the exams. Other than that, solid course.

I wouldn't take this class unless the rest of your schedule is easy or you're a C++ expert. Projects made me lose many nights of sleep. Longest midterm I've ever seen with difficult content. Average was below 50 on midterm and 57 for final. He allowed up to 125% on P3 which is why I got an A. Letter grade ranges B and below were expanded.

Very good class if you like systems and low level programming. He could def be clearer in lectures sometimes as he mostly assumes everyone understands. Assignments are hard but there are opportunities for a ton of extra credit. He's from CMU and clearly stated that the class is basically a fork from CMU version so idk why people are complaining.
class is copy and pasted directly from CMU; i wish i was exaggerating. prof is getting paid to do nothing and the class is unreasonably hard. do yourself a favor and take literally any other class

Hi folks this is tungtungtungtungtungtungtungtungtung sahur, remember to take ENGL105 before this class which is a hidden pre-req, and treat his tests as DLC of ENGL105 final, then you will do great, but don't ask me why I don't want to take this class again

Professor Berg was actually fairly understanding, as he realized his expectations were way too high and he made attempts to adjust. He was available outside of class and worked with me when it came to assignments. That being said, if you don't already understand how to write concurrent programs, take literally ANYTHING else. You won't be taught it.

Professor Porter is a charismatic and highly knowledgeable instructor. I recommend not procrastinating on the assignments, as this allows enough time to make use of office hours. The exams tend to be more conceptual than computational. Students who review the lecture slides and understand how the assignments are implemented should be well prepared.

Porter is awesome, funny, knowledgeable and really cares about the material and his students. It's a lot of work and material - so do be prepared for that - but you'll learn so much and get to listen to his lectures! Cannot recommend enough.

Dr Porter is great at lecturing and explaining concepts but this class was SO difficult. If it was ratemyclass I would give 0 stars but since it's ratemyprofessor he gets 4 stars. He's truly a super kind guy and really cares, but those labs were the bane of my existence.

Prof Porter's course structure is a big issue. It relies heavily on readings, which while okay, shouldn't hinder the professor from teaching concepts. He often skims over slides. The lab instructions were also unclear, making it difficult to understand what to do. The exams were hard, requiring deeper insight than the lectures provided.

Great class for learning OS. Difficult by nature of content, but Porter did well in teaching the content and having applicable labs. The exams do require a solid understanding of the material, not just memorization. Labs are no joke and should not be done the night before, but definitely doable. A lot of EC chances.

I had such an awful experience with this class. I dreaded every assignment, grading criteria was unclear, and the median was around 50% for both exams. The content is interesting but I felt like it didn't matter how much I studied, the exams just tested a random bullet that got talked about for 10 seconds or an anecdote from the first day of class.

I have A's and A-'s in every CS course taken at UNC. COMP 283 was a breeze in comparison to 431. Jeffay crafts his exams to be impossible to finish in the allotted time. Jeffay offers 0 partial credit for good attempts while expecting you to memorize every slide. He expects perfection. Achieved above median and still got a C. Doubt he curved.

Interesting class, but don't take it unless you can dedicate a LOT of time to understand EVERYTHING. Professor Jeffay is nice, but very firm on how his class operates and won't listen to what students say they need. Almost no study resources provided, test scores are extremely low, and no way to prepare for tests. Just have to hope you're ready.

The class was interesting, but also incredibly stressful. Grading criteria on programming assignments and exams were unclear, scores were poor across all exams, and content was very difficult to grasp. This class gave me more issues than any other at this school. Possible to enjoy it, but would certainly not recommend taking it as an elective.

Prof. Jeffay delivers interesting content, but his lecture style can be boring. He's accessible outside of class, and the programming assignments are decent, but instructions are verbose. Exams and homework are unnecessarily challenging and poorly structured, mainly due to a lack of review resources. Overall, an interesting yet frustrating class.

I love Stotts. I took every class I could with him and this semester with 523 was my favorite. He was super helpful and understanding with difficult situations and hiccups with our project. He is super funny and while group projects are not my favorite, I felt comfortable in this course because I knew he would be an understanding grader.

With this def being the last semester where he'll keep the class format, his 590 class felt like a breath of fresh air when compared to the brutality of other CS elective classes. He kept the course interesting, applicable and incredibly fair. It's really sad to see attendance drop during the sem, but he keeps a good spirit and is super sweet.

I'd say the lectures were pretty boring but the class itself was good experience. If you have a good team and a good client, I'd say that the class gives you an awesome intro to SWE. I really enjoyed working on a real project for our client and enjoyed the opportunity to take this course. It is a lot of work, but it's nice to have that experience!

Dr. Stotts was a super chill professor, who's lectures and assignments were driven from past semesters. But don't let that fool you, the resources are really good, and I enjoyed working on them. They teach you all the concepts you need from the lectures, and they're fun do to. Only downside is that lectures are a little stale, but still useful!

Stotts seems like a nice guy- very sarcastic, some witty jokes, but people are not lying when they say the lectures will put you to sleep.  The whole class is zoned out.  If your good at teaching yourself and did great in comp110 then you can get away with not going to class.  Just look at the slides that he posts because he will test you on it.

Very easy class if you do exactly what the assignments ask. The grading on assignments is very lenient as long as you know what you are talking about. He takes attendance on random days. Only a midterm and a final, and you can make a cheatsheet.

Not a great class, but not too hard. I skipped a lot of lectures because I found them boring and not helpful. There weren't too many assignments and they all built off of each other. Everything in the class is graded by an in-person demo. Super stressful and annoying for me personally. The exams were not very hard.
Nothing in this class is difficult besides the exams, but even those don't make up a large portion of the overall grade.

Most of the complaints on this professor are from students who likely ChatGPT'd their way through the course and couldn't explain what they did during demos. Also he posted lectures and videos for the topics. If you understood what you were doing in your assignments and projects then this class is ridiculously easy. Nirjon is great professor!

Don't really understand what all this hate is about. This professor was better than others in the CS department and his expectations were really clear. I mean if you just read his announcement, he practically told you each question on the final. His tests weren't bad. His expectations were clear and the class was easy if you study and do your work.

Despite the deteriorating CS faculty at UNC, this class is one of the few that is truly amazing. It's the hardest class I've taken, but it genuinely teaches you. Saba is generous with grading, and there's a TA for every schedule. It gets much harder after the first midterm, so reconsider if you're very busy. Otherwise, this class is a masterpiece.
Amazing class and genuinely learned so much. Nicest professor ever. I love that guy!

This will probably be the hardest class you ever take (content-wise; passing isn't as hard). You may understand very little because the content is super theoretical and might not even exist. Saba lectures fast, so LOCK IN and go to every one! Homework's hard and graded harshly but exams are easier and curved by free points. Take 455,550,M381 first!

While the content was difficult, Saba and TAs are more than willing to explain throughly during office hours. The problem sets always require a bit of ingenuity, but the programming assignments are almost always very straightforward. The exams were challenging but the final grade cutoffs are rather generous. I really enjoyed his class.

Agreed with other reviews there was a great opportunity to learn through this course; however, grading was at times punitive or ambiguous. Rubrics were never posted before assignments or projects. Verbal explanations of grading often felt vague, and quizzes were quite difficult, with some questions ambiguous or asking for material not covered.
I had 0 formal coding experience and this class was tough.

Has an incredible industry background, and is always happy to talk about what he's done and how he got there. A great lecturer in all the material we covered, from applied linear algebra to concepts on virtual reality hardware. Homeworks are long, but feel really useful towards your own skills and what you'll need for the final project. Awesome guy

I took the honors class and it was a nightmare. His lectures were poorly executed, as he would often rush past important details; i had to teach myself everything. He also had a propensity to only call on the male students in the classroom. I often felt like he was talking down to many of the other female students and didn't take us as seriously.
Great person and professor. If you do assignments and go to class, you'll do well

This course is pretty easy if you have previous coding experience and it is structured very clearly. There are a few homework assignments and required labs, but the attendance is not mandatory and he posts all of the lecture slides on canvas. He also allows cheat sheets for exams, so you don't really have to study at all. Overall good prof!

Interesting class and the prof was pretty funny sometimes. Very knowledgeable. Barely anyone ever showed up to class which is probably why the other reviews didn't get a good grade. If you go to class, you learn a lot. If you get a good group for HW and final project you can easily get an A. Generous grading and a good guy.

Waste of a semester. unengaging lectures. 4 grades TOTAL. hw required to be done in groups-easier to grade ig, yet grades werent returned until end of semester & was limited to single letter grades rather than question-by-question score breakdown (including midterm). too little feedback far too late. didnt learn much. better off reading a textbook

I have no clue what to say, I don't even know what is going on in this class. Avoid at ALL COSTS. He's too busy working his job at SAS to teach us. 

But... that's just my opinion.

This was probably the worst class i have taken at UNC. Throughout the semester, we had 2 hw assignments, one midterm, and one final project. The midterm was hard, worth 30%, fully written by chatgpt, and we didn't get our grade back until finals season. his grading criteria sucks and his lectures are disorganized and he uses someone else's slides.
Hes the GOAT. DA GOAT
don't take if you haven't taken a coding class before, you will be confused.

No required attendance and slideshows posted so you can teach content to yourself if you like. Cheatsheet allowed on the three quizzes and final is a group project that isn't too bad. Overall recommend, pretty easy especially if you have previous cs or stat knowledge.

I took data 110 with professor lee and the class overall was very easy, we were allowed a cheat sheet during exams and the final was a pretty fun project that could be added to your resume. Overall, was a great insightful class that was not too challenging.

She doesn't care about her students and is extremely rude. Ive seen other students come to her with personal matters and instead of being understanding she refuses to give extensions or any type of understanding for a 100 level class.  Her grading is also all over the place, lectures are boring, and her TA's are accusatory.

Harlin is a great person and very caring of her students. The class is not hard to do well in at all, but her lectures tend to be really boring and I found myself not able to pay attention. Her lecture slides are also not very clear and can be really confusing. I had to teach myself most of the course content, but it wasn't hard.
goat. super chill, derives ideas bottom up. dense but well organized lectures
The exams for this course are difficult if you don't have a very strong math foundation. However, the assignments and teamwork are very helpful. You can learn a lot from them.
Do not take this class.

Prof. Oliva is fantastic. Amazingly intelligent, articulate, and he stands out as one of my favorite professors at Carolina. That being said, he teaches a difficult course—be prepared to work. There will not be much hand holding and lectures will focus on key theoretical concepts instead of implementation.

Junier Oliva is by far one the best professors I have ever had. The class itself (Machine Learning) was extremely difficult, requiring somewhere about 5-10 hours per lecture to supplement, understand, and digest the material, especially for some later lectures. However Oliva makes sure to teach the nuance and it makes for a very eye-opening course.
goat. don't listen to 560 reviews. knows and teaches material extremely well

Shashank is the most brilliant professor in the university. He is also one of the best instructors in the CS department. The course is very math-heavy, but he cares deeply about the students' learning, and has an extraordinary knack to explain complex math concepts and equations in English.

There are homework and quizzes almost every week, and the classes are pure math calculations, and the homework after class is all code. 
But I understand some of the knowledge of deep learning by learning to derive these formulas. These are somewhat useful for my job search because I will be asked these questions in written tests and interviews.

At the beginning of class he said that lectures wouldnt be recorded, and there would be no practice midterm. But students kept on asking him for it, and he finally started recording classes and gave a practice midterm which was not useful at all in preparation for the exam. Overall, a pretty difficult course.

There was only 1 TA with 1 hour office hours a week. The professor had an appointment based 1 hour weekly office hour. There was Piazza for unlimited questions. If you are taking more than 12 credit hours and taking this course, I would advise not to. Make sure that you have a good foundation in Probability before taking this course.

Szafir was denied tenure at CU Boulder in March of 2021. Instead of fulfilling his teaching duties for the remainder of the semester he offloaded lectures to TAs, stopped grading and stopped answering emails. He quiet quit before the term even had a name. Dan Szafir is still a laughing stock back here and we'll always remember it despite formality.

This is a review for COMP 581 - Intro to Robotics
Great class with hands-on group projects where you build your lego robot and program it to complete tasks. The material covered in the lectures and exams is relevant to help you build your robot.

My only complaint is that sometimes the lectures move slowly covering basic introductions for too long.

Why are there negative reviews? Because there were no quizes or exams, and no one studied regularly. When professor asked students questions, no one answered because they did not study. Because of this, Professor would never offer a course again where there is no quiz or exams. And its the students fault.
Definitely one of the few professors who really care about students.
A hidden gem in the CS department!

Mike Reed is an amazing professor. Lectures were so good and genuinely taught me so much about graphics, different ways in mathematics to do the same thing, and how to write very performative code. Take this class if you wanna learn from one of the best in this field and have it lectured to you in a very clear and intuitive way. I love that guy!

I dont think people realize that Reed is a graphics legend. We are talking about the same dude that made skia (and sold it to Google). You won't learn cool 3D techniques (hence the course name) but for people interested in graphics it builds a solid grasp of techniques used in the field. Taking this class is not even negotiable if you like graphics
This guy would teach you how to do your finals.

Reed is a great lecturer and professor. His passion for graphics and teaching couldn't be more clear. His lectures are easy to follow and informative. Assignments are difficult, so start early and follow along. Very clear grading scheme, most of final grade is from 6 projects. You will learn a ton about programming skills, graphics, and c++.
Mike clocks in as the greatest graphics professor I've ever had.

Best and clearest lecture slides in the COMP department. Labs were only hard because the sensors were not reliable but tests were pretty easy. Content is very interesting and I learned a lot. Would definitely recommend taking this class. But remember, there is a lot of unpredictability and a lot of uncertainty in robotics...

There wasn't too much homework, but there is a group project where you build a robot and design it for the given lab days. The labs get progressively harder and make up the bulk of the class grade, and took my group anywhere from 5 - 10 hours a week outside of class to prepare. The assignments/exams were fairly challenging (mostly trigonometry).

I took COMP 89 with professor Alterovitz. SUPER easy class. 3 assignments and 2 projects for the entire semester, no homework. Class can be boring sometimes but Professor Alterovitz is super passionate about what he researches and studies. He does not take attendance. I highly recommend taking this class for a FC and easy freshman launch.

He's very passionate about tech., only talked about robots, AI, & internet instead of many topics. Grade based on a few assignments and 2 presentations (7 & 15 minutes). Attendance policy is unclear but as far as I know, he did not record attendance (syllabus said he would take it 3 random days). Lectures are somewhat boring but the class is easy

Dr. Arguello is patient and cares about his students! The material could be confusing, but he explained assignments clearly in class. You can even replace a bad take-home midterm with a take-home final. He's passionate and lets you choose your own project topics and groups, making it more enjoyable.

Nice guy and interesting content but does not seem qualified to teach this course. Seemed like he was relearning the material as he looked at the slides. Still remember when a student asked to demonstrate smoothing and he acted like it was tall task when all he had to do was some basic addition (that was literally already in his slides). Easy A

Best professor in INLS. Professor Arguello is very kind and really wants his students to understand the material. Class isn't necessarily mandatory, but would be helpful for exams. 3 Assignments, and Take Home Exams. As long as you put in the effort to understand the material you should be good!

Best professor ever. So kind, humorous, really wants his students to understand the material. I would suggest going to class because the slides are great, but sometimes the homework is hard to do solely based off of the slides. Only grades are 3 homework assignments, a takehome midterm and final, and a group research paper. Grades very easily.

This Professor was amazing at teaching and really cares about his students, but the class should be marketed as a math class than comp because of how demanding it is for linear algebra and some differential equations. Also, his TAs were amazing and was helpful even outside of their office hours.

Although the professor is a fantastic teacher, if you are not a math major or at least highly interested in matrix algebra and controller design the class is challenging. You are expected to memorize the formulas for exams which isn't undoable but notable. The toughest part is there is no partial credit on exams so you essentially get it or don't.

She's an amazing lecturer. Yes, she might come off as rude but she is genuinely not trying to be based on my long conversations with her. She helped me a lot with understanding NLP concepts. She's definitely a gem of the CS department given her experience and the fact that she takes her job seriously.
Made the course a lot difficult. No lecture recordings, no practice for exams, homeworks are difficult. I would avoid taking a course with her.
The content of the course is good. The Professor was rude.
A good lecturer, but not a good person. Half of the class could not get their projects right, and fundamental mistakes were caught. It proves how much less support she gave the class. Dont take.

She can clearly explain concepts fairly well. She is incredibly unresponsive, though. She mostly uses Piazza for group discussions, however we don't get an answer for days or sometimes even weeks. There are some challenging concepts in the subject, therefore having a supporting lecturer would be more beneficial.

Dr. Gotz is nice, accessible, but I wouldn't recommend this course. As a statistics major, I found the content too basic. While JavaScript is used for projects, his teaching is extremely minimal, and you're expected to learn it mostly on your own. If you're seeking a more in-depth or challenging experience, this course may not be the best choice.
Amazing! Lectures were asynchronous and easy to follow. Seven coding assignments, an ungraded midterm (participation grade as long as you do it), and a final. Very accessible outside of class.

Tough course and rewarding. Roni explains things well if you have sufficient background in computer vision. If you keep reading and follow the course you will learn a lot. Overall the project is not too much. The grading is rather generous.

Really challenging but rewarding class. The lectures are pretty math heavy, but Roni makes them interesting and is willing to review concepts. Her grading scale is also very generous. Would recommend if you're even slightly interested in computer vision.
Speaking style can be a bit hard to follow at times. Difficult midterms. Not an easy 590 but if you put in the work you have the opportunity to learn a lot.

This was a rough class, but I can't see it being taught any other way, since computer vision is a complicated field. Might have preferred another lab doing something with OpenCV over the mathy hw. This is an extremely mathy class btw, don't take it if you aren't a linear algebra god.

I'm taking COMP 590 for both times, 1 information visualization and 1 design. Both classes are really good and relatively easy. 80+ students enrolled in the 24'fall class so you can imagine how popular Prof. Szafir is. Great professor and great class!

Her 590 was interesting & had a very manageable workload! The TA, Matt, graded our projects very kindly :), & outside of 3 projects & a final project, the only other grade was participation. Explored different methods of visualizations, including using D3 & making physical representations. Sometimes the topics felt a little too abstract though.

Szafir is an excellent professor and amazing lecturer! The content of the course is very interesting, assignments are light and engaging, and there are many opportunities for extra credit. We got introduced to D3 as well, which is super useful. I would definitely want to take another class with her again.

Professor Wu was fantastic, engaging, & informative. I learned a lot, and would absolutely take another course with him. There were only about 3 assignments total, and the semester focused on building final projects, making the end manageable. He cares deeply about his students and creates a collaborative environment. Highly recommend INLS 382!

I learned a lot and really want to take time the amount of effort Professor Wu took to make sure his students learned the topics in the most meaningful way. Always went above and beyond and really a gem of the INLS department!

Danny is such a caring professor. The course is graded based on three short homework assignments and a multiple-choice final, for which he provides the correct answers beforehand so students can study.

Chill class. How much you learn highly correlates with how much you put in. Class projects can be hard for people without computer security background. But the most important pre-requisites are OS and Comp architecture. Prepare to learn before enrolling. The professor is knowledgeable and approachable.

This was his side channels class. Was reading papers with 2 labs that weren't too hard; better to start labs in advance though, so you don't get stuck. Really enjoyed this class and felt like I got a lot out of it. This is Andrew's hyperspecific research area and so he can talk in great depth about the papers and also what the paper don't tell you.

lectures can be very tedious and hard to get through. graded 90% based on midterms and final; one midterm was dropped. all of the content you need to know is in the lectures and accessible through slideshows. if you like abstract cs topics, you'll love this class. if not, really study the homeworks and lectures and you'll be fine.

His lectures are hella boring and sleep-worthy; within one week, about 75% of the class stopped going to them. His slides are WAY TOO DETAILED, and the content is hella challenging to understand. At least your lowest homework grade gets dropped.

The material is difficult, but Dr. Anderson does an amazing job of explaining it in a digestible manner. He may not say it, but he truly cares about  each student and structures the course in a way that is intended to foster passion, challenge you, and enhance learning. Work hard, ask questions, and study with others and you'll learn a lot! :)

He's a researcher not a teacher. Whole class was confusing but he's some kind of genius. He doesn't lecture, he assigns someone new to lecture every class day. Great class for feeling out how much you like AI /multimodal. Everyone from undergrad to PHD students are in this class. His feedback ranges from dissertation to “ok”

Very nice professor and good at explaining stuff, sometimes can be a little fast, but willing to explain if you ask questions. The course itself is a little rough, especially if you are not good at imagining 3D, don't think it's her fault.
My favorite class this semester. Prof Mclaughlin is an interesting teacher, great notes, nice lady.
dont even go to class just self study. no textbook, her notes r overly complicated, bad lecturer. everyones grades go up once they self study. says a lot.
You're better off self-studying than going to any of her lectures. It's rare for her to post any practice exams, and she'll never post a key.

the exams are actually bs and she doesnt explain things well whatsoever. there is no textbook and her handwritten notes have just random stuff that isnt tested on in it. the exams are actually unfathomably bad.
It seems Dr. Burkhead genuinely enjoys students' failure and struggle, even considering it as her achievement in teaching.
The worst thing ever happened to unc ccs

Graded recitation quizzes every week, midterms were harder and longer compared to other sections, so final was easier than midterms. Multiple homework attempts and grades dropped from each category. Decent lecturer, accessible and nice during office hours, gives good explanations one on one, but sometimes snarky. Calc 3 is inevitably really hard.

She is the WORST professor I've ever taken with at UNC. I think she just did not care about this class that much and basically did not know how to teach an introduction analysis course. Sometimes, she lead students astray.
DO NOT TAKE 521 WITH HER.
David's lecutres were all over the place and made learning the material very difficult. His office was also a mess, so I can see why he never responded to his emails.

Lectures were very disorganized and he procrastinated until a couple days before finals to post notes. Just understanding the concepts is not enough. The online HW is no help but the HW worksheets and in-class worksheets should be studied for exams. The third exam was easy but everything else was a mess. Did not give partial credit on the final.

The class is very test heavy (80%) and test difficulty varies a lot (but questions are pretty consistent with HW and worksheet content). Lessons are quite hard to follow and participation worksheets are part of the grade. May still be a easier class than other MATH347 professors due to less weightage on proofs and a pretty easy grading scale/curve.

David is a really sweet guy, and wants his students to succeed. However, his lectures are incomprehensible, and slides are hard to follow. It's possible to self-teach, since it's mostly computation. ULAs/TAs are helpful, especially for hw--tricky hw problems often were tested. Most proofs were pulled from slides. Check grading--often has errors.

Easily the worst instructor in the school. Attendance mandatory but lectures are incoherent. He never responds to emails, and exams (80% of grade) test random proofs from lecture slides written in hieroglyphics. He talks about Julia (a random software) like it's his wife. Attendance mandatory too. Still got an A, so IDGAF. Philippians 4:13 innit.
The lectures are not up to standard and content covered in class is nowhere comparable to what is tested on the exams. 100% of your grade comes from 2 midterms and a final.

Rozansky is really funny and you can tell he cares about his students, but his lectures are extremely disorganized and hard to follow. Not a bad class if you can self teach. There's 2 midterms worth 30% and a common final. His midterms aren't difficult, but 381 is pretty hard so the common final was rough.

Entire grade was from the tests. He did curve a lot but that says more about his teaching than anything. Basically, you're better off learning this from youtube videos and the textbook than him. Tests were very reasonable and easy, however. So, if you're confident in your self studying skills, he may be a good teacher for you.

Extremely disorganized and sometimes hard to follow lecturing. Despite this, he's so disorganized it's insanely funny. He also gives problem-by-problem breakdowns of exams which are immensely helpful. Don't schedule a class 15 minutes after because he will 100% run overtime every single class. Outside of this, he isn't too bad for UNC math profs.

this review is for math 529. honestly i had him for dif eq as well and he is a bit disorganized and its hard to know how you're doing in the class but he really wants you to succeed, is very helpful in office hours, and even gives you a list of topics for the final as well. you will have to be motivated and do a lot of self studying.

Thomas is a fine professor. Her tests were fair and very similar to the notes, not the homework. Her lectures are clear, and she answers questions. That being said, she fell behind often due to snow days and other things. The catch-up was very stressful, and she's not accessible outside of class, which caused a lot more stress.

Overall this one was a good class. The slides she went over in class were similar in difficulty to the 3 mid term exams. What neither me, nor the students I talked to after the semester ended, were prepared for was the final. The final was unreasonably hard for what we had been previously tested on. My grade dropped 12 points due to the final.

Dr. Thomas was, overall, a great professor- her expectations were clear and fair. The HW was harder than the exams, but it prepared you well. The kicker of this class was the final- made by the overall math department was way harder than the other exams and honestly, completely unreasonable. No hate to Dr. Thomas but def hate towards the department

Dr. Thomas is a pretty good professor! She is a good lecturer, her tests were fairly easy, and she is genuinely such a sweet human being. The only things I would comment on are that she did not prepare us too well for the final exam, but I don't think any calc 1 teacher did. Also, sometimes she falls behind on notes. Besides that, she is great!

She is a really good professor. However, this poor rating stems from how terribly she underprepared the entire class for the final. I had a 94 going into the final and began following her guidelines 2 weeks prior. Suffice to say, I do not know anyone who scored over a 90 (after what was likely a 20+ point curve). My grade dropped from an A to a B+.

Great lecturer, class material is clear. Linear algebra is normally difficult, as is this class. The main concern comes in the exams. The exams have proof questions which are very difficult, demanding abstract thinking of newly learned concepts. Memorize definitions to death and practice how they can be used to answer different questions. Big curve

Best math professor! His lecture is pretty good, although I missed some of his class because of the first class in the morning. You can still learn it by yourself.  He explains the concept easily. I did not know how to solve a question in the final, but I still got an A grade. Many similar questions to homework and written homework. A huge curve!
Exams were really hard and didn't accurately represent material learned in the course. Be warned
It was a difficult class but I felt that I learned a great deal. Professor Rimanyi is very knowledgeable but can be slightly terse on occasion.

This dude is so knowledgeable! I should have went to his office hours (he is active on Piazza as well), but AI is getting so good at clearing up computational/theory questions. Prof. Rimanyi is great at explaining the theory (I highly recommend watching 3Blue1Brown alongside his lectures!) behind linear algebra. He's a tough love kind of guy.
It's a math class. It was my fault for the grade. Amazing professor. He's very funny and super into math. He recently graduated from UNC, so he knows a lot of the newer jokes and whatnot.

Professor Compton is a very good teacher, but the homework and tests just ruin it for me. The grading is unfair for how difficult the homework and tests are, and the difficulty did not help me learn more. You need to teach yourself all the homework and make this class a very high priority. Terrifying grader and hardest class in my life.

Go to office hours, do the homework, and ask questions. The class is very hard and requires significant study time. The first test is manageable, but the second is much more difficult. Dr. Compton is super nice and explains the material very well; just be prepared to put in the work. Im on track to get a B.

I love Dr. Compton! He was an absolutely amazing professor. His tests are hard but are always fair, if you study the materials he gives you you'll do great. Make sure you study, you cant pass this class without doing so!
This is a super hard class. other sections easier
He basically cannot teach. The class is very disorganized, and everyone in class is sleepy and distracted by sth else, doing their own things.

For math majors who are taking math 528 as a degree requirement, it is truly a waste of time to come to class, since you cannot learn anything useful immediately in class. You always need to do extra work after it. He is always trying to cover up the fact that he teaches very poorly by treating students with pleasing.

The lectures aren't very helpful, so I just went to class for participation. You're going to have to self study a lot, but the topics covered on the tests are VERY clear, he tells us exactly what kind of questions to know how to do. I basically taught myself everything with the textbook, but the class is really easy to get a good grade in.

MATH 528: This guy is nice, but sometimes he will go off on tangents talking about his research or loosely related topics.. like please just get back to the lesson. Also, that second midterm was extremely difficult since it involved proofs, computer output, and sum/difference trig identities.

Never understood any of his lectures. Rambles a lot, and his class notes are not useful for tests only the homeworks are which he doesn't teach us how to do. Has in-class worksheets so attendence is mandatory but chooses the easiest questions for them. Midterms are strange and not what we do in class.

A solid choice for 521. Final was hard but he curved it and has a generous grading scale. Soft spoken lecturer but his lectures are organized and his handwriting is really clear for a math prof. Read the textbook and prepare to study.
Very nice. Not a great professor to teach lower level math, he expects a lot from you and does not explain clearly. You'll need to learn a lot on your own.
He does not reply to email. He will tell you he will respond within 24 hours on Piazza but he doesn't. Does not submit grades on time.

Prof Assani cares about his students. His lectures make 521 like learning a new way of thinking. Go to office hours and ask for help, he wants you to succeed, so he will help. My sole complaint was the disparity in difficulty between this year's final and the material we had seen all semester/last year's final, but he does challenge you to think.
GOAT of a professor. Super awesome guy and really cares about the material and about his students. GO TO OFFICE HOURS! He will help you. Super great guy, really recommend.

His lecture is better than other professors in the department but still overcomplicated concepts. Test worth 80% of the grade but exam preparation materials from TA doesn't match the difficulty. Lagging on feedbacks of the worksheet from TA. He teaches 233 without any graphs but all written words on slides. Promised a curve but never gave.

Prof.Christianson is great and his lectures are fun. 
Previewing some content would be nice if you want to understand the proofs and some extra content (not covered in exams). However, I'd say it's really not hard to succeed in his exams, as they are quite calculation-based. Go to his office hour for some great extra knowledge=)

Not my favorite prof in the math department. His tests asked easy questions but too many of them. Lectures were of poor quality. He lowk can't explain math to save his life. He did not release final grades or curve like he said he would. Made me sad.

Homework often went ungraded for months, while curves and score cutoffs were unclear, making it difficult to know where you stood. This is common in the Math Dept, as well as 80% of your grade being determined by 3 exams. Grading was extremely harsh, and lectures consistently overcomplicated already difficult material instead of providing clarity.

Love Dr. Christianson! Beyond being a great teacher, he's also a really nice guy and super helpful during office hours. I will say that his grading criteria isn't very clear on the syllabus, and with your grade being 80% exams it's important that you do pretty well on all of them (so you don't need to rely on potential curves at the end).
Great professor (taken 2 course with him as instructor)

Professor Mitran was the best math teacher I have ever had. He lectures incredibly well, and clearly has deep knowledge and passion for linear algebra. The class is very difficult, but the extra credit opportunities are ample and if you put in the work you'll be rewarded. The concepts in the class will stick with you.

Mitran is a solid professor, but very uptight. Thinks very highly of himself for sure. He does go very in depth to the content you are learning, and tries to connect it to applications. I feel as though he would likely be better off teaching a higher level course as opposed to more introductory courses. Prepare to work hard if you want to do well.

I'm a math person. My worst grade in a math class before this was 89. The first time we got real feedback on our understanding of anything was the midterm. He showed us a chart of everyone's scores, where 70% of the class had gotten less than 6/10, and more than 1/3 got less than 2/10.
At that point it's not the students' faults.
Awful professor. don't take this class. doesn't reply to emails. extremely rude and condescending and grades really hard.
For 383. She is accessible. But you will lose a lot of points in the exam if she doesn't like the format you write. The class is highly disorganized and homeworks are graded harsh.

This is for MATH383: Just wanted to recommend people to not take this prof if you want to learn. Although grading is fairly lenient and the class is pretty easy, it's because she basically covered half of the class curriculum throughout the semester. Like 3 chapters. She isn't very organized and also makes quite a few mistakes.

I was taking 383 though. Course was very disorganized and she basically cannot finish the syllabus by the end of the semester. Midterms are graded pretty harsh. Your points always get unexpectedly deducted.
NOT A GOOD TEACHER! SOMETIMES A GOOD GRADER!
Don't be afraid to take her, she has improved as a professor and she's very nice. Lenient grader and is often understanding of circumstances.

Very smart professor, but a poor lecturer. Lectures are unclear, assume prior knowledge (even with an A in a combined linear algebra and diff eq course, I could barely follow him), and you'll end up teaching yourself everything. Physics and lin alg are hidden prereqs. The final was far harder than anything covered. Managed a B.
DO NOT TAKE! Worst lecturer i've ever had. Unclear about the syllabus and weight of assignments. Diffeq is easy material but he makes it 100x harder than it should be.

Midterms and Homework were simple and open note. Final was actually impossible and nothing like what was taught/what was on the homework or midterms. One of the hardest finals I have ever taken. I would reccoemend not taking it with Roberto, he's nice but his lectures are also so boring and make no sense, he should not be teaching.

Incredibly unprofessional. Did not provide any syllabus until a week into the semester, and when he did, it was only a schedule with his contact info. Changed the weighting of the final from 25% to 40% on the LAST day of class. Then, he made the final impossibly hard compared to the midterms (final exam avg: 62%).

The midterms weren't bad (they were the same as the practices) but the final was much harder out of no where. We weren't told what the weighting of the final would be until after the exam. Lectures were not very clear, and self-studying is very necessary. Professor Camassa is very intelligent, but it can make the class more difficult.

Notes are easy to follow, labs are mostly unrelated but easy. Her exams are quite difficult, sometimes with questions distant or only tangential to reviewed material, beware of trick questions and don't run overtime on exams, they don't account for class time well. Take MATH 347 with or before this class for some overlap. Decent curve

Katherine is nice but she isn't a very good teacher. Easy and hard content were taught at the same pace, the tests had few questions and sometimes had questions barely covered in class if at all. The labs were graded randomly with little feedback and there are daily worksheets, both of which provided little to pad your grade from the harsh tests.

Struggled a lot due to the unconventional nature of this class. She is a nice person, but never explains or teaches in a way that most understand and instead talks longer about concepts/motivations not in the tests. Labs were nonsensical and my TA had no idea what he was talking about. I'm great at Calc but this class made me feel stupid. Take 233.
pretty good, just do the hw urself and you'll be fine

Makes the content seem far more difficult than it actually is. Most labs were ChatGPT generated and none of the content on them was taught, so it was just extra unnecessary work. Often seemed unfamiliar with the slides (which she didn't make) and didn't have worksheet answers ready. Asking questions usually resulted in more confusion than clarity.

This class prepares me well for my second thesis project as a PhD student. I want to model biological questions, and the lectures give me a strong foundation. The two-week homework schedule gives me time to think on my own. Shahar is very supportive, and his office hours help me see where I get stuck and how to improve. Take this course if you can!

Could not recommend him more for MATH231. He is hilarious and genuinely cares about his students. His exams are fair, and he will actually answer your questions thoroughly if you are confused about anything. Edfinity homeworks during the week and quizzes, with 2 midterms and a final. The final can replace the lowest exam, and he gives extra credit!

He did not perform his duties as instructor at all. He never puts himself in others' shoes. When students are in great need of help, he always chooses to ignore it, which is such a blow to the students. He is indeed kind to this course, but he is definitely not an understanding and caring prof.

He always uses the excuse of having a lot of classes to teach and being very busy to not fulfill the necessary duties of instructor, such as providing support to students in need of special assistance, which he basically completely ignores. It's basically impossible to reach out to him by email. Don't want to take any classes with him anymore.
Hilarious, fun, and easily approachable.

Super caring about his students and very accessible outside of class. He would cover questions regarding the homeworks in depth during office hours. Supposed to be one of the harder undergrad maths at UNC but he made the class super interesting and showed how the techniques we learned in class are used in areas of applied math in an engaging way.👍

The grading is very harsh, with assignments often losing 10%-20% of marks and exams graded strictly. Even for answers you're confident in, expect around 80%, while uncertain responses might score only 20%-40%. Test preparation materials provided are minimal. 521 is a difficult course, Marzuola didn't make it easier, or harder.
Really good lecturer teaching a tough class; Goes above and beyond to help students understand course content. Super nice dude overall.

Dr. Marzuola is really excited and passionate about math and for me that made the lectures engaging and fun. The midterm tests were too long for the testing period and I had trouble finishing them, but you get cheat sheets for the midterms and the final and he curves final grades. Overall I loved the class and I loved Dr. Marzuola!

I found his lectures hard to understand sometimes. He definitely gets into some abstract stuff which I couldn't always follow but I understand that the reason was usually to explain where something comes from or why it's useful. His midterms were ok, though kind of hard overall. The final went really well somehow. Going to office hours helped.
Prof Rose doesnt really understand what his student want in his class. most of the time he seems taking to himself. The homework is graded through quizzes which is very unfair.

brilliant dude. Knows what he's talking about when it comes to discrete math. However, for people who have no exposure to the subject, he moves a bit too fast. If I could redo this course, I would read the textbooks prior to each lecture so that i can follow what he's saying and ask good questions. This can be a challenging class
Not a great person, hard to communicate with. He makes mistakes and students are the people who have to pay for his mistakes.
No homework solutions or test solutions but extremely helpful in office hours. He very clearly wants everyone to succeed and was super nice. Really wish I could take another class with him.

A good seminar on knot theory. Material was a little difficult to understand in the last half of the class, but Professor Rose is passionate about this topic and willing to help. No homework, and projects and tests were graded very lightly. There's a clear grading scale, and tests weren't a large part of the grade.

Professor Cherednik was a nice and funny professor. He has an extremely thick Russian accent that can take a while to understand. He is too smart to teach a class such as MATH231. Had a weird grading scale that ended up making a lot of people have their final exam as 80% of the final grade. Overall, I would recommend with someone else if possible!

He has a very heavy accent but you will get used to it. He is a funny guy and he kind of goes all over the place while teaching. In my opinion he is too smart to be teaching 231 to the point where he glosses over the importance of certain subjects.

Dr. Cherednik is a kind and caring professor. Unfortunately, he has a super thick Russian accent, so learning discrete mathematics from him is a challenge. He is very clear about what to prepare for the exams and is very understanding. The class, especially the homework, is complex, but he makes himself accessible and his notes are helpful.
It was a very difficult class but Professor Cherednik provided comprehensive preparation for each exam, which helped tremendously.

Do not take this course unless it is required. The professor has a very strong Russian accent, which makes lectures difficult to understand. On the first day, I was completely lost and could not follow what he said. However, he reviews material for the midterms except the first take-home exam. Later midterms usually have about four questions total.
don't take
gave homework of spring break and lectures are impossible to understand

I started the semester liking him but the lectures just got more and more confusing. He only teaches in theory and basically does no practice problems so you have to self study outside class. There is no study material so if you are confused it's your problem. The only good thing is how easy the first midterm was.

I reccomend taking 548 with Smirnov. Tests and Final were very easy, homework is all from the textbook. I skipped the majority of lectures and did well. Unfortunately he doesn't post lecture notes but as long as you do the homework you'll be fine.

Despite his confusing lectures and insanely tedious homework, I had a solid B+ going into the final exam. Midterms were graded leniently by the TA, turned out final was graded incredibly harshly by Smirnov with seemingly no curve. The average was a 61% and my final brought me down to a C-. Absolutely ridiculous way to conduct a class in my opinion.

521 is a brutal class but Jason makes it digestible and enjoyable. He wants each and everyone to succeed. He provides plenty of practice materials and holds review sessions before each exam. Exams are pretty difficult and are graded fairly. If you put in the work, you will do well. And take 521 with Jason.

Dr. Metcalf was an excellent professor to have for this class. However, this is an AWFUL class. Def a humbling moment for a lot of math students, everything has to be intuitive on tests which means a lot of independent work outside class I didn't have time for. He provides a lot of the guidance you need in class, but you have to implement it.

He is a great instructor. Lectures are well-designed and the homeworks are generally pretty good, though they had high variance in difficulty. I felt that expectations regarding grades were not well-communicated. I expected B based on my grades and ended up with an A, which was good, but also concerning in that I had no idea if I was doing well.
Super chill guy, tests are more challenging than other calc teachers, but he teaches well and you'll be really well prepared for the final.

Professor Metcalfe is an extremely kind professor who will answer any and all questions. The midterms are tough but they make you very prepared for the final. Would definitely take a class with him again.

He was a super nice and funny guy, and taught well. I felt like I understood most things after he explained them. Make sure to follow along with his examples, it's easy to get lost if you zone out. The midterms were super easy, but the final was way more difficult.

I was looking over all the reviews, and there is one thing people forgot to mention. He does not post the notes on canvas from the lecture. He uses a blackboard to write notes, so try to sit in the front. We have 2-3 homework assignments every week online with four tries per question, 2 very easy midterms with 5 questions each, and a common final.

Dr. Varchenko is approachable and willing to help students. His accent may take a short time to get used to but does not interfere with learning. Lectures cover most material, but some concepts on the final may not be discussed in detail, so reviewing the textbook can be helpful.

Lectures are indeed dry, though they are enlightening imo until the last few units. He does make mistakes on the board so it can be hard to follow. The midterms are easy as long as you understand larger concepts, I learned more during my recitation sessions. You have to self study for the final using other classes resources if you want to do well.

Lectures are not necessarily engaging but he gives you all of the information you need to do well on his mid terms that aren't too difficult. You may need to read the textbook to gain a deeper understanding of information for the final. He's nice and willing to explain concepts. The final is harder than his mid terms. Overall a good experience.
For example, it's EAZY.

TAKE Professor Hong for Math 233 if you can!! His exams were so straightforward, absolutely no surprises at all. However, there is weekly homework that can be quite time consuming. His accent makes him difficult to understand, but he's really sweet and will answer your questions as best as he can.

Homework seemed very sporadically graded, as students may have the same answers but different grades on certain questions. Lectures were average but sometimes it seemed like even he didn't know what he was talking about, and sometimes seemed annoyed at answering questions. Exams were tough but very fair if you know the content.

Lectures were fine if you have a strong background in calc. He sometimes makes mistakes on the board but he's open to questions during lecture so just ask. The homework was a huge pain but standardized across all profs. Tests were so easy you reasonably don't have to study more than an hour for them if you're confident on the material.

If you're decent at calc take him. Bad at explaining but his tests are by far the easiest. If you just start preparing a few days before and watch some YouTube vids you can get 100s on them. Edfinity homework is annoying, if you can do the first 5 problems in each, you will be fine. For the final just do the past tests, it was almost a replica.

I highly recommend Dr. Belkale! He is a passionate professor who loves math and appreciates hard work and dedication. Plan to read the textbook and attend lectures. I always felt welcomed in his OH and I encourage attending! His lectures follow the textbook precisely, and exam reviews give test hints! His class reminded me why I love math!

He lectured for 75 minutes straight on the chalkboard so you had to interrupt him out loud if you had a question, but he explained clearly. If you like super neat notes, he is not the guy for you. But if you can follow along with "arrow logic" you'll be fine. Midterms are EXACTLY like the practice ones he gives, but the final was departmental
Lectures were fairly straightforward. Exam questions were similar to the exam review examples. One homework per week, which was very manageable. Good choice for 548.

The syllabus is not clear: expect 90 is suffice for an A but actually only suffice for an A-. Only part of the homework is graded, so you have to go to office hours to ask him if you got the rest correct. Sometimes you don't get homeworks back before the midterm, making it hard to review. Exam questions are similar to homework questions.
No online resources, but exams are very similar to what he taught in class
took 566 with him. pretty chill, just do the hw urself and you'll be fine

Math 590 is a great course if you're interested in applied math or data science. There's a moderate amount of homework which also includes coding assignments, a group project, and a final exam (but no midterms). Boyce is a great lecturer and pretty fair when it comes to grading.

Do not listen to the hate here honestly. Lectures were fast-paced, but he posts slides. Exams were easy if you studied and there is extra credit. By far the most generous curves at UNC. Linear isn't easy, so the bad reviews come from people blaming him for that. Posts great study problems too. Take linear with him and you will get a good grade.

Skip his lectures, read the textbook a lot and do the Strang assignments and edfibity they help, DO THE EXTRA CREDIT! 

Wouldve been a 1/5 hadn't he not curved each midterm and final

Stopped going to lecture, and started reading the textbook bc 1) goes too fast, 2) spends too much time on proofs (barely tested) 3) textbook had better explanations. Tests were majority of the grade, and pretty straightforwards. Pretty generous curve too. Just do the homework, learn all the computations, and be ready for one or two proofs for an A
seemed like her exams were a little more straightforward than other professors but there were only 2 midterms and then the final. no complaints.

Midterm exams were not too difficult (in comparison to other sections), but I barely got anything out of going to class.  She would get through the simple concepts in class but then the homework was nothing like any of the problems we did.

MATH233 is hard regardless of your professor. Dr. Moosmueller made Calc 3 manageable. However, I spent hours outside of class reviewing. Midterms and the Final weren't bad but that might just be due to the amount of studying I did. Two lowest homework and recitations dropped. Attendance is optional but I recommend going to every lecture.

I found this class to be incredibly difficult and had to put in a TON of hours studying. Caroline was a great professor and very accessible outside of class. I will say that the final this year was very hard, even with all the ULA review sessions, practice exams, etc. Pretty sure the final course grade was curved by 1-2 points, but not 100% sure.

super helpful and straightforward. The midterms were similar in difficulty to the practice tests she gives us. Using outside resources, like Professor Leonard, is recommended to make some of the content easier to understand. She also drops the two lowest homeworks and recitations. I thought it was a manageable class overall.

Professor Kumar is nice and accessible through email and office hours. The class is graded based on weekly homework, two midterms, and a final. The exam questions are not hard but require some proficiency. Although the exam average is low, he curved a lot.

Many students dropped from this class, most stopped showing up after the first exam, and there were uncertain grading criteria, and I believe these facts in themselves show what kind of professor he is. Just don't.

Dr. Kumar is a great lecturer and clearly knows a lot about algebra. The midterm averages were very low I think mainly because he grades very harshly, which was my only qualm with him as a teacher. But he wanted us to learn and also curved well!
Super condescending in class and office hours, blames students for asking questions. Fails 1/3 of the class in the first midterm without improving his poor lecturing skills. AVOID!!!
JUST DON'T!!!!
Very hard class, practice exams are no where similar to the actual exams and you don;t feel prepared going into the exam.

If you ever become sick in his class, your grade will feel it, he gives pop quizzes every single week on random days and doesn't do make ups. Lectures are boring, and he expects you to know everything in the course.

Knows the material, can't teach. Basically a self-taught without recorded lecture or notes. Being in class isn't helpful either, since he mumbles and assumes knowledge of future material. Exams are 80% of class grade, but aren't too bad. Little to no practice available, only the homework and maybe a 10 question review without answers.

I do not think his lectures were boring. I found them very engaging, and he covered good example problems. The tests made up almost the entire grade but they were extremely fair if you study. It is crucial to do the homework and make sure you have all the material down. I liked Sawon a lot. I would really recommend him for math233.

sawon is so kind and this class was not too difficult, but the lectures were painstakingly boring and hard to follow. he was kind of all over the place and the most monotone lecturer. however, the two midterms were easy and the final was doable. just do the edfinity and read the textbook and you'll be fine. GREAT guy bad lecturer
This professor was an incredible lecturer and is great to talk to both in and outside of class. Take a class with him if you can.
Best math professor I've had at UNC. He explains concepts clearly and makes math feel genuinely learnable. Attending every lecture really sets you up for success.

The workload is very similar to other math classes. If 80% of grade being exams is a problem, you should probably stop taking math classes. However, the textbook sucks and lectures are tedious. Mistakes are not tolerated because on exams he uses vague wording to trick you, and if you read the question wrong, you will get 10% off. He is chill though

Overrated. The prof is a decent lecturer, but the overall experience was poor. The homeworks were extremely long, and 80% of the grade comes from exams. Also, grades below a C- are converted to Fs. Although his online real analysis lectures are good, I would recommend avoiding MATH 523 with him. Many students I spoke with had similar experiences.
It always feels like teaching this course is his second task.

he's kinda awkward but seemed to really care about the class, and would always try to re-explain tough concepts if you missed them. midterm 2 was pretty difficult (especially compared to midterm 1). i thought the final was on the harder side but if you take the time to do all his practice problems they prepare you well.

Maybe discrete is just hard to understand but Tosteson didn't really make it easier. I never understood the more difficult concepts in class when he explained them because he would kinda just write the proofs down and not explain it in a way that makes sense to someone learning for the first time. Hard final, lot of self-teaching required.
takes points off for the smallest mistakes, confusing

Really cares about the content and is a nice but awkward guy. Lectures were okay but if you didn't understand how he explained it the first time, he struggled to explain it in a new way during office hours. Midterm #2 and the final were both pretty challenging, despite putting in hours of practice and going to office hours. Not the worst, not best

Can tell he cares about teaching, but he's a bit all over the place. Would go over time every class & doesn't put any grades in Canvas. But homework is fair & good practice for midterms. Practice exams very similar to the actual one. He encourages questions and does a good job teaching a hard class. Overall, much better than most math profs.
Amazing lecturer, she explained difficult topics super well. 521 was definitely a learning curve as it was different from other math classes I'd taken before but she made it easier to adapt.
Professor Bhattacharya is fantastic! Math 521 is a very difficult class but she made it very fun and easy to learn and understand. I can't recommend her enough!

Dr. Bhattacharya was an amazing lecturer despite the abstractness of a lot of the concepts in real analysis. The subject was hard, but she was very fair and generous with grading, along with guidance outside of class during office hours or by email. Incredibly hard class, but she made it more tolerable.

I actually love her. She's genuinely so smart, and this is a hard class, so I'm aware of the challenging content. But still, her lectures were great, and she stopped for questions a lot. The first midterm was definitely not easy, but she was generous with grading and made the second one easier. She's a very responsible teacher.

The first exam average was 51%, which isn't unusual for MATH 521, but grading has been pretty unclear. We were only told a "nonuniform curve" will reduce the number of students who fail, leaving many students completely unsure how they will be affected by a curve. Lectures are generally good but often feel a bit rushed.

I took the honors version of this class, and it was extremely difficult. I cannot find a single student in class who didn't use AI on our homework, but the tests were incredibly easy... until the final, which had Fibonacci and Catalan numbers. I think Professor Weng is definitely one of the best professors for this course, but brace yourself.
Go to class and study a lot for tests and you'll be fine, his class is hard because of the tests and their weightage.

Discrete was a hard class for me, but Daping is a good teacher and very fair. Gives you a lot of homework, which might not be the most applicable. However, practice midterms echo the real midterms. The final was a bit difficult, but he did give us a curve! Just make sure you put in the time, cause it is a time-consuming class.
Loved this class. The work is hard and you really have to put the time in but Weng makes it easy to understand in class. Funny guy.
Lots of fun

Don't know why so many people give her bad comments. I enjoyed her lecture and easily got an A. If you go to the class everyday, it's easy to do well in this class. The practice exam is almost exactly same as the real exam. And there's no homework other than edifinity. I love her.
Lectures could be a bit boring but this class is SUPER easy. Def take this is you want an easy A

Lectures were disorganized, difficult to follow, and somehow felt rushed even though she was going slowly through the content. Exams were easy. If she continues to use Gilbert Strang's textbook, then I highly recommend watching his lectures on MIT OpenCourseWare (18.06SC)—he explains everything much more clearly and makes the class a breeze.

Editing this review bc it was too harsh when I first wrote it. Lectures are confusing, but you can still get an A if you work hard outside of class. Yifei wants us to do well, and I'm sure the course will improve more from when I took it (Yifei's first sem at UNC). I didn't have the best experience, but I still learned a lot abt linear algebra.

She wasn't a tough grader and was very lenient with the syllabus, dropping a midterm that she originally had counting for credit. She wants to help your grade. Lectures aren't the best but material isn't bad and it's not set up for you to fail.

I absolutely love Professor Canzani. She teaches in such intricate detail and genuinely cares about all her students. One time I did not do as well as I wanted on a midterm and she sat me in her office and went through the test with me and gave me a cookie. She is so sweet and caring and wants success for everyone. She pauses for questions as well.
Yaiza was one of my favorite professors last semester for calc 3. She was so nice and graded super fairly! Overall a great professor and person!

She truly cared about her students and wanted them to do well. She was very accessible outside of class. The material could be difficult, but with her lectures, the homework, and some outside videos you can do well in this class. The tests are all structured the same which is nice. Attendance isn't mandatory but definitely worth going.

Professor Yaiza is probably the best professor to take MATH 233 with. I took the launch version of the class, so it was a smaller class size. She was able to spend more time on certain concepts and made sure everyone fully understood what was going on. Definitely start studying for tests early!

i love yaiza!! i took the FC launch (smaller class ~30) and had such a close knit of classmates. initially it was a bit hard for me to understand her but that was only because of the fast pace of the class. we would finish a section or two of a unit a day so it was really important to put the work in outside of class. would def reccomend her.
Sweet guy, genuinely wants you to do as well as possible. Also we had 2 home exams! Would recommend, especially for a second semester senior

Greg is a hilarious professor and his jokes made lectures entertaining. His teaching style wasn't for me (he tried to cover as many examples as possible rather than explaining what he was doing). This class is extremely test heavy, but he gives decent review sheets. At the end he changed the syllabus so everyone got the highest grade possible!

Dr. Forest is very funny and an open book. I think he truly wants his students to do well. His style was often rather chaotic, and this was sometimes frustrating. He releases a practice exam before any exam that he promises looks exactly like the test, and this is true sometimes even to the question.

One of my best classes ever. Although I had taken calc in hs this was a  much harder and required studying. He made sure to explain each concept and for our final exam he went through each students grades to adjust scores to make sure we got the highest grade we could also also rounded many people. Would 100% recommend taking calc 1 with him.
Really kind proferssor and I can tell how much he wants the students to get an A in this course, would love to take again!!!

MATH231-Sped through content and you can't understand w/o learning by urself.I stopped going bc its unhelpful.He'll push due dates if asked.The practice tests are similar to the tests/final,do them.He'll say if an answer is incorrect on tests and tell you how to fix it!would take him if youre going to learn by urself and use him to do good on tests

In my opinion, he's too smart to be teaching math 231, but I highly suggest taking calc with him. The class was very difficult as he teachers very quickly, and I had to do a lot of learning on my own. But he is SUPER helpful during exams (even the final), and will straight up tell you if your answers are incorrect. A really chill guy.
Dr. McLaughlin will make sure you understand the material. Though he'll push you to your limits, he really cares about his students and is super sweet. You can tell he really loves math.

Nice guy, but awful for lower-level math. Teaches way above Calc 2 level and teaches things we don't need to know. I stopped going to class—just used Khan Academy (Calc BC) and office hours, which saved me. He records lectures. He's flexible with homework and gives hints for exams, but they can still be tricky.
