Effort = extra points.

Lectures were very confusing and often unhelpful, especially when it came to the homework and common final. Midterms tracked pretty close to study problems but did not adequately prepare me for the problems on the final. Good with partial credit, extra credit, deadlines.

McClean is a good professor, but the course is ridiculously hard. The course description did not mention the endless amounts of coding. I went to tutoring for 2 hours a week, spent upwards of 12 hours a week on homework and studying, just to get a B-. Attendance is mandatory, but it only makes up 2% of your final grade. He is super nice tho.

Professor McLean seems like a pretty nice guy, but his lectures can be pretty boring. His midterms are also super difficult and he had said the class averages were very low, but doesn't curve the grades. It's doable if you actually put in the work.

Lectures are good and the readings are very helpful if you get confused. The hw and labs are a lot of work, but they're also the only way to prepare for the exams. I wish that there were practice questions to study, but he is good at answering questions in office hours. He is a little awkward, but very nice if you make the effort to be friendly.

Very code-heavy class. Lectures focus on syntax and go through stats quickly. About 2 HW assignments a week that are graded strictly, but aren't too hard. Attendance is lenient, but the final was difficult. He makes lectures interesting, but isn't very accessible outside of class. I'd recommend it, but prior stats knowledge is really helpful.

Decent prof. Awkward to talk to but nice & willing to explain things. Barely a stats class, basically only coding outputs that most of our tests were based on. No practice beforehand but tests only weighted 30% total and 20% final, so HW/labs brought up my grade. Not sure why this class needs a lab, but decent class overall.

Ji's lectures are confusing and his homework is difficult and irrelevant to the test material (prepare to use AI). The tests are easy-ish but no one knows their grade because Ji's TA doesn't grade any homework. Literally only 10 people come to class but it's funny because probably over 50 people show up for the exams.

This professors' lectures are very sporadic and unstructured. His notes give little to no detail as to what we are learning, and he constantly go on tangents during lecture. The homeworks, while easy so far, is not representative of what we covered in class.

Cons: Lectures pointless. He goes on tangents every class and does not stay on topic. Does not respond to emails. Does not even upload grades to Canvas. Changed the entire format of the final on the last lecture of the semester, from open note/open internet to closed note written response. 
Pros: crazy grade curve at the end of the semester

Lied about the content of every exam. Said they would be multiple choice only to find out when the test is passed out that its free response. Said the final would be open note, ldoc says lol nevermind. We basically had to bully him into allowing our lowest midterm to be dropped. Not a fair professor, not a good experience.

Granted this was his second time teaching this course, it was very unorganized, grading was unclear, the midterms did not reflect what we learned, slides were repetitive, and he kept interrupting students to get his point across when they asked any question

This course is not too hard but not too easy. If you put in the work and STUDY THE HOMEWORKS you will be fine. Pataki gets hated on because he can be very direct. Personally, I think he is a great instructor that means no harm. Can be very funny too. Take this class for the BUSI math requirement.

Gabor Pataki massages your brain to think outside the box, iykyk. He cares lots about his students, and it shows during his lectures. If your bad at math this class is pretty easy, just put in some work and you will be fine. He does have an accent, but worry about his handwriting more. He's also pretty funny too, in a mathematical way.

I would definitely recommend taking this class with him. He is good at his job, and the class is not very hard. Just make sure you take notes and put in effort to study, and you will be fine. Not really sure why he has bad reviews. He can be a little blunt sometimes, but I don't think that's an actual issue or reason to not take the course.

He assigns too much homework for how little it impacts your grades. If you do not do well on one exam, you are going to have to get around 94-100% on every other midterm exam and final to end with an A in the class, and if you do bad on more than one you are basically screwed.

He's a great professor for STOR 113. He's very respectful, teaches with intent, and constantly checks in with the class to make sure everyone understands the content. He's always happy to answer questions, explain a concept more, and is very helpful outside of class.

Solid class for learning R and films lectures. Overall, he teaches well and is easy to learn content if you follow along. He is very blunt and doesnt rly care to be accommodating. DONT send him emails; he WONT respond. 25p of your grade is an assigned group project, so if u get a bad group, you're cooked.

He is very passionate about what he teaches and he teaches slowly and checks in with students during lectures to ensure they're following along. He made an 8am lecture worth going to. He is a little tough at grading but also he gives you all the proper resources to do well in the class as all his assignments come straight from his notes. Take him!!
lowk the goat. if you put in the work and effort youll be fine, he really does want his students to learn and is always willing to help.

This class and Professor Abayomi's teaching was not very intuitive for someone who doesn't have any coding/limited stats experience. It isn't necessarily extremely challenging, but I would recommend taking this class when you have a lot of time to rewatch Abayomi's lectures and dedicate to the labs. He is funny and friendly, but pretty strict

I loved this class. Dr. Abayomi makes sure no one is left behind when teaching new content. He is very nice and didn't make it feel like an 8 AM. SQL is a straightforward language and has some cool nuances. Not that much work outside of class so can't complain. Exams aren't too bad and you have a cheat sheet too.

Exams are worth 85% of your grade in this class, so be ready to study outside of class. However, Dr. Lassiter was very straight-forward in his lectures and got straight to the point. Honestly such a chill guy and is very open to giving feedback during office hours. Definitely recommend.

In his class currently and really enjoy it. Attendance is not mandatory. The tests are lengthy but doable if you study. You also get a two sided cheat sheet for each exam that you can use your ipad to make. He is very accessible and always willing to help you go through a few practice problems in office hours which has helped me a lot.

Dr. Lassiter was an engaging professor. Homework was weekly (sometimes he extended deadlines) and manageable. The exams were a bit long so it will be hard to finish in time if you get stuck, but most if not all of it is addressed in lectures or practice exams, so I would say they are fair! Although I didn't go to office hours he encourages us to.
It was a chill class, you get cheat sheets, extra credit, and some easy bonus points from watching his Mini Lectures. Some homeworks do take some time. Overall good class for stats major!

Very fair class with opportunities for extra credit. You get a cheat sheet for exams however they are somewhat difficult, particularly the final. Lassiter really knows what he is doing and takes time to ensure everyone understands.

The homework is online tablesets you can take until you get 100. The rest of your grade is midterms and the final. He is a good lecturer and wants you to understand. Go to his office hours if you are unsure of a topic. I failed to ask questions when I needed help, so do that. He is nice and caring, and sometimes funny. The content is not too hard.

Professor Banerjee is clearly very kind and passionate about the topics but this class was just difficult to follow. I attended every lecture and did well on the homework, but found the practice exams and midterms to be significantly harder. Beware midterms are multiple choice and very short - I got 3 wrong on the 1st midterm and got an 86.

The exams were just like the practice exams he gave out, and we were allowed a cheat sheet on the 2nd midterm and the final. the first final was pretty easy, but the next 2 were a little more difficult. overall, manageable and he curved slightly at the end.

Very nice and passionate professor, but lectures were unnecessarily confusing. After the 1st midterm, I stopped showing up to class and just self-taught myself everything, and this worked better than the lectures. Practice exams similar to the real exam and cheat sheets allowed. Midterm 1, 2, and Final averages are as follows: 79.5%, 77.7%, 78%.

Professor Banerjee is a really nice guy and is really smart, however, I feel like his teaching was really confusing. I found the class very easy because I took AP stats in high school, but if I didn't, I would've been completely lost. Basically your entire grade depends on the two midterms and the final so if you don't do well on those, it's over.

This class is decently difficult but Professor Fraiman is absolutely amazing. He is very kind and passionate about the subject and is always willing to dive into more detail. His lectures are helpful and his exams are very fair and quite lenient on the grading. He also scales down grades based off how the class does to make it easier for an A.

Professor Fraiman is an amazing lecturer and very kind, respectful, and understanding. His exams are very fair and based on homework. There is a good bit of homework for this class, but it is worth it as it helps solidify the content gone over in lecture.

Awesome professor. This class is not easy, however Professor Fraiman is an absolute gem and wants the students to succeed. Highly recommend anyone to take a class with Professor Fraiman especially this one.

Really hard class but really great professor. Dr. Fraiman is knowledgeable, kind, and passionate about the subject. If you go to his OH you will learn a lot more. The examples in class can be really hard and might be quite challenging to people without a strong foundation in math

Had him for STOR 120 in the FY launch section. It was easy for people with coding experience, but if you don't have any coding experience, this class has a steep learning curve. took a bit for grading to get fully developed for grading to be fair, but eventually became enjoyable to work with the coding environment. Also, made a 90 an A.

AVOID HIM AT ALL COST! He is not caring his students. I went to the office hour with my friend. He totally ignored us and stopped explaining unless we continued asking. During his lecture, the classroom is as a hell since no one can understand. His TA use chatgpt to generate the solution to hw, even though the solution is wrong.

Could've gotten a better grade if I invested some time in the course. Guanting is nice, but his second exam and final were really really difficult. After the first exam, I didn't know why people were stressing over this class but learnt why after the second exam. Study hard 445 is a difficult course.

Very very hard class. The hardest class I took at UNC. Guanting is a great guy, and I can see myself grabbing a beer with him. That being said, he was not a very helpful teacher for such a difficult class. Though, that does not by all means he is mean or rude. Ignore other comments about him being rude because he isn't. A nice guy who can't teach

Good guy but not a good teacher. If there is another option, I would go for that instead of taking it with him. STOR 445 is a very difficult class, and he just doesn't explain things enough while speeding through everything. There was only a 7 point curve despite low exam averages and him telling us that there will be a big curve.

Good guy but may not be a good lecturer. Teach yourself everything through textbook and ChatGPT, and go to office hour for questions instead of emailing him. Homework is hard but you can also use GPT and counts for 40pts. 2 midterms and a final  only count for 10+10+40 pts but still provide a 7-pts curve and A is >92.9. Therefore easy to get an A.
He always helps students to learn and improve. He gives students a lot of support and cares about students.
The GOAT in UNC STOR department. no doubt.

Love Dr. Zhang! Gives out a bunch of extra credit, open note exams, and is overall a nice guy. I really didn't have a hard time understanding him, and even if you did, his slides explain the content well. I was almost never confused in this class. If you need a 500 level STOR elective, take 556 (or anything) with him.

He is definitely very knowledgeable but his knowledge is blocked by the way he speaks. I don't think it's the accent issue but just the way he speaks. So in lectures, it's hard for students to receive knowledge from him. But overall he is a very caring and knowledgeable professor. Please take his class if you could learn from slides.

I simply really like Dr. Zhang. He's very caring, approachable and has an excellent grasp of his material. I took his STOR455 and STOR556 courses and got an A in both. If you're worried about your grades, he always offers extra credit opportunities. The only downside is his accent, which might take long, but finally still fail to get used to.

Look, 445 is an evil class. But Ziya teaches it in a way that makes it doable. His lecture notes are exactly what is done in class, and self-studying for the last half of the semester really helped my understanding (complex explanations in class had me frazzled). I ended feeling comfortable with stochastic modeling and a B+ out of a 70% final grade

Prof Ziya has done a great job of making a difficult class understandable and enjoyable. He is really good at lecturing and gives a TON of review material for the tests that is super helpful. If you go to class and do the review material you will be fine. I would 100% take this course with him!

**FOR STOR 113 - only a few homeworks, tests make up majority of your grade 3 midterms and one final. I had taken Calculus in highschool so this class was easier for me, but if you haven't before I think it would make this class a lot more challenging (exam averages were normally around a 65). Very generous curve, kind person.

STOR 113 -- Prof. Ziya gets a lot of criticism on here, which is understandable due to his sometimes confusing lectures. That being said, he's very helpful if you reach out and this class really isn't that hard material wise, although exams are often harder than class problems. Very generous final curve. DO NOT take STOR 120 with him though.

STOR 113 The exams are challenging and make up almost 75% of your grade. Averages were quite low but most people did not go to class. If you go to class and his office hours you will be fine, Ziya is very accommodating if you can't make it to his office hours. The exams aren't curved but final grades are curved significantly.
Definitely in the top 2 STEM professors I've had at UNC. STOR 435 is very hard material but he is hilarious and great at explaining the material, and there are many office hours every week.
Great class. Teaches very well. Only issue was loads of homework.

Shankar is incredible! STOR 435 is a very hard class, but he explains things so well and makes the course as straightforward and clear as possible, and the exams are very fair. Lots of homework, but they are very helpful. He is also hilarious.

Highly recommend taking him for 435! Very fair exams and grading, and he allowed us to use a cheat sheet on the final. The hws take a while, but the exams were all variations of hw or practice exam problems. He's also very accessible outside of class and explains difficult concepts in 435 well. Definitely would have done much worse if not for him.
Amazing guy. Super funny and knowledgeable. If you have to take 435 with anyone make sure it's him.

I feel bad writing this rating but...the last rating is just not true. I got an A in the class so this isn't a "bitter student rating." He's a very smart man and nice but that's where it ends. He does not know how to teach students, he doesn't give good feedback, and disorganized. And the worst part was the exams and the format...
I love his teaching style. He always helps students to learn and think.

Ridiculously hard and lengthy exams and lectures are not at all helpful as he goes too fast, is very condescending, and is unaccommodating. Has homework due during university holidays. Plus, his textbook is extremely unintuitive to use. Avoid at all costs if you want any chance at saving your grade.
Nice guy, but posts no lectures and then has homework questions that he does no examples of in class. Seems counterintuitive.

Prof. Kulkarni is clearly very smart and knows a lot about the subject, however he does not teach the info. in an effective manner at all. His lectures were very difficult to follow, you really have to teach yourself most of the information. All tests are open note, but exams also make up the vast majority of your final grade. Attendance is graded.
Very kind and funny, listens to class feedback well, and flexible. A little bit of a slow lecturer but good overall.

If you're thinking of taking STOR 120 over STOR 155, I would take 155 instead if you have little to no coding experience. Weekly labs and homeworks take about 2-3 hrs each. His lectures consist of him doing the code and you copying it down but fairly quick. He is a nice guy but overall goes quickly and lots of homework.
Professor Pipiras is a very impressive man with plenty of knowledge and accompanying wit. The class is difficult, but doable. You get out what you put in.

You had to calculate your grade yourself, which isn't ideal, but overall the professor is nice, and he helps a a lot.
This class, however. It's hard if you have little to no coding experience.  Stor 155 was better

Professor Pipiras was great. Although teaching seems very fast paced content coverage was excellent.
If you do put time in you will definitely get an A . Extremely easy tests and if the entire class is not doing good in it he reduces 0.5 points instead of 1 point for every wrong question. Very easy going grader .
Recommended for everyone.
Very Easy class. Never went to lecture. Only big grade is group project that is also very easy. Very Easy A!

Easiest class ever as long as you do the work required. Attendance isn't required and there are no exams just a final paper and project. Overall would recommend Prof Li as you can learn a ton from her without a lot of stress.
She inspires me to kill myself, takes 50% off if your are even a second late to turn things in, avoid if you can

Great professor, very clear with lots of materials. None of the assignments were too difficult, and despite having a group project where some group members left much to be desired, and our final deliverable was suboptimal, it was very easy to receive a good grade in the class. Definitely the best of all STOR 320 professors, take it with her.
Super easy class, graded off out of class homework and a final project. No one really goes to lecture as she just posts them anyways.
Difficult course, but Budhiraja was a great lecturer and was extremely helpful in office hours.

Lots of pretty hard homework, but the tests draw heavily from it. The textbook also helps with homework, and he does practice problems during class. It's a hard course, with lots of work, but he's a good teacher for it.
To all future students, if you're planning to take STOR555, get out while you still can. Find another way to get credit for this class, because this is not the way you want to do it.
Nobel's lectures are clear and very concise. I compare to other lectures from the same classes of different schools, they are basically similar. He is very nice and approachable outside class.

It was the worst class I have ever taken. Long homework and harsh graded exams with no answer keys, no practice. BEYOND THAT, his lectures were awful, and he was too stubborn to listen to student feedback. He sees “poetry” and “story” in statistics problems and acts as if students are crazy for not seeing them.

cant find a 555 option, so im putting this under 565. its laughable how bad bro is at lecturing. tests are needlessly long. only thing that saved me from failing was decent hw grades. hard agree with the other person who said this dude says you tell me to every question.

I had him for STOR 555, not 565, but there is no 555 option (those two classes are completely different btw). I genuinely don't know how people ever succeed in that class. Any time someone asks a question, he will only, and I mean ONLY, respond with "you tell me." Like, dude, WE SHOULD BE TELLING YOU THAT. YOU'RE THE TEACHER, NOT US!
Overall, I'd say she was okay. The content was genuinely hard, but she allows a cheat sheet for midterms which was nice to have.

Dr. Zhong is smart and decent in teaching a tough 500-level course. The course is fast-paced, but the same for all ML classes. She curved very hard, both on non-exam components and overall. You will learn a lot if you keep up with the contents, while the curve won't make your grade too bad after all, since hw and projects are graded leniently.
Not a very good lecturer, but she is super nice, the final project was graded insanely easily, and homework makes up 45% of your grade, which is an absurd level for a 500-level course.

Seeing a lot of negative reviews after the first midterm but she clearly realized it was too difficult and made the second midterm easier - class avg went from 65 to 80. 

a lot of homework but no final and shes a really generous grader. will give you full credit on the final project if you put a good faith effort in.

If a professor sees the class average sitting at 60, that's usually a sign to look in the mirror. A genuinely kind one would probably throw in a nice curve or tweak the course a bit—but Professor Zhong doesn't really seem to notice there's an issue.

Prof. Liu is by far the best professor to have for Stor 320. He is extremely approachable, encourages questions, and is a fair grader. When the grading TAs took off an excessive number of points for the midterm, many of us spoke to Prof. Liu, and he gave us points back. The group project is intense, though, so choose your group members wisely.

Dr. Liu is very knowledgeable in data science and has a light-hearted personality. His Jupyter notebooks were very organized. Take-home midterm, bi-weekly homework, and no final exam. The final group project was tedious and split into multiple deadlines throughout the semester, but he is a fairly lenient grader and lets you choose your groupmates.

He was a fine teacher, but one thing to be aware of and why he is not getting a higher rating. He assigned 33 assignments in 15 weeks and was horrible about grading. Grades for this class are due in 4 days and I still do not have a grade back for an assignment that was due in Oct. Giving us this much work and then not grading it is unacceptable.

Professor Liu genuinely cares about his students and challenges them in a way they can grow. Exams weigh significantly, so doing poorly on one exam can affect your whole grade, but Professor Liu is very willing to help you in ways he can. Lectures are very important in understanding the materials, and the course itself is quite fast-paced.

Second midterm was rough (class average ~50 %), but Prof. Mo curved hard so the final grade turned out fine. The Python labs add extra work compared to STOR 435. Attendance isn't required, but his lectures really make everything click.

STOR 555 was the hardest class I've taken at UNC, but Jan was overall really good. The HW was brutal and lectures can be hard to follow, but Jan is really friendly and will help. Just make sure you go to office hours early, you're probably going to need it.

Jan is great person and professor. That being said stor 555 in my opinion is the most difficult undergraduate course in the department. HW were difficult but they did teach you how to do the course materials just be prepared to spend a few hours on them each week. Tests were much simpler. Material can be hard but Jan tries to makes class fun
The HW was very tough, but tests were made easier. He is very smart, so sometimes his lectures can get a little in the weeds with the math. Overall, he was a great prof.
Alright prof, a little boring and terrible at algebra. Exams were very easy, homeworks could be tough.

He is one of the best in STOR department. He explain everything well and really care students. His practice match with exam and grade easy. He is also approachable in OH, unlike those who does not care students. Overall, TAKE THIS CLASS.

I throughly enjoyed the lectures but do wish he spent more time on releasing material more than around 30 minutes ahead of class time. Myself, and I'm sure other students, prefer to work through lecture content ahead of time to be prepared with questions for class time. Aside from this, I enjoyed his lecture style and the homeworks hard but doable

Professor O'Neill is an easy going person. His lectures are decent and provide you the knowledge you need to do well on the exams. He is also very open to all sorts of questions during lecture, after lecture, and during office hours. As long as you take advantage of all of that, you will be in great shape for this course.

O'Neill is alright. Not a great lecturer, but a pretty nice guy with fairly reasonable expectations for the course. Easy exams, weekly HW. Wouldn't recommend going to class outside of exams; the lecture notes (the provided notes compiled by other profs) are a far better resource. You'll be fine, this is generally a pretty common sense course.

VERY hard but rewarding class. Long homeworks (10ish hours). HARD final exam - but curved. Lectures are great. Group project similar to the one in 320. Professor is reachable and knowledgable. If you are serious about a career in data science/ML this is your class. Clear grading and curves grades. Overall great choice definitely recommend.

HARD class. He moved up the final, so I started to prepare for it around the late of October. If your project schedule happens to be near around this time, everything could be really challenging for you. The homework is much harder than the quiz and exam. The professor is very nice, easy to talk, very accessible, and a very useful class!

Great lectures! I really learned a lot by just attending lectures, and you may have multiple uh-ha! times during his lecture. Prof Liu is very knowledgeable and very easy to access during office hours. He would adjust course material and exams by students' feedbacks (though I think there's no need to change). Overall a great course with Prof Liu!
Great lectures though a bit notation heavy at times. I enjoyed his lengthy but interesting homeworks but do think the level of difficulty on the exams was unnecessarily complex in comparison.
Best STOR prof I have had. Explains things very clearly. HW was a little tough, but glancing at the textbook and going to lectures make it doable. Very useful class.
Took him for 323 and 565, free A.

The professor is very supportive and approachable. He is easy to reach outside class, office hours are helpful, and he patiently clarifies any questions.The course is challenging, but he makes it manageable. Homework is flexible and helps learning, and midterm grading is lenient.Overall, a great instructor and you will learn a lot. Highly recommend

Had for STOR 565. Homeworks can be challenging and are assigned monthly  but graded very lenient. Tests much easier than homework and also graded lenient. Decent lecturer but very inaccessible outside of class.

Would not recommend! Very disorganized and takes forever to grade. Her exams were way too long for the testing period, and almost the entire class did not finish. Online homework helps your grade. She responds to emails quickly. Uses poll ev for attendance.
She takes forever to grade and is so disorganized. Definitely do not recommend. The midterms and final were nothing like the practice exams provided in canvas.
The rumors are true. Avoid at all costs.

This is the worst lecturing teacher I have ever had, classes genuinely put me to sleep and make the subject of chemistry feel like you're being forced to eat sand. His hand writing is illegible and comparable to a second graders.
I took the final for this class approximately two years ago and it still brings tears to my eyes when I think about it

Do not underestimate the rumors that Todd is trying to make the UNC chem department one of the most difficult in the country. He's a nice guy, but lectures are boring, exams are more difficult than he really prepares you for, and he seems to enjoy your suffering a little bit.

Funny and kind person, plus Todd's a great lecturer for Orgo. He teaches the material very cohesively and ensures you're well-prepared for exams. In class, I noticed people in his section often did better than those in other sections. In Todd we trust!

Professor Bliem was genuinely amazing in a small class setting. She was funny and kind, making a connection to everyone. She is like a sweet grandma who wants you to do your best in chem. Although her tests are difficult, they never felt unfair and there was extra credit and a grade replacement built into the syllabus to offset this.
Very good professor. She also wrote the textbook so she knows what she's talking about. Exams were also more fair in my opinion compared to other professors.

The UNC chem department in general isn't awesome, but Dr. Bliem is a great bet for gen chem. She is kind, good at explaining concepts, and her handwriting is legible. Definitely study in advance for exams (the first one sneaks up on you), but don't blame Bliem for difficult exams - that's just 102.

She is 
Exceptional teaching and I really wonderful
Professor Bliem is the best professor I ever had.

Dr. Beaver is a great teacher during lecture and really breaks down the material in an enthusiastic way. That being said, his exams are very difficult and contain a lot of niche material. I gave him a 3 because he is quite rude and condescending to students who asked him questions on 1 to 1.

I loved Beaver for orgo 2, genuinely wants his students to succeed and get the most out of his class. Orgo 2 is a hard subject theres no workaround, but if you put in the effort it is very doable. Listen to his suggestions on how to study. Attend GPSS and do the ULA worksheets. Sit in the front, ask and answer questions in class.

man he's a good prof but unless you're confident you know the material he's not worth it. his exams are the hardest out of the entire department and the stress is unreal. i got out with an A but then my physical health genuinely declined due to the stress solely from that class. unless you want your entire life to be chem, dont take him.

Beaver is a great guy and probably knows the course content he's teaching better than anyone. Yes the midterms are very challenging but the final is much more fair in my opinion. If you got through orgo 1 with a decent grade, you know what to expect with Beaver. Study hard for the midterms and practice practice practice and you will succeed.

Dr. Beaver is one of my favorite chemistry professors at UNC. He genuinely cares, will listen to student feedback, and is amazing at breaking down difficult concepts. His GPSS is super helpful and his class is very engaging. I highly recommend taking 262 with him.

Dr. Beaver is a great teacher during lecture and really breaks down the material in an enthusiastic way. That being said, his exams are very difficult and contain a lot of niche material. I gave him a 3 because he is quite rude and condescending to students who asked him questions on 1 to 1.

I loved Beaver for orgo 2, genuinely wants his students to succeed and get the most out of his class. Orgo 2 is a hard subject theres no workaround, but if you put in the effort it is very doable. Listen to his suggestions on how to study. Attend GPSS and do the ULA worksheets. Sit in the front, ask and answer questions in class.

man he's a good prof but unless you're confident you know the material he's not worth it. his exams are the hardest out of the entire department and the stress is unreal. i got out with an A but then my physical health genuinely declined due to the stress solely from that class. unless you want your entire life to be chem, dont take him.

Beaver is a great guy and probably knows the course content he's teaching better than anyone. Yes the midterms are very challenging but the final is much more fair in my opinion. If you got through orgo 1 with a decent grade, you know what to expect with Beaver. Study hard for the midterms and practice practice practice and you will succeed.

Dr. Beaver is one of my favorite chemistry professors at UNC. He genuinely cares, will listen to student feedback, and is amazing at breaking down difficult concepts. His GPSS is super helpful and his class is very engaging. I highly recommend taking 262 with him.

The only positive thing I have to say is that she was generous with curves & EC. However, she is very petty, extremely hard to follow during lectures, creates ridiculously long exams, and gives very few practice problems that I did not think looked anything like the exam. I only did well because I am comfortable with stats and put in a ton of time.
Erin Baker graded almost solely based on midterm exam scores, and didn't assign much homework to practice lecture material.

She did change the grading scheme because of issues with AI which was unfortunate, but although the exams became weighted higher, I have never seen a professor give such generous curves on exams as her (even if average = 70s). She does problems slowly and in detail, and super open to questions. Exams are long but every 241 professor has long exams

very poor lecturer. was extremely difficult to follow along - expect to spend tons of time outside of class teaching yourself the content. 

tests weren't difficult but very long, so there was never enough time to check over work. 

but, if you are math-minded, you will find this class easier than other 241 sections because of its focus on stats.

Makes ARS absence accommodated students do a problem you didn't learn how to do (only blank slides provided) and submit it by 5pm the same day for credit. Admitted to people outside of the class that she made exams extra difficult on purpose after the AI issue. Punished class for some people's wrongs to be "fair." Taught unnecessary extra stats.

I took the honors section of chem430 and loved it. We did a capstone project and got to present our work at the Undergraduate Research Symposium. I was able to learn new how to work with data and even apply some of what I did to my own research! It was hard at first but I learned a lot about myself and team science. This was my fav class at UNC.

Dr. Brunk is kind and clearly cares about her students. She is very busy, as most research faculty are but she answers questions when you bring them to lecture. The flipped classroom can be an adjustment, but it reflects med school style learning. Biochem is challenging (DUH). Homework load is heavy-it helps and can be a grade saver for exams

This class was really hard but really good. It changed my view of medicine and it changed what I want to do with my life. I love the real world applications and learning how to work with data. I loved the lectures on precision medicine and things that are really happening in science.

Took Brunk because Hogan's section was full - worst mistake ever. My friends in Hogan's section had a walk in the park compared to what Brunk put our class through. Prone to mistakes in videos/lecture, very confusing explanations when asked for clarification (which I had to ask at office hours bc she never responded to my emails!), tricky exams.

She is rarely available and actively makes it harder for you to find help. She doesn't let you email her so you can only get help from the TAs. I had to have my final exam rescheduled and she refused to help me out. Keep in mind it was an EXTREMELY easy fix. Don't expect any notes or videos to be posted, and her modules don't match with the exams.

Dr. Alexanian is a sarcastic funny guy, teaching style (talking at us) was ok but the pace is fast and lots of review for nuances in reaction mechanisms is required to understand what was going on. He's doing his best but he is def a research prof.

So far Dr Alexanian is one of my favorite professors at UNC. He was slow during his lectures, but it did not hurt me. However, during QA sessions and office hours he was extremely nice. His exams are so much harder than Dr Eskew's; however, questions he asked were fair.

Would ramble in class about his lab -- unclear on what we had to know as fact vs fluff? The midterm he made was ?? (average was a 63), and our class's grades were lower than Eskew's, but no curve. Final was also not easy. Condescending to questions in class. Interesting guy though, with a dry sense of humor. Allegedly gatekept things at his Q&As.

Erik is maybe the most mid CHEM professor at UNC. He's pretty nice and funny during class, but if you like a fast paced class, he moves EXTREMELY slow. His midterms were not too bad, the second one was rough but the other two and the final were really easy. He was very dismissive of people that asked stupid questions, but I liked him overall

This class has pretty fair midterms. It goes pretty well for me until the final, which was insanely hard. Erik is nice and understanding, but just be ware of the orgo final, which was the hardest chem exam I have ever taken at UNC.

The classroom is purely flipped. Class sessions serve as problem solving sessions rather than instructional time. Office hours are useful for difficult course content. Exams are difficult but fair. Sizable (~5%) curve added to final grades.

P Chem 1 is going to be hard, but Professor Cahoon has designed this class to be as effective as possible. It's a flipped classroom, which is kind of annoying, but it's much better than the alternative, since you can pause and rewind the lecture videos. He offers plenty of office hours if you need extra help, and his explanations are always good.

Dr. Garland is clearly knowledgeable about biology, but is the most unorganized professor I've taken a class with. Her exams are majority select all that apply with no partial credit and she is difficult to reach outside of class.

Dr. Garland is very approachable and makes it easy to ask questions. She genuinely cares about her students as people, not just grades, and takes time to explain concepts clearly. Her exams are challenging, but they push you to really learn the material. Overall, a great professor who wants her students to succeed.

Dr. Garland is very kind and accommodating, especially when I had to be out of town. Lectures move quickly and cover a lot of material, so I rewatched lectures at home. Exams have many multiple select with no partial credit. Class is interesting, but be ready to look over bio 103 if you took it over a semester ago. Reviewing everyday helps.

I took this class first semester freshman year and didn't do as well as I wanted to. However, Dr. Garland is a very intelligent professor and I thoroughly enjoyed her class. Her midterms are designed to trip you up, but if you memorize the learning objectives (aka the study guides) you WILL do well in this class. Be prepared for busy work though.

I took this course as a freshman. I was a bit nervous bc the last bio class I had taken was 10th grade (AP Bio). Honestly a chill class and a good transition to unc! She's caring and always willing to explain a concept in office hours. Last 2 units are a bit rushed, but lock in (esp on first 2 units), and an A is very manageable.

The class was definitely challenging, but Dr. Ott provided resources to help. Between GRQs, office hours, and study tips in class, there are resources available. Yes, the class is hard and takes a significant amount of time, but I learned a lot. Lectures could be long.
loved her for 103, absolutely hated her for 252. take anatomy with another professor. she skipped a lesson, and her exams are very tricky. she has had to drop and clarify a few questions.

By far, the worst professor, ESPECIALLY for anatomy. I've never hated a professor this much. She teaches cell bio and honestly should stay in that lane. Her wording on exams makes not only the questions, but also the answers, tricky to select, even when I knew the material. She skipped a whole lesson as well. Take an anatomy with someone different.

I was not a fan of Dr. Ott in the beginning, but she for sure grew on me and I started looking forward to learning from her each week. Unit 1 was the toughest for me (I got a D on the exam) but I stuck it out and became super ultra locked in and by unit 3 exam, I made a high B. Dr. Ott gets unnecessary hate, yes the class is hard, but she does well

This class was more challenging compared to other 252 sections. The exams were sometimes difficult because some answer choices felt tricky and could be confusing, even when I understood the material. I put in a lot of effort, and it was frustrating to feel like I was being tripped up by wording rather than tested on my understanding.

All the work Dr. Hastie gives out is very manageable but I wouldn't say it really helps much with the concepts in class or the exams. He's a fun guy and very approachable but I felt like attending lectures didn't do much for me. I found that I learned better at home when I taught the content to myself.

I would've rated him highly. But during the final, he wrongly accused me of cheating in front of a large lecture, refused to check my computer when I offered, and had a TA monitor me closely. It was extremely stressful and hurt my performance (A to B+). Afterwards, he admitted he was definitely mistaken and laughed in my face. Be aware!

Dr. Hastie is super nice and engaging, class content is just difficult itself so I don't really think the class is difficult because of him.  3 exams (1 dropped), GRQs before each class, and final exam constitute your grade.  Classes are recorded and attendance not mandatory but encouraged.

Oh Hastie, what can I say about you? He's a good guy and definitely sociable, but I'm not too sure about his expertise in Anatomy. Lectures don't help on the exams, but attendance isn't mandatory so you'd be better off teaching yourself via the textbook. The way he writes questions is also confusing and sometimes there can be two correct answers.

BIOL 105L is a pretty pointless lab/lecture. It's fairly easy to make an A, just put effort into the final project and don't slack off. That being said there is way more work for this class than there needs to be, and it's mostly busy work. It's supposed to teach you about research, but a majority of the semester you're just wasting time.

Dr. Z has interesting lectures, and as long as you show up, take good notes, and rewatch lectures when needed, the exams are doable, although they are hard. Just learn most everything that she says in lectures! The hate she receives is over exaggerated, she is great if you truly listen and try. The outside participation is annoying though.

This class is definitely hard, but if you pay attention in lecture and take good notes, you will be fine. You definitely have to learn most everything she says, including little details, but it is doable. Her lectures are interesting. The outside participation requirement is annoying though.

I love Dr. Z as a lecturer, and she is very accessible/kind. However, we have had so many asynchronous lessons. For the first exam, we had 2/6 in person (which was not really her fault because of snow), but for the third exam, we will have only had 1.5/6 lectures in person. If you learn well this way, then take her, but if not just be aware.
Absolutely love her. Her lectures are easy to understand, she makes it clear what she expects you to know, and her exams are very fair. Highly recommend.

don't take this class with this professor. she picks favorites, won't answer your question if she thinks it's not good enough, and does a poor job at teaching the content to the extent we're tested on. she expects students to spend 10+ hours outside of class time to watch videos because she didnt get through content quick enough in class.

Professor was extremely kind. Class just consists of a lot of content but keeping up with assignments, rereading the notes and just asking questions, you will do fine. He is really nice and a great professor.

Dr. Claytor is for sure a cool guy, but holy moly was biol 104 way harder than anticipated. I felt like I would understand the material, participate in class, study hard, etc, but I made three straight Cs on the exams. Exams are on Gradescope, and the wording is just like ????????
Dr. Claytor is great! He's just a chill guy. Beware if you're hard of hearing though because he does mumble and can be hard to hear.
Dr. Claytor is awesome! BIOL104 isn't a hard class but I think a lot of people also underestimate it. When you're studying for the exams focus on the slides and don't worry about the GRQs.

Jordan Claytor is the GOAT! He is so nice and office hours are super helpful. Sometimes the grqs didnt align but I'd still recommend them. Tests werent too bad, pretty straightforward with a 1-2 difficult questions. Highly recommend!

Lacking professionalism and disorganized. Little respect for your time, and will post assignments at 11 pm, even though they are due at noon the next day. Little to no time-management skills, which will put you in a bad position heading into finals.
this class is making chem 101 feel like a cake walk right now. its genuinely so upsetting. thats it. dont do this unless you want to feel constantly defeated.

Dr. Rea was disorganized and often late in posting assignments and other announcements, but her exams were very fair and aligned with her lectures, which are always posted. The assignments were straightforward with lots of grace points. Also, a very sweet person and wants you to do well if you schedule meetings with her and show up to reviews.

She is such a sweet lady, and she is brilliant. However, teaching is simply not her calling. She spends more time off topic than on in lectures, doesn't give proper (if any) prep materials for exams (which are in total, 73% of our grade), and currently is 5 lessons behind all other classes. Avoid if possible, but don't be too hard on her. She tries

Nice and passionate about biology, but extremely disorganized. Short notice on assignments and posted assignments after ldoc. Went on tangents during lecture and got behind on the material. Exams were full of select all that apply with no partial credit, but she did offer some extra credit on exams. Would NEVER recommend, but not end of the world.

Haven't finished the class yet but Dr. Johnson is by far my favorite Biology professor that I have had so far. He is really funny and very willing to help you if you are having trouble understanding a concept. Explains content clearly. Responds to email way faster than the average STEM professor. Class isn't easy but is doable with some studying.

This class can be very difficult if you don't make it a point to study the Anki in between every class session. If you do use the Anki and go to open labs, the exams aren't too difficult but are definitely still challenging. There is a lot to memorize in this class, but it is definitely doable!

Dr Johnson is really caring and very knowledgeable. He is easy to reach outside of class and is super accommodating to all of his students. His exams aren't super difficult if you go to class and study the material, as well as the only work outside of exams is a Mastering due before each class period. Would highly recommend!
I absolutely loved Dr. Johnson; he was a great teacher and had very fair exams. Would definitely take again.

Should not be a 1 credit class. It's hard but not impossible. DO THE ANKI and practice provided and you'll be pretty prepared. Is all memorization with some application -- the application depends on what you memorize. Review sessions, open labs, and LAs are excellent resources. Hope for a good TA they can make or break the class and your graded

Dr. Burch is just confused about a lot of content within the course. We ask her questions to which she says "idk." Her lectures are very dull and you never really walk away with any insight on what will be tested. Good luck!

This woman was so passive-aggressive it was ridiculous. Half the time she couldn't answer student questions. She was very contradicting with her words and overall made the course dreadful. I can't imagine what her first course teaching at UNC was like because the lectures were terrible! Slide shows had pictures only with no explanations.

I have never met a professor who is so unknowledgeable about what she was teaching and also somehow is beyond rude to her students. any time a student asked her a question she would say I don't know. Not only did she not know what she was talking about a house plant would have been more entertaining to look at for an hour than her

Dr. Burch is a fair professor and makes a difficult class a bit easier than it would be with other profs. I think Dr. Burch gets too much hate for the job she did in her classes, she keeps things organized and digestible for students, I think she's' a solid professor.

Do not take this class unless you have no other choice! Her lectures were confusing/made no sense; she would often berate and yell at students and would publicly announce students' poor performances in front of the class. She said very inappropriate things and would grade based on her assumptions about you and not on your work itself. 
USE CAUTION!

Communication was often condescending and arbitrary rather than constructive. She was quick to anger and often spoke disrespectfully about others. The subject matter was interesting, but the interpersonal side made the experience unnecessarily uncomfortable.

only here bc i saw this class recommended by Gidi again and had to say something. incredibly racist, vague rubric, deaf and too decrepit to be teaching. Will LIE if you upset her. If she's sick, prepare to use unc technology without any sort of crash course, and have her get mad at you if you can't get it. WOULD NEVER RECOMMEND EVER TO ANYONE EVER

Dr. Matthysse is very knowledgeable and inspirational, but assignments are very vague and graded harshly with no rubric. Don't take this class if you're not confident in your humanities abilities, or if you might regularly be late to class. Staying on her good side is difficult but does make the class more enjoyable. In no way is this an easy A.

Avoid this class, highly unorganized especially the syllabus and assignments. Attendance is mandatory but it makes it hard when the class is so unorganized and boring. I spent 80% of the semester not knowing the purpose of taking this class. Luckily, I took this class for fun. I had 0 fun though.

Dr. Slep is one of the most boring lecturers ever. This class is so terrible and I would not make my worst enemy take it. Tests are extremely difficult and if you think they get better after the first exam you are telling yourself one huge lie. DO NOT TAKE.

Took w/ Slep + Roger, regret it, only option that sem, lectures useless, doesn't show up to his own office hours, even TA didn't know how to help, exams 80%, expected to have orgo and biochem background, flop on one test? no chance for grade to recover. DO NOT TAKE

Do not take Biol 240 with Slep & Rogers. Exams are worth 80% (4 exams- 20% each) and quizzes are worth 20%. Asked him how he felt I could succeed in the class and he dismissed me and said to look at the recommendations sheet online (which was not helpful). Exams are not like the practice exams. Focuses on things in class that are not on the exams.

Dr. Slep (paired with Dr. Stephen Rogers) are the two best professors I've had at UNC. Both professors are so knowledgeable in their disciplines and clearly care about students having positive experiences in the biology department. If you have a chance to take 447L with Dr. Slep and Rogers, do it, it is one of my favorite courses I have EVER taken.

Whoever wrote "How to study: I do not know" is correct. Practice exams were nothing like actual ones. Lectures were understandable, but the material came only from the slides (and in speaker notes) even the info was not deep enough. I thought I'd know the material but always score Cs. The TA could not answer anyone's questions during review either.

Professor Matute is not a proficient lecturer and is quite disrespectful. I did very well in this class, with 100's on both midterms and an A+ on the exam. I am pretty well vetted in the topics of this course, and I would never take Professor Matute again. He is very unenjoyable. The class is great, though, and I would recommend it, just beware.

His lecture slides do not include anything except pictures so ensure that you do not miss his lectures. He goes into a lot of depth and has you read a lot of biology jargon filled articles just to ask questions like 'what is population genetics?'. So definitely pay attention when he lectures. Be sure to get general ideas and you'll be fine!

Oh hell nah. When he taught his part in IDST 113, I was so confused, and his slides are basically pictures and graphs, which did not help anyone study for exams. Pier and Terry were chill and gave insight on exams and what they covered, but Matute did NOT. He was super strict during test-taking and was sort of rude.

In IDST 113, his slides made no sense and his exam questions were hard for no reason. He was also extremely rude to students on exam day and talked to them like they were kids. When the two other professors attempted to prep the class for the final, he gave no assistance on the bio section, which is always the hardest part. Would not take him again

Matute was kind but I wouldn't recommend for IDST113 unless prepared for long readings, confusing graphs, and random lectures. Some of it was fun and If you pay attention and do the readings it's not hard to get a good grade plus he does post the lecture slides (but they don't contain much information so they honestly aren't helpful for studying).

Take home exams- as long as you go to office hours, he will help you get an A on all of them. As long as you participate in class every so often, you will do fine. Definitely a bias grader, helps if he likes you and you seem interested. Going to office hours is enough to get an A in the class.
Absolutely horrible. Worst class I've taken at UNC
Super smart professor and challenges the way you should think and approach problems. Take home exams were tricky, but going to office hours helps.
Very smart researcher. Absolutely horrible professor. Doesn't give feedback. Doesn't teach anything. DO NOT TAKE. YOU WILL REGRET. DO NOT TAKE. YOU WILL REGRET.
Do not take this class with this professor, whatever you do do not take this class. Exams are hard and are overly graded. This class is worse than Orgo.

I am unsure why Dr. Sockman has bad ratings- he is literally one of my favorite professors in the biology department. Did he go on a lot of tangents? Yes. Were they hilarious? Yes. The class material is super interesting and the quizzes are easy they're literally online. Don't get the textbook, just write down everything he says. Curve is HUGE.

Took Avian Bio in Maymester. Dr. Sockman is passionate and wants active participation (heavily weighted). Tests are lecture-based with a curve, but he grades tough. Slides are vague, so note-taking is key. Textbook not needed. Loved his experimental design teaching style. Class is a bit unorganized but very engaging.
His exams are really hard but he curves. However I enjoyed his class a lot mainly because I enjoyed the content he taught and his awkward jokes are funny.

He makes lectures worth going to, he is really funny and makes the subject fun. You really should take notes during lectures bc thats what the tests are based on. But also the book helps alot, he also gives videos on canvas covering stuff from that lecture. The tests are curved huge! all open ended questions and memory based. Quizzes r quick n easy

The quizzes are very easy, but they are quick. Its really just finding correct phylogeny trees with the groups names he gives you. If you look at the book the night before and draw the tree out it will be the easiest thing you have ever done.
Funny guy and makes lectures so interesting.
The best bio class ever taken.
Tests were all memory based

Love Dr. Willet, he is sooo incredibly kind and willing to work with you! However, grades are extremely test-heavy, and the tests are worded vaguely. Boring lectures :(. Class was harder than it had to be, and exams didn't reflect lessons. Beware: he input grades the day of the final exam for me (B to C+ in one hour).

Lectures make it very difficult to not only learn the material, but also to stay interested in the course and go to class. Cheat sheets were allowed on exams however exam questions were worded very vaguely making it not what you know but more of a test on how good you can interpret what he is attempting to convey/ask. Take 260/240 instead.

He was a sweet professor, but could not teach. The content itself is super boring, and the lectures did not help. Exams were difficult, but could use cheat sheets. Did not enjoy the class. Honestly, wouldn't recommend this class.

Willett is a good guy but unc bio is just a pain. you miss two questions on exams and youre already at a 90. Records all his lectures and always available on email to answer question. Exams are more like reading comprehension tests. Cheat sheets allowed

I literally wanted to be an evolutionary biologist but Willett was just sooo boring and not engaging that I felt turned away from the field. his lectures are about random studies and i never understood the purpose of his tangents. i literally couldnt take notes bc he was all over the place so i stopped attending class. snooze fest!

Dr. Goldstein is one of the best professors I've had. His exams are almost all lecture content, but he explains it well if you pay attention/ take notes. Grades are 100% exams, but the median for our 1st exam was a 98. He truly cares and makes class fun. If he is co-teaching with Dr. Maddox, keep your grades as high as possible before her section.

Dr. Goldstein is probably my favorite professor I've had in UNC biology. His lectures are engaging and interesting if you're really there because you want to learn cell bio, and his tests are fair (if you take the practice exam and attend the review session, you'll do well.) It's a shame that he co-teaches the course with Dr. Maddox (AVOID HER)

He really cared about students and had several opportunities for students to ask questions and feel prepared. His practice exams are almost identical to the actual exams and he lectures in a way that feels like a story and makes things easy to retain. Got hundreds on both his exams!

Dr. Goldstein cares a lot about his students, evident by the time he puts in for the class. You must go to class to get a good grade, and grades are only based on tests, but his tests are very fair. Dr. Goldstein is a very good lecturer which makes the information much easier to retain than in some other bio classes.

Bob truly cared about his students. He included interactive days to keep us engaged and held VERY helpful review sessions in preparation for exams. He tells funny jokes and interesting stories during lecture. He was helpful during office hours and is overall a great person. If you have the opportunity to take this class with him do not hesitate.
I will return in Avengers: Doomsday

This class almost took me out. It was the worst class I have ever taken and lectures did nothing for me. The tests were awfully worded and made to be confusing. The TA does not even really understand most subjects and it is extremely difficult to learn anything. If you have any dignity or self value you will not take this.

Dr. Rogers seems nice but he cannot get a sentence out to save his life. Class is purely lecture-based and is a non-stop snooze fest. Exams are weirdly worded and get progressively harder. They also act as though it is their first time working with canvas (or ever functioning in modern society). Take it with anyone else if you want peace.

Class was 80% exams, and Dr. Rogers just read off the slides. Difficult exams with convoluted questions. He was nice, but his teaching was not interactive and most of his lectures were very dense. Avoid this class if you can.

Dr Rogers is one of the most intellectual professors here. Loved taking this class with him, he really knows his material and his passion shows when he teaches. Spent just a bit during office hours with him during finals season and you can tell how much he cares about his students- he's willing to go way above and beyond. Take the class with him!!
Dr. Kieber is a legend and signal transduction was the most useful course I have taken in this university.

He is a great person, but the lectures were way too fast and contained little information related to exams. It was very hard to study using materials given. Class was extremely challenging and the grade was completely from exams. Hard to succeed when there is little outreach/homework/class materials. Sweet guy just not the best teacher.

Class is 90% exams. He moves way too fast, and his powerpoints have basically nothing but diagrams on them. He asked very specific questions on exams, which were very tricky and confusing. In office hours, he was condescending at times. Regretted taking him the whole semester.

this class was really, really hard and ive heard from grad students that we were being taught at an above-mcat level. with that said, professor kieber was one of the nicest and most understanding stem professors ive had at unc. he knew that it was an honors class and made it hard because of that, but he also curved the grade up to reflect that.

Idk why his rating is so high tbh. Lectures are scattered and moves too fast so its hard to take notes on everything you need to know. Tests are too long and written to confuse you, with answer choices like "all of the above" and "two of the above" tagged on the end of mcqs, SAQs, that he grades like you broke into his home and stole his valuables.
Great professor! Take him!

My favorite professor so far at UNC. His exam was so easy and was straight from the study guide. His lectures were interesting and he sometimes brought his dog to class. This course is taught with two other professors and he by far was the easiest one.

This is one of the best professor's I have ever had, he really cares about his students and is very passionate and knowledgeable about this course and topics. Definitely take this class, very easy and super interesting.

One of the best professor ever met! He really supports and wants student to learn how to do research and understand sustainability related topics. Grade is not a concern for this class (he's grading fairly and  leniently) but understanding science & research is the goal.

Dr. Bruno was an amazing lecturer, and super kind! The questions in our quizzes from his lecturers were often difficult, so make sure you take detailed notes on some of the exact numbers discussed in class. He has a really cute dog he brought to class as well! Super cool dude!

A knowledgeable and approachable professor who pushes you when she knows you're capable, which I value. She truly wants the best for her students and treats them accordingly. Beyond being an excellent professor, she's a remarkable woman you can learn a great deal from.

I took Dr. Gordon over the summer abroad. She is such a wonderful, knowledgeable and caring professor and I can't recommend her enough. The class was a series of paper presentations by the students, and sometimes she would lecture afterwards more in-depth about the topic. Every paper was fascinating and the workload very reasonable.

This course was a refreshing break from 200-level classes. Dr. Gordon is understanding and a fair grader. The class focuses on stem cell papers, with online, open-note tests that are straightforward if you keep up. Be ready to present, as one group presents each week. Also participation is 30% of your grade.

Dr. Gordon is one of the smartest, most kind professors I've had here. She genuinely cares for her students' well-being. Her lectures are amazing because she explains tough concepts in ways that are easy to comprehend and comes up with great analogies. Her exams are challenging, but they are fair and she is generous with partial credit.

Gordon is amazing and so enthusiastic about what she's teaching. This class is definitely challenging, but she is very pleasant to work with and will take time to explain difficult concepts if you ask. The tests were all on the long scientific papers and could be quite difficult. Overall, I would recommend Gordon!

You'll get out of this class what you put in, so engaging in discussions, asking questions, and keeping up with the work will result in a comprehensive understanding of the subject matter. Dr. Burmeister is very fair and interesting to listen to. She asks challenging questions in class, but it's all to help you learn. Favorite class in my 4 years!

I am literally crying as I study for my midterm a couple of hours from now I have not slept. Amazing class, great content, great professor, REALLY challenging. dont take if youre juggling other stem classes, I might fail..

Her teaching style is very rough, especially for it to be covering a first year seminar. She is very vague in her lectures and the tests are very difficult. The lecture slides only cover the rough aspect of what you will see on your tests. She's also very difficult to communicate with, even came off as quite rude when communicating through email.

For a first-year seminar, this class was extremely difficult. She is unclear with instructions and barely gives rubrics, so you must go to office hours for clarification. Tests are extremely hard, but she offered test corrections which greatly helped. You can do well if you go to office hours and put in effort, but I don't recommend this class.

Dr. Burmeister was not helpful throughout the rest of the course She grades things at the last possible minute, does not provide insightful instructions, and also does not record her lectures. When questions were asked in class she was very condescending to students and was not effective in office hours.
Lab class. He was very knowledgeable & was good at explaining things. Recommend!

This lab is super chill. All the assignments are very quick. It's easy to get lost trying to understand the content but they guide us through everything. I don't think about this class other than when I am actually there

Great guy! We did some group projects and he was definitely on top of things throughout the semester, prepared. If you aren't interested in the subject matter, don't take the class, but if you are it's a fun time.

Super interesting content but lectures were a bit boring. He was accessible for meetings and was very helpful in answering questions about the content. Two group presentations but no rubric, however I assume were graded easily. Exams test heavily on conceptual thinking. I would recommend taking if you have an interest in biotechnology!
Great professor, learned a lot. Nice guy and clearly knowledgeable.

Me, loving animal's wanted to take this class and even after reading the bad rate my professor reviews on her exams I still took it. One of the biggest mistakes I have ever made. The information itself is fairly simple but her exams are quite literally the definition of unjust.I had a 75 by the time of the final, somehow clutched. Don't take.

Instead of taking the bus to get to class, I should gotten UNDER the bus. 

Exams are ~80% of grade, so many studies, exams/quizzes meant to trick you (A + B only, only C, all choices). Test 1 average was 60. Very kind lady so writing this sucks, but absolutely don't take. Barely scraped by as someone who studies lots. Don't buy book for the 1 HW.

Dr.Lohmann is a wonderful lady but man this class is almost impossible. Your entire grade is pretty much three exams, all of which I studied for two weeks beforehand, and still ended up failing them. Her questions are designed to trick you but in the most unnecessary way possible. Do NOT take this class unless you absolutely have to, extremely hard

She is kind and really does love animals, however her lectures are insufferable. She fills the ENTIRE time with case studies. U learn like 7 studies every lecture (which is SO boring.) Midterm 1 mean was 60. Should be higher test grades for what this course is. Easy info, but it's so much for no reason. Doesnt add ur letter grade until after final.

Professor Lohmann is super nice and approachable, and the class and lab are really interesting if you're into animals! BUT you need to know that the class's 3 exams are pretty much your entire grade for the semester, so you HAVE to do well on them to do well in the class. Study the case studies and know them like the back of your hand!

BIOL 545 was one of my favorite courses at UNC, largely in part to Dr. Shiau's teaching.  The 3-hour class structure was sometimes a little hard to sit through (not her fault), but she did her best to make the entire lecture engaging.  Literature-based teaching style was great and I would definitely take the course again.

Dr. Shiau was clearly passionate about the material, but lacked organizational skills. The class material was incredibly interesting and the small class size was great, but I often felt like she emphasized small details rather than the big picture. Overall, she is a great person, but the 3 hr lecture format of this class was not ideal.
Dr shiau was nice and helpful when needed but the class period was long. Class was also generally interesting with lots of topics. I think she treated students equally

Professor Shiau is a very nice person, however her teaching style can be difficult to grasp. Her exams are very straightforward and directly off the slides, and while long, her GRQs do prepare you well for class. She genuinely cares about her students, but her lack of practice exam questions can make studying for the first exam difficult.

This class is a challenging class. The way that she taught was very different from the first professor in the class, which is why her first exam is so hard. She is very lecture-heavy on exams, while I prefer textbook heavy. She also makes every class worth a bit. I don't think she is a bad professor, just different from the first half.
So, I didn't actually take BIOL89, I took BIOL79H, but that wasn't listed as an option. I loved this professor. He was so funny and he really cared about all of his students.

This professor is very sweet and understanding. He is obviously very interested in what he does and his class isn't overly difficult. The only thing is that he is a tough grade and sometimes does not give clear instructions. But regardless random class field trips and cdad jokes and to be expected.

Dr. Hurlbert is fervent when it comes to the topic of citizen science, a field I have quite enjoyed to gain insight on. The projects in this class did take some effort but we were provided clear expectations on what to do in order to do well on them. If you are looking for an easy gen-ed class, take this one.
Lots of papers to keep track of, but he cares a lot about his students.

I don't know what is with the teaching style, but it is very dull to the point where it is uninteresting to learn. The material is fascinating, but the teaching style just isn't it. The prof talks to himself rather than to the class. The course is PAPER HEAVY, so be prepared to take notes on each research paper since they WILL show up on the tests

SO MANY PAPERS. Remembering all of them was insane. Tests were all on the papers. Exams make up the majority of the final grade. Participation matters. Must take notes on each paper, and ask for help, because it will show up on the test!! Prof was solid. Recommended to have previous dev bio knowledge.

Article DENSE class, tests weighed a lot and mainly focussed on articles. Analytical and evidenced-based exams. It was a decent class to handle and understand only because of my previous developmental biology background.

Helps to have dev bio background. Research article dense class, be able to analyze, infer, expand, dissect papers and feel confident in providing evidence based answers on tests. Lecture content can only get you so far, so you must know papers well to do well since tests weigh a lot combined. Prof is great and helps build on bio 205 material.
Friendly and funny made class very enjoyable. Not very difficult and no homework, grades are just tests.

One of the best professors I've had for a science course. Dr.Hedrick knows how to break down complex biological concepts in a way that sticks. Lectures are well-organized and never feel like a waste of time, and they genuinely care if students understand the material. Office hours are helpful—take advantage of them if you're struggling.

Dr. Hedrick is one of the nicest profs I've had at UNC, and his class isn't super hard. All lectures are recorded and well-structured. However, your entire grade is two midterms and a final, and the exams can be a bit tricky. I took this class as a bio minor without taking A&P first, which I wouldn't recommend, but was overall a good elective

Dr. Hedrick is one of a kind and truly amazing. Yes - there are only 3 grades. BUT, the exams are very fair. Take good notes in lecture (not just on the slides but the things he says) and put into a quizlet. That's all. Also grades on improvement. Attendance is not required and lectures are recorded. He is 10/10 amazing. Loved this class so so much

All of the lectures were recorded and attendance wasn't mandatory. I recommend watching the lectures before the exams though, as a lot of the questions were based off of stuff he said rather than just what was written in the powerpoint. The exams were a lot harder than the practice exams, so be prepared. CURVES HEAVY on exams and the final grade.
She published the Exam II review slides.

Dr. McCoy is a sweet professor. If you need a fairly easy 400 elective, take 466. Most work is graded for participation so do the work & you'll be fine. Only thing I will say is she is often forgetful about posting assignments/grades in a timely manner but is flexible and will move dates if need be. Overall, enjoyed her class & would take again.

** BIOL (260) I really loved Dr. McCoy, she was a very caring and nice professor. The test material was straight from the lectures, and as long as you paid attention you could easily make A's, despite a few mistakes on the format of the tests. In my opinion this class was just a better version of 104, so I HIGHLY recommend.

(BIOL 260) Dr. McCoy was the GOAT! I loved her class, and as a junior, I'd say this is probably the easiest biology course you'll take at UNC. Her tests were definitely based on her lectures—no required pre-reading—and she encouraged students to collaborate on all assignments. She was excellent at breaking down ecological concepts. 10/10.
Soooo disorganized. Basically inaccessible outside of class. Doesn't respond to emails. Don't take

Dr. Conlon was genuinely amazing. If you put in an ounce of effort, you will get an A. 3 exams, got a 76 on the first one, & still ended with an A+. Group projects every week, but we did them the night before & they took our group (not exaggerating)
The best class I have ever taken at UNC. Dr. Conlon makes you want to come to class because the material is so interesting you don't want to miss out. You get really close to your group b

Dr. Conlon is great! The class includes three exams (75%) and group presentation participation (25%), but Conlon grades generously, gives partial credit fairly, and offers plenty of test resources. The course material was genuinely interesting and Conlon showed enthusiasm when he lectured. I definitely recommend this course as a 400+ BIOL elective.

Prof. Sekelsky was a caring teacher and engaging lecturer.  He was accessible outside of class for questions and went out of his way to be helpful.  Problem sets were given far in advance of deadlines and content was from class lectures.  His exam was also exclusively on content from lectures and very fair. 
 Bonus points for his genetics shirts.
Great professor, designed handouts for students which is better than any textbook. The exam is fair, and the course materials are very straightforward.

Professor Blaimont was pretty great. I genuinely enjoyed her lectures and was interested in the content even though I am not a Bio major. Just try not to miss any homework or quizzes because she won't let you make anything up unless you get hit by a car or something. Overall pretty epic teacher.

Blaimont is awesome. Her lectures are interesting and she is great at explaining things. Expectations for assignments are clear and exams are fair. She is very knowledgable and you can tell she cares. I definitely recommend taking her!

Blaimont is an amazing professor. She is very kind, very enthusiastic about the subject (she is young and relatively new), and very straightforward about what she wants from her students. She is also very accessible outside of class for questions, help, and anything else. 100% the best option to take for 220!

God-tier of a professor. Definitely take Dr. Blaimont for BIOL 220! Very fair exams and grading on assignments. Patient on answering questions. I wish she would answer questions more directly, but she is already doing an amazing job for her 1st semester at UNC! Study the ppt and journal club, then you will be fine!

Dr. Blaimont was amazing! This class had a lot of content and work, but her lectures and teaching style always prepared me. Her exams were difficult in the right ways, testing things in an understandable manner from lectures and work. 220 had a lot of content, but I feel like she made it as manageable as possible. Take her if you have the chance!

Really good high-level bio class, especially for a lab requirement. Made learning about invertebrates engaging and fun. I enjoyed the unique test questions - instead of boring recall questions, he writes creative and absurd ways to test your knowledge. It is heavy on memorization but I think it builds upon itself well, the beginning is crucial.
I love this class. The best class I've ever taken here at UNC, made me consider changing my major to marine biology.

Dr. Lohmann is the BEST professor in the biology department. Hands down. Amazing course content and really interesting topics were covered in class. He was always understanding and will do everything to help his students succeed.
