Awesome professor! Teaches everything in a clear manner which is easy to understand. Exams are fair and not tricky. If you know the content, you'll do fine. Great course and easiest >400 course I've taken.

This class was awesome. Dr. Ahmed was super layed back and the entire course reflected that. Your grades off of 2 midterms and 1 final (not cumulative) and 2 group presentations. It's very much show up do the work and an A is almost guaranteed. I don't think anyone got under a 80 on any exam or presentation.

This class was very easy in comparison to other bio courses. All midterms and final was take home and were graded very fairly with clear feedback. Only two group presentations and you get to choose your groups. Overall, I highly recommend this class to help boost your science GPA.
Great prof, never showed up to class and only showed up when it was my turn to present. Take home test and finals. He also records lectures and host it on zoom. Easy BIOL 400+ class!!!

I actually took 546, but it was an easy class, as long as you take enough notes to understand the material, all you have to do to succeed is rewatch the lectures and write down what he says with your understanding on the open note tests. He clearly wants everyone to get an A and grades group presentations nicely. Helps if you go to office hours.
This man does not tip servers when he goes to restaurants.
Riddell is a good lecturer who cares for his students. It consisted of a lecture with many group discussions in each class. I loved this course and the material.

This class was great and very interesting since Dr.Riddell cares about what he is lecturing on. There were three exams and no final only a 10 article literature review(On an approved topic of your choice) plus a short and easy presentation to the class. I even had questions about environmental science careers and he made the time to speak with me.

Professor Riddell is very good at keeping lectures engaging and fun. He encourages group discussions and helps students when reading scientific literature. I took Global Change Biology (464) and the content was so much fun to learn about- it even had me reading the textbook. Get ready for some fascinating conversations about phenotypic plasticity.

He is a wonderful professor who will always make sure that you are taken care of and will extend deadlines whenever you need it! Take Dr. Riddell's class, it is a wonderful class with an amazing professor.

Dr. Riddell is calm, warm, brilliant, and an expert on global change. I frequently wished that classes were longer or that we had more of them because he had so many interesting things to say. Quizzes were not super hard. The textbook was a useful, but not totally essential supplement to in-class material. Take this class.

Food Science & Fermentation. Easily the highlight of my semester. Fun to take even if you don't drink. Group research project & hands-on lab (molec bio & kitchen). Might not recommend if you will stress about getting an A; take it to enjoy it! Dr. Jones is a fantastic guy, very kind and funny, & cares a lot about his students.

This class is one semester long group project, with the deliverable at the end being worth 75% of your grade. Dr. Jones is fun and caring, and he really is excited to see what students can do. This class is good for people who can work in teams, set deadlines and stick to them, and who like data analysis and research.

The class is very chill, and he's a very fun and laidback guy. The only qualm I have is that he doesn't tell you your final grade breakdown, it just appears in CC. And there are no midterm grade evaluations, just one grade at the end of the semester, so you have no idea how you're doing until the very end.
took BIOL 490!

Dr. Mitchell, my glorious king. I barely had to put any effort into the class because it was heavily discussion based but still learnt so much from it. Very accommodating if things come up for you. Attendance policy was pretty chill. Please take this class.

This class was easy and discussion-based—few lectures, just student-led conversations on the readings. He was clear, responsive to emails, and very understanding if issues came up. The discussion format was super valuable, and I learned a lot in this class. We could ask questions about anything—bio, research, even current events. Highly recommend!

Dr. Mitchell is one of the kindest professors I have had - seminar style class with students rotating leading. Come to class, read a section of the textbook/article, and ask questions. He wants to see that you learned something new, and is very flexible and gives a lot of grace with grading/presentation topics/leading discussions. Highly recommend!

Discussion-based class. 2-3 readings, come in and talk about it. Very kind and understanding professor, very fun class. Understands not everyone has a strong background in the content and is open to questions. Highly recommend.

Dr. Chen is very kind and responsive to emails. Lectures were mandatory but I wish they were more interactive. Exams are open-note and based off the lecture slides. Homework was light and did not take long. Overall a decent 400+ level class.
he was amazing!!

Dr. Chen is a charming and obviously knowledgeable professor, but he is not the best lecturer by any means. That being said, the exams are all open note, and he provides review slides that directly reflect the content on the exam. Overall, the class was pretty easy, but really boring since you have to go back and teach yourself the content anyway.

Class was a lot of content and for me it was hard, but the exams are open note. He really tries his best to get students to ask questions if confused. He does really great at answering questions.TA was also really helpful and kind to everyone. Would recommend, but I wouldn't say it is an easy A. 2 midterms and 1 final.

Dr. Chen is very knowledgeable in his field. You will learn a lot about paper-reading and analysis through well-prepared lectures and GRQs. The most manageable bio course ever with no tedious work. As long as you read and participate, you will get a good grade. However, lecture is monotonous and can be more engaging. A balanced and safe optiion.

He is super nice and so interested in CRISPR! There is 1 midterm and you get a cheat sheet. The average was a B+ and he gave us 2 questions back that people missed. Otherwise, the only other grades are a semester long group project and the "final exam" which is a prompt that you get ahead of time and can prepare. Definitely recommend this class.

Dr. Nimchuk has been by far the best biology professor I've ever had at UNC. This course was my absolute favorite this semester and I always looked forward to sitting in lecture. He is very knowledgable with the CRISPR-CAS9 system and has so much insight to provide. Everyone should take this class and witness how marvelous Dr. Nimchuk is!

The best professor I've had at UNC. Very clear on his expectations and willing to explain difficult concepts. The class material was interesting for people interested in genetics and molecular biology. If you read and understand the papers, you will do well.

Loved Dr. Z! His lectures were engaging and entertaining to learn from. He sent out a weekly email that outlined the class we had that week and showed how much he cared about us. This was a 1-credit seminar, but he's teaching a full course on CRISPR next semester which I would highly recommend!

Great professor and person. Very knowledgeable on the subject matter and cares about the students. Seems as though he wants students to succeed and learn from his class. I highly recommend his course on CRISPR if you're a science major and interested in genetics. Final project was a lot of fun.

David is kind, funny, and passionate in his work and teachings. He is EXTREMELY fair in terms of recognizing fault; he eased up on our second exam after understanding what students didn't like from the first exam and offered extra credit. He's also very helpful outside of lecture to thoroughly explain topics that I didn't understand well in class.

Dr. Pfennig is an amazing professor. The class was amazing and very discussion based. The structure of the course was amazing for learning the material deeply. There was one presentation, a grant proposal, and a few weekly assignments. All were amazing and things I looked forward to completing. 100% recommend.

Great teacher and great class! graded by 4 things (HW, which is a 100, participation, a grant proposal, and a presentation you work on with a group). He gives you great feedback but also grades leniently. Highly reccommend!

Great professor who made lectures incredibly enjoyable to attend. He is passionate about the topic and transfers that feeling to the class. Lecture slides contain the exact content you will be tested on. Only difficulty is the tests can be a time crunch, so you have to know your stuff.

Dr. Pfennig was fantastic, and the relaxed nature of this course really worked for me. There was a lot of reading, and everyone had to do multiple group presentations as well as a significant amount of individual/group writing, so the workload is somewhat high, but it seemed like the work was very applicable to what would be done as a scientist.

I highly recommend taking this course, it's one of the best bios I've taken. Showing up and participating is the biggest thing with a few homework assignments, two exams, and a presentation/discussion leading. Dr. Pfennig is very clear and thorough in lecture and truly care about students success.

Dr. Pfennig is the kindest professor I have ever had here. She is passionate about her research AND teaching, which makes a huge difference in the quality of the class! She only wants you to succeed, and it's not hard to do in this class! As long as you attend class and participate in discussions, you should be on track for a good grade.

Probably the best above 400 level biology class at UNC. Can't recommend the class more, even if you don't really care about behavior. Available outside of class and wants you to do well on exams. Exams are online and timed, but questions are based on what is discussed in class.

I took BIOL564 with Prof. Yitbarek, and he's a really nice guy who obviously cares a lot about his students and what he is teaching, but he does not explain things in the most intuitive way. It's often hard to follow what exactly we're learning in class. Easy grader though tests are hard. Not many assignments besides tests.
Kind of a disorganized class in BIOL 564 but Dr. Yitbarek is very chill. I enjoyed the class, but be warned that there is a ton of math and plenty of basic calc.

Dr. Yitbarek's lectures were very engaging, and he was always willing to give feedback in office hours. He also offered multiple extra credit opportunities because he wanted all students to succeed. I really appreciated how the class was project-based with lots of opportunities to interact in groups. Take this class for your upper level bio!

Dr. Yitbarek is caring and obviously wants you to succeed. His class is strictly project-based, and you will do well if you put in the effort. The topics of the projects are flexible giving the freedom to do what you want. If you are interested in ecology, this class is a fantastic overview of many ecological subtopics. You will learn a lot.
Absolutely loved this semester with Paul. He is a super caring guy and extremely smart. The class was a little disorganized, but I didn't mind because he was so kind. This was BIOL 649.

Paul is an expert on microscopy, and his lectures were super interesting! He also assigned a group project as a final project and encouraged students to run creative experiments for it. However, he was impossible to reach outside of class, and the structure of the class overall was disorganized, which made it more stressful.

Alain was a fantastic professor. Great attitude and loves to help. Assignment criteria can feel "vague" at first, but assignments are easier than they appear. Use all available resources (including generative AI), and work with your peers, and this class rocks. Course itself is not super bio-heavy; focused on computational methods/programming.

Alain is so great! Learned a lot in his biol 528 class about coding and learned how to use Chat GPT! Took more away from this class than a lot of other classes at UNC, and he made coding fun somehow! Highly recommend!

She gave back 0 grades, and didn't use canvas. Not even the syllabus was on it. She never gave feedback. Impossible to know how we were doing, until the final grade was entered. She also rejected my final exam reschedule request, and implied I was lying? Avoid unless you are a public speaking pro.

I think this was her first time teaching. It shows. The first day of class she divided us into groups and we stayed in those groups the entire semester. Presentations from the groups every other week. It was interesting to learn about neural engineering but you're basically teaching yourself.

This is an amazing class, Colin Wallace is a great professor. The grading is based on attendance, homework, lecture tutorials, 3 midterms, and one final exam. There is a lot of information and it can be difficult if you do not pay attention to lectures or do not do readings, but if you really participate it is super understandable and so fun!!

Colin Wallace is an amazing professor. This is a great class to take for anyone regardless of prior knowledge on astronomy. Graded on attendance, homework, lecture tutorials, 3 midterms, and the final exam. There is math involved but it is very easy if you are paying attention to lectures and doing the readings. (Its not actually about aliens)

He brought a lot of excitement to class everyday.  Attend lectures and you'll do well on the exams.  ExpertTA work was not relevant to lecture and was not tested.  Good teacher and many opportunities to answer questions in class.

Dr. Wallace is great for PHYS 115!! I struggled in 114 so I was nervous coming into 115, especially since I heard it was harder. However, I have had a great experience in 115. Dr. Wallace's lectures are engaging, he has a great attitude, and he genuinely enjoys physics. His exams are also clear and straight to the point.

If you want the easiest gen ed class ever, but also don't want something super boring, take this class. I would have preferred it to be harder, honestly. I studied for about 30 minutes before each test.

Theres a worksheet due before each class. Go to the office hours of the TA's, you need to go a certain amount of times to get credit. It's a theory heavy course, so don't worry too much about the math aspect of physics. Also, make friends in the class and start working on labs early. You'll have to do a couple of labs with other people.

119 has the potential to be my worst course in college. The lectures are droning and boring (though Muxin is much nicer than Dr W) and hard to stay engaged with. The tests consistently average low 60s and are confusing for no reason. Dr W is extremely passive-aggressive, in person and online. The instructors have no concern for difficulty. BAD.

50 min mandatory lectures twice a week, but are incredibly simple and not helpful for homework or tests. Unreasonable grading of labs, and no communication with TAs on how to actually support us in labs. Don't reach out over email and expect a helpful or caring answer. The class is fine if you know what you are doing, the professors are lacking.

I LOVE JENNIFER WEINBERG-WOLF. When she started talking about shooting neutrons into an apple, I was inspired. She completely changed my perspective on life. Imagine shopping in a grocery store... LOOKING for the perfect apple to shoot a neutron into, ultimately turning it into a Hulk Apple.

She is a nice person, but this is a hard class to take as an introduction to physics, especially for people like me who aren't physics majors. The class requires a lot of time outside of class to understand the concepts and examples in order to answer the questions the way she intended them to be answered on exams.

He does not seem to care for our success. We do much of the learning ourselves so he is essentially the placeholder that contributes relatively little to our understanding. The actual learning is done during the 3-hour lab sessions. Would not recommend the class but there is a very limited selection of instructors here who teach this, so no choice.

Dr. Deardorff was a bad explainer so I mainly had to learn through Studio labs, my peers, or Dr. Hansen. Practice exams and exam prep sessions are the most helpful when studying. Exams were quite long compared to the 50 min we had, so making a mistake could cost you time.

First time taking physics, but it was manageable with freshman courseload. Gives multiple homeworks/warmups a week. Office hours and the online vectorious worksheets helped me most to prepare for exams. Exams were hard, but Dr. Deardorff offers extra credit for improved performance. Studio takes a lot of time. Final was the easiest exam for me.

He was ok. I preferred the other professor (Dr. Hansen). Participation is SUPER important so go to all the labs/lectures. Labs were hard. I failed every exam, but I was able to get a 70 at the end with help from CramBetter and practice problems. If you're not doing well, it's ok just try to get as many points as possible.

Exams are only 50% of your grade so studio (25%) really helps bring your grade up. Partial credit is generous, but the class is very self-taught. Manageable as a freshman. 70% of the class got a B or higher, ~75% class average on exams. Utilise practice exams, Vectorious problems, and Friday exam prep sessions.

Very blunt and straight to the point (probably why people rated him so low)
But very helpful and does give great teaching if you go to his office hours!! Doesn't come off as very friendly but is always willing to help you out.
Key note: very, very blunt!!

Puts his heart into the class, which I appreciate. If you want to learn how to program micro controllers, this is the class for you. He provides plenty of opportunity and incentive if you screw up as well. Downside is the lack of grading and some labs are a bit wordy, but that's about it.
Unclear grading rubric for most assignments and very little feedback since 75% of assignments are still ungraded almost a week past LDOC. I would not describe him as open and inviting.
If you want to be verbally abused then this is the prof for you :D

he is a narcissist. wont answer your question directly. writes paragraph responses to you but it makes everything more confusing. disorganized. unclear. einstein said "if you can't explain it to a six year old, you don't understand it yourself". dr j does not understand physics or anything really yet believes he knows everything

Dr. Mann is a great professor and he genuinely cares about the course content as it is VERY applicable if you are looking to do any observational astronomy in the future. His homeworks are long and difficult, but his and the TA's office hours are very useful! Overall, his lectures are great and he throws in a lot of humor to keep you engaged!

This class is far too difficult to be a gen ed. Classes are solely lecture, and when he would ask practice questions, even if 50% of the class got a question wrong he would move on anyway. I took pages and pages of notes every class and studied countless hours but still got 50s-70s on all exams. If you struggle with stem classes at all then avoid!!
Dr. Mann is amazing!! It's obvious that he loves his field and that enthusiasm comes through in the way he teaches. Great guy and would take another class with Dr. Mann in a heartbeat

Dr. Mann was great, and I managed to get by without having to attend class. There's so much going on though - with HWs, lecture tutorials, warmups, etc. Just a lot to keep track of, and I missed so many that I tanked my grade. If you're on top of all of these then it's an easy A. Very fair exams - just keep doing the practice tests!

Dr. Mann is an impressive prof. While he is passionate about astronomy and extending his knowledge to students, he doesn't take himself too seriously like other UNC STEM profs. Really enjoyed this class and Dr. Mann. Shoutout to his cat, Bandit.

Dr. Tsui is a very unclear lecturer and is not engaging at all. He mainly reads off the slides and barely explains practice problems. If there are two professors teaching the section and he is one of them, you should do alright because the other one will most likely be a better teacher.

He is a very nice guy just not the best lecturer. Some times the way he explains things is confusing and he can go on tangents sometimes. Phys 115 is a hard class in general so its hard to grasp the concepts when they arent explained too well in lecture.
Very kind man who wanted students to understand the material

He knows his stuff but the lectures are incredibly boring. Just reads off the slides and spends 15 minutes explaining the easiest question while skipping over harder ones. Not bad but definitely didn't ignite a love for physics within me.

He was a very kind, caring man but I do not feel he explained content amazingly. He seems very intelligent and 114 might just be a bit too low-level of a class for him. Great person, but might not be the best professor unfortunately.

Took a mentored research class with Prof. Lu, which is a more chill setting than a traditional class with lectures. He helped me learn medical imaging physics, assisted with work on an impactful research project, and connected me to one of his PhD students for help with the project. Definitely a memorable, chill class.

Dr. Lu was a pretty fun and engaging lecturer. PHYS 118 naturally is a hard class, but I thought he did a decent job explaining the concepts. The only minor issues were that he has a bit of an accent (which isn't his fault) and didn't get what students were asking a lot of the time.

Dr. Lu is not a particularly good lecturer, especially for such a difficult class. It will be hard, you'll get through it, but there could certainly be a better teaching professor teaching the content.
Nice guy with decent lectures. Only like 50% of the class was graded by exams with lots of partial credit. This class takes up a lot of time tho with the studios, lectures, and homework.

There is so much content in this class, don't start skipping. Midterm questions are hard, use the past midterms as much as possible. I didn't find studio too helpful for learning, but it was a good grade boost. I recommend skimming the textbook, reviewing slides and using youtube.
Lectures are the most boring thing ever.

Lectures, textbooks, homework, and studios are kinda redundant -- I went to studio and did the homework, but I normally skipped lecture and never read the textbook. I did fine. 

(attendance is theoretically 'mandatory', but they replace participation with your final exam grade if it's higher. that provides an 'out' to skippers who perform well.)

Dr. Henning was a great professor for PHYS118. He and another professor would rotate lectures. His lectures were very clear and he was open to answering any questions. This class has mandatory attendance for lecture and studio. Exams are 50% of your grade. The class has very clear expectations for its students. Study hard and you'll be fine.

As someone who took an algebra physics class in high school, 118 was manageable. Studios were annoying but TAs were happy to check answers. Henning only lectured about half of the classes and he was fine. Exams were fair if somewhat tight for time. Can earn up to 1% bonus to course grade for surveys throughout the semester.

PHYS 118 is not an easy class by any means, but Dr. Henning teaches it well. Lectures were easy to follow for the most part, and he did his best to demonstrate the concepts when possible. You can tell that he has great passion for what he teaches. He is transparent about the logistics of the class from the start, so nothing comes as a surprise.
Love Dr. Lopez. He is very caring and willing to answer the questions for students outside the class.

He takes forever to get to the point and his lecturing style is not the best. I also have a very hard time deciphering what he's trying to say bc he has a thick accent. Also for whatever reason he taught the hardest concepts for PHYS 115 which was very frustrating since it is already so difficult to understand these topics, let alone understand him

i was always a bit confused and lost in lectures. If the class doesn't understand a concept, he doesn't stop and just pushes on, which was a bit annoying because then I wouldn't understand the rest of the lecture. Sometimes made somewhat rude comments about how it was easy and we should understand it, or things like that, but he apologized.

One of the co-professors for PHYS 115 and I swear every time we walked into class and realized he was holding a lecture that day, there would be a cumulative sigh. Not good at explaining material at all. Everything comes seemingly intuitively to him and his explanations are over-complicated. Friendly! But I don't want friendly, I want competent.
He doesn't know how to lecture. Accent is too thick and it's hard to understand him.
The best physics teacher I've ever had.

Dr. Erickcek is knowledgeable and does a good job covering the material. The material is difficult though, and your grade comes down to how well you do on the midterms so study accordingly! Still, they are fairly graded and often were curved slightly (including the final) if she saw the majority of the class struggled on certain sections.
She is really nice, gave good feedback on all assignments, but the material is vast and she doesn't give exam practice problems. So it is difficult to study for those exams.

Dr. Erickcek is amazing, this class made me switch my major to astrophysics! Fantastic class, lectures, and professor. The homework can be difficult but is totally manageable if you start on time. Having python experience can be helpful.

She is an exceptional teacher, her lectures are so thorough and well-planned. She is so helpful if you make it obvious that you're trying to understand. The content of the classes are super challenging. she doesn't downplay the difficulty. She delivers excellence in her teaching and expects excellence from her students. It's intense, but worth it

Dr. Khveshenko is one of the worst professors ever. His lectures were useless and completely unrelated to exams. Anything he wrote on the board was impossible to decipher, and his accent is difficult to understand. He cannot answer any questions, and talked to us as if we are PhD students in physics. Please avoid for your sanity.
DON'T take physics with Dmitri he's hard to understand and doesn't show his work. He just clicks through the slides. Bad lecturer.
just a bad lecturer, will just read off of slides and click through how problems are worked out. 50% of the time when students ask a question he answers a completely different question

I wish I could give him zero stars. I have never encountered such an AWFUL professor. It is obvious he does not read the slides before class, nor does he care about teaching the material. No work is ever shown and explanations are always unclear. Take this course with a different professor, or at a different institution. AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS!

I wish I could rate him zero stars. I have never encountered such an AWFUL professor. It is very clear that he does not read the slides before he comes to class, nor does he care about teaching it. He shows no work when solving examples and provides unclear explanations. Take this class with another professor or take it at a different institution.

Lectures were easy to follow and went by the textbook layout identically. Hints at certain problems that might be on exams. The course was fair, and homework + lecture + reading was enough to prepare you for exams.

He's a little bit of a dry lecturer, but course is pretty clear cut. Especially after being traumatized by E&M I, this class felt very fair. Exams were hard, but nothing unexpected. He graded very generously, and homework assignments weren't too bad either.
Doctor Wu is super enthusiastic about physics. He is really caring and understanding. The only thing is his expression sometimes is not clear enough. Overall, he is good.

The TA teaches, grades and creates homework for the lab section. Professor simply gives the recitation lecture. In those lectures he is a slow speaker, does not get through all the material in a timely manner. He is also a poor communicator outside of the classroom. He once forgot to tell us about an assignment and then tried to blame us.

Lecture plus supplemeting with textbook make the material clear and manageable. Fun lecturer and engages with students. Grades exams rather strictly, (i.e., you want textbook work) but is very lenient when it comes to getting points back. Akaa wants you to succeed, and he was very forgiving with final rounding. Put in the work and you will do well!

Great professor. The midterm exams were both a bit disorganized, but he was honest and fair when grading them. Doesn't take attendance traditionally, but gives pop quizzes throughout the semester as a substitute. Overall very understanding and a funny guy in lecture.

Professor Akaa is a caring instructor and wants us to do well. He goes through lectures very diligently and they are easy to follow. Pop quizzes are nice to help provide extra practice problems outside of hw and midterm reviews.

He's a very decent professor. He's a funny guy and clearly knows what he is talking about, and he expects that from you as well. If you put in the work, you'll do just fine. Only reason I didn't get an A is because I was lazy.

10/10 class, and 10/10 professor. It was certainly not an easy A, but most definitely not unachievable either. Quizzes, final exam, and final project were all very fair and was the perfect amount of challenging. Highly recommend.
Best class - the TAs and prof really cared. So much fun

I loved professor law and his TAs they are all very kind and helpful, but if you are not good at astrophysics do NOT take this class. The description is very misleading as we didn't start actually talking about aliens until halfway through the semester and there was a lot of math to do with this class.
Overall, he's a great professor and a great guy but the class was pretty rough and hard to understand. Confusing concepts and lots of math. This was a rough semester for him in general though.

dr. law & his TAs are fantastic. i'm a humanities major & really struggled with the class content, but they truly care & are more than willing to work with you. the class & especially the tests are tough but law is great. i'll also add that he was fantastic during the shooting incident this year. i wouldn't have wanted to be in any other classroom.

law is such a sweetheart. as a person who came to unc with no love for physics, I'm starting to get interested in it and will be minoring in astronomy because of him. he's very supportive and his TAs are amazing too (s/o to lawrence, doctor procter, will). they all go out of their way to make sure you understand the content. 100% would recommend.
DO not TAKE HIM

This is for PHYS115 but I agree this guy needs to take a public speaking class before he can lecture.  I can't imagine him teaching his own class don't take him if you don't want to raise your cortisol levels.

This is actually for Phys 115. I cannot hear Qiu, he just reads off the slides and WHISPERS. He doesnt explain anything or write anything to solve it he just talks. Please avoid him, you will not learn.

Prof. Qiu does not explain concepts in class: he reads straight from the PowerPoints. He doesn't even try, even if students ask him to write out explanations in class. Stay away, if possible. I do not know why he is teaching intro physics as he does not seem to care whatsoever.

Professor Qiu struggled to read the slideshows, which made understanding difficult physics topics even harder. In fact, I got a better grade in 118 than 114 just because of how bad professor Qiu was. He also never answers questions, always seems nervous to lecture, and seems so out of touch with his students. if you have a class with him, DROP IT.

So so nice! It is clear that he is passionate about what he does. Basically gives you what is going to be on the exams. I would say to make sure you know what time assignments are due so you don't miss one, he doesn't make them due at 11:59 and there are very few of them. Also can be a little condescending if you answer a question wrong in class.

Professor Zhou is a great instructor. He's passionate about what he teaches and his tests are verbatim the concepts taught. There was little homework but that meant that the class was test-heavy. The content was interesting and we were able to go to the labs! You needed to be present in class to understand concepts as his slides were confusing.

Dr. Zhou is such a king! You'll probably feel intimidated at first but he tries so hard to make the topics interesting and make jokes during class. Very few grades and he doesn't really use canvas so you won't really know how you're doing in the class. Slides are not detailed so go to class and pay attention and the exams won't be too bad.

Dr. Zhou is a great professor and taught some really cool things about nanotechnology and science! Class is pretty chill with few homework assignments, a midterm and a final for the year. I just wish lecture slides could be more detailed, but if you pay attention in class, you should be fine!

Dr. Zhou is a character. The class feels a little just thrown together, but the material is not difficult at all. Very little homework while is nice, and as long as you payed attention in lecture you will be fine. Incredibly laid back class that I would recommended for your first year seminar if this sounds interesting to you.
Clear lecturer, minimal studying required

Dr. Gruszko was extremely accessible and answered questions thoroughly. The class was difficult, but don't let the midterm scores discourage you as the studio grade will bring you up. If you find the time for it, go to the review session before the midterms and the final, they are a great way to remind yourself of the many concepts covered.

Lectures are a MUST. You will not glean enough information by simply going through slides. Work the problems out in class, do the homework, and pay attention in lab. This class was sneakily difficult for me, but if you practice, pay attention, and work to do well on the final, she will take that into account. She is also super nice and accomodating
AMAZING PROFESSOR!!! Always listens to students, if you have any questions ask and she'll always be there to help you. Office hours are incredibly helpful and she knows what she's talking about.
has an accent, but it isn't bad and she explains things very well in the lectures

Dr. Branca is the best. She was very caring and accessible through Piazza and O.H. and really cared about helping as many people as she could. For someone that has never taught PHYS 119 before she did a really amazing job. You will definitely have to put in a lot of effort on your own, but she really does want to help along the way. Great professor

dr. branca taught phys 119 pretty well for her first time. there were times where we didn't cover the last little bit of the lecture but that was remediated by the studio work. tests were reasonable except for that second one D: lots of help available and the hw is great practice.

This course is very very hard, It is for physics students only. These are concepts that are super hard to wrap your head around.There isn't enough visual learning to justify taking this course in person, all you do is read of the board, the homeworks are very difficult. My understanding of the topic increased minimally.

Very funny guy. The class is hard regardless, and he did a pretty good job of making things go as smoothly as possible. Always asked for feedback and for questions, and worked with students' requests quite a bit. Definitely recommend him.
The best professor at UNC.

All around wonderful guy. Class is made up of weekly homework and quizzes with a large semester project at the end. Attendance isn't required but you're better off going to most lectures (or attending on zoom). Grades leniently and cares for his students.
He is a great guy!
Very passionate and easygoing professor!
Chill teacher, really is passionate about what he teaches and makes class accessible to all!

Class itself is easy. Learned content from outside resources and then reviewed the slides to understand the class. Topics aren't hard and plenty of opportunities to ask questions in ~3 hours a week of office hours.

I did good in this class because of Youtube learning. His lecture slides made no sense, and they had errors on them. I learned absolutely nothing from Rodriguez. Shows up late and unprepared, and he doesn't care about teaching. Grading/tests were not as bad as I was expecting, but If I didn't have Youtube or ChatGPT, I would have been cooked.

Literally awful. The content of the class is presented on slides that he just repeats it. Nothing makes sense. I was taking 383 during this class and I literally didn't learn or comprehend a single thing unless it was something that we had gone over during 383. If u haven't taken linear algebra and a proof based class (like math 381), do not take.

Professor Rodriguez is a great professor. He will take time to explain things that you do not understand and enjoys teaching. PHYS331 is a tough class if you have not taken linear algebra and a proof based class. There are tons of proofs in the homework. It seems they need to update the prerequisites for the class. Otherwise, it is a great class!

He's a great professor. Low stress high learning environment. Unfortunately, class was at 8 am, so made it hard to pay attention, but that was no fault of the professor. Highly recommend taking a class by him: he's clearly a very smart, very experienced individual. Lucky to have him as a professor.
The literal greatest professor/teacher I have ever had. I actually looked forward to ASTR101 on Tuesday and Thursday.

I looked forward to this class every time I had it, Professor Barlow gave me a newfound love for astronomy. He is hilarious, and keeps his students engaged at all times. The 3 exams + final are based off of the homework and in-class quizzes, and require studying. He cares about his students immensely, and is fun to chat with after class.

Dr. Barlow is truly one of the best professors you can have. He cares deeply about his subject and you can tell. If you have a chance to take a class with him, DO IT! His office hours are extremely helpful and he will explain things down to whatever detail you need and make sure you understand it. He is extremely kind and willing to help.

Amazing, best econ professor, literally study the exact way she tells you by grouping practice problems and you will be fine, flipped classroom is annoying but it works, no other econ professor compares, if you did well in calc you will do amazing in this class

I had the pleasure of taking Professor MSA for both ECON 410 and 415. ECON 415 builds on the concepts from 410, but she does a great job of reviewing the fundamentals needed to succeed in the course. While 410 was a bit trickier, both classes were very manageable as long as you stayed on top of the material and completed her practice exams.

Without question the best professor I have ever encountered. She is welcoming & considerate & leads lectures extremely well and efficiently, giving students plenty of chances to ask questions and absorb info. She is accessible via email and office hours (DEF RECOMMEND GOING), knows how to teach in a way that students will understand...0 complaints.
MSA is a great teacher. This class is not very hard if you are good at calculus and have prior exposure to a lot of the math beforehand. Very fair grader and wants students to suceed.

I like everything about how MSA does ECON 410. She is warm and caring but commands the room and lectures with confidence. She provides a wealth of helpful resources and gives fair exams. If you can get the math down (hardest concepts are exponent rules and partial derivatives), then nothing is more difficult conceptually than the stuff from 101.

Take him, that's all I can say. Econ is hard regardless, I felt prepared for all the exams so far, and he's already posted so much stuff for the final. He tries to relate to the students, take a hard class, and make it something understandable. HW is complete, and there are tons of ways to boost your grade as long as you just do your work.

McDonough is truly an amazing person. Treats every student with the utmost respect and is so intentional with how he teaches and responds to students. You can tell he puts SO much time and care into his course and students' progress. Exams are hard, but it's UNC. If you have to take ECON, take it with McDonough!!!!
great lecturer and there's a bunch of attendance & requirements dropped so you could get a higher score.

Great class and great professor. ECON 101 gets a bad reputation, but it really isn't too bad if you pay attention and do the homework. Robert really cares about people understanding the content and ensuring people have ample opportunities to do so. Tests can be tricky, but they are very fair and with enough preperation you'll do fine.

He was a good economics professor and did a good job explaining topics. If you have taken 101, this will be the most boring class. If you need this OR 101 - definitely take this! Hes really nice and the class is relaxed with a few problem sets and kinda difficult but fair exams. 2 midterms, 1 final exam, 7 problem sets. Easy workload, not easy A.

A tough class, but once you get the hang of how everything flows it's manageable. He is a great professor - very organized, has clear guidelines, and offers a lot of resources for help. One of my favorite econ professors I've had even though stats isn't my strong suit.
bro got the magic of explaining things so clearly and organized

By far the most organized professor I've had at UNC. Gives incredibly clear guidelines, grades everything in a timely manner with plenty of opportunities for feedback. Provides plenty of resources for each exam and is always in his office happy to help his students. I love Christopher Handy.
Hard class but great professor.
Very fair class structure. Lots of materials available for studying. Tests were very similar to the practice he gave and he makes the material very easy to follow throughout the semester.

I have taken 420, 425, 525, and 490 with Dr. Roark. He's the best professor I've had in my time at UNC. The QFE is the most valuable experience I've had in the department. I would recommend taking any class with Dr. Roark.
Roark's QFE is the most valuable program in the econ department

Professor Roark is great! I've had him for both 420 & 425. He is generous with curves, accessible, and provides PLENTY of test prep. There are weekly assignments, but he will sometimes extend deadlines. Had an injury I was out for a month for, so he met with me via zoom to keep up. Hard content, but if you do hw will do fine. Take with Roark!

I like this better than 420, and he's genuinely a really nice guy which makes it a lot better. It isn't super easy content, but my complaint is really just that he makes the homework sometimes unnecessarily hard. One question will be completely fine, easy even, and then the next has math so convoluted that a TI-84 can't process it. Just annoying.

I generally liked Roark. The structure of the class was logical, and content built so by the end I still remembered early stuff for the final. It's not a super easy class, but I preferred it to 410 and thought it was a little more intuitive. Lots of graphs, and some math like derivatives and iffy algebra. I did feel like it got harder throughout.

Ignore past years' reviews. He's an incredibly caring and kind professor and the material is not easy but he's always accessible outside of class and willing to help. He's also a funny guy and makes boring microeconomic theory better!

Complaint #1 – Sergio is a bad teacher
Yes, but he clarifies if you ask.


#2 – You need to learn a new coding language

Yes, but it's not too difficult. All coding is at-home work.


#3 – The midterms and problem sets are different formats
Yes, but the midterms are MUCH easier than PS.

Mostly open-note. Only non-open note for conceptual stuff.
The class was ok ish because I already took a course like it, however it was a lot of self studying to do well in the course.

Sergio's lectures weren't very clear but but the class is interesting enough to make up for it. The homework and pop quizzes were often unnecessarily difficult and over-weighted in the grading. Heavily study the readings for his midterms, and you'll be fine. Would recommend taking this class over some of the other 400-level econ courses.

He's a super nice guy, but an awful college professor.

His lectures are unorganized, unengaging, and bland. He tests on content that often is never present in problem sets or lecture discussions, I'd even go as far as to say that he misdirects you by assigning you practice on concepts that he never even tests on!

Difficult to get an A but not hard to get above a B. Lectures were not helpful in doing the homeworks, which ended up being 80% of the coursework (there is a homework question on both exams). Overall, just not a great course to learn in and had to teach myself how to work out most of the homeworks/exams. Very passable class.

Very lecture-heavy with no posted slides or notes, so attendance is important. Grading is based only on homework and exams. six homework assignments and two exams. The exams closely reflected the study guide, and one homework-style problem appeared on the exam. Grading is done in blocks, so small point differences can affect final letter grades.

Yates is a funny and straightforward guy, and lectures were enjoyable. Tests were similar to hw and practice ?s, and although grading scale made it very difficult to get an A, the tests were graded leniently enough for a B as long as you practiced. If you would like to learn, make sure to attend and pay attention in lecture.

Professor Yates is fantastic! Super funny and down to earth guy. Econ 410 is a hard class, and you WILL need to study for at least 20 hours before exams, but Yates has great lectures that really clarify the material. Although attendance isn't mandatory, if you want an A, you need to go to class. Overall, great class with a great prof.

Yates is passionate, but the class felt unfair at some points and did not feel like he wanted us to do well. His grading scale is super difficult because there's no room for mistakes. You need to do significantly well on both exams and perfect on homework if you want an A but a B is doable if you utilize econ aid.
He was openly racist
Go to class and put in the time and he's great. If you don't you will fall behind quickly - Fair and results reflect effort

The material is very difficult and lectures do get dense, but he make it easy to pass, and even have a good grade without having to go crazy. He makes very clear what he wants in the exam, and if you follow the class it can be very interesting and useful. The final project was also interesting to make.

Took 410 with him before and felt terrible. This time he is pretty decent. Knowledgeable and the lectures give you insights. Exams are materials from the lecture. Simply putting everything he writes on board on your cheat sheet will be fine. Only note--the lectures are not based on textbook and he does not publish lectures, so attendance matters.
Was not a big fan of his lectures, but he did seem to be a much kinder person than reviews suggested.

Graded 50% midterm 50% final, not sure what he teaches is economically/historically/politically correct but he is entertaining... holds office hours from 6am-8am lol. Exams are so stressful but there's no work other than that and you just have to regurgitate his opinions. But ik a bunch of people who did REALLY bad so proceed with caution!

Ignore the poor reviews. This class is absolutely worth taking. Extremely knowledgable professor who has taught this course for 50+ years. Part of the UNC experience is getting to listen to individuals who are highly knowledgable and connected in their fields, this is certainly that case. By far one of the most interesting econ electives.

If I could give Rosefielde a 6/5 I would. Best econ class I have ever taken. Extremely interesting and relevant lectures. Teaches precisely to the test which allows for a deeper grasp of the material. The class is graded on midterm and final which are essay style. He prepares everyone perfectly as long as you attend class. 10/10 experience.

I was skeptical at first, but Prof. Rosefielde proved me wrong. He's a brilliant, caring lecturer. The class is lecture-heavy, but the exams are fair. He teaches objectively, critiques all sides, and welcomes open discussion. Many bad reviews reflect implicit liberal bias—not the quality of this insightful, unique, and well-taught course.

Most problematic professor I've had at UNC—creepy toward female students and prone to weird comments about students' ethnicities. Frequently makes inaccurate claims about Russia's political system and espouses centrist/conservative views. He's critical of "wokeness." The class is easy for a 400 level elective which still makes it worth taking.

Absolutely phenomenal professor! Very caring and genuinely wants everyone to do well. She gives so many resources to prepare for tests and assignments and she is always willing to meet at any time to help you understand. 10/10 recommend if you want to take an Econ elective, take it with her.

The class was difficult for me, but the resources she provides are amazing. The homework and exercise problems are great preparation for exams. She is very sweet, caring, and accommodating, always ready to help if you have questions about a topic.

The class is somewhat difficult but as long as you study and go to class you will be okay. Vaidyanathan is clear on what can be covered on the test and the practice exams prepare you well for the exams. Overall she is a great professor and wants to make sure everyone understands the material. Just don't slack off.

The content, in my opinion, gets harder and harder, so it's better to get high score at the first two midterms. She applied curves at the end. Really good class, but takes good effort to get an A. Also the final in non-cumulative.
Enjoyable class, learned a lot. Great teaching style. Only complaint is can cold call and if you don't know the content she's asking about thats a hard situation to be in.

For the Econ 420 class, the lecture is ok, but not very clear. The exam is long and difficult. The exam time is less than 75 minutes, which is too rushed for students to finish in time. Although she curves a lot on the exam, it still makes students feel unprepared for the class. However, Professor Chao is a very nice person, caring about students.

The exams are completely different from the lectures and homework. To make it worse, multiple-choice questions can have up to two correct answers. I fail to see the educational value of setting up assessments this way.

She's nice, but she can't teach. The course is unclear, quiz answers on Canvas conflicted with the answer key, there was basically no feedback. The slides aren't helpful, and the exams were so hard and too rushed. She rarely grades homework on time.
She is kind but be sure to see her outside of class for the best understanding of things because her lectures can be confusing

Having exams worth 70% of your grade is unreasonable when lectures are unclear, the slides are filled with AI and memes, and the textbook offers little support. The exams don't reflect what's taught.

She is kind and accessible outside of class, but I don't think that makes up for what you'd get out of it.

Sets clear expectations for tests and assignments and provides a list of topics that will be on each test. There's always an in-class review before the test, and Verdier always paused to make sure everyone understood the lessons. Clearly wants you to succeed, and if you study well, you will easily finish with an A in the class.
Very good teacher. Lectures are engaging and useful. Tests are honestly pretty easy if you study all of the materials; generally, the test averages were ~92%.
I guarantee you guys that all your effort will pay off. Not a challenging class, if you study hard, u will get an A.
Goat of Econometrics. Take class with him and enjoy an A
Approachable and explain the lecture in detail. It's easy to follow up if you forgot what u learned in the past.

The content of the class was pretty easy but he's such a bad teacher it made it more difficult. Genuinely learned more from the TAs.
He is a nice guy, and grades pretty fairly, and he added a pretty big curve at the end though.

Gary is a VERY nice guy. However, he taught us like we were phD students and I took nothing away from this class. All lectures were power points and there was 0 practice(besides extra textbook questions which were unhelpful). Help in the aide center was not helpful in any way either. If you want to learn 410 or even get a good grade, do NOT take.

Funny and knowledgeable guy indeed, but a bad professor. He literally taught us the same way he taught his phd students, you can't understand anything from his lecture. Do yourself a favor and take msa's econ 410 plz.

Very tough class. Tests make up 90% of the overall grade.

The homework was barely useful and the lectures were hard to follow.

The professor lied about adding curves and completely changed how he weighted the exams in the last few weeks. Also, I did not see a single grade posted until the final grade was posted in ConnectCarolina.

Great professor. Pretty funny guy. The exams are not too difficult but the grading is ROUGH. Believe this is partly from the TA who pretty much took over the class when it comes to grades. Class averages on exams were usually in the mid 60s, and the curve was around 3 points. Would have been a much better class if there was a different TA.

Professor Gong was a great professor because she actually put effort into teaching rather than the flipped classroom style of some other econ classes. Problems challenged us and really tested our understanding of the material without being "trick questions." I loved this class and thought it was relevant to contemporary policy problems.

Dr. Gong is one of the best econ professors I've had. You can clearly tell that she is passionate about the subject, and mentions real life applications. If you're confused on anything, Dr. Gong will clarify everything during office hours. The class isn't as easy as some claim, and you do have to put in effort.
Easiest Econ elective, very few homeworks. Fair exams.

Dr. Gong is a really great professor and lenient grader. She really wants you to do well which is uncommon in UNC Econ. Best professor I have had in UNC Econ not because I did well but she is so passionate about teaching. This is good to see and something that UNC Econ needs more of.

Excellent econ professor, wants you to learn and that's it. This was the easiest 500-level econ class ever; great choice for an easy semester but still worth taking seriously because the material/skills are important and Dr. Gong really cares a lot.

He is a good professor itself, but the classes have nothing to do with the test. Both mid terms and final exams were completely different to what he gave in class as "practice exercise for exams". He is not very good at answering emails. Notes no provided/slides. You either go to class or you dont have anything to study from
Unprofessional

He provides very little material for an incredibly challenging course. All of his lectures are on blackboard and are not recorded, and he doesn't post lecture notes. Very arbitrary grading for assignments, he never provides clear rubrics or outlines. Extremely hard tests that required huge curves. Overall very unpleasant class and poor professor.

Prof. Francis is a nice professor and explains topics well. 
He is lecture heavy, and doesn't provide a lot of material besides the lecture. I would recommend attending the lecture and keeping track of what he is saying, because he might announce something, or highlight a topic and if you miss it, it's gone.

For a 500-level econ class, this one wasn't that bad. Neville's lectures are pretty confusing at times, but if you study and do the practice problems you'll be okay. He curves the grades, and if you come to class and show that you're trying, he won't let you fail. He's not very accessible outside of class and often won't respond to emails.
LOL easy class. just study by yourself, no need to go to class.

It was straightforward and largely reiterated the material from ECON 420. Most students didn't attend lectures since the textbook covered the content just as well. Simply taking notes, completing the practice exam, and doing some studying was more than enough to ace the class. There were also a few homework assignments, all of which were easy!

This course really deserves a different professor given how important and applicable the content is for economics. Lectures are extremely uninteresting and provide no incentive to actually come to lecture and learn information - readings are better than lecturers. Not a lot of communication and you're on the hook for this class. Take with diff prof

Genuinely one of the laziest professors I have had in the econ department. Like, not once in the entire course did she pick up a marker or use an apple pencil to write something out. She straight up walks into class a minute before it starts, and shuts her laptop and bolts out of the room the minute class is over. Arbitrary grader. 0/10
If you want to experience the best courses and professors in the UNC econ department, choose her!
LOL easy class. just study by yourself, no need to go to class.

It was straightforward and largely reiterated the material from ECON 420. Most students didn't attend lectures since the textbook covered the content just as well. Simply taking notes, completing the practice exam, and doing some studying was more than enough to ace the class. There were also a few homework assignments, all of which were easy!

This course really deserves a different professor given how important and applicable the content is for economics. Lectures are extremely uninteresting and provide no incentive to actually come to lecture and learn information - readings are better than lecturers. Not a lot of communication and you're on the hook for this class. Take with diff prof

Genuinely one of the laziest professors I have had in the econ department. Like, not once in the entire course did she pick up a marker or use an apple pencil to write something out. She straight up walks into class a minute before it starts, and shuts her laptop and bolts out of the room the minute class is over. Arbitrary grader. 0/10
If you want to experience the best courses and professors in the UNC econ department, choose her!
unclear expectations for class projects. not considerate of students.

Professor Babii is clearly very well-versed in the topics of this class. However, he is an extremely boring lecturer. His slides are long and extremely detailed, but his monotone way of presenting them doesn't emphasize the importance of actually important details. His homeworks actually take forever. Extreme bias towards R over python.

I loved this class. He does a great job keeping the class interesting. Homeworks are long, but helped a lot to prepare for the research project and final exam. They are doable as long as you do the labs. Professor Babbi is well-organized and his class has good structure with detailed references to textbook chapters.

Prof. Babii's lectures rely on textbook slides. Homework takes forever, though beneficial, but occasionally covers untaught material. He's often very disorganized, and not very communicative. Also, our research project lacked clear expectations. Previous exams for reviews are good, and this is an interesting class, it just needs better structure.
One of the most useful Econ classes. The material is hard but he makes it doable. Professor Babii was very helpful with the research project throughout the semester.

Professor Peter is the best Economics professor I've had at UNC. Her class was very well structured to account for the stressful times that senior year can cause (recruiting season, etc.). Very Stata heavy but if you did well with coding in 470 it shouldn't be too difficult. Highly recommend.
Dr. Peter is very knowledgable in labor economics. She really wants everyone in her class to succeed. Very approachable outside of class, and offers generous help with Stata coding.
Best professor in the econ department!! She really cares about her students.

I rarely leave RMP reviews but for Prof. Hendricks, I will, simply because he is one of the best Economics professors at UNC. Prof. Hendricks is someone who really wants you to understand the intuition behind the macro models. He's extremely patient, helpful and accessible outside class hours. Econ520 was so smooth and easy with Prof. Hendricks.

While his lectures were a little bit dull, if you attend class and pay attention you should be ok overall for the class. He was really accessible and willing to help you with the two assignments, and has a really relaxed grading scale which makes it easier to do well in his class. Overall, not the worst Econ 500 level course you can take.

Although the lectures can sometimes be a bit dull, Prof. Hendricks is very clear in what he wants you to know. He is a fair grader, the class and the curve the class is graded on is very lenient. He also gives help if asked for it. Genuinely not a bad 500 level at all.
Great guy, makes macro easily understandable. The lectures can be boring but theres only a few grades and he really wants you to do well, so he helps a bunch. Really interesting class
He is an amazing professor, he tries his best to make the material easier. He is a very easy grader, if you put a little bit of effort, he will give you the points. Very easy.
Covers a lot of basic economic concepts using relevant examples. He was very organized and thorough in his lectures but required a lot of readings before class, even though only a few were covered.

He is great in teaching those concepts. But I would say his part was the hardest in the entire IDST 111 class. You have to answer the question from all perspective to get a good score. But if you pay attention to class and do your homework, it will be an easy A

Professor McManus did a great job covering the Economics portion of IDST 111. Although I already had ECON 101 credit from getting a 5 on AP Micro and Macroeconomics, I learned a lot of new things and fields within economics from his lectures. The only downside is he does take off points if your answers are not exactly what he is looking for.

I loved how concise he always was during his lectures and he does engage the class. He is very willing to help but won't just give you an answer straight off the bat because he's trying to make sure you learn. Note that the grading criteria can be more specific for short answer questions than question prompt asks.

Do not take the PPE triple I with Brian. His multiple choice questions are confusing and he grades open responses harshly. Lectures can be confusing. Take a different triple I. Taking PPE with Brian will wreck your GPA. Do not recommend

Dr. Williams cares a lot about teaching and is a great lecturer. I wish there was more time to hear him talk about IO, but 70% of the course grade is based on a group project, so he knows that we know that that's the focus of the class. He is a compassionate teacher and grades fairly (if a little on the forgiving side).

The class does involve a lot of independent learning as not much is taught in lectures. It is also almost all group work. However, Prof. Williams is an incredibly nice guy and he is always willing to help if you come to office hours and lend a hand. He wants you to do well even if you might not understand a lot at first. Overall, good experience.
Can he please update his slides for Econ410. You can tell he would take it as a compliment if half of his students failed his class. Not a girls girl

He was not helpful. During meetings regarding my project topics, when i asked him what would be his advice be, he pretty much throw the question back at me. He was pretty condescending and tried to act as if he was being as helpful as he could. His lectures regarding coding were not clear either.
Will is an awesome professor. He makes the topics really engaging, and his slides are very useful. The exams were really manageable, and the content is genuinely fascinating
Will is an awesome professor. He makes the topics really engaging, and his lecture slides are extremely useful for studying. Certain

Professor Jungerman is clearly a very smart person and knows so much about macroeconomics. However, this doesn't translate to good teaching. He cannot explain any math derivations and that's what most of the 150 slides in each of the lectures are. He's very slow to post practice problems and answer keys were missing solutions.

Will is a very solid instructor. His command of macroeconomic theory and its application in current and past research makes for genuinely engaging lectures. The content itself is high level, but the exams were easier compared to all the content discussed. I really think it would be hard to teach this class with a better way than Will's approach

Worst class I ever took at UNC. Graded almost completely on exams with no homework or relevant practice material given. William didn't proctor the exams so we couldn't ask him questions. Giant slide decks full of impossible derivations and no clear expectations about what we are supposed to know for tests. Unresponsive to questions and emails.

No attendance but you should show up bc she explains topics in depth you won't understand as thoroughly by reading the slides. Truly cares about you understanding the material. The workload is very manageable, haven't needed the textbook. Her shoe game is subtly strong and her resume is impressive (she was on Biden's Council of Economic Advisers).

Chari is the best econ professor I've had, by a longshot. She has yet to be corrupted by the denizens running the econ dept. She gives ample extra credit, actually cares about us learning, and has the professional experience to back her up. I didn't take 460, and I did well despite putting in minimal effort. Highly recommend taking 560 with her.
Having him for math camp is like a match made in heaven :)

Dr. Sturgill is a very miserly, nitpicky, and unaccommodating professor. He expects you to know the content after hearing it once. On exams, he asks overly specific questions and will deduct more points during regrade requests. He takes an eternity to write and grade HWs and will not respond to Ed posts. Do not be fooled by his happy demeanor. -HY
very stubborn. Don't understand any kind of issue he will just go with his opinion. If it is possible please don't take him. Trust me by taking him you will loose your mental peace.

He don't care about his students. It doesn't matter if your GPA gets affect and class average is really low for all the exams he will stick to his " I don't believe in free points so I won't curve" and not open to accept any alternative or extra credit. Grades everything so hard. Sometimes he do negative grading too. Please avoid him!!

I enjoyed Operating Systems with Dr. Sturgill. The course is challenging and he holds his students to a high standard, but I never felt unprepared. He is an excellent and knowledgeable lecturer. I recommend this course if you want a rigorous OS experience and are ready for the time commitment. You will leave feeling like you mastered the material.

For an exercise I put down (example only) 1100 1111 instead of 11001111 and got no credits for it. Asked him for a regrade since it is otherwise identical answer and clearly showed that I understood the concept. He looked into it and said he won't give credits. Don't bother wasting your time with him. Dude doesn't care about his students.

She seems nice, but I do not like the lectures. Fast-paced, lots of info presented, and not getting enough from them. Projects take a lot of time, and they have components with deadlines during exam week. I wish I had started P1 sooner. Thankfully, we were allowed a partner for the final project.

If you go to every class and take good notes then you can stay on top but if you miss even one class be prepared to do a lot of work to catch back up. The projects are not hard but the instructions are unclear, so you will often spend many hours rewriting code, because the instructions and the staff test code expectations can be different.

Easy class with two lab projects constituting bulk of the grade. Midterm and final were each 15% only. Dr. Heckman lectures a lot with several participation activities in class. Attendance is mandatory.

Dr. Heckman is a good lecturer but she moves really fast. However, the class is really just hard. There's project milestones and lab deadlines on the same week as an exam.

Project directions also contain typos, confusing language, and broken links. And not to mention the MILLION research studies they keep hounding you to participate in.

The course has been difficult to follow because the lectures rarely align with the lab work, leaving many concepts unclear. The instructor's explanations feel scattered, and her interactions with students often come across as curt, which makes the class atmosphere uncomfortable and discouraging.

Balik provides everything you need to be successful in this course and it's clear she genuinely wants everyone to do well. The only downside is that the lecture is a bit slow and dry but if you make sure you are caffeinated she moves at the perfect pace for you to take notes and actually absorb information during lecture.

lectures are required but its so hard to learn from them, she is unorganized, doesnt record the lectures, and makes the slides impossible to study. 
waits until last minute to grade big projects so theres nothing you can do for your grade if it drops. confusing and unorganized project requirements. exam two is a GIANT jump of topics after the first
She just sucks as a teacher and person. do not take her class and nc state should of fired her 500 years ago
I didn't wear a seatbelt on my way to class. If you can infer what I mean.

The lectures were dry but fortunately the class itself is pretty easy so it doesn't really matter. Tons of homework and a mandatory online textbook. Cares a lot about her students but the tests/projects are graded really harsh. I would recommend Balik if you are new to Java, but if you are already familiar you'll be very bored.

12 Weekly coding workshops will take anywhere from ~2-5hrs. Very straightforward instructions. His slides are good for studying. Tests are very similar to workshop-written activities. I struggled initially, but got the hang of it. Semester project (proposal, coding, reflection), but it is spread out nicely. He is a cool dude who uses Kahoot a lot.

Dr. King is a caring professor teaching a challenging course, and student frustration with the material is sometimes unfairly directed at him. While his lecture style wasn't my personal preference, he is a good professor who puts real effort into making DSA engaging. This is an important course, and the effort you put into it will pay off.

Dr. King is a solid lecturer and provides recordings of all the lectures so you can watch them at any time. The workshops can be a lot of work but are manageable and helpful for studying. The class moves quickly, so it's important to stay on top of the assignments. The tests are long for the amount of time given, so you need to work efficiently.

Dr. King is a good lecturer and very knowledgeable, but is overly strict especially about academic integrity. The class is very fast-paced with lots of content to learn. Exams have too much content and not enough time, so memorization is key. Workshops will take up time but will be your best friend. Beware of the MGR and form good study groups. -HY

Very fair professor, knows we have an insane amount of content and does give a lot of work. But the assignments aren't super hard and aren't strictly graded. And we get late penalty waivers. Tests not too hard and they are fair. Great class organization. You must put in a lot of work, and I caution taking with CSC 230. But King is great for 316.

The flipped style of the classroom renders the excruciatingly long and boring lecture period for the class to be entirely unnecessary. No one pays attention during lecture. Also, in our class, Dr. Battestilli wasnt great with technology aside from Matlab.

Dr. B genuinely cares about her students. The lectures are long and labs are longer, but the work given is good practice and prepares you for exams. It is helpful to come in with previous coding knowledge, but is not necessary. The exams were fair, labs and weekly "graders" were helpful, and projects were difficult but you can work with partners.

She loves to say that flipped classrooms help the students learn, but all the material she gave us was pointless. The labs and the TAs were the only reason I understood the class at all. She tries to be motivational, but no one pays attention during lectures because they get nothing out of it other than participation. Attend the online section.

Dr. B truly cares about her students and has awesome TAs. The labs are long and boring, but they're relatively easy. Projects are significantly harder, but you can work with partners if you want. With it being such a big class, it takes a long time to get grades back, but that's not a huge deal to me. Lectures are boring and not too helpful to me.

I have no personal issues with Dr. B, however the class setup for CSC113 is egregiously mind-numbing. Despite it being a 3 credit hour class, the amount of work you have to do outside of class (projects, online learning, mini coding work) takes up your whole week. Projects are long and can be hard. I feel she expects too much for a beginner class.

This is one of the only sections that requires you to buy the $100 textbook for your grade. If you're shooting for an A+, you need to buy the textbook. Getting an A without the textbook is doable, but I strongly recommend finding a different section.
Very useless class. Very easy though. Nothing against the professor that much, she and Dr. Adams were occasionally funny. But just work on other assignments during the lectures.

This class was really good. And you will learn a lot about DSA that is directly relevant to LeetCode problem-solving. He provides generous grading and curving. Best of all, he teaches really well, and if you follow the class (no mandatory attendance policy), you should be able to get an A grade easily.

Extremely high-quality class and one of the best professors at NCSU. The tests make up most of the grade and they are definitely not easy but with curving and generous grading its more than fair.  Take thorough notes, especially when he steps through an algo in class and be familiar with algebra and simplifying terms like 2^log_2(n)

The professor is very passionate about the subject, and extremely clear about the grading, homework, quizes and final exam. It's an interesting and scoring subject if you study regularly. The professor even gives mock exam papers with solutions before mid term and final exams, so the students can prepare and have an idea on what to expect in exam.

Prof Heber is really passionate about the subject which can be seen through his enthusiasm and teaching style. Would definitely recommend taking CSC 505 under him in the Fall semester (some other prof teaches it in the Spring semester).

He Included a lot of mathematics in the course that was kinda unnecessary but overall really helped clear some of the basic concepts for CSC505 and liked his teaching style, also liked the fact that attendance was not mandatory and I could just watch the recordings.

The course includes group projects and homework requiring consistent effort. Grading policies are strict, including penalties for unsuccessful regrade requests. Lecture style may not be engaging for all students

Her strict "no partial credit" policy is frustrating - she probably thinks wrong answers mean you don't understand, but I was swamped with other coursework, even making presentations the night before exams with little sleep. While generally decent, this harsh grading dropped my letter grade penalizing me juggling multiple commitments.

Dr. Chi is a kind person and good lecturer, but I cannot emphasize enough how terrible the TAs are. Regardless of how many times a question is asked, the TAs reply: "find it in the lectures somewhere," even if the question cannot be answered by the lectures. This would be a great class if the TAs would bother providing the slightest bit of help.

Professor is very knowledgeable, but a tough grader. This is not the hardest class I took in my Masters, but it is the lowest grade I've gotten. TA's fairly useless, telling people to "check the lecture" on questions where people confirmed they already checked. Grading is all-or-nothing. One rounding error I made in a homework lost me 30 points.

Dr. Chi's class is entirely group oriented, except, conveniently, the exams. All your grades, apart from exams, will be shared with your group despite supposedly half the presentation being graded on how well you presented as an individual. Professor is also profoundly lazy when it comes to grading, specifically pertaining to how she grades.

Hate is a strong word, and as a child of God, I really try not to use it toward anyone, but this woman tested every bit of that restraint. She is completely disorganized and barely teaches. This class was an absolute waste of a semester and a total waste of my time.

Many students take Prof. Kemafor's class only to fulfill credit requirements, not because she's a  great professor. She lacks teaching skills and professionalism. I even heard she once asked a student for a picture of her medicine bottle and purchase receipt, even though she already provided a work note. This is privacy violation! Such a shame.

Worst professor. Always unprepared, unresponsive, and doesn't seem to care about her students. Instead of focusing on teaching well and making the course enjoyable and rewarding, she's more concerned with questioning students integrity. She even scans students' exam paper to compare what they wrote before and after restroom break during exam.

Class consisted of 10 hws, 3 tests and a final. Given an online book and lectures given from 2+ years ago. Tests are all written response. Not a terribly hard class but given so little material, resources, and grading criteria. It makes it 2x to 3x harder and more time consuming than a normal discrete class. Doable if you put in the time and effort
